title: Citrus Botanique cologne - a fragrance for men 2011
description: Citrus by Botanique is a Citrus fragrance for men. Citrus was launched in 2011. The nose behind this fragrance is Armando Alonzo. The fragrance features Mandarin Orange, Calone, Yuzu, Orange and Bergamot.

reviews.name: killer_blow
reviews.comment: Citrus by Botanique is the definitive citrus fragrance. A men's cologne that is versatile enough to be worn by women too. It's casual enough to be worn everyday, with good sillage and longevity.

reviews.name: perfumed_garden
reviews.comment: This is a great summer fragrance that smells fresh, light and hmmm, happy? No wonder it gets compared to Clinique Happy for Men but I think it also smells a lot like Body Shop's Satsuma cologne. Both have the same cucumber-watery freshness and the brightness of mandarin oranges. There's also a lot of green notes here which is not detailed in the fragrance notes, and a hint of jasmine too. I think this fragrance can be worn by both men and women, which probably owes to its popularity.

title: Citrus L'acqua Di Fiori perfume - a fragrance for women 2011
description: Citrus by L'acqua Di Fiori is a Citrus Aromatic fragrance for women. Citrus was launched in 2011. The fragrance features Lime, Bergamot, Lemon, Pink Pepper and Amberwood.

title: Citrus fuwally perfume - a fragrance for women and men
description: Citrus by fuwally is a Citrus fragrance for women and men. Top notes are Lemon, Grapefruit, Green Apple and Pear; middle notes are Lily of the Valley, Rose and Jasmine; base notes are Raspberry, Peach, Cedar and Musk. Citrus by fuwally is a fragrance designed for both women and men. The scent features a blend of fresh citrus and fruity notes in the opening, floral elements at the heart, and a soft, woody-musky base.

title: Citrus L'Erbolario perfume - a fragrance for women and men 2009
description: Citrus by L'Erbolario is a Citrus fragrance for women and men. Citrus was launched in 2009. Top notes are Pink Grapefruit, Grapefruit, Blood Orange, Lime, Yellow Mandarin, Yuzu, Buchu or Agathosma and Orange Blossom; middle notes are Galbanum, Pineapple, Ylang-Ylang, Pear, Gardenia, Plum, Red Apple, Peach and Cyclamen; base notes are Cassis, Lemon Leaf and Palisander Rosewood. Citrus was introduced in 2009 as a mixture of beautiful essences of agrums, mandarin, lime and pink grapefruit. Besides EDT, the accompanying collection includes body care products and soap.

thisPerfumeRemindsMeOf.title: Acqua di Parma Blu Mediterraneo Arancia di Capri

reviews.name: LuluCoconut
reviews.comment: This is the scent of happiness. The line is getting discontinued now, such a pity (according to a local SA). A summer stapple for me, this EDT embodies both a promising sunny morning and the joy of a surprise summer rain. Not very long lasting, but soooo enjoyable. Refreshing, citrusy, green, bitter-sweet, but not too sweet. Only positive reactions so far, and I've been wearing it for years every summer, complemented by the other products in the line, including a home scent.

reviews.name: naturelle
reviews.comment: LOVE IT! Please L'Erbolario complete the whole Citrus line!!!

reviews.name: anna.dkr5
reviews.comment: @lizwikis: I know the longevity sucks. I liberally spray it on my clothes, and it lasts longer. It's a happy scent that puts me in a good mood. :)

reviews.name: lizwykys
reviews.comment: Wow! The first splash of this completely wins me over with the brilliant grapefruit presence that I totally crave... especially in hot weather. Sadly, on me that subsides way too soon, and the drydown is a lot less exciting, dialing back into just a vaguely pleasant very low-key generic citrus scent. This is so close to almost being the sparkly grapefruity, citrusy fragrance of my summer dreams. (This is from a sample received with a recent order of another L'Erbolario scent.)

reviews.name: anna.dkr5
reviews.comment: A spark of energy! Fresh and invigorating. It puts a smile on my face. Out of all the fragrances (be they cheapies or high-end) I've tried, this is one of the few that seriously boost my mood. :) The notes are very well-blended. Two hours after applying it I can even detect a faint, but definitely noticeable, creamy sweetness (like french vanilla) mixed with the citrus. Sillage is soft/moderate, and longevity is where this fragrance falls short. I got about 4 hours on my skin, but it's a really good and affordable scent. If it lasted longer I'd probably make it my signature. I'd still like to get the body lotion and see if layering does the trick. Fingers crossed! :) UPDATE: I still haven't got the body lotion, but I've tried spraying this on cothes, and the longevity has definitely increased. :)

reviews.name: 5354alice
reviews.comment: I had it: it's very refreshing, energizing and make you feel happy! In Italy Erbolario is really common and cheap...so it's a relly good choice.

reviews.name: adriana.rizzati
reviews.comment: Fantastic! Crispy and refreshing grapefruit and lime at the beginning, it develops with a gentle, sweet floral-fruity blend of mandarin, peach, pear, red apple, plum, ananas, ylang ylang, gardenia, cyclamen, cassia, rosewood... and finally it comes back to be sparkling with lemon zest and black currant notes! I wouldn't define it an aggressive scent, but... after the exuberant earliest notes, I think it's a very mild citrus, with a vibrant but gentle character at the same time. Very fresh, charming and feminine: delicious! I love Citrus, it reminds me a lot a perfume I used to wear ten years ago, which drove many people crazy... An outstanding fragrance for the hottest days, but I cannot wait for the summer to wear it!! One note to Fragrantica: Citrus is an EDP, not an EDT.

reviews.name: melia
reviews.comment: Waaah, sour, bitter! Al kind of citrus-skins mixed together! Strong and really something extraordenary... But I NEVER could have it near my skin again. Just not a perfume.. It's 'a scent'. But if you're looking for a interesting take on citrus, try it.

reviews.name: Enchantra
reviews.comment: I had it and it was fantastic, especially in summer. I noticed that men who casually came in contact with this essence, found it a little bit agressive for women...

reviews.name: nanano
reviews.comment: It is real cold grapefruit juice in very hot summer day. Last about 3 ours, but i like to put it on myself along the day.

title: Citrus Korres perfume - a fragrance for women 2020
description: Citrus by Korres is a Aromatic Aquatic fragrance for women. Citrus was launched in 2020. Top notes are Aquatic notes, Fruits and Citrus; middle note is Mint; base note is Musk.

title: Citrus Samouraï cologne - a fragrance for men 2021
description: Citrus by Samouraï is a Citrus Aromatic fragrance for men. Citrus was launched in 2021. Top note is Lemon; middle notes are Geranium and Peach; base notes are Cedarwood and Musk. Citrus by Samouraï is a fragrance for men, marketed by Lalique Group / Art & Fragrance. This scent features a refreshing blend of lemon in the top notes, complemented by geranium and peach in the heart, and grounded by cedarwood and musk in the base.

title: Citrus Brasilis Granado perfume - a new fragrance for women and men 2025
description: Citrus Brasilis by Granado is a Citrus fragrance for women and men. This is a new fragrance. Citrus Brasilis was launched in 2025. The nose behind this fragrance is Quentin Bisch. Top notes are Mandarin Orange, Lemon and Bergamot; middle notes are Jasmine, Kumquat and Mint; base notes are Musk, Violet Leaves and Patchouli. Citrus Brasilis is a true celebration of the joy and freshness of citrus notes, which have been an essential part of Granado’s history since its beginnings. Since its founding, when José Antônio Coxito cultivated a variety of citrus fruits in his orchard, these fruits were transformed into medicines, such as aromatic orange blossom water, compound lemon spirit and bitter orange peel syrup. Today, citrus fruits take center stage in an intense and energizing creation. The top notes of bergamot, mandarin and lemon are an invitation to freshness. A delicate floral touch balances the composition, while musk envelops the fragrance in comfort and sophistication.

pros.label: Clean, elegant, and sophisticated unisex scent
Non-offensive and safe for any environment
Versatile for office and daily wear
Smooth blend of mandarin and jasmine
Excellent for layering with other perfumes
Sophisticated alternative to Hermès Le Jardin

cons.label: Significant recent price increases
Performance fails Parfum concentration expectations
Scent disappears quickly in some conditions
Intimate projection with low sillage
Inconsistent longevity across skin types
Lacks originality within Granado's portfolio
Perceived as overpriced for a citrus
Generic musky dry down after opening
Redundant for owners of Hermès fragrances
Cheap-looking bottle label quality

reviews.name: Amnesiac_Lex
reviews.comment: To me, this fragrance is misleading. I expected a blast of citruses, but to me it's VERY floral with a touch of fruitiness instead. I literally can't smell any of the top notes listed. The jasmine is very present and brings a bit of that indolic character, wich I don't like. Also, to me it leans very feminine. It's not a bad fragrance at all, but be sure to try it out before buying.

reviews.name: Menotti
reviews.comment: I love Citrus fragrances and living in Brazil, the Citrus Brasilis should be a “must have”. Fortunately a friend gave it to my wife and I didn’t need to go buy one. Overall it’s a good perfume and the opening it is what it adverts: very citrus, which is very promising. You kinda start to understand the hype and can’t wait to see how the perfume develops in the dry down, but here is the thing. It has a very linear development, it lacks depth! Don’t get me wrong, it is still a very good fragrance, but It gives you almost everything he promise just in the opening. The performance also could be better. If you want to use it as a signature perfume and want people to notice that you are wearing it, you will need to respray it maybe one or two times in a day. I still need to try layering with other perfumes. Maybe I didn’t explored it full potential. For now, I don’t think it worth what they are asking. I’ve seen perfumes with similar performance and quality for less. UPDATE May 20th 2026: It had been a while since the last time I wore this perfume, and today I quickly remembered why. I wasn’t in the mood for my signature scent, so I decided to give Citrus Brasilis another chance. Definitely this fragrance has absolutely no evolution whatsoever. What you get in the first five minutes is exactly what you’ll be stuck with until it finally dies. It feels incredibly lazy and one-dimensional, like the perfumer gave up halfway through the formula. By the second hour, I was already tired of smelling it and just waiting for it to become a skin scent. If it were significantly cheaper, I could maybe see it working as a safe school fragrance for kids. But at this price point?!

reviews.name: Vitobertolli
reviews.comment: Love the smell, lack perfomance based on other citrus from the same category even cheaper.

reviews.name: oscar.sinclair
reviews.comment: Picked this up earlier this year on Oscar Freire, São Paulo. A brand shaped by Quentin Bisch. Deeply rooted in Brazil, now reaching beyond. Hard to describe without the heat, the rhythm, the pulse of Brazil. 8/10 — a carioca citrus explosion. This one has to be lived. Wearing it in 30°C heat — and I’ll keep reaching for it. P.S. My daughter noticed it… even in the pool

reviews.name: Esfr
reviews.comment: Absolutely lovely, such a wonderful citrus, and develops beautifully into the jasmine and musk. When I first tried it I was disappointed with the sillage and longevity but I was in a very crowded environment, still the scent was ingrained in my memory so I ended up buying a bottle. My other go to citrus for high heat was Light Blue, which had better projection and longevity but this one is fair for a citrus. About 6 house later I can still feel it sometimes when moving my hair or if I bring my hand to my face. I recently tried it close to Jardin de Monsieur Li and they are nearly twins, although there’s some in JML that don’t quite enjoy as much as this one, but I can’t put my finger on it

reviews.name: AlchemyAge
reviews.comment: I purchased the 10 bottle tester kit from Granado, and initially this was my least favourite, but got better over time. The opening spray was citrussy, green and somewhat bitter but as the scent develops, it ends up a lot more fresh and uplifting, and I agree with the comparisons to a luxury sun tan lotion. The mandarin, kumquat and lemon are of the high quality, but the balance seems slightly off with the overall presentation and is very much a skin scent that lasts around 6 hours. Citrus Brasilis feels like a high end spa cream, its clean and fresh, but does not do anything particularly daring. I don't love it, but difficult to really dislike. Unassuming and inoffensive, a decent summery spritz that would work well as a blind reach or for those hot beach days.

reviews.name: PapaGainz
reviews.comment: Quentin Bisch cooked with this one. Its such a nice, clean, freshie. It literally smells like kumquat trees. Its fresh, green, citrusy, orange. Reminds me of when I was a kid, there was few kumquat trees growing around, I used to just go and eat the fruits. Also since its a Brazilian brand, not alot of people know about it so its not something you going to smell often. Its a unisex freshie my wife uses it all the time, smells great on her. I like to layer it with mont blanc explorer 👌🏻

reviews.name: everyonelovesbebell
reviews.comment: really well done but that's all kinda, safe citrus for a blind buy

reviews.name: arielina
reviews.comment: If you ever have the pleasure of visiting the beautiful country of Brazil, you must visit Granado and pick up a bottle of this wonderful fragrance. I was having so much trouble finding a fragrance that instantly wowed me but of course, the Virgo in me fell for this clean citrus bombshell. Citrus Brasilis is a daytime fragrance and perfect all year round. In 2024 I wore Chanel No5 Leau - Citrus Brasilis is not the same, it is much fresher and in no way powdery, but both contain lemon, bergamot and mandarin orange at the top, jasmine in the middle and musk at the bottom. So basically I’ve figured out the thing, those notes that I’ll always go back to, an experience true fragrance lovers rejoice over. I’ve been wearing this beauty since February and I’m almost out! People love this on me. I wore it to a summer evening soirée and received compliments, I wore it to work on a frigid December afternoon and received compliments, this fragrance is loved! The woman that wears Citrus Brasilis knows herself very well, she is true to herself and brings a sweet energy everywhere she goes. She’s soft and refreshing. And everyone adores her!

reviews.name: MistAurora
reviews.comment: Best, most interesting citrus fragrance ever created, hands down. Quentin has done it again

reviews.name: felipevicentinii
reviews.comment: Não acreditem em quem está falando que não tem performance. Ótima fragrância, uma bergamota muito bem trabalhada. Fixação na minha pele gira em torno de 7-8hrs e projeta relativamente bem nas primeiras 2h, passando para uma projeção ok a partir daí. Ótimo perfume para dias quentes, de praia e sol. Antes que eu me esqueça, é um ÓTIMO perfume para layering.

reviews.name: andresantos
reviews.comment: If you're into naturals and you know Quentin Bisch, you'll probably love this one. Not groundbreaking by any means, instead, calming musky orangey or mandarinesque. Not too musky to the point it's creamy, but not as sharp as well. Very uplifting after bath/shower scent that's very hard to dislike.

reviews.name: igorvida87
reviews.comment: It has the DNA from Les Eaux by Chanel combined with Nectarine Blossom and Honey from JoMalone. It smells like an expensive shampoo or a sun-lotion from L’oreal. It’s not bad if you like the fragrances I mentioned, considering the price point, but it’s not remarkable amongst Granado’s fragrances portfolio.

reviews.name: Majid Abdul Hakim
reviews.comment: Citrus Brasilis from Brasilian House Granado de Perfumery is a Citrus Floral Musky Unisex perfume. This is a new fragrance, and it was launched in January 2025. The nose behind this fragrance is Quentin Bisch. It opens quite citrusy but not sharp sharp, sharp, and with a fresh minty accord; Soon a bitterness arrives that bothered me a little but fortunately soon dissipated with the arrival of a white floral adding more freshness and leaving the fragrance very pleasant. A clean musky pleasant in drying consolidates the pleasantness of the composition as a whole; Without a doubt it is a delicious perfume! And for the cost benefit it adds a certain quality. I thought that this launch of Granado in the Parfum concentration would solve the brand's chronic problem: low performance. And that with that I would surrender and be able to acquire something from this traditional and now globalized carioca brand, but no, leaving any boastfulness aside, the performance of Citrus Brasilis is also very weak 🥴; ... even if it's just my first contact with the fragrance.

reviews.name: Su do brechó
reviews.comment: Uma amiga minha testou e disse que vai super na vibe do Le Jardin de Monsieur Li da Hermes, o meu está a caminho, logo venho contar minhas percepções!

title: Citrus Olfactive O perfume - a fragrance for women and men 2024
description: Citrus by Olfactive O is a fragrance for women and men. Citrus was launched in 2024. Top notes are Bergamot, Lemon and Champagne; middle notes are Grapefruit, Mandarin Orange and Marigold; base notes are Musk and Amber. Citrus by Olfactive O is an Eau de Parfum that embodies the essence of a free spirit, the bohemian, and the sunseeker. Inspired by the shimmering colors of champagne and the golden light of long lazy afternoons, this fragrance is reminiscent of lemon groves and the Mediterranean's spiritual warmth. It was created in the spirit of celebration after the founder, Olivia, recovered from Post Natal Depression, with a portion of the profits supporting the PANDAS Foundation UK.

peopleWhoLikeThisAlsoLike.title: Fleur de Peau Eau de Parfum

reviews.name: GrenadeGirl
reviews.comment: This is a stunning fragrance, and it lasts forever on me! The opening notes of champagne and lemon immediately surround you in this gorgeous, airy, vibrant, happy citrus cloud, and this stage actually lasts on me pretty well. The transition to the grapefruit, orange and marigold mid notes is easy and it really rounds out the scent, and even when the musk and amber base notes start to peek through, the warm citrus remains.. in fact they are all still detectable on me some 8 hours in. I really, really recommend this one - it is superb quality, and just the best citrus blend I have found!

reviews.name: Paula80
reviews.comment: This is a bright, energizing citrus scent with musky undertone to it. We have here a mix of fresh, zesty citrus fruits and fizzy champagne. The note of marigold applies a dry, slightly woody quality to the fragrance. If you ever smelled this flower, you know what I mean. It gives me a feel of warm, sun-kissed skin. Musk smooths out the whole composition and amber adds some more warmth to it. Citrus is a sparkling, fresh, uplifting and very natural smelling fragrance. It is not sweet nor sour; just perfectly balanced and very easy to wear.

title: Citrus Bath & Body Works perfume - a fragrance for women 2024
description: Citrus by Bath & Body Works is a Citrus fragrance for women. Citrus was launched in 2024.

reviews.name: SUMLIGHTZ
reviews.comment: If they sold this as a car/room spray I would not know the difference. This is as basic as it gets. It is more soapy than I expected, but it's no Demeter. The citrus notes are light and artificial. This smells a bit comparable to B&BW Cucumber Melon, although nothing will ever be as bad as that mess. I think this one is better after it's lingered in the air for a while, the first spray of this isn't too defined. @Lepx0x's description nails that portion pretty well.

reviews.name: PutITOn
reviews.comment: This stinks as body spray but it makes a good bathroom spray.

reviews.name: fanntonyoscar
reviews.comment: I don’t know why I’m not impressed with this. It’s decent enough. It just seems like so many other citrus fragrances I’ve tried… there’s nothing unique or realistic here. It’s a good spray for my car, though.

reviews.name: lepx0x
reviews.comment: I love this. First initial spray you get that salty citrus, like the smell of lemon and lime in a glass of water. As it dries down the stone fruits start to emerge and the citrus becomes a fruity citrus with a slight powdery background. The dry down is gorgeous clean, fruity, fresh with a tiny bit of sweetness! Great for the summer.

reviews.name: swedishmilk15
reviews.comment: Reminiscent of B&BW Limelight from 1996, one I've dreamt of often in the interim. Very bitter, tart opening, like Limelight, but unlike that one, dries down a bit sweet with a hint of creamy vanilla. Love it.

title: Citrus Riviera BDK Parfums perfume - a fragrance for women and men 2020
description: Citrus Riviera by BDK Parfums is a fragrance for women and men. Citrus Riviera was launched in 2020. The nose behind this fragrance is Ralf Schwieger. Top notes are Italian Lemon, Neroli, Italian Mandarin and Fig; middle notes are Watery Notes, Green Accord, Eucalyptus, Orange Blossom, Strawberry, Immortelle and Jasmine; base notes are Haitian Vetiver, White Musk, Tonka Bean and Indonesian Patchouli Leaf. "Leaving Paris, heading south, I reach the Cap d’Antibes. It’s here in this white house that I find those forgotten feelings again, of the breeze that freshens the veranda, of the warmth of the steps leading to the garden, and of the turquoise water gleaming in the pool. I lie down under the lemon trees, in the shade, and scan the blue sky, where a few clouds drift, lost. The song of the cicadas is slowly replaced by laughter. The sun’s rays stroke my face, and I am soothed; seduced by the sweetness of summer." - a note from the brand.

pros.label: Vibrant aromatic complexity beyond basic citrus
Realistic and zesty Mediterranean fruit notes
Unique aromatic lift from eucalyptus and fig

cons.label: Leans more towards masculine
Lacks distinctiveness given the high price
Opening may evoke citrus cleaning products
Polarizing bitter, metallic, or earthy herbal dry-down

thisPerfumeRemindsMeOf.title: This is Not A Blue Bottle 1.5

peopleWhoLikeThisAlsoLike.title: Acqua di Parma Blu Mediterraneo - Fico di Amalfi

reviews.name: Parmigianoparfait
reviews.comment: For lovers of fresh fragrances, Citrus Riviera could be one of the safest and most satisfying choices. I wouldn’t say there is anything truly unique here, but it is very well valanced -bright citrus, clean green notes, a subtle aquatic feeling and a polished woody musky base. It smells expensive and well made, not cheap or sharp like many fresh scents can be. Simple but elegant and very nicely tailored 7/10

reviews.name: Mistasupaman
reviews.comment: I have this one for test, I can say it will be a buy for me. I get a lot of clean white florals with some bright citrus. Decent performance as well. I am a fan!

reviews.name: Tangturnleft
reviews.comment: For me it smells like Sedley from Pdm

reviews.name: negligentgardener
reviews.comment: Beautiful. I cannot stop smelling this. Incredibly fresh, watery citrus opening softened by a subtle fog accord cocooned by a supremely comforting base of tonka, patchouli, vetiver and just a touch of musk. I reject the narrative that this is a strictly or strongly masculine fragrance and will loudly and proudly be wearing this this summer.

reviews.name: Cornhole2!
reviews.comment: I absolutely love this fragrance. Also, I don't feel like it's very strong, but one time I was wearing it two separate people literally stopped in their tracks and started sniffing the air and ask who is wearing that nice cologne? They were both about 10 ft away from me. That made me really happy to realize that even though the sillage isn't the heaviest the projection is quite admirable.

reviews.name: may_julia
reviews.comment: The opening is genuinely lovely - natural lemon zest rather than synthetic citrus, with neroli brightness adding an uplifting freshness that feels clean and effortless. Within the hour the green patchouli heart takes over, giving it a beachy, earthy quality that leans distinctly masculine. The drydown stays the course - skin close, grounded and woody. This is a well-crafted, high quality freshie that does exactly what it sets out to do - it just sits firmly in unisex-to-masculine territory. If you love clean green citrus with an outdoorsy edge this could be your summer signature. For me personally it drifts too far from my usual creamy feminine world to be a keeper, but I can absolutely appreciate the quality and the vision.

reviews.name: thutmoset
reviews.comment: At first, I didn’t like it. It was weakly performing without the advertised fruitiness. After 1 year it matured and now it’s one of my favorite summer fragrances.

reviews.name: MoMarie_
reviews.comment: Lovely bright spring/summer spicy citrus. Leans more masc; would prefer it on my husband, but am still finding it cheery and enjoyable to wear as a sample myself for the day.

reviews.name: thecellophaneflower
reviews.comment: I'm confused by the reviews that say this is a straight lemon citrus scent. I smell something gently spicy right off the bat. I definitely smell the fig, vetiver, and patchouli -- supporting the fresh lemon and mandarin. Leans masculine and the citrus is the star, but there is some depth. Also -- decent longevity for a freshy.

reviews.name: Glassjawfan13
reviews.comment: This one was pretty disappointing synthetic almost warm citrus scent gives off soapy floor cleaner vibes 6.4/10

reviews.name: Hoax42
reviews.comment: This was a failed blind buy for me. I do like the scent, but I found it to be too soapy. I think if I was physically on the beach it would be great, but walk down the street and you smell like a lemon clorox wipe. It does get better after an hour but not for long.

reviews.name: Jax22
reviews.comment: I like the opening which is fresh zesty citrus not the bright type but interesting but after an hour the dry down comes which some how takes away that liking factor and I don't enjoy it ,there are many other options much better and interesting, mine is available for sale full bottle if someone is interested in Europe ? It's missing about 5 ml

reviews.name: 2000sLover
reviews.comment: I kind of like this! Its super zesty, refreshing, slightly salty and ozonic. It just works, simple as.

reviews.name: thutmoset
reviews.comment: Citrus Riviera, much like premium wine, becomes better with time.

reviews.name: Frok
reviews.comment: I enjoy this scent, very citrus, while in the dry down I get some sort of animalic note, i belive it’s most likely from the patchouli. I don’t get any strawberry (which i was kinda looking forward to) so that’s kinda sad. I actually think this is not worth the price point if I am going to be completely transparent.

reviews.name: Gaberade
reviews.comment: this smells like how I would imagine Steve Harwell from smash mouth would smell.

reviews.name: marrna
reviews.comment: So BDK said “Azur”, but forgot the damn ocean. This isn't the Mediterranean. It's the lemon grove next to it. And someone’s hungover, shirtless, and pissed, ferally squeezing citrus into their lemon-green detox drink like it’s punishment for last night. The opening’s brutal. No soft lemon invigoration here. It’s a limoncello bitch-slap. Amalfi mob style: sharp, fast, and coming for your throat. 🍋🔪 Italian lemon and mandarin hit like you just called their mama a llama. Tart. Acidic. Straight-up aggressive. Neroli shows up next, dragging you deeper into that lemon grove with her bitter green claws. The fig is nice. Green. Milky. But barely there, whimpering. As if it knows it’s not making it out alive in this citrus turf war. And that “Strawberry Neo Jungle Essence” on their website? What's that even? Lol. A new Marvel villain? Whatever marketing spin that was, I smelled no strawberries. Just a citrus garden on steroids. Like a juicer exploded. BDK, spill… did you even *try* to make this aquatic? Where’s the salt? The marine air? The blue? 🌊 This was supposed to be at least a little oceanic, but we got no sea breeze, no calone, no waves. Just lemon. And more lemon. The bottle screams yacht party, but the juice shrieks lemon furniture polish with a eucalyptus chaser. After that punchy opening, it fizzles fast. They call it an EdP, but it performs like a cologne on life support. Short burst. Rapid fade. I had to spray it thirty-three times just to get it to exist. It dries down into soft white musk, hedione haze, and pale woods. Very CK One. Or your ex’s aftershave. But if I wanted to think of my ex, I’d dig through old texts and pour bleach in my eyes. 📱💀 Not spray this. Now onto the better part. Where this would smell criminal on a man's skin. But not just any man. The man. The kind of guy who showers with the door open and steps out wearing nothing but a towel and a smirk. He doesn’t rush. Just keeps prowling the house, tall and tanned, towel still slung low on his hips, steam rising off his chest as he pads barefoot across cool travertine tiles. He mists more on his arms. Then his collarbones. His whole body. Tosses the bottle onto the marble counter like he’s got five more. And when his girl walks by? The towel drops. Casually. But not accidentally. It’s a dare. Yeah. That’s Citrus Riviera’s final form. Not the climax. The tease. Just citrus and the heat of a man who knows he’s the fantasy. Bottom line: it’s totally masculine-leaning, light-bodied, and smells like money and minimal commitment. That said, I believe I’m one of the few women who can actually pull this off. And no, @liamjordan, I’m nothing like what you described. I’m worse. But lucky for you, I’m not on Tinder. I wore it to a club the other night. Some girl clocked the scent, turned expecting a 6’1 finance bro, and got me instead. All iced-out, lips curled in amusement. Her face? Glitched. The disappointment. I winked and bit my lip, just for the hell of it. She full-on short-circuited. ⚠️⚡ Ahem, zooming out of that scene, still not full bottle-worthy for me. But if a moody brunette ever insists on recreating a towel-drop moment on a slow Sunday, I might reconsider. I don’t want to wear it. I want him to.

reviews.name: LoafyLady
reviews.comment: Lemon Clorox wipes. I liked the wet spray but as soon as it dried down it was the lingering lemon Clorox smell after cleaning. And I love my citruses. Ugh.

reviews.name: PerfumeCurator14
reviews.comment: 2/5 Whew, the name did not lie. If you don’t LOVE citrus—like, “slice a lemon and snort it” love—walk away. Super sharp, borderline astringent, and not balanced enough to feel luxurious. Smells like summer… but in the tough love way. ✅ Fresh heads only 🚫 Not for soft, creamy girlies

reviews.name: J_TheWombKilla
reviews.comment: Lemon rind is tart and the eucalyptus lifts the scent well. However, I wish there was more to it. More vetiver, more fig, something.. anything to elevate this from just good to very good. Mark Hesterman from Coastal Elites wears this.

reviews.name: Patsy8
reviews.comment: I wanted something classy but fresh as my usual taste leans towards stronger evening perfumes . I absolutely love this scent it conjures up the Italian Riviera every time I wear it, it projects well in the first few hours before it settles closer to the skin, a mix of citrus and aromatic notes . I feel it’s very unisex , and perfect for spring and hot summer days, just beautiful, fresh and sophisticated.

reviews.name: ws1482
reviews.comment: Smells really strange. It's the eucalyptus that's ruining this fragrance for me

reviews.name: Milan78
reviews.comment: Citrus rivera is a nice freshie, I find it quite unique for a citrus fragrance, and would recommend it , if you buy it on discounter site. I paid about 135 usd on fragrancebuy.ca

reviews.name: lukeknight
reviews.comment: Opening is really fresh lemon citrus, then after 10 minutes I get a slight cleaning solution / toilet puck citrus vibe for the next hour or so, but it's not offensive. Dries down into a creamier floral citrus which is quite nice. Performance isn't great.

reviews.name: scentbar
reviews.comment: Citrus Riviera by BDK - A Sunlit Escape to the Mediterranean, a fresh, luminous, and invigorating fragrance, It captures the essence of sun-drenched citrus groves, salty sea breezes, and the lush greenery of coastal gardens. A scent of effortless elegance, it is both vibrant and nostalgic—a memory of summer lingering on the skin. The Perfume Journey - The fragrance opens with a radiant blend of Moroccan neroli, Italian mandarin, and lemon, creating a crisp, sparkling introduction. As it unfolds, the heart reveals Moroccan orange blossom, jasmine, strawberry, eucalyptus, and immortelle, adding floral warmth and depth. The dry-down is soft and sensual, where white musks, Indonesian patchouli, Haitian vetiver, and tonka bean weave a lingering embrace. The Fragrance Vibe - My Story - Leaving Paris, driving south, after reaching Cap d’Antibes, am now in the white house, I find my forgotten feelings, the breeze that freshens the veranda overlooking the sea, the warmth of the garden and the turquoise water gleaming in the pool, am laying under the lemon trees in the shade scanning the blue sky where a few clouds drift, I am lost in thought, the song of cicadas, the laughter of friends, the sun’s rays stroke my face, I am soothed and seduced by the wonder of just being here.

reviews.name: cypriant
reviews.comment: If you want to smell like your grandma's home-made clothes soap, get this fragrance! I've seen this in so many floral fragrances lately. Maybe wear it if you're a woman, but test it first. Definitely NOT unisex. 2/10

reviews.name: ppalm
reviews.comment: i didn't like this at first sniff - masculine smelling. but as I was walking around the store i kept thinking someone smelled really good and it turns out it was me! I am getting the jasmine and eucalyptus, with watery sea notes and greenish - which honestly are not notes i would be drawn to. but in the air i have a nice little scent bubble. I haven't hit any of the base notes, nor the berry. its really hard to tell what i am smelling to be honest. but this mid smell has turned completely unisex. I doubt this would ever be a love for me. but it is interesting at the moment.

reviews.name: Célia Pais
reviews.comment: It seems simple... But it isn't... Using this perfume is a real experience. You can't draw conclusions with 1 or 2 sprays... Although it smells divine from the beginning... But believe me... You have to use it to understand what I'm talking about. And whoever does will understand. Very good! ❤️

reviews.name: Swashbuckler9x
reviews.comment: Lovely freshie. Very uplifting, surprised at the amount of fruit notes here (fig? strawberry??). The price tag for what it is in the end, though? High. I found it really clean and refreshing, but lowkey wish a brand like Acqua Di Parma put it out instead.

reviews.name: Cologneinhaler
reviews.comment: Nice overall scent, it includes strawberry and white florals and yet somehow manages to stay completely unisex. It's not your typical summer fragrance, and contrary to the name it isn't dominated by citruses. Very soft, and very....nostalgic? There's a familiarity that brings me back to the 80's but it's hard to put my finger on it. Suntan lotion vibes without coconut, and it puts you in the mood. I can see why some say it reminds them of M Di Amalfi, as they can be cousins, but each are distinct in their own ways. It's performance is mixed, it's not the type of scent profile that favors strong projection. It's more of a short scent bubble type fragrance, minimal sillage and average freshie longevity. I give it a 4 based on longevity and price point, I like the scent but these are some of the factors that don't make it a 5 for me.

reviews.name: cesardiosXO
reviews.comment: Sunny beachside summertime in a bottle. I received multiple decants and official samples recently, with three BDK fragrances among them. Given the weather where I live I tried out Citrus Riviera today and it's really nice. The initial blast is a very likeable and realistic orange scent with an underlying sweetness from fig and maybe strawberry. When I test sprayed it on a paper it reminded me a lot of Eros Energy, but on skin it's definitely a more complex scent than that, Energy is just lemons whereas this has the extra fruity notes. I jokingly said I smelled like orange Fanta and I kind of stand by that, but the scent never came off synthetic or screechy to me at any point. On the dry down the citrus is heavily subdued and the other fruits become more prominent, alongside a green accord. I guess I do pick up a faint eucalyptus smell but it's not really prominent, it just smells green. Overall it's a really likeable scent, kind of invigorating but not as much as something like Millésime Impérial, super fresh and will definitely make you think of the beach. I'm definitely interested in trying Sel d'Argent and Villa Néroli too. Performance wise it's really decent for a fresh fragrance. It's been 7 hours since I sprayed it and I can still pick it up faintly on my skin. Projection and sillage are both moderate, it doesn't seem to project far nor will you be smelling it a lot coming off you, but I suppose it's not that bad as the citruses and green notes might become irritating and the scent as a whole might be a bit cloying if it was super strong. My gripe with performance is that it becomes a skin scent rather quickly, I wish it pushed for longer, but other than that it's pretty decent for a fresh fragrance.

reviews.name: expiredmatcha
reviews.comment: This scent is so nostalgic for me and I can't remember what it reminds me of but it takes me back to my childhood in the early 2000s. My dad used to wear something that smelt like this and I wish I knew what it was. This is a perfect summer scent. Light, citrusy, clean yet not at all boring. Perfectly unisex. My only gripe is with the longevity. You won't get more than 5 hours with this.

reviews.name: luvoxlixir
reviews.comment: A sunbeam slipping through citrus groves—fractured light, dappled yellow, a breeze that carries both the zest and the leaf. The opening strikes first: lemongrass, but not in a polite, spa-like way—this one is sharp, nearly green, still clinging to its stalk. Neroli follows, bitter and bright, its edges lined with waxy florals and a flicker of orange peel oil. That distinct aldehydic snap, too, I get this pleasant pop of citrus skin between fingers Then, the sour tang of lemon and orange starts to mellow, juices mingling into something rounder, softer. Pure citrus in its most unbothered form, D-limonene working overtime, sparkly and true. A suggestion of herbs trails behind—watery, stemmy, as if a sprig of basil had been muddled into the mix, just enough to give the composition some breath. The scent dries down with a quiet, a cooling hush. It lingers subtle like the memory of a sun-warmed coastline, the air still humming with citrus, but distant now, dying like the last flicker of a dream before waking. Pleasant, refreshing. But ephemeral.

reviews.name: Zahammad
reviews.comment: Starts off so beautiful with a sharp and effervescent citrus note. About 3-5 minutes in all I get for the next 2-3 hours is sour and damp cat piss. I wanted to vomit every time I got a whiff of my hand passing my face. I tried to scrub it off but it’s strong and get glued to my skin. After about 3-5 hours the cat piss smell fades and there’s a light much more pleasant floral note. This was difficult to like even though I wanted to like it so bad. I really tried to give it time to develop and for me to appreciate the nuances and blend but I just couldn’t come around to this. It wasn’t even a dislike, it was a pungent visceral sour note anytime I could pick up my own scent bubble. I think the vetiver and green notes mix with the florals early and turn everything sour and very unpleasant. I wish the opening lasted longer, if it did I’d actually tolerate the mid in order to get to the dry down

reviews.name: enders
reviews.comment: Rereviewing this. I wanted to like this so much but ultimately the green notes ruined this for me. Someone mentioned that is smells like there is celery in here, and once I heard that I couldn't unsmell it. 3/5, there are better citrus fragrances out there for less money.

reviews.name: j.dowd123
reviews.comment: To me it smells like soap, eucalyptus and lemon rind.

reviews.name: Youstink
reviews.comment: Very good but takes time to really understand like with most niche fragrances the Italian citrus Italian orange and vetiver notes are sharp almost like a sour vibe this throws a lot of people off but after some time its dies down and lifted with sweet strawberry and tonka bean it’s turn from a ok sour smell to a secret blended masterpiece very underrated but the rating threw me off but I have learned to like what I like and try it for myself and I suggest you do the same Smell 9.1/10 Projection 7.8/10 Sillage 7.2/10 Layering 9.8/10 Overall 8.5

reviews.name: emailahmed
reviews.comment: It isn't very good. The opening is borderline offensive and smells like cat urine-soaked salad. The dry down is better, but you have to get through that first 30 minutes of piss. Refer to Knightsbridge9's comments. Dead on! #celery

reviews.name: tylerg822
reviews.comment: Was quite underwhelmed honestly. Not sure if i gave it enough time or not as i had washed it off after getting this image of a urinal pad with warm urine on it. It was pleasing at first but it just had this radiating warmth with the citruses which just didnt go well for me. I'll try it again some other time but for now quite disappointed as i really wanted to like it.

reviews.name: Nightster
reviews.comment: Love it! Very underrated!

reviews.name: BjornenBamse
reviews.comment: This smells really good but it is to expensive for what it is. If you manage to find it for a great price then go for it!

reviews.name: yungspam
reviews.comment: Though this is a summer frag, I find myself reaching for this over and over during the winter. The realistic lemon note, combined with an aquatic accord, is as invigorating as a Mediterranean breeze. At $220 new, it’s expensive, but smells expensive as well. I purchased this after asking for an aquatic fragrance at Scentbar NYC (a must-see if you are ever in Manhattan), and while I think the ultimate aquatic for me is still out there, I am more than happy with my purchase and have used up 30% of the bottle after a year of ownership. It just makes me feel happy! I am so curious about the other scents in their Collection Azur that I may even blind-buy once I save up some more cash. I understand Rouge Smoking is “the fragrance to buy” from this house, but I doubt anyone will be too disappointed with this perfume. And no, I do not get the celery scent that others are complaining about— not even in the slightest sense.

reviews.name: RedDawnn
reviews.comment: This does high-key smell like celery, but not necessarily in a bad way. The drydown is definitely more green and less citrus-y. There's nothing that special about it, it's definitely not as basic as your typical blue designer fragrance, but it's not terribly innovative. I think it's pretty mass appealing, but it's a little too green in the drydown for my taste. 6.5/10
Realistic Lemon freshness like a blast of lemon, mandarine and water directly in your face but still pleasant perfect summer scent. Can't go wrong with this if you like fresh citrus fragrances.

reviews.name: davidg28
reviews.comment: I’m not even gonna do in depth with this one. It just does not smell good.

reviews.name: ZeaMoore4
reviews.comment: Opens with a photorealistic lemon with eucalyptus. The lemon is like cutting a lemon open and sniffing the flesh of the juicy ripe lemon. That, unfortunately, does not last for long. The mid is more orange then lemon, and the more green and floral notes start becoming clearer. Dry down is floral green with vetiver and orange peel. It's quite nice all the way through, but that opening lemon is the absolute highlight. I'd call this a summer fragrance, but it should work well for spring in warmer climates. Unisex, but with a slight masculine lean. Daytime scent. Casual, slightly dressed up. Personal score: 7/10. A great fresh and cool opening that would be a 9.5/10 if it lasted the whole lifetime of the scent. If I could get 2 hours of the opening. I'd be perfectly happy.

reviews.name: F48l4N05
reviews.comment: This reminds me a lot of Xerjoff Nio. It has that bright bergamot and floral opening with the green dry down style. It’s got a pretty light to medium projection but you can definitely tell it’s there cause it makes its presence known quite often. I’d say longevity is very good cause it lasts for a long while. With its lightness, it would definitely work great in hot weather. It’s a really nice easy to wear smell that’s discreetly classy that be worn by anyone.

reviews.name: AnthBach
reviews.comment: smells like straight celery

reviews.name: OTA Mom
reviews.comment: Mandarin and neroli. A masculine citrus with very little sweetness.

reviews.name: spin_n_spindle
reviews.comment: The opening gives bright citrus and something like cypress. That part is great. 5 mins later and I get a marine note that makes me nauseous. There are no marine notes listed; had I known I’d have passed on this one. Must be some combination of notes that creates this earthy-algae accord that I cannot handle.

reviews.name: Elgan
reviews.comment: A nice bitter, citrus, and green fragrance.

reviews.name: Cat103
reviews.comment: A niche version of a typical "blue" men's summer fragrance. Nothing wows me here.

reviews.name: sv2002
reviews.comment: A great citrusy-white floral fragrance. Bright at the opening with the with citrusy and green notes. Then the white florals take over seamlessly giving the illusion that the citrus lasts longer than it actually does. I will say, I'm not a fan of white floral fragrances, but this is an exception. Also similar to Rirana Salty Citrus, but not as metallic and sharp with a bit more complexity.

reviews.name: Rodcamz
reviews.comment: Just got a good deal $70 for a 90% bottle, a blind buy. Not the best citrus scent in niche perfumery, not even top 50 haha but i think is a nice citrus acuatic floral scent, i do agree that something feel off putting tho. i wish the green notes/nuances weren’t there, they are unbalance imo. overall 6/10

reviews.name: Knightsbridge9
reviews.comment: Smells like celery and piss! Cannot believe this is a real perfume.

reviews.name: Ivanqa
reviews.comment: Beautiful summer scent with interesting subtle nuances.

reviews.name: Ojvin
reviews.comment: Green, rotting apples, leaves, and something close to lemon. Reminds me of Nautica Voyage, but slightly better quality. Does not live up to the 190 euro price tag. Would consider buying 100 ml if it was 70 euros. Big disappointment, as I usually enjoy BDK. Smells designer. 2/5.

reviews.name: ambroos
reviews.comment: a wet dog that ran through lemon zest in a dry hayfield and then basked in the sun for a few hours to partially dry, over a watery/green accord. the green accord has an earthy/hay quality similar to black caraway that is very offputting. disappointed. glad i did not buy a bigger sample as this was well recommended.

reviews.name: CHESTDAYBABY
reviews.comment: disappointing, such a scrubber.

reviews.name: iranifragrance
reviews.comment: Amazing fragrance. I didn’t expect the fig note to be as prominent to my nose, but it was and I really enjoyed that. Perfect for hot summer days dressed down or up, doesn’t matter.

reviews.name: Bnknowsfrag
reviews.comment: I love this, I think that this is one of the best summer fragrances because of how realistic the citrus is and how the dry down smells of the colour green I also love the fig note in the beginning and also the neroli and the other floral notes. I might pick this up in the future because for 100ml you can buy it for £175 which if it was a 50ml I probably wouldn't consider buying it. The peformance on this is pretty decent for a summer fragrance and for sure this fragrance will pull lots of compliments Smell 9.3/10 Sillage 7/10 Longevity 7/10 How much I would pay. 100ml: £175

reviews.name: Arzxe
reviews.comment: I normally don’t write reviews on perfumes I get but I have to with this one. I'm honestly quite disappointed because it has some really promising notes but on skin it just smells like celery and stays that way. Theres a kick of citrus at the beginning but other than that i only smell celery. Could’ve been a solid 9/10 but its a 6/10 for me.

reviews.name: HugoSlime
reviews.comment: It's hard to explain that weird thing goi g on in the background, if I was being harsh I would compare it to "wet dog". I try to ignore it because, look at the note breakdown, this should be the only summer fragrance you need. Unfortunately for me it's been relegated to an in the house or gym scent for me.

reviews.name: ōyiss
reviews.comment: I enjoyed smelling this fragrance from the atomiser of my sample, however it’s not playing well on my skin. I applied a few sprays behind my ears and to the back of my neck to give this fragrance a proper wearing after a shower. On skin I get pool water + bitter citrus in the opening; as it’s drying down it’s turning a little screechy - it’s just not working for me. I can see this working for the right person, sometimes skin chemistry is everything. Edit: I didn’t want to have a shower to wash it off so I decided to try an unconventional layering combo - I sprayed over this fragrance with Intense Cafe. I was hoping that it would result in a comforting Summer breakfast on a Sunday vibe and it totally does! Imagine you’re sitting at a beachside cafe enjoying a coffee & some fruit as the sun comes up over the glistening water. If you have both of these scents I totally recommend giving this combo a go! Edit 2: the drydown of this layering experiment smells a little like Uden - but with more coffee/amber. I’m liking it!

reviews.name: Bboy555
reviews.comment: Someone wrote that this scent lats 10 hours?? are you FKM?? on my skin is a slightly noticeable skinscent in 2 hours . it smells nice, but for euro 150 for 100 ml I can get better and cheaper fragrances. And lets be honest here, citrus freshies mostly all smell the same. I will be buying Eau de Rochas pour homme thank you very much! euro 50,- for 100ml basically the same scent family great value and I can buy 3 for the same price.

reviews.name: LongNoseLong____
reviews.comment: Was really hoping to like this as a nice, clean summer fragrance, but I just can't get behind it. There's something harsh/dark and burnt plastic-y that is offensive to my nose.

reviews.name: rmnnnnn
reviews.comment: This is Nautica Voyage with citrus opening and a slightly better performance.

reviews.name: ClosetGeek
reviews.comment: Has anyone compared this to Fico Di Amalfi?

reviews.name: LLfragrance
reviews.comment: First of this is an amazing fragrance. It opens with beautiful citrus fruits together with patchouli and vetiver and of course the florals. Before I got the scent I was scared that the florals would make this fragrance too feminine but that is not the case. The florals help with giving a better depth for the scent without taking over which I haven’t seen many fragrances do. IMO: Scent 8.5/10 Performance: 7/10 Wearability: 9/10 Price: 9/10 (150$ for 100 ml is a steal)

reviews.name: ginandrice
reviews.comment: Huh. The green accord here is leaning hard into vegetal and slightly spicy territory for me. To my nose, this has quite a strong vibe of celery, kitchen spices, and sea salt. To be honest, it's making me think of Caesar drink rimmer a bit. However, it's not all savory, vegetal, and salty; I also get a bright, zesty citrus element, and just a distant hint of elegant white florals. I kind of like it, even though I don't think it's meant to smell as savory and celeric as it smells on me. It's like drink rimmer, salt water, and late evenings in the Mediterranean in summer.

reviews.name: SleepCat
reviews.comment: Fresh and zesty, like peeling oranges and lemons. Very realistic scent to me with good depth. Lasted quite long and smelled fresh and clean all the way.

reviews.name: OtherPeople'sChildren
reviews.comment: Sparkling lemon open, and then a delicious aquatic midnote, with a dry musk to round it out. As a truly femme woman, this is what I’d always hoped a men’s fragrance would smell like on me. It’s like a refined Acqua de Gio, but with a crowd-pleasing citrus. Definitely is unisex, but does read masc.

reviews.name: KerriZ
reviews.comment: A tangy tart citrusy burst up top followed by the green and pachouli with a light lingering citrus. It is a nice clean fresh scent for summer. Lasts several hours which is always a plus for a citrus forward fragrance. Leans very masculine IMO.

reviews.name: Verdure80
reviews.comment: The opening is beautiful but there is something weird going on in the background. It's a shame, as this looked like the most promising freshie from the brand. Wish they made a warm weather fragrance as stunning as Gris Charnel Extrait is in the winter.

reviews.name: LouD23
reviews.comment: I am in love with this zesty, bright, airy, slightly aquatic sunshine scent. I don't think there are any aquatic notes in there, but since it's like a fresh sea breeze with wafts of tart citrus, it gives off the implication of water. That could be the juices of citrus fruits being squeezed and not sea water. I love how well it is blended, how natural the ingredients smell, and how expensive it feels. It's the high quality that makes it stand out from others with the same or similar scent profile. Yes, I have smelled this smell many times before, but it's usually much more synthetic. This is incredibly refreshing to my nose, and makes me feel like I'm being transported somewhere else. I first tried it over a year ago and have never forgotten about it. I'm not sure why I haven't bought a full bottle yet, but I intend to. Very unisex, straightforward, no nonsense citrus freshie. Full bottle worthy because it makes me feel happy and carefree when I wear it.

reviews.name: tosel3
reviews.comment: On paper she’s not my favorite. On skin, she’s stunning. Sweet ripe lemon garden with the sea in the background. Almost alleviates any headaches with how fresh and calming it is.

reviews.name: Enrium
reviews.comment: A citrus cologne is a summer scent staple, but it is near impossible to reinvent the wheel when it comes to cologne. While it is an easy scent profile that I love in hot weather, I don't need many, nor would I spend much money on them, as they are cheap to make as a rule. Citrus Riviera is, predictably, a citrus freshie, but it is an aquatic take on citrus - not my favourite thing. More synthetic than dewy, there is a window cleaner edge to this scent - an unfortunate pitfall of aquatic scents sometimes. CR opens with a light lemon note and some fig. The aquatic undertone is present from initial spray, synthetic and watery. It takes from the fresh fruity notes of the opening. After a disappointing start, CR thankfully gets much better. As it develops, the orange accord becomes dominant, thanks in no small part to the neroli, and the aquatic accord fades a little here. Musky jasmine rounds out the neroli nicely. There is a fresh green undertone, complementing the orange accord to perfection. I get some sweet-yet-fresh strawberry after a little while. Patchouli follows, adding some earthiness. It becomes softly woody and increasingly musky as it dries down. Sillage and longevity are moderate, and it is made for summer days. Its performance is pretty decent for its scent profile. A unisex scent, this aquatic citrus freshie is pleasant overall, serving its purpose, but it is imperfect and unexciting. It is also overpriced for what it is. Not bad, but there are countless better similar scents available. 2.5/5.

reviews.name: Whateverwhocares
reviews.comment: Lemon, neroli, mandarin and fig. Accurate and off-putting. Or maybe artisanal. It's hard to call it. The fig note stands out and doesn't exactly vibe with the other elements of this composition. I'd compare this to a nice guitar that's just slightly out of tune. Citrus Riviera is an acquired taste and not an instant crowd pleaser. More mature than dressed up; just as good at the beach as in a suit. Personally yeah, but objectivly meh, especially compared to the myriad of alternatives that wouldn't benefit from some ommision. Seroosuly, I dig the vetiver in the base, but how about more strawberry and less immortelle. Anyway, just barely makes grade. 🤟🙂

reviews.name: r6
reviews.comment: I was tossing between a full bottle of citrus Riviera and villa Neroli. Though villa Neroli had better performance with respect to longevity the citrus Riviera has a brighter, zestier tone, which is what I really chase in a summer fragrance.

reviews.name: Fountain30
reviews.comment: Lovely dynamic citrus face punch opening before morphing into boring freshly washed laundry

reviews.name: Ashcroft87
reviews.comment: The citrus brother/sister of Sel D'argent, nothing more, nothing less. It is really pleasant and the riviera sandy/saline effect is just as good as you would imagine. There is zestiness, there is that solar note feeling, there is also that warm skin effect drying under the sun, right after coming out of the beach. In a sense, I find it more complete than Sel D'argent. But on the other hand, I never truly enjoyed fruity marine-as much as a full-on marine fragrance. In the dry down there is a "sandy-dirty-salty sweat" effect. Could it be cumin? Immortelle? But thanks to the fruity side, it never takes over to spoil the fragrance. Bravo Ralf! Sel D'argent is still my preference. As it displays a more fleeting character without pretending too much with any citruses or fruity nuances (which in this case keeps you company throughout 70% of the journey with an "arm around your shoulder" invigorating effect). Aside you can also sense a piney fresh feel (like in Every Storm a Serenade Imaginary Authors) along a touch of masterfully dosed calone. Very long lasting 10hrs+ and excellent sillage. A well done blend with a lovely development and story transition from initial spray until the last burning ember of these palette materials. Highly recommended.

reviews.name: Farrque
reviews.comment: Lemon, orange blossom, grapefruit, ambroxan and a hint of cedar. Easy but likeable, atleast if you really enjoy heavily aquatic scents, which this turns into in only about 15 minutes. My least favorite BDK offering so far.

reviews.name: toosimilar
reviews.comment: Not that much aquatic as the reviewers here are saying It is more about citruses

reviews.name: WitchQueen
reviews.comment: 3/10 Too masculine leaning for me and too feminine leaning for my husband. Neither of us liked the smell.

reviews.name: nbazza
reviews.comment: Very beautiful and aquatic - like a breath of fresh air off the amalfi coast (no I have never been there, but you get me haha). It is sooo similar to Replica Sailing Day. I'm surprised this has not been voted in the similar perfumes, so I just did. I get plenty of compliments on Sailing Day, and I'm sure this will get many compliments. It's unisex and you smell super clean, fresh, slightly summery, breezy and most of all aquatic! Love those unique scents.

reviews.name: flem
reviews.comment: Sharp citrus, but not sharp like zest. Synthetic sharp, astringent. Reminds me of Sundrunk, which I attributed the bizarre chemical note to. I don't know what caused it here. Gets powdery fast as it dries down. And it gets a little bit better smelling too. But I don't really like powder, so this one's not for me.

reviews.name: Wpietri34
reviews.comment: I see the vision. Wow.

reviews.name: Patr70
reviews.comment: BDK "Citrus Riviera" is Decent Quality, but is a totally forgettable scent that has very little depth or layers to the scent. 5.5/10

reviews.name: overtsaceie
reviews.comment: What I like : Citrusy, freshness, marine-like and longevity is more than 8 hours What I hate : Hay-like, earthy scents that kick too early which reminds me of sunscreen

reviews.name: alessandrolertora2000
reviews.comment: The moss in this one is way too much for my taste. I don’t mind earthy and kind of dirty scents, loved terre de Hermès and similar Hermès scents since I first smelled them but god damn it this thing is goes so heavy on the moss is just not for me. I sprayed it on my wrist and although it has been 7 days since I sprayed it and the scent is obviously gone from my skin the mossy scent is still going strong on my hoodie sleeve. I would specially never spray this while wearing an expensive suit, it’s just to bitter and I don’t think it’s just my skin chemistry. Makes clothes smell like they need to go to the laundry. I tend to like more mature and complex scents way above what people my age wear but this is just not it, just smells way too damn dirty. I have never sprayed before a perfume which smell lasted so much on my clothes.

reviews.name: RGH01
reviews.comment: Citrus Riviera is perfect for the high heat in the summer and definitely an underrated release from BDK. It opens primarily with a sharp lemon and soft neroli, with touches of sweet mandarin and a slightly earthy fig. It then develops into a soft composition of green, aquatic notes and florals - with a whisper of sweet strawberry. This fragrance is very soft and delicate, which is partly what makes is so pleasant to wear. It’s very smooth and well blended, and the materials smell high quality - however it does not last long on the skin at all unfortunately. Overall, it’s a very enjoyable scent to wear but I wouldn’t recommend paying retail for it simply due to the lack of longevity. However don’t let that take away from the fact that it’s a very good fragrance.

reviews.name: Not_So_Perfumy
reviews.comment: Way too fruity to be called Citrus Riviera.

reviews.name: iro222
reviews.comment: Orange heaven to me, totally uplifting, totally unisex. I see why some people could think it is more masculine but i only like feminine, light scents and i have no problem with this one so i do not think its masculine. Summer, fresh fragrance, would not buy FB for my short summer season in Germany though. Would purchese at Pafory the travel size instead.

reviews.name: Chicago Tony T
reviews.comment: Every house has a basic light fresh Summer scent so this is a must in the BDK lineup. It won't blow your socks off but is necessary. Very likeable but not lovable. Citrus,greens with a light fig note basically sums this up. The base notes are non existent.

reviews.name: Snozzberry
reviews.comment: Opens with citrus and neroli with some sweet fig in the background. I get some slight hints of strawberry as well, but nothing too pronounced. Quickly transitions into a very basic, light shampoo scent.

reviews.name: clintjr82
reviews.comment: Very fresh and crisp. Orange, Neroli, Soap and Eucalyptus. Bottle worthy. Blended good, I could only make out these few notes. Orange is the most notable scent. Everyone loves this on me. Quality scent. More comparable to Clinique Happy men than any other scent.

reviews.name: Afrikano
reviews.comment: Sampled this twice now and I see why people say it “reminds them” of Mandarino Di Amalfi by Tom Ford. I have the aqua version and I see the similarities but I prefer the Tom Ford more. This to me smells borderline like a bathroom cleaner and for that reason I don’t really like it. Even though the Tom Ford smells similar to it, the citruses are more pronounced in it so that the white florals don’t take over and give it that bathroom cleaner aroma. I mainly get the neroli, watery notes and what I think to be the eucalyptus blasting off my skin. Glad I sampled this and I’d suggest doing the same before committing to a full bottle

reviews.name: Ash_H
reviews.comment: 清爽水生調 橙花油與柑橘皮的開場 中場則是滿滿的橙花油+綠葉+微微的白花香 最後是皂感麝香 結尾稍微有點。。。清潔劑感覺 😔 持香及擴香都普通

reviews.name: FragBoy Stewie
reviews.comment: Opening: L'Eau D'Issey with the Yuzu watered down After 30mins: Fresh, aquatic designer-ish scent IMO, decent buy at $100, but not at retail.

reviews.name: Ba2siK
reviews.comment: In my opinion, it smells like a better version of Issey Miyake's l’eau d’issey pour Homme.

reviews.name: heracles
reviews.comment: An oriental citrus that brings to mind the synthetic white florals of Atelier's Bohemian Orange Blossom, the strong yuzu note of Heeley's Note de Yuzu, and the oriental spiced-mandarin of Perris Monte Carlo's Mandarino di Sicilia. I don't think there is much similarity to Tom Ford's Mandarino di Amalfi. There's a mild watery quality to Citrus Riviera that gives this more of a designer aquatic feel than those 3 but this still smells distinctly niche; a designer variant of this sweet-fruity aquatic scent would be Loewe's Agua El. As it dries, the woody base notes come out and this is where it gains some similarities to Aventus - particularly Aventus cologne, or perhaps one of the more expensive oriental clones. It is not identical by any means but such is the success of Aventus it is hardly surprisingly that so many other fragrances take elements from its composition. All told this is an enjoyable refreshing citrus fragrance that gives way to a mild woody spicy dryness. It has an unmistakeable oriental/Arabic slightly spicy quality to it which I am not so fond of but those who enjoyed Mandarino di Sicilia in particular but want a different shade of the same fragrance will no doubt enjoy this about Citrus Riviera. Performance is very good.

reviews.name: amanda0727
reviews.comment: Opens like a typical freshie citrus perfume. Then you get the white florals and fruity notes in the drydown. The white florals and fruit makes it lean a bit feminine IMO.

reviews.name: rainydays
reviews.comment: A pleasantly green floral with a HUGE dose of tart, bright lemon. It's a little too tart for me but as whole the fragrance is nice. I think this would best suit someone who prefers masculine fragrances on a hot summer day. Don't get me wrong though-- it's definitely unisex and would be great for anyone who prefers a more natural, vibrant floral that isn't powdery or sweet.

reviews.name: Ilikewhatilike
reviews.comment: I can see the allure of this, but unfortunately on my skin it gets too tart and bitter. Must be my skin chemistry because on card it smells very nice and fresh.

reviews.name: jroyogi
reviews.comment: in some ways similar to Tom Ford our mineral, in a. good way (marine/driftwood)

reviews.name: Neetzxo
reviews.comment: Extremely fresh! Strong citrus notes and in my opinion very masculine. I can imagine this smelling really nice on a man. I enjoyed the sample more than the full bottle. I don’t think it last long either, have to keep topping it up.

reviews.name: twiggy48
reviews.comment: This is a standard “fresh” or “sport” smelling scent, with a strong scent of lemons before the blue-type vibe. It is unambiguously male to me because I don’t know women who wear these type of scents. The materials seem high quality enough, but I wish BDK would make another scent pairing that lemon, which I loved, with something else fresh but not aquatic. This would work for a guy after the gym. It smells less artificial than many sports scents, and the lemon touch is nice, but it difficult for me to imagine it outside of a casual setting. From my personal standpoint, I have had enough of watery scents like this (which don’t smell like water anyway, to my nose) so I may not be giving this its due. On the right young guy on a summer day it could be nice.

reviews.name: Ksj_
reviews.comment: I get reminded of Versace Man Eau Fraiche and Riviera Drive by Atelier des Ors when I first spray this. Side note: It actually may be FBW, and it is definitely worth sampling. Get your nose on this if you’re looking for a safe summer fragrance.

reviews.name: waldoseye
reviews.comment: Very pleasant very forgettable. In the vein of Afternoon Swim but a different citrus

reviews.name: antoniov
reviews.comment: This is a nice freshie which smells strongly like lemons. On my skin it's very close to cleaning product lemon though and the closer I smell to my skin the more this feeling intensifies. Good for those who look for a clean/fresh lemon fragrance.

reviews.name: Arber Cami
reviews.comment: Overall a nice citrusy and white floral unisex scent. The strawberry isn't much pronounced but you tell that it it in there, it gives this perfume a really light fruity twist. Nothing out of the ordinary to be honest.

reviews.name: kigyo
reviews.comment: Ahh, it is just absolutely fantastic. There is a note in there I've never smelt before. Maybe one of the flowers. Master blend. I find it very seductive.

reviews.name: stalal
reviews.comment: Nice rounded Neroli and Orange Blossom!! Decent projection. I already have better Neroli from Bastide so I will not buy this at this stage. If you want a Neroli this could be nice; in fact better than other regular designer nerolis (TFs and sorts), it’s not unique though so take a pick! Edit: Immortelle keeps popping its head this summer, which is a deal breaker for me.

reviews.name: Gazel
reviews.comment: Lemony notes at the opening then fresh green notes with a tiny bit of fruity and floral note for the drydown. That is clearly not a masterpiece but it stands out. The quality of the components are undeniable. A scent for hot seasons. 💜 Unisex 🔥 Hot Seasons 📌 Personal Note: 7.5 / 10

reviews.name: ingeneuxo
reviews.comment: -Tart citrus scent with a hint of green. -Has an extremely clean dry-down that's distinctly refined. -Citrus Riviera might just be one of the only citrus scents that manages to avoid veering into air freshener territory. Well, citrus scents that don't feature gourmand notes like cotton candy (Byredo-Sundazed) or tonka bean (Guerlain-Limon Verde) or mint (Goutal-Ninfeo Mio) (-)

reviews.name: Miracle.Fragrance
reviews.comment: Nice scent, smooth and long lasting. I use this more often for the office.

reviews.name: JoeyC
reviews.comment: There's something in this that I really don't like. It's not intolerable and I could see it growing on me but I know right now if I sprayed too much I'd get lightheaded and/or have a headache. I'm sure I don't know what I'm talking about here but the particular note smells like a high school body spray or a 50 year old in the club like @lianeliz said. Its pungent and sticks in my sinuses behind my eyes. But that's really only the one note as far as I can tell. I'm not sure if its the vetiver or something else. The top notes are great! It's a wonderful, welcoming freshness of citrus and light florals that combine very nicely. The middle brings watery notes and green with more botanicals. The base is solid but I wish the vetiver wasn't present. I'm still learning and developing my nose so maybe the vetiver isn't the culprit. Maybe it is and it will grow on me. I really want to love this, so I'll keep it around for another chance.

reviews.name: Shan101beauty
reviews.comment: I get a lot of Eucalyptus in this scent which i am not a fan of., however the dry down is okay. I however think i would prefer it on a guy more than myself.

reviews.name: alanwake
reviews.comment: A beauty. This is a more masculine take on Mandarino di Amalfi Acqua. Very joyful, floral, tranquil, and elegant. Great name for a fragrance as well. As the picture I imagine, is sitting at a cafe, enjoying a ristretto while staring out into the riviera on a warm, but brisk sunny day.

reviews.name: lianeliz
reviews.comment: This smells like a 50 year old European man wearing Armani is shouting in your ear at the club. And if that's what you're into, this is the fragrance for you. I was hoping for something a bit sharper, fresher, and more citrus-forward but this definitely has a heavy, masculine musk to it.

reviews.name: IAmStaka
reviews.comment: Was expecting a lot more fresh citrus in this. It's not bad, I like it, but it's almost tamed down, muted infact. If you like green freshies, chances are you'll love this, it's very easy to like, but to me it falls a bit short to impress, like it's missing 1 key element to be a complete freshie perfection. Not a bad attempt, I just expected more. If you want a spring/summer freshie from BDK, go for Sel D'Argent, it's way better.

reviews.name: heatherrose
reviews.comment: The citrus and fruits are zesty, bright, and refreshing. The base notes keep it grounded. There's a complexity and vibrancy to this fragrance that keeps it interesting, addictive, and very very sexy. I will be buying my fiance a bottle of this asap.

reviews.name: AMajeedQT
reviews.comment: Wonderful freshie. Citrus Riviera (CR) starts with a blast of citruses, mixed with fresh aromatic green notes, & touch of soft white flowers that add beautiful tenderness to the scent. The lemon sourness is softened with the orange note, creating a zesty fresh citrus accord without the sharpness. As it dries down, the water note starts to come forward, giving the scent a watery & little sharp classic aquatic smell. The neroli note is not overt & in the combination smells like a toned down seaweed note. Citrusy, fresh, green, leafy, white floral, aromatic, aquatic. CR is a very fresh scent, mixing citruses, water, & green aromatic notes for a very invigorating scent. It smells so calming & pleasing. It smells like a garden in bloom filled with lemon & jasmine trees with fresh water springs running through it. It is really nice. This is in the vein of Bvlgari Aqva. However, while Aqva is the sea water fragrance, CR is the fresh water, riverside rendition. While this is not a ground breaking scent, which is hard to create with fresh scents to begin with, CR is a very beautiful summer scent that would definitely please any nose. It is just very loveable! Summer time, any age, any sex wear. I "Love" it.

reviews.name: Q80
reviews.comment: Bitter, aquatic, metallic blend... I get bitter eucalyptus with doses of immortelle, & citrus cocktail. It starts with an aquatic citrus with fresh twist but later the immortelle, and the metallic rises up quite big forming a sea breeze, and ocean spray bitterness. This could be a hit for those who enjoys the "For Men" blends with that aquatic metallic notes. meh.

reviews.name: Forever Neil
reviews.comment: best of 2020, neroli heaven (5 of 5) in no particular order

reviews.name: uchini
reviews.comment: I have smelled thousands of neroli scents and I can say 100% sure that this is the WORST neroli fragrance ever created, for sure. If you want a neroli fragrance go for Xerjoff Nío, or Neroli Boheme by Carolina Herrera. Also Kobe is a very good Option but more complex or Cologne Indelebile but this fragrance is boring and a bit harsh

reviews.name: gelkita
reviews.comment: Mandarine + Neroli + Citrus cocktail with no soul. It has an Orange Sanguine Atelier Cologne vibe but lacks freshness & juicyness, Citrus Riviera leans definitely more masculine... boring 🥱

reviews.name: Bubbles1964
reviews.comment: It helps to test several citrus centric scents in a row, as it’s really hard for me to hate on anything with lemon, mandarin, tangerine or yuzu in it. But compared to Heeley’s Note de Yuzu that I wore yesterday, this is coming across mediocre. I still like it, but it’s really straddling the middle of the road. And that’s because Citrus Riviera smells like so many other citrus/neroli based scents. For this kind of perfume, I’m getting performance that’s above average, probably due to the kind of quality synthetics used. First hour Citrus Riviera is projecting, then several hours more before it disappears. What would prevent me from buying: the soapy green dry down which is failing to hold my interest.

reviews.name: amosfiocchi70
reviews.comment: I am not typically drawn to Neroli fragrances and in some cases they are even revolting to me. This one is very palatable, though. As others have said it definitely has a designer vibe but only in its mass appeal. Kind of has an aftershave thing going on. The quality is high both in ingredients and perfumery blend. Well done.

reviews.name: Donkar
reviews.comment: Very basic designer like citrus. I feel like the best citruses in the game are by Atelier Cologne, Acqua Di Parma & Etat Libre d'orange. Hope you get what I mean here without sounding rude. @Douglas_zzz I agree, past the opening this one smells like the infamous Orange Sanguine by Atelier cologne (a glass of orange juice), a bit synthetic/harsh. 6.5/10

reviews.name: Isa Ramirez
reviews.comment: I have a sample of this perfume. It lasts around 8 hours, which, taking into account that it is a very citrus perfume, is very good. With a high intensity the first 2 hours and then at medium intensity. Of course it is a perfume that begins with an important citrus aroma, but soon you begin to feel many nuances in the perfume, the most fruity trees are not sweet, they are like fruity acid, like strawberries, but the most important thing is the aromatic herbal part that will accompany to citrus notes. This set makes the perfume have a very large projection. I want to highlight the delicious olfactory set of citrus with eucalyptus, of course there is much more but it is extremely pleasant. When time passes, about two hours, the citrus fruits lower the intensity, but they always remain as if they were the scrapes of the citrus rinds, on the herbal part, eucalyptus, vetiver, patchouli ... and the floral notes give it delicacy , highlighting the neroli. Unisex? It is totally a perfume that I cannot say is more masculine or feminine, a perfume for those who enjoy its aroma. Does it have peculiarities? I think it is a perfume that does not have particularly strange aromas to highlight in its aromas, to point out something, is that it is a perfume for people who enjoy the citrus and herbal aroma, without more, very fresh, very clean, very pleasant. ..

reviews.name: Forever Neil
reviews.comment: The superstar of the BDK lineup. 🌟🌟🌟🌟🌟 SUPERSTAR! OSME Perfumery has this in stock. Mr. Miami Cuddles on youtube youtu.be/LGUHbfAgZjY

reviews.name: Forever Neil
reviews.comment: The best neroli fragrance I've ever smelled. Head and shoulders above 4711 or Neroli Portofino. Nice mix. Great summer fragrance for Miami. In stock at OSMEPerfumery.com (sample sizes available as well) Miami, FL youtu.be/FxxkHWIfPb8

reviews.name: Douglas_zzz
reviews.comment: Your average watery sweet citrus. Like the ones in atelier cologne.

title: Citrus Matcha Dossier perfume - a fragrance for women and men
description: Citrus Matcha by Dossier is a Woody Aromatic fragrance for women and men. Top notes are Bergamot and Bitter Orange; middle notes are Matcha Tea, Fig and Vetiver; base notes are Cedar and Musk. Citrus Matcha is an exceptional blend that intertwines the distinct scent of matcha tea, bitter orange, and fig, resting upon a foundation of musky woody notes like cedarwood and vetiver.
