Harmony is struck between soft, creamy accents and earthy, bitter tones, seamlessly enhancing each other’s characteristics. Every spritz evokes a sense of reassurance and depth, instilling inner peace and serenity. Citrus Matcha is effortlessly wearable, exuding a subtle yet delightful signature scent that is both highly distinctive and captivating.

cons.label: Heavier musk than tea notes
Short longevity compared to original
Lacks depth of the original scent
Not creamy or lactonic
Overpowering fig note ruins balance
Repetitive, basic Dossier brand DNA
Can develop unpleasant, sour aroma
Very little detectable matcha tea note
Quickly becomes a skin scent
Noticeable dill pickle aroma

thisPerfumeRemindsMeOf.title: Bvlgari Eau Parfumee au The Vert Extreme

reviews.name: crimtopia
reviews.comment: it smells like piss. like if you walk into a public restroom and the piss is combined with cleaning chemicals. it's just piss.

reviews.name: Jmpman22
reviews.comment: Great matcha scent but the only reason why I dont 100 percent love it is that the opening is a very earthy green scent that just puts me off. The dry down on the other hand is a nice creamy, woody, matcha that makes it go from a 6/10 to a 9/10.

reviews.name: CristinaVanillaBeana
reviews.comment: This review is from wearing it on skin for several hours. This is a bitter orange, vetiver, cedar fragrance tamed with fig and lemon. The notes are married and loud in the beginning, but dry down very soft and pleasant. This is a true wood scent that will likely smell 'spa like' and 'herbal' to most noses. If you like the OG, Matcha 26, and/or herbal spa smells, you will likely enjoy this scent.

reviews.name: T-TOPS
reviews.comment: Dossier Citrus Matcha. I really enjoy this fragrance. It's advertised as unisex and I believe that it is, however, it tends to lean more feminine to my nose. I pick up on the bitter orange right of the top which I enjoy, only problem is, it doesn't stick around very long. I do get some tea notes and it's there, but the green from the fig tends to be the star of the show. Longevity and projection aren't very good but overall, I enjoy the scent.

reviews.name: HeyDeez
reviews.comment: Starts off with citrus and matcha powder. The dry down it gets a little earthy like straw and dewey damp soil with citrus. I do get other reviews comparing it to santal 33 and also the slight pickle note. I dont get pickles or pee but can understand where it might get pickly to some. Overall I like it. Weirdly its clean and fresh like being outside after a spring shower.

reviews.name: savage
reviews.comment: I am SO SAD. I love tea frags, fig, every note in here. I live in a fragrance desert without easy (or sometimes even possible) access to high-end and niche frags. I've been dying to smell this or thé matcha forever and finally bought the dossier version. Wellll....I've worn this a couple times since purchasing it just to test it out, but gave it a full wear tonight. Getting into bed, I pulled my comforter up and got a whiff of cat pee. I freaked thinking one of our cats wee-weed on our new comforter, but unfortunately, realized after a minute it was THIS. And came here to see others have mentioned it too. Wut?? WHERE is the pee note coming from?!!! As I've typed this, the pee-pee note has already mostly dissipated, but it's still very unsettling. I'm baffled. And sad. I'll see if I can stomach that long enough to keep this in rotation...

reviews.name: nicole.3
reviews.comment: Yikes! I love many of the notes found in Citrus Matcha, but all I'm getting is sharp, somewhat pickle-like sandalwood and an unpleasantly sour fragrance overall. Perhaps it's just my body chemistry, but the scent is reminiscent of generic, woodsy men's body wash. Will be returning. :|

reviews.name: Kittenwhiskers
reviews.comment: A coworker said I smell like herbalessence

reviews.name: NextPaigePls
reviews.comment: This is such a creamy matcha, a hint of citrus but not overpowering. I cannot compare it to The Matcha 26 but I love this so much that i bought 2. I love the creaminess of it so much that I even wear it to bed.

reviews.name: crumble124
reviews.comment: Bought a decant of le labo matcha 26 off scent split and loved it so much! I bought this so I can save up to by the real one later. To my nose this is very similar but not quite "identical". matcha 26 has more depth and pulls more woody on me, where as citrus matcha is way more citrus on the first spray. Its more light and airy and lacks the depth of le labo. I do get a slight fruity note from le labo on the initial spray but dossier is more of a bergamot/lemon type of citrus. And its not as strong. The dry down is soo similar tho. Matcha 26 is more stronger and longer lasting but I don't feel guilty respraying the dossier one so I guess that makes it up.

reviews.name: barcelonaboi10
reviews.comment: I used to like this, but the longer I’ve had it the more I notice an unmistakeable “pissy” and unpleasant aroma. It’s a chop for me love….

reviews.name: coraldud
reviews.comment: Did a side-by-side with The Matcha, and can say this is closely identical (although a coworker mistook this for Santal 33 on me :)). Loved the creamy drydown, but unfortunately got old pretty quick as a daily scent on me. This was heavier on the nose for me (less tea, more musk) vs Santal 33. On packaging: it's a pretty quality bottle and atomizer with a magnetic lid.

reviews.name: emzeiz
reviews.comment: Okay so, my signature scent is thé matcha 26 and I will never stop re purchasing from le labo. However, I was curious one day about this dupe and I decided to give it an order cuz I could just return it if i wasn’t a fan, I also just wanted to have this on hand if god forbid anything happened to my bottle or if i wanted to carry this one with me to re-apply to save product. This arrived this morning and I have a few things to say since smelling it, first off this is nearly identical to the beloved original i was genuinely shocked also cuz of the reviews both here and on dossier saying its so far off that it isn’t even worth it? I dont understand that perspective but ok to each their own. I find that this one is slightly more citrus leaning which i don’t mind at all cuz it’s such a minor difference. Also the lasting power isn’t anything like the original which is understandable because it’s a dupe. This fragrance is perfect for what i intended to buy it for though which was reapplication/backup!

reviews.name: spfbaddie
reviews.comment: I want to love this fragrance but it's straight dill pickles to my nose. Not nearly as much as Santal 33 but a softer, toned down dill pickles. I don't get pickles from Thé matcha for whatever reason.

reviews.name: Maenad1
reviews.comment: I get very little citrus or matcha from this one. Instead, the fig note is overwhelming and ruins this fragrance for me.

reviews.name: annaxsmile
reviews.comment: This would be a decent duparooni for The Matcha if it wasn't for the peepee note in this :(

reviews.name: hblakes70
reviews.comment: I recently got my nose on this and another couple from this brand. Don’t do it. I have yet to find a dupe company that can really make a dupe that performs?! I find dossier can nail down the initial spray yes, but the dry down isn’t ever the same and they ALL have this certain basic dossier smell?? It’s odd. Don’t do it it.

reviews.name: hellosmile
reviews.comment: I personally really like this one. I got it because I liked the sound of Thé Matcha 26, but didn’t want to pay the price point. For me, I mostly smell fig and citrus, with a slight earthiness from the matcha and woodiness from the cedar. The matcha doesn’t smell like matcha, but instead just adds a little depth. I got a chance to smell Thé Matcha 26 recently to compare them, and I didn’t think they smelled exactly similar, although I could see how this was duping that. I think I actually prefer this one since it was a bit fruitier, compared to Le Labo being more like… Sophisticated?

reviews.name: nottorinever
reviews.comment: smells very close to the matcha 26, albeit a little less "santal 33-esque" and a little more medicinal (to my nose at least). very creamy during the dry down, but not in a vanilla/cashmere kind of way, it's like smashing a fig against some cedarwood and placed a bunch of vetiver on top. i smell no matcha in this fragrance, and only trace amounts of bitter orange during the initial spray. if you're familiar with santal 33 and its trillions of clones, this fragrance is like a more dreamy, smooth, quiet, and almost ethereal version of that. longevity is okay, and the projectivity is not too strong, it's a skin scent for sure after a few minutes, but yea its pretty good :3

reviews.name: SmellMaestro
reviews.comment: This is not close to the original. It just smells really annoying.

reviews.name: Junedy13
reviews.comment: This is citrusy and very herbal to my skin. Not in a medicinal way but more like of spa-like scent. Very relaxing and calming. I can’t detect any matcha note in this. If you’re looking for a Matcha Latte scent, this ain’t for you. It’s not creamy nor lactonic.

title: Citrus Korres perfume - a fragrance for women and men 2005
description: Citrus by Korres is a Citrus Aromatic fragrance for women and men. Citrus was launched in 2005. The nose behind this fragrance is Lena Korres. Top notes are Calabrian bergamot, Orange, Green Mandarin, Yellow Mandarin, Grapefruit Peel, Citron, Pomelo and Kumquat; middle notes are Hedione, Citron Leaf, Mandarin Leaf and Green Tea; base note is Musk.

title: Citrus Poetry Les Soeurs de Noe perfume - a fragrance for women and men 2022
description: Citrus Poetry by Les Soeurs de Noe is a Citrus Aromatic fragrance for women and men. Citrus Poetry was launched in 2022. Citrus Poetry was created by Jérôme Epinette and Pierre Wulff. Top notes are Lemon, Ginger and Black Pepper; middle notes are Orange, Apple and Jasmine; base notes are Ambergris and Moss.

pros.label: Exceptional longevity for a citrus scent
Clean, modern molecular base adds depth
Natural, juicy orange and lemon profile
Works effectively as a winter pick-me-up
Lasts over ten hours on clothing
Sparkling, fizzy cocktail and lemonade vibe

cons.label: Base notes clash with bright opening
Requires frequent reapplication to maintain freshness
Lacks groundbreaking or unique character
Polarizing scent resembles floor cleaner
Sillage stays very close to skin
Opening features a temporary synthetic sensation
Sharp, piercing acidity can be fatiguing
Becomes overly sweet like orange compote
Drydown feels too masculine for some
Harsh amber-molecule base causes nausea
High price point for simple composition

reviews.name: EvandAdam68
reviews.comment: Just amazing, what a discovery wow

reviews.name: Sumsar
reviews.comment: Nice perfume! The drydown is very masculine, not what i expected. It reminds me alot of Coach Platinum, like Sauvage with Vanilla. Not what i expected!

reviews.name: Celestya
reviews.comment: A fresh fragrance, perfect for hot days. 100% unisex, it is like a fresh orange juice. The vibe? a summer, during a healthy breakfast in front of the Mediterranean Sea... Energy & Happy!

reviews.name: YjiX3MR
reviews.comment: Light, ethereal juicy but also a bit creamy and flowery. It's not a fresh bomb although freshness is a backbone of this frag. For a citrus scent it has a good sillage and longevity. The scent is nothing groundbreaking or special just a solid citrus scent. Citrus Poetry definitely has Atelier Cologne vibe.

reviews.name: Hocico
reviews.comment: Juicy, sour-sweet orange, in the first minutes literally overwhelming with a wave, gradually reducing the pressure, but at the same time exceptionally persistent (for citrus flavors) - after five hours I still catch it in the trail and from the points of application it is also heard not at all close. It closed the topic of citrus for me after a couple of years of searching.

reviews.name: nanokot
reviews.comment: Very interesting and surprisingly persistent. The first of the tried fragrances of the brand, I did not expect anything from it at all, and I took it because with citrus it is much easier not to miss both in my tastes and in the composition. Definitely a flavor for cool weather, because of the concentration is more than colony, in the heat is not the best acidity. As it is, good sour oranges/lemons without the bitterness of grapefruit. Threw another bucket of sour green apples on top and lastly peeled some ginger. Surprisingly persistent for such fragrances, 6 hours can sit quite well on the skin, and not staying just a woody base, but quite retaining the citrusy caramels. There is practically no sweetness, as well as blatant flowers, which is very fond of slipping in other fragrances. For citrus lovers to try and buy, can hardly be too upsetting. For me closed the gestalt on the sour orange, the base is practically not perceptible and does not irritate in the process of wearing, as it was with FcDc Le re Noir, although it is certainly even more sour. Of course, I would like to buy a little cheaper, there is always such an impression of citrus. In some places I still get the feeling that I've heard these slightly chemical apples somewhere. Having scoured the first perfumer's fragrances, I really found the intersection with the works in Anna Sui brand, something from a little naive, childish and bright a little flowed here too.

reviews.name: eau_tanya
reviews.comment: I was enchanted at the beginning when incredibly juicy citrus like lemon lollipops coated my wrist. The enchantment lasted for about two hours until I started to smell the hated FN Ex Nihilo. I don't think it's my skin games. Never previously have these components sounded like this on me FN lovers should appreciate the disclosure. Alas, I'll be looking for other such apple citruses

reviews.name: Slavyanak
reviews.comment: What great citrus! If the prices for this brand were not so high, I'm sure it would be one of the top sellers in many stores. All the magic of this fragrance is revealed exactly in the trail, listening to it up close, it may not charm you at once. And in the trail it's just a bit of prickly citrus, slightly sour, like opening a bottle of Fanta, and flowers. It's beautiful without being too sweet. Persistence is excellent for a citrus fragrance ;)

reviews.name: Kleopatra 131984
reviews.comment: Such cool citrus with ginger in the beginning, reminded me of Aqua Allegory and D&G line of citrus fragrances (lemon, orange, pineapple). It sounds monotonously for 2 hours, then ambergris appears, I don't like it, it is very unpleasant for me, exactly in niche fragrances. In general, it is a beautiful fragrance, sounds expensive, but personally I am hindered by ambergris (((.

reviews.name: Mediterranea
reviews.comment: Clean, transparent citrus fragrance in a modern way, at the beginning - there is a feeling of synthetics, but with time very gently settles on the skin. I also hear sweet juicy apple, ibir, pepper, moss and molecularity in the base. Very decent persistence for citrus - even after a shower there are soft intimate echoes on the skin. In the spirit of Diptyque Oyedo, but more persistent. Loved it💚

title: Citrush Boy Smells perfume - a new fragrance for women and men 2025
description: Citrush by Boy Smells is a Citrus fragrance for women and men. This is a new fragrance. Citrush was launched in 2025. The nose behind this fragrance is Jérôme Epinette. Top notes are Italian Lemon, Black Pepper and Pomelo; middle notes are Mandarin and Mint; base notes are Ambroxan, Musk and Iso E Super. Citrush by Boy Smells is a citrus-woody fragrance released in 2025. The scent features prominent notes of pomelo and black pepper in the opening, followed by frozen mint and terpenes at the heart, and anchored by ripe musk and ambroxan in the base. Citrush is described as ripe, velvety, and vaporous, and is now available in a 50ml bottle. “With Citrush, I wanted to create something bold, clean, and energetic- centered around a crisp, edgy citrus. On top, bright lemon from Italy, juicy pomelo, and black pepper all come together to leave an invigorating, fresh, and lively first impression. The frozen mint and mandarin in the heart keep things cool and playful, while the drydown leans into this smooth, skin-like warmth- ambroxan, iso e super, and a hint of black musk that lingers. It’s clean, vibrant, and addictive- just how I like my citrus.” - Perfumer Jerome Epinette.

thisPerfumeRemindsMeOf.title: Light Blue Eau Intense Pour Homme

reviews.name: myadkov
reviews.comment: It’s bright but gives me the impression of LV Pacific chill rather than poppers.

reviews.name: biblicalviolence
reviews.comment: i have the body spray cuz i couldnt buy the perfume yet. this is sooooo yum. ive never done poppers so i couldnt tell you whether or not it smells like poppers or not but i'd doubt it.

reviews.name: Thewayherhairfalls
reviews.comment: Citrusy sweaty armpit heaven! Perfect blend of dirty and fresh. Sweating into a nice deodorant. Spilling a vodka soda all over yourself. Awesome. Pit sniffer approved.

reviews.name: jermany.clark
reviews.comment: Citrush is incredibly fresh and wickedly enticing in a way that feels almost instinctual. I have the body spray, and the projection is surprisingly powerful—clean, bright, and alive without being overwhelming. It wears beautifully on skin and feels tailor-made for summer, or for those close, intimate moments when you want someone leaning in just a little closer. One reviewer mentioned that it smells like sweat embedded in clothes after a night of dancing—and honestly? I get it. Not in an unclean way, but in that raw, human, end-of-the-night heat kind of way. The kind of scent that lingers on someone’s neck and makes you want to keep sniffing just to figure out what it is. It’s refreshing, subtly sexy, and effortlessly wearable. For the price point, Citrush is absolutely worth it—easy to love, easy to reach for, and dangerously addictive.

reviews.name: kuntofmontecristo
reviews.comment: Sexy, salty, musky. Smells like a good workout or like coming home after dancing all night, sweat still baked into your clothes and vodka sour still on your breath. Really adore this one, a perfect clean dirty, but it is polarizing. Probably will become my favored errand-running or pre-workout morning routine scent for the summer.

reviews.name: xmissingpeacex
reviews.comment: Omg this is sooooooo gooood. It's fresh and light. The pomelo is so bright and I get a tinge of cold from the mint note. I also get the black pepper. Smells nothing like p0pp3rz IMO, but it's cute to have something inspired by such an iconic item. My mood has improved just by putting this on!

reviews.name: hasthemunchies
reviews.comment: Very green. Definitely masculine leaning, but still unisex. I got it at a very good price, seems Boy Smells has really lowered their prices especially with the Holiday sales. This is the first thing I've tried and I'm not super impressed. If I had paid anymore for it, I'd be disappointed. It's not terrible, a green citrus. Like an underripe lemon. There's something creamy underneath it, that almost smells like a doll. I do get the melon too. I'll need to try this again in summer, but right now it kinda gives me a headache.

reviews.name: okurrrpybara
reviews.comment: I was worried it would smell like the sniffer stick lol which is why I got both, but it's its own scent and the perfume is much more wearable. The marketing for this was a little wild lol but I'm here for it because I love gay people

reviews.name: Epsilonyx
reviews.comment: Opens with a burst of citrus that is deliciously heavy on the mandarin (sits at the top for me, rather than the middle as described) and a sprinkle of lemon. And then…in a shocking turn of events…it climaxes (pun slightly intended) with a pungency that I can only describe as one strikingly akin to Secretions Magnifiques by ELDO. Not sure if this rings true for anyone else, but it was true on my own skin, and I definitely did not love it. But then again, I suppose this is logical considering Citrush’s conceptual aspirations! In this middle, it is raunchy, sensual, and dirty— lovers, stained sheets, and glistening skin(s). That sensuality persists, but its severity is whittled down, and it becomes significantly more palatable (to my nose) by the dry-down. Here, it’s softened and cooled by the mint, but the spark kept bright by the black pepper. A cool warmth. I’m back to enjoying it. Do I love it? No, but I’ll sit with it a little while longer. Aftercare takes time. :)

reviews.name: babepdx
reviews.comment: This is a really beautiful scent. Bright, fresh, juicy pomelo & black pepper. A little sour and funky from the mint. It does come across floral, although there aren't any florals listed in the notes. There's also something a little aquatic about it. This is not a photorealistic scent, but it's well blended, fun, and intoxicating!

reviews.name: InfusionDeZanzibar
reviews.comment: I love this scent. The peppery pomelo last FOREVER. It’s simple but enduring and quite nice to layer with other things that could use a little extra citrus kick. Cute bottle, very nice quality packaging.

reviews.name: SGJ91
reviews.comment: The marketing for Citrush won me over, but I bought this one blind. (I don’t normally blind-buy fragrances, but my local Boy Smells stockists don’t carry this one.) Does it smell like Rush? No, not really, but it does smell like a lovely citrus fragrance. It's perfect for summer.

reviews.name: claypaperbirds
reviews.comment: This is so sexy... super juicy and fresh on the top with beautiful complexity from the mint and pepper. Gives me mojito vibes. The skin scents on the bottom are sooo sensual and make the whole thing feel a bit more soft and intimate. Love the Rush concept and branding and I was expecting it to be loud and raunchy because of that. Not a bad thing tho! More like a few summery cocktails and a smooch on the dancefloor, rather than huffing chemicals at a circuit party :P

reviews.name: gamusu
reviews.comment: A really nice fresh/floral citrus scent that is nicely blended, but a bit ephemeral... I have both the 2024 and 2025 formulations and they smell identical. Obviously, this fragrance is intended to serve as an *interpretation* of the essence of the product it seeks to emulate. It is a rather gentle and inoffensive interpretation. Bright but intimate, this is a great easy daytime summer wear. There is a slight discrepancy between the notes listed on Fragrantica and those listed by Boy Smells. The main discrepancy being that Boy Smells does not list mandarin and lemon as notes. I think the pomelo note is driving the "citrus" element. If lemon and mandarin are present they are definitely in the background. Overall, this is a nice and joyous, albeit somewhat simple, fragrance. A very good value.

reviews.name: evanbobeven
reviews.comment: This is my new favorite fragrance. I saw a tiktok where someone said that they used up their bottle from 2024 and were so sad that they could never get a new one. Needless to say, when I saw the re-release I had to try it. It's incredible. Every note is perfect. I don't use those words lightly. It isn't for every season. Summer is the perfect time to wear this, I doubt it will be used later on in the year. If you're on the fence, you won't regret it. I'm surprised how little coverage this is getting in the community, it outmatches most of my collection.

reviews.name: UntitledLoveSong
reviews.comment: I am utterly, completely in love. I blind bought it as a novelty, basically as pride merchandise. I never tried the older versions of this fragrance so I don't have them as frames of reference but here is what I can tell you. The sillage is intimate to moderate. You aren't filling a room as long as you aren't overspraying yourself, but I can smell it on me and I keep getting really pleasantly surprised. The longevity is decent. I was still detecting it about 5 hours in without putting my nose to my arm. The opening is a massive lemon BOMB. Comparisons to cleaning chemicals on the opening are both fair an accurate to what Citrush is trying to replicate, if you know anything about the history of poppers. That citrus overload does settle into something much less overpowering, though. The pomelo really comes through. It's citrus still, but less overpoweringly so. The mint comes in and makes it super cool and refreshing. I don't get a ton of musk until a few hours of wear, but the pepper comes in basically the moment it dries and adds this nice spice to it. It's a blue fragrance, though and through. I'd put it right next to Bleu de Chanel and Light Blue. And of the blue fragrances I've sampled, it's my second favorite, only beat out by Bleu de Chanel. Boy Smells' price point is super affordable for what you get, imo. You'll be able to get some Blues for cheaper than this on grey market sites like jomashop but I think this competes with Bleu de Chanel in terms of quality for a much more affordable price. I have never had a fragrance so quickly become one of my favorites. I really hope this becomes a permanent member of the line or, if not, at least comes back next year for pride. So good.

reviews.name: Jag02010
reviews.comment: ETA: Ok, so I brought this on a summer weekend away to Lake Michigan. I don't think I've ever gotten as many compliments on a fragrance as I did this one. A group of women passed me in the street, got down the block, and then turned around and ran towards me to ask what I was wearing. Multiple shop owners or restaurant hosts throughout the weekend complimented it. This could be a new staple for me; I definitely feel hot wearing it! I like this a lot. I never tried the original 2024 formulation, so I cannot attest to any similarities or differences. What I can say is that this does feel like a spicy, musky, citrus that I was hoping/expecting. The mint, terpene/cannabis, and black pepper notes augment the citrus rather nicely. It's not so adventurous that it isn't suitable for daytime, but it does definitely smell like your light, airy, bright citrus that you might be expecting for a more office-ready scent. The musk dry down reminds me of the musk dry down in the original formulation of Vanilla Era (of which I haven't tried the 2025 relaunch yet), another fragrance which had a strong presence of spice (black pepper in particular, if memory serves?) so it does have a bit of that OG "risky" vibe that Boy Smells is/was known for, to me. Is it as provocative as the source material might imply? No. But does it fill a hole in my citrus fragrance wardrobe? Absolutely. Also, I don't get the hate for the new packaging. I think its fun and, now owning 2 fragrances in the line, I want to complete the rainbow, so to speak.

reviews.name: liliazeib
reviews.comment: I really like this!! I didn't think I would, but I'm so glad I listened to those who recommended me to just go for it. Citrush is Boy Smells' limited edition launch for Pride. It's based off of the smell of poppers. Citrush starts off vibrantly, I'm sure to recreate that "uppity' feeling poppers might produce. The lemon and pepper are prominent upon opening, but once it sits for a minute or so the pomelo really starts to come out. It's super lush, I didn't know what to expect with a pomelo note but it's like if a lemon and a guava had a baby. It's refreshing and subtly sweet. It's super musky. The black pepper starts off strong, obviously, and I don't know how they did it, but it dries down into like a musky just-cracked pepper in the background as the mandarin comes through. Fragrantica missed the terpenes note in the middle, so there's a subtle "weed" smell to it. It's not prominent so I wouldn't worry about it. The mint is beautifully subtle. I really do love how lush, citrusy, musky, and woodsy this is. It's very well-blended and full-bodied.

title: Citrus Batikanga Maison Crivelli perfume - a fragrance for women and men 2019
description: Citrus Batikanga by Maison Crivelli is a Citrus fragrance for women and men. Citrus Batikanga was launched in 2019. The nose behind this fragrance is Bertrand Duchaufour. Top notes are Bergamot and Cardamom; middle notes are Chili Pepper, Pomelo and Rhubarb; base notes are Vetiver, Myrrh and Fenugreek. "In line with its creative approach, Maison Crivelli continues to surprise us. For September 2019, the Maison takes inspiration from an unexpected encounter with a perfume raw material and launches a unique and contrasting Haute Parfumerie creation. Words, sounds, notes and textures resonate simultaneously, stirring up the senses. 37 degrees in the shade. Citrus fever, cut kalamansi, spicy bergamot. Framework: squeaky teak. Urban jungle, fabric farrago. Ba-ti-kan-ga! Rythmic mélange. Roasted hazelnuts. Squeezed, struck, sliced: bodies alight. Tasting a freshly squeezed bergamot mixed with chilli inspired Thibaud Crivelli to create a citrusy, spicy and woody accord, providing a totally new approach to this key perfume raw material. The juicy, effusive and luminous facet of bergamot is balanced with orange and gives way to a hot chilli heart note. This citrusy-spicy duo contrasts with warm notes of myrrh, while the hazelnut facet of vetiver prolongs the rounded aspect of the iconic fruit." - a note from the brand. "I used the iconic top note of bergamot in a very original way in this fragrance, as here it becomes the heart of the perfume. My focus is not only on its zesty facets, nor on its floral aspects, but rather on its subtle bitterness and touch of sharpness. This fragrance is structured so as to amplify bergamot. Its bitterness is coupled with pomelo and rhubarb, and then heightened by vetiver and fenugreek base notes. Its fresh sharp touch is facetted by the noticeable presence of cardamom." - Bertrand Duchaufour.

pros.label: Intriguing spicy chili and cardamom twist
Refreshing and invigorating in high heat
Smooth, creamy, and resinous dry down
Excellent longevity for a citrus fragrance

cons.label: High price point for performance level
Becomes a basic, generic vetiver scent
Leans masculine for some feminine wearers
Weak longevity and intimate sillage
Polarizing culinary and food-like aroma
Sharp, screechy chili note for some
Harsh opening smells like oxidized citrus
Unpleasant animalic or sweaty skin reaction
Lacks mass appeal; not a crowd-pleaser
Reminiscent of household cleaners or pesticides

reviews.name: negligentgardener
reviews.comment: Omg, yum. This is both possibly the most unique citrus I’ve ever smelled and also the most unusual kind of gourmand—spicy, savory and warm. I understand why the consensus is that this leans masculine but this reads pretty unisex to me. Such a cool, special warm weather fragrance and another winner from Maison Crivelli.

reviews.name: austinmdpedi
reviews.comment: Love many fragrances from this house. This one is distinctive and perhaps worthy of a purchase if you love citrus and want something unique. There is a pleasant pungency to the citrus opening. The dominant notes after an hour are a clean woody Vetiver with cumin and cardamom. Performance is fantastic, 10+ hours with good projection. Only requires a spray or two so decent value. Wouldn’t be a bad choice for a more character driven warm weather signature scent.

reviews.name: reinkaos
reviews.comment: Tested it on my skin. On me it smells like Reid for killing cockroaches.

reviews.name: grigorian2
reviews.comment: A refreshing departure from your typical citrus fragrance. This is such an unusual take on citrus - f something that doesn’t smell like every other bergamot-heavy fragrance out there. With so many brands basically copying each other these days (and perfumers selling the same stuff to different houses), it’s nice to find something genuinely different. Duchafour often times is hit or miss for me. Trayee is absolutely incredible - probably his biggest masterpiece - but some of his other stuff just doesn’t click with me. This one definitely works though. The opening bergamot-pomelo combo is really well done, bright and sparkling without being sharp, even invigorating. Then it settles into something much deeper and more resinous. The projection and longevity are both solid - it stays noticeable without being overwhelming or cloying. Everyone talks about the chili pepper but honestly I’m not getting much of that. What I do smell are these interesting spices, definitely some fenugreek and what might be cumin. Really adds character to the composition. This is my only Maison Crivelli so far but it’s got me curious about what else they’re doing. Definitely worth trying if you want something different in the citrus category.

reviews.name: Ttan_o
reviews.comment: Cold, refreshing, and quietly playful, resting on a base that reminds me of Absinthe Boréale, but with a brighter, more dramatic twist up top. It’s vibrant without ever turning sharp, perfectly balanced, and incredibly easy to wear. Unique and tasteful, it carries an icy edge that playfully dances with just the right amount of chili pepper, never overpowering and just enough to spark interest. One of the best summer perfumes in my rotation, and just as fitting for the softer days of spring. An aromatic, fresh spicy composition that feels refined yet effortless. Give it a proper try on clean skin and let it bloom. It grows warmer, deeper, and more intriguing with time.

reviews.name: the.scent.filles
reviews.comment: 🌶️Market in Mumbai 🍊 I squeeze through the colorful stalls at a market in Mumbai. A vendor stirs cardamom with fenugreek in a sack – the aroma spreads through the air, warm and spicy. Nearby lies fresh pomelo, sour and juicy, and at the next stall, someone is slicing rhubarb - its sharp scent stings the nostrils. The air is filled with chili - it burns, but pleasantly - blending with the bergamot from the tea the stall owner is drinking. Underfoot, dust and wet earth after the monsoon smell of vetiver - earthy, deep, like after a storm. Everything blends into one composition - sharp, sour, spicy. A shame it’s so fleeting. @the.scent.files

reviews.name: Crubicleworlds
reviews.comment: A bright opening with a kick of spicy. The first thing my mind goes to is really good Indian curry. The citrus evens this out so it doesn't smell only like a curry chicken dish. I get bits of vetiver and cardamon once the citrus starts to leave. It's a nice smell and pleasant for the summer daytime. The biggest worry for me is people smelling this won't think it's a fragrance. They'll think you just ate lunch and it's a little too close to an actual food smell to appreciate the perfume.

reviews.name: adonisse
reviews.comment: Light and lemony with a spicy kick that gives it depth. Definitely leans masculine.

reviews.name: Horizon77
reviews.comment: I wanted to love this fragrance—truly. After spending months exploring scents with the help of ChatGPT, which has come to understand my personal style, preferences, and overall vibe, Citrus Batikanga kept coming up as a strong recommendation. And for good reason—my track record with blind buys has been surprisingly successful, and this one had all the right elements on paper. On a scent strip, I get it. The citrus is zesty, vibrant, and slightly spicy. There’s something intriguingly different about it—unique without trying too hard. But the moment it hits my skin, the story changes completely. What I hoped would be an exotic citrus burst quickly morphed into something that smelled exactly like Thai food on me. That sharp lemongrass and spice combo clung in a way that felt more culinary than olfactory. I think this is one of those fragrances that proves two things: 1. Fragrance is deeply subjective, and 2. Skin chemistry matters—a lot. What might smell like a zesty, spicy citrus dream on one person can become a satay stir-fry on another. I’ve read others describe it as vibrant and adventurous, and I believe them. I just wish my skin told that same story. If you’re someone who can sample before buying, definitely do that here. If not, know this one might be a gamble—one that unfortunately didn’t work out for me.

reviews.name: Katakiuchi
reviews.comment: I'd say as with majority of my experiences with a plethora of fragrances from Maison Crivelli, this is yet another well made suberb release. Opens up with a dry but tart Citrus note with a Spicy kick to it, that effortlessly blends into the main part of the dry down, which is characterised by the aforementioned slightly dry and tart citrus which becomes smoother going into the mid and dry down, and a warmly spiced pimento/chilli note, behind both of these notes is a soft Rhubarb and Vetiver, that both act as a counterbalance, yet also an amplifier to accentuate both the smooth, and refreshing feeling of the citrus, as well as the kick and pleasant sharpness from the Chili. It stays more or less like this throughout the whole wearing and I don't particularly get many Resinous notes on my skin even in thr far dry down as listed but it is fine notheless. In terms of wearability, due to it being a citrus (as in the namesake lmao) forward fragrance, it most definitely is most at home in summer and spring, but I can see a usecase for autumn, I'd say either genders could wear this, but something about it (maybe the way the aromatics and spiced are handelded?) Makes this feel like it leans a fair bit masculine in my opinion. In terms of occasions to wear it, it could easily pass as both and formal occasion fragrance, part of me also thinks this could be an excellent office fragrance, it's not too complex to understand foe the layman, and on top of that it's not (on my skin) the most projecting fragrance of its class, speaking of that, on my skin I get a medium projection roughly but it fluctuates between soft and medium quite a lot. In terms of longevity I get around 5-6 hours. Final Verdict is a 7.2/10

reviews.name: izza96
reviews.comment: Absolutely love this one, it’s my office scent workhorse, lasts a long time and creates a fresh halo around my face that lasts the long day. Quite unique citrus, nothing like it.

reviews.name: picknenders
reviews.comment: I really enjoy this one. It’s a completely different take on citrus - and leans fairly heavily to the masculine side. I don’t think it smells like food, personally. It’s a spicy citrus, as many have said - and it really shines after the opening fades. Really enjoy this one. Side note - chat gpt recommended this fragrance for me and I must stay, it nailed it.

reviews.name: Loose Goose
reviews.comment: Fresh citrus opening that leans bitter but not overtly so, mixed with light spices, on a bed of mild vetiver. Not truly spicy or savoury, just a pleasant light citrus. I would recommend Mr. Vetiver from Une Nuit Nomade for anyone interested in a proper pepper freshie. Source: 1.5ml Official Sample, from official website Date of Purchase: 23rd of April, 2025

reviews.name: ErenCna
reviews.comment: Wanna smell like thai food? Go ahead. Someone in the office was hungry, end of story.

reviews.name: focusblast333
reviews.comment: This is a dry and warm spiciness with a huge bitter citrus opening. Its zesty and tart which can make people compare it to cleaning product, however the dry warmth under it doesnt just make this a summer scent only. Fans of strong citrus will really like the warm herbal undertones in the dry down and the versatility it creates. After the opening the citrus and dry spice mix really well, but this is still undoubtably a citrus forward fragrance. 7.6/10

reviews.name: tooksie
reviews.comment: The opening smelled like cat pee to my nose but instantly changed to a beautiful powdery scent. Would like to test further.

reviews.name: Blyg_Viol
reviews.comment: On paper it was really nice - freshly sqeezed lemon and lime. But on my skin it was nothing but mosquito spray. Had to wash it off.

reviews.name: Fragranooga
reviews.comment: Came here to make sure I wasn't crazy and sure enough the last review backs me up - this smells JUST LIKE an expired citrus perfume in the opening. That tell-tale smell you have when the citrus top notes burn off of something over time. I thought my sample had somehow gone bad at lightning speed or something. Horrific opening. Dries down to the promised spiced citrus and becomes nice enough but it is not at all worth getting through the way it smells when you spray it on.

reviews.name: JasKiew
reviews.comment: Fresh citrus tea scent like Opening with some spiciness. Quite soothing to smell but nothing too impressive. Abit old school smelling. Dna caters for more matured ladies n guys. One thing to note is the citrus at the opening does reminds me of a turned old citrus opening that's oxdised alot and has this old turned citrus smell, not that strong but bits of it

reviews.name: Peat13
reviews.comment: Love love! Not as beloved by my "here! Smell me!" testers, but a "knocks it out of the park" favorite for me. Delightfully unexpected. Warm and spicy but simultaniously refreshing. Has that element of unusual that I adore, while still remaining approachable, in my eyes. I want this so bad, I'm mad that I'm out of my sample and luckyscent doesn't carry it anymore because now I can't sniff it while saving for a full bottle. 9.5/10 half point deducted for my unimpressed sniff testers

reviews.name: elliottbuckley
reviews.comment: Get big Fleur D'Oranger notes, with some green too.

reviews.name: IAmStaka
reviews.comment: It disappeared extremely quickly.

reviews.name: Ebichuunee
reviews.comment: I tried the entire eau de parfum collection by Maison Crivelli, and while I enjoyed the journey, none of them truly captured my heart. Citrus Batikanga evokes two things for me: the zoo near the big cat enclosures, with its distinctly animalic scents, despite the lemon trying to lighten it; and the overcrowded subway, where I often find myself stuck in the unpleasant armpit zone due to my small stature. This fragrance gives off a sweaty and macerated vibe, albeit with a touch of sophistication. It's not for me, clearly.

reviews.name: aspirina
reviews.comment: Citrus peels, green tea, spices. Coriander and fenugreek ends up smelling like chilli or curry for a brief period. The late drydown shows some myrrh or a very soft citrus incense. I don't hate it but can't imagine myself wearing it either. Also, it doesn't project or last much. Overpriced.

reviews.name: Angelasaurus
reviews.comment: The opening is green tea with lemon. The lemon mellows after a few minutes then mint and pepper surfaces. It’s quite interesting. I’m not a big fan of citrus but if it’s done like this, I can get on board.

reviews.name: Enrium
reviews.comment: Citrus Batikanga is an intriguing, unusual scent. It is an original take on an aromatic-citrus scent profile. It is not the most wearable, and certainly doesn't have mass appeal, but it is an impressive piece of olfactory art that succeeds in breathing new life into a tired scent trope. CB opens with tart bergamot, followed closely by freshly-chopped chilli pepper, damp yet spicy. It is punchy and aromatic, almost mouth-watering up top, with peppery green cardamom firmly bringing it to Indian dish territory early on. It still manages to be airy and bright, with aromatic freshness throughout. As it develops, I get some fresh yet slightly bitter rhubarb, and the vetiver makes its presence felt, dry and woody with a slightly astringent quality. As it develops, the musky amber base comes to the fore, smooth and slightly sweet, melding nicely with the woody vetiver and grounding the spicy-fruity accord nicely. It is surprisingly cohesive, but does remind me of curry, perhaps due to the emergence of the fenugreek note at the drydown stage. It fades to a musky, woody scent, spicy and aromatic until final fade. Sillage and longevity are moderate, and it is masculine-leaning in my opinion - best worn in spring/summer. An unusual spicy citrus with a curry component, CB shouldn't work but it does - it is a creative risk that pays off. An unexpected gourmand of sorts. 3.5/5.

reviews.name: midsommarhäxa
reviews.comment: Citrus and aldehydes. 🤷🏼♀️

reviews.name: Redzzzz
reviews.comment: A mix of citrus + chili pepper + curry. Weird

reviews.name: smellimelk
reviews.comment: wow wow wow?! at arms length, it's very palatable and maybe even flirting with aquatic. all the while, cardamom is a peppery vessel for the freshness of cut stalks and peeled rinds. nose to skin - the fenugreek feels savory and starchy, like fluffed biryani? the vetiver acts as a bridge when the savoriness dries down to a soft and sweet, resinous skank. i'm obsessed.

reviews.name: OTA Mom
reviews.comment: Unusual and inspired spicy citrus combo. Leans masculine.

reviews.name: smelling.sniffing.swan
reviews.comment: Opens with a juicy hit of citrus. I also keep thinking of a delicious spicy margarita with a salted rim. Sparkly and invigorating, refreshingly wet/aquatic. Deliciously savoury. Bay leaf, and cardamom with a quite grassy, vetiver base. The vetiver keeps the top from being too sharp. A nice clean fragrance that could be interpreted as a touch detergent-like in the dry-down by some. Makes me want to relax by a pool and wear gorgeous jewellery. Or sunbathe on a boat in the mediterranean wearing expensive linen. Definitely unisex, but traditionally speaking, leans more masculine. Pretty good projection here too. VERDICT: Very cool, in both senses of the word.

reviews.name: Torky
reviews.comment: Generic citrus opener with a drydown that leads into citrus, red pepper and vetiver. I like that the twist is red pepper and vetiver. Actually quite savory and does make my tongue water a bit. Slightly weak to average performance and sillage like with most other MC EdP. Interesting concept otherwise. 7/10.

reviews.name: amandawilliamspalmer
reviews.comment: This is a spicy winter perfume. Citrus Batikanga + Lys Solaberg = Xerjoff Starlight with a great floral note.

reviews.name: RubyMint
reviews.comment: I love this fragrance! To me it smells like I am staying at a five star hotel, and this is how the scented lotion in in the toiletry kit smells like. It smells clean, warm, slightly spicy, and very well balanced. I'm not usually into citrus forward scents, they can be screechy at times. Not this, this is smoooooth. Smells like straight up luxury to me. The warmth in it also makes it suitable year round, in my opinion. It doesn't scream "summer" to me. Very unisex, I'm a woman and I adore it, and I think it would smell just as good on a man.

reviews.name: bigtool_4u
reviews.comment: Love the chilli pepper with citrus combination, its a bit fine dining! Haha definitely a scent for arty guys

reviews.name: afternoir
reviews.comment: At first whiff, it's a blend of sumac, pepper, and a tangy, citrusy allure. Delving into the note list, the mix of chili pepper and rhubarb seems to create this effect. The top notes offer a refreshing Bergamot touch, while cardamom brings in spice rather than warmth. It's an intriguing scent, one that provokes contemplation. I imagine wearing this with lightly colored outfits—perhaps a clean white blouse or even clean gym wear after a workout. It's the kind of fragrance that makes you yearn to release your hair from a ponytail and let its scent waft through and around you. The earthy undertones create a divergence between fresh, green, aromatic notes and sweet, woody earthiness. This fragrance feels suitable for a first date—light, approachable, yet undoubtedly captivating. Overall, 7/10

reviews.name: Myrtillajus
reviews.comment: A citrus cocktail with a hint of chilli. Citrus Batikanga is a light, citrusy fragrance, which, I admit, didn't impress me much. Pleasant in summer, medium persistence, remains very skin scent.

reviews.name: YourFragranceFriend
reviews.comment: Citrus Batikanga opens spicy and citrus. I’d put spice first and citrus second in intensity. Upon opening, I’m reminded of grooming products my dad would use. It leans masculine. When the opening dies down and the mid comes out, it smells more dry and fresh spicy. Once the base emerges, I get the myrrh. It’s definitely blended in with other notes and helps to smooth and round the fragrance but I can pinpoint it because it smells similar to the myrrh in Jo Malone Myrrh and Tonka. These two may be a good layering combo for a summer-to-fall transitional scent. I wore this all during a trip to DC, Charlottesville, and Charlotte in the summer. It didn’t fully stand up to the heat but was pleasant enough while I could smell it on myself.

reviews.name: aleckzandurh
reviews.comment: For someone who doesn't like citrus, Citrus Batikanga is nice enough. My issue with citrus notes is that I associate them with sporty, blue men's colognes. This citrus is softened and mediated in equal parts with all the spices. It actually smells really savory and edible—like a spice blend you'd find at Trader Joe's to sprinkle over fish. That sounds terrible, I know. But it's just a very culinary scent.

reviews.name: Damian1239
reviews.comment: Great citrus spicy fragance....very unique. Love it in the heat...perfomance is ok for a citrus fragrance....well done!

reviews.name: lithohydrobio
reviews.comment: Yet another unisex fragrance getting downgraded by leagues of females, because it smells masculine. Please stop making "unisex" fragrances, and please label them appropriately as male or female. Downgrading a fragrance because it smells like its meant more for "men" is not a reason to report low stars on a fragrances scent profile at face value when its googled. I dont blame women for downgrading something that isnt perfectly unisex. This is the industry being fearful yet claiming to support fearlessness. yeah now theres a bunch of 3 star "unisex" frags just flying out of the factory all the time.

reviews.name: amandawilliamspalmer
reviews.comment: Great fragrance. Female wearer here. I have struggled with the spicy opening but I love the dry down. Also I love to overspray but this would kill me with a savory spicy chokehold. So I'm Layering this one. So far I'm testing this plus Zara Vetiver Pamplemousse; this plus Versace Crystal Noir; this plus Jo Malone Myrrh and Tonka; and this plus Lys Sølaberg. I'll report back how it goes. I may put it with BR540 or Bal d'Afrique. We'll see how we go but it is a great tool to spice up something you're a bit bored of.

reviews.name: Shui-Xian
reviews.comment: What a nice performance in Duchaufour's words... What a dissapointment, that my impression is just a few sad notes. I'd like to respect this fragrance. The composition seems to be fresh, zesty, delicately bitter and spicy. But the result for me is just citrus, which smells vegetable. On my skin turns masculine and cheap. Maybe not compatible with my personal chemistry... Pleasant projection, moderate longevity. It could be interesting to wear it all seasones. I belive it has a potential to change the developing in different temperatures.

reviews.name: SmellyCat101
reviews.comment: My favorite citrus of all time. Thank you Maison Crivelli for making a unique citrus in a sea of so many same old samo's. It also has amazing longevity and projection for a citrus perfume. It is a beautiful, fresh, and energizing with a spicy twist. I love the hot pepper scent mixed with the lemon it really works. It almost gives a fresh ginger effect but different and I completely get the hot pepper from initial spary to dry down. This perfume is completely unisex and smells so natural, clean, and like amazing quality.

reviews.name: ismellsomething
reviews.comment: First few minut is citrus, after that it reminds me of painful experience with red hot chili peppers and later it settles into dried orange.

reviews.name: Blacktundras
reviews.comment: Ak! By the gods, this smells like manly, spicey, lemon pledge. If there was an ultra/ intense version. Pow! That's what I get the moment this is sprayed. It takes you by senses without mercy. Easy pass. The worst citrus scent I've smelled so far.

reviews.name: LMBelvi
reviews.comment: I found this boring which is a shock because I have never found one of Bertrand’s scents boring. I gave it a couple wears. It just wasn’t good on me.

reviews.name: Sniffathon
reviews.comment: Beautiful unisex cologne-like fragrance. Not a bright citrus as you may expect from the name, but pleasantly spicy, fresh and aromatic. Dominant vetiver in the dry down. Absolutely love it for when I want a fresh fragrance with a masculine hint. It loses a star for longevity, lasted about 3-4 hours after which barely the faintest fragrance remained. Full bottle worthy but only at a great price. EDIT - dry down smells like a sweaty male with cheap cologne on.

reviews.name: likesleet
reviews.comment: not a bright citrus. ehh. light spiciness that is pretty unique.

reviews.name: jazzyraspberry
reviews.comment: It's i n t e r e s t i n g. Gently cirus, gently spicy. Gets better as it settles.

reviews.name: Cherry_Darling
reviews.comment: Lots of things happening here. Citrus, spicy, a little herbal, subtly masculine but sweet...but if I step back and feel it as a whole, to me this is a little underwhelming. I was expecting more zing from citrus and a little more spice from the red pepper - this is more of a slightly herbal, subtly spicy subtly citrussy sweet mix. Anyone have Brooklyn from Gallivant to swap for this sample? (And many other samples UK - check my custom shelf for what I have, lets do a bundle swap!)

reviews.name: trabuquera
reviews.comment: Grrrrr. Two samples into my rather pricy sampler set, and Maison Crivelli continues to be ... a bit exasperating. Promises SO MUCH. Spiky spicy non-conformist citrus from my personal nose idol M Duchaufour? I was rooting to be razzle-dazzled by zingy, zestful success. I was foiled. It fizzled. It doesn't get there. But hints oh-so-frustratingly at far greater things. It's just sooooooooo quiet. Now Crivelli seems to be all about airy transparency, and St Bertrand of Duchaufour is a master of letting light and air into scents which still linger, so fair enough. But with a maximum life of 2h and a maximum diameter of - what, about 5cm? - from skin, you are getting a very VERY limited result for the money. That is one very tiny bubble of happiness to live in! Agonisingly, I get clear signals of the theme - dry, brown-tinged, powdery-warm-spicy citrus with gorgeous quirk, a funky tropical-modernist take on an old song - but turned down to no more than a whisper, at any distance, for any amount of time. Some of that might be merciful restraint. Fenugreek spells FOE to me in almost any scent, it's such a fetid sweaty bully, but here, wisely limiting it to absolutely trace levels means I can sense it's there without hating it. So that's one minor miracle amid the disillusion. Rhubarb seems to have failed to show up at all for the audition, at least in my sample. Yeah, nice myrrh - though like everything else in here except that damn fenugreek, I want MORE of it. Chili and cardamom are briefly perceptible and nice enough but really just phoning it in. All have been done more dramatically in other fragrances. Batikanga is definitely original, certainly unisex and so unbelievably soft & understated that you could safely wear it anywhere. The warmer, spicier layer underneath may cling to skin for up to 3h on you but you'll only know that by sniffing yourself really deeply, up close ... which I regularly do in public without shame, though it's kind of embarrassing. But honestly - what's the point of this? So: reminded me strongly of Berdoues Scorza de Sicilia in being an excellent dry-spicy-citrus-with-a-twist that still failed. Scorza made me feel like throwing my toys out of the pram in a failed-fragrance-quest tantrum. This one has the same effect, only more mutedly - and I do respect M Duchaufour sincerely - so the verdict this time will have to be: no, I'm not angry, dear scent sensei ... just disappointed!

reviews.name: Deelecto
reviews.comment: I find many of the Crivellis wispy, oddly balanced, more like tentative experiments in perfumery than high-priced luxury products. This one and Iris Malikhân were redemptive in their starting lineup. Here Duchaufour comes to the rescue with a well-composed piece, present on the skin though low-projecting. Not a masterpiece, but at least a master at work. The opening is strong on the nose-tickling chili pepper note. It's exactly the same note in L'Homme Ideal L'Intense. Those flankers can be unobtainium so I know that's not the most useful point of comparison - I was just surprised to smell that unusual accord again. In the heart the spice calms down and it's more of a creamy, earthy vetiver and lightly citrus blend. True to the Crivelli MO, it just barely touches on the citrus of its namesake, and simmers away to a skin scent all too soon, but the drydown at least lasts a good long time. If it matters to you, I’d say the opening is more masculine and the drydown more femme. In all, quite lovely and a very solid "like".

reviews.name: Ksj_
reviews.comment: Too spicy for my liking, but it would be nice to wear in the high heat while in Florida or somewhere along those lines.

reviews.name: Luna Aurora
reviews.comment: Love at first sniff. What an unusual, well done, refreshing, invigorating smell. Don’t know anything like this. I went to a Crivelli counter to try and buy one of two other scents I was considering based on notes. After experiencing the whole line in person and purchasing a sample set to explore, Citrus Batikanga was a winner from the first try all the way through multiple tests that followed. Citrus + spicy red pepper works so incredibly well with cardamom as a bridge that gives warmth in the early stages. Something slightly soapy about it, but not in an iris/neroli kind of way. Just clean and solar. Too bad I do not pick up any rhubarb. It does change over time, but not a shape shifter. Citruses mellow out, the cardamom remains and plays well with a drop of myrrh and vetiver. Those also give this scent depth in the mid and late stages of wearing. Something quite unusual for a citrus dominant fragrance. Vetiver is also not a dirty kind, just green and quite elegant actually. I sense some musks in the background. Maybe that is what gives me the sense of cleanliness. This does lean masculine. But I am very seriously considering a full bottle. That hot pepper and cardamom won me over big time. A little talked about masterpiece.

reviews.name: alyllauren
reviews.comment: This really exemplifies the "fresh spicy" label to me. It's a nice citrus, and the combination with spices gives it an expensive, unusual, slightly masculine flair. On my skin, I really get the bergamot, cardamom, and chili pepper more than anything else. I live in a climate that's hot year-round and this is perfect for that: it's warm but without ever becoming too heavy or cloying because the citrus lends it that sharpness. It softens over time but remains pretty linear throughout its 6-7 hour wear, and was fairly intimate on me.

reviews.name: librabeing
reviews.comment: This starts out super citrus-y in a nice way, but settles into a really basic vetiver men-centric scent that smells a bit too loud.

reviews.name: emerson10
reviews.comment: I love this -- I feel like I smell Citron in here - it's giving me the same fresh, mossy, just-got-out-of-the-shower citrus vibes I know and love in Jo Malone Grapefruit, but the citrus is a little more rounded, a little warmer (not stronger, just a different citrus). Zesty, spicy, fresh, clean kick with some warmth -- easily rising to the top for me in their discovery kit (which, btw is out of this world and a synesthete's dream).

reviews.name: cranky137
reviews.comment: Very generic masculine perfume

reviews.name: DemonicCupcake
reviews.comment: An instant love, smells like a cup of fine black tea, comforting yet citrusy, the cardamom and myrhh really takes this to an almost creamy place. When I tried L'Artisan Tea for Two, this is what I had expected. (That scent disappointed me)

reviews.name: Houdini4
reviews.comment: Wow! My sniffer is spot on!!! Fenugreek! Sorry for blowing my own trumpet, but I'm like a cold reading psychic, when I hit, I have to brag about it. Immediately the top notes of this perfume are extremely muted. It's a grower not a shower, and this bergamot/lime smell has no really typical citrus tropes, it's not lend sharpness or sweetness by it's surrounds. That's a good thing for me, makes it a different prospect from your average citrus. I think it has an aspect of lime pickle (the Indian condiment) it's a flat bergamot impression like a dhm or bergamal. but again I like it, it's different.

reviews.name: Kasper
reviews.comment: Maison Crivelli is for my a new perfume house that I fell in love with. Citrus Batikanga is an exotic citrus fragrance that set itself apart from all other citrus fragrances on the market. It starts a little sharp, but the dry down is softer, more subtle spicy. Definitely a must-try!

reviews.name: rafaelsoaresfranco
reviews.comment: Love at first sniff! What an amazing scent, reminds me of my mum and I’ll cherish this forever. Thank you Bertrand!

title: Citrus Craving In The Box cologne - a new fragrance for men 2025
description: Citrus Craving by In The Box is a Citrus Aromatic fragrance for men. This is a new fragrance. Citrus Craving was launched in 2025. Top notes are Lemon, Blood Orange, Sicilian Bergamot, Grapefruit, Lime and Mandarin Orange; middle notes are Pink Pepper, Black Currant and White Amber; base notes are Oakmoss, Musk and Patchouli.

reviews.name: mansorkonrrado
reviews.comment: The perfume quality is excellent, and the scent transforms as it evolves. The opening is a citrus explosion of sharp lemon and a scent reminiscent of wet straw, evolving into an Aventus, Explorer DNA, that familiar smoky note. I'm not a fan of citrus, but the perfume is better than the inspiration. Use 12 to 15 sprays. Moderate longevity and projection. I recommend it, but I won't buy it again as I prefer other perfumes.

reviews.name: mozzka
reviews.comment: Ele realmente dá aquela pancada de limão em primeira impressão, mas maturando por uma semana ou duas é possivel perceber que as notas de base (principalmente o patchouli) ganham uma força maior, o que na minha visão o bota na frente do Versace Eros Energy

title: Citrus Circus Snif perfume - a fragrance for women and men 2023
description: Citrus Circus by Snif is a Citrus fragrance for women and men. Citrus Circus was launched in 2023. Citrus Circus was created by Ugo Charron and Patricia Choux. The fragrance features Creamsicle, Lemon Soda, Grapefruit, Lime peel, Neroli, White Amber, Cedar and Violet.

pros.label: Nostalgic push pop and soda notes
Layers exceptionally well with other scents

cons.label: Requires frequent reapplication
Turns sour or bitter on skin
Short-lived longevity on skin
Synthetic and artificial orange notes
Dries down to generic soapiness
Linear and lacks complexity
Reminiscent of industrial cleaners
Masculine-leaning dry down
Poor projection and low sillage
Scent varies significantly with skin chemistry

peopleWhoLikeThisAlsoLike.title: Heal the Way by Alex Elle

reviews.name: babepdx
reviews.comment: If you're looking for a photorealistic citrus, this is not it. It's very generic department store-y, like a very heavily perfumed woody orange liquid soap. We've all smelled this exact scent profile a million times since the 90's. If you want photorealistic citrus, I recommend Summer of '84 by Kerosene. If you're looking for a creamsicle scent, try Nest's Creme De Clementine.

reviews.name: egged
reviews.comment: I loved Crumb Couture and was so excited for the other samples I got, but every Snif fragrance I try afterwards has disappointed me to one level or another... they all smell cheap and synthetic. Such a bummer. Citrus Circus smells like a delicious creamsicle for about one second after spraying and then curdles into a sour, slightly bitter smell reminiscent of the nasty orange hand soap my dad used to use to get car grease off his hands. Once I made that connection I couldn't un-smell it... editing--ok, ok, it's grown on me. if I use more than like half a spray it still curdles on me, but somehow if I'm very sparing with it then it keeps that juicy/lactonic creamsicle smell. It doesn't last too long and the sillage is low, but it's a nice enough summer fragrance. I do think there are much nicer citrus fragrances out there, but the price point for this is low enough that I'm not too mad about it

reviews.name: tw1gthewonderk1d
reviews.comment: I can't get over how bad the sillage is on this. It smells SO. GOOD. But I can't even smell it on myself without smelling my wrist directly. I love how creamy this smells, how smoothly the orange blends into all the other notes and leaves it smelling like the remnants of a melted popsicle, I love the way the amber and cedar pull through and make this richer, bolder... but I can't get that without sniffing myself or my clothes close up. Which makes me sad. I know citrus isn't typically gonna project super loudly, but like. I've certainly seen citrus perfumes that perform better than this. I'll use this when I want something light and unnoticeable, in high heat.

reviews.name: HummusLover33
reviews.comment: I get creamsicle from this for about 5 seconds and then it turns VERY masculine on me, it is a very nice scent don’t get me wrong I just don’t like masculine leaning scents on myself. I could have sworn there was sandalwood in this but I guess it’s the combination of cedar and the citruses that make me think of a masculine leaning sandalwood fragrance.

reviews.name: MajorJank
reviews.comment: I really enjoy this! It has an orange creamsicle/sherbet opening reminiscent of orange push pops from my childhood that dries down nicely into a cozy kind of vanilla that lingers well beyond the citrus notes. The only thing that I have to say is that while I really enjoy the opening, the other day I realized that the urinal cakes in my office smell just like whatever citrus notes are in this. Maybe our building services just have good taste but I can’t smell this without making that association now! It’s more funny than gross to me. I’ll still wear and enjoy it.

reviews.name: Snowflake8000
reviews.comment: This is a bit too boring and too linear for me. Maybe this does well in Summer time, but this did nothing for me. I found the dry down very soapy. Am I the only one who noticed that? It ends with soap. Update - kind of a woody dry down. Very soft fragrance. Not my favorite but might try in the highest heat of summer to see if that changes anything.

reviews.name: OTA Mom
reviews.comment: Fragrance after fragrance claims to smell like an orange creamsicle. This scent actually does. Dreamy deliciousness.

reviews.name: myhoneybee16
reviews.comment: This has been my everyday scent for over a year-- very true creamsicle scent, although the zesty notes do wear off quickly, the sweet base notes last a while. It is also my most complimented scent by FAR-- have had multiple people (even strangers) stop me to tell me how much they love the scent. It is pretty unisex, I find it reads feminine when paired with my normal body care scents, but could probably be more masculine if paired differently or worn on someone else. Also can't beat the price - I've repurchased this 3 times now.

reviews.name: aspirina
reviews.comment: Fresh, bright, sweet orange scent with a masculine dry down. I think is very nice but would prefer it on a man. It does remind me of Aqcua Di Gio, it also has a faint tone similar to BR540 with an orange twist.

reviews.name: scentofpeace
reviews.comment: Oceanic notes can sometimes hit wrong for me. I’ll smell something that smells funky or off and it’s when the salt air doesn’t marry well enough for me. I’ve tried this one a few times and it literally smells different almost every time to my nose, so that’s impressive. I’ve thought to myself “yeah, this is a well done fresh citrus style perfume” but just meant for someone else, not me. If you’re looking for a fresh citrus scent that isn’t sharp or astringent and not too sweet, this will probably rock your world. For me, it’s good and pleasant and would probably get me compliments too, but I think I love my more gourmand citruses more.

reviews.name: Nikkisouth79
reviews.comment: I really have grown to love this scent. When I received it last year it felt too light and the notes were so linear. Took this on Vacation with me in April and mixed with the beach air it smelled amazing. I need to purchase another bottle...this scent was made for the beach! Orange sorbet goodness....

reviews.name: Sliced_strawberry999
reviews.comment: This is really good I actually wore it to the circus and the vibe is really there the only thing I wish was a little different is that there should be a little more creaminess and possibly a spun sugar note. This perfume is a really airy orange cream and lemon zest type of smell that's still has a green vibe to it this is sweet but zesty in the way that it truly feels unisex not sweet enough to be perceived as too feminine and not aromatic enough to be perceived as overly masculine it really is a nice middle ground scent that I think would smell great on anyone :)

reviews.name: _gracemoore
reviews.comment: This is okay to me. I wanted more creamsicle from it but I mainly just smell a tart orange. I’m going to keep it in my collection because I think it will layer really nicely with vanilla scents but on its own I wanted more.

reviews.name: islandgirlmaranda
reviews.comment: Very sexy masculine spicy orange citrus fragrance that would be tempting on a man! The opening is oranges but the dry down is Armani Aqcua Di Gio

reviews.name: Heavenly Heather
reviews.comment: Dreamsicle dreams.. upon first spray I get a blast of orange. It’s bright and juicy, then the cream comes into play and it’s the most delicious combo. That delightful creamsicle note that brings back memories of childhood. As it settles, it mellows out a bit and I can pick up on the floral notes and even some of the soda which I find light and effervescent. Reminds me of a lot of popping open a can of 7up. Such a joyful and sweet fragrance!

reviews.name: cottoncandydream
reviews.comment: This smells delicious (in a fun, very unserious way), the projection is terrible by itself, even with a lotion, but! It layers EXTREMELY well with both JHAG’s Not a Perfume and DS&D’s I Don’t Know What. I ended up getting a full day’s wear layering it — wish I had thought of it sooner before overspraying the first half of the bottle. For people who say it smells synthetic, well - how much does an orange creamsicle taste like a real orange? It’s all in the perspective I suppose. Smelling it a few months later, I think it reminds me of Curve? Again, in a nostalgic way.

reviews.name: dancingdear
reviews.comment: Smells like orange gelato. Definitely a delicious citrus scent without going too gourmand. The cream scent smells like cream not too sweet or vanillic but it rounds out the scent. I like the woodsy base definitely gives the scent more depth. Unfortunately like all the Snif fragrances I have tried the projection is not great. Like a lot of clean fragrance brands the scent seems to really alter depending on skin chemistry but I do enjoy it. I wouldn’t compare this to a body spray though. I don’t get any synthetic grossness.

reviews.name: yeesa_
reviews.comment: I really liked the intial opening as I got the creamsicle note. Once it dried down it turned very woody on my skin and just didnt work.

reviews.name: Razorbrown
reviews.comment: I was colossally disappointed in this. I get maybe a second of orange creamsicle and then it goes into cheap, generic woods. Furthermore, the Snif website would not allow me to post my negative review. Not digging this brand at all.

reviews.name: vibrant.girl
reviews.comment: Like all Snif scents, it's synthetic and the longevity is mid. But... they got the 'creamsicle' note really spot on. It's like a orange soda ice cream float: orange flavoring, fizziness, cream soda. Honestly, if you were ever given the half-and-half orange/vanilla ice cream cups in school that you had to eat with a wood 'spoon'? That's exactly this. The cedar note gives a dab of woodiness. I loved wearing this one through the hot hot hot summer. It's not for everyone but since they're going to carry this one at ULTA too, it's definitely worth a 'snif'.

reviews.name: rainydays
reviews.comment: Really adore this one. It's somewhere between fresh and gourmand. I get quite a bit of lactonic vanilla throughout, but there's something about it that's still fizzy, effervescent. Just a hint of neroli but I wouldn't classify this as floral. Longevity is mediocre but I find myself reaching for this over and over anyway.

reviews.name: Otterbot12
reviews.comment: This scent is truly disgusting. Musky fake orange. So artificial it's immediately offensive. Scrubbed and scrubbed and even that couldn't make it much better. Bought the mini and threw it away.

title: Citrus Noir Molinard perfume - a fragrance for women and men 2019
description: Citrus Noir by Molinard is a Citrus Aromatic fragrance for women and men. Citrus Noir was launched in 2019. Top notes are Citron, Calamansi, Bergamot, Pepper, elemi and Calamus; middle notes are Cypress, Amyl Salicylate and Jasmine; base notes are Vetiver, Incense and Amber.

reviews.name: selinacat
reviews.comment: I have this, and am a true Molinard STAN. If you want niche perfumery without the price tag, they are my go to. Every one of the bottles I have in my collection including their repro of Habanita have been sleeper hits with surprising twists. They take risks you wouldn’t expect and are definitely for the more adventurous. They’re unique, but approachable. Citrus Noir is the ideal citrus scent for someone like me whose interest in citrus typically consists of bergamot & orange blossom. It opens with a burst of super realistic yuzu (lemon, lime, & grapefruit all in one without the bitterness) then, there is a beautiful woodsy drydown that is the epitome of unisex. The woods are androgyny. Gentle and unassuming with a smokey edge. This would be great to spray over a gourmand to make it less syrupy. If you’ve never tried a Molinard fragrance before, please treat yourself. You won’t be disappointed. Go with your fav note to start off with. If you’re a citrus lover, I guarantee this is like no citrus scent you’ve tried before so it’s sure to delight!

reviews.name: Cologneinhaler
reviews.comment: Very unique fragrance. It's citrus, herbal, woody and peppery, giving this citrus freshie some character. I don't think I smelled anything quite like it, it's like a dark citrus that gives me the same type of vibe as eau de citron from hermes, although they don't really smell similar. The incense is the dark horse, not overwhelming the fragrance but certainly peaking out and showing it's presence. Overall this is a nice fragrance that you wear when you are tired of the typical citrus woods combos you see everywhere. Edit: The more I wear this the more I like it, just a unique scent and long lasting especially for a freshie.

reviews.name: RisingChaos
reviews.comment: Sparkling lemon-lime soda with a tinge of earthy, smoky spice. Like black pepper sprinkled over an ice-cold glass of Sprite. Moderately soft projection fades to skin over 2-3hr, longevity 10-12hr.

reviews.name: PAxBR
reviews.comment: Citrus Noir by Molinard is a refined, quietly sophisticated citrus fragrance with a dark twist. Sparkling citruses open bright and crisp, soon joined by subtle spices and a whisper of smoky woods that add depth and intrigue. You can clearly sense the use of high-quality ingredients, which gives the composition a polished, luxurious feel. Thanks to vetiver it manages to stay fresh yet slightly mysterious, perfect for those who enjoy a citrus scent that isn’t just another sunny cologne. Scent: 4/5 Longevity & Projection: 0.5/1 Presentation: 0/0 Quality/Price Ratio: 2/2 Compliment Factor: 0.5/1 Uniqueness: 0.5/1 Overall: 7.5/10 Office-friendly: Yes Blind buy: Yes Recommended age: 30+ Occasion: versatile, semi-formal, casual, leisure Season: Spring, Summer, early Fall

reviews.name: scentasart
reviews.comment: Reviewing a bottle from 2025. Citrus Noir was launched in 2019. This is a suprisingly good citrus aromatic woody fragrance. Citrus Noir opens with smooth, not screechy, fragrant citruses ( calamansi, citron and bergamot). These citruses are balanced by a touch of elemi and spice ( pepper and calamus). Over time an aromatic woody freshness from vetiver and cypress add depth rounded out by clean mildly indolic jasmine. Incense and amber at the base are unobtrusive - there to support the longevity and add complexity - something which I find missing in many citrus forward scents. Quality ingredients with no off-putting notes. Well blended - no perfumer is listed but whoever created this should be commended. Quality- excellent Unisex Performance- good sillage, lasts 3-4 hours on my dry skin which is great for a citrus scent Overall, Citrus Noir is an underrated gem as well as a bargain. One of my fresh hot weather reaches along with Un Jardin en Mediteranne, Neroli Amara and Saharienne. 7/10

reviews.name: ClassyBob
reviews.comment: As always am mourning the short presence of the citrus sensation, still the dry down is not uninspiring. Definitely not a cheap, boring smell! Agree with others , more leaning to the maskuline side.

reviews.name: Made by Kouros
reviews.comment: Citruses are the most fleeting of all notes. So the question becomes: where to go to after that opening? Molinard and Hermes have chosen different directions. Hermes Citron Noir quickly becomes an ambroxan scent - not a bad ambroxan, but still too heavy for the summer for my nose. Molinard Citrus Noir smells like a mix of Iso E Super, cashmeran and vetiverol. You know: that cozy, soft musk-wood of sawdust and pencil shavings, like if trees emitted their own type of wood musk to attract... hmm other trees to copulate? So this is 15 minutes of nice smoky citron and then hours of pencil shavings. It's nice, light and won't displease anyone.

reviews.name: ccperfume
reviews.comment: It is an interesting perfume! I can clearly smell milky sandalwood on my skin after the top note although it does not mention on the list. Worth buying!

reviews.name: O+M+D
reviews.comment: Starts off as delicious lemon rind, goes into lemon black tea. At some point, I get this heavy tobacco note that makes me think this is what TF TV would've been like if it was Tobacco Citrus instead. This smells expensive. If you're into expensive-smelling citrus like LV's Imagination, this is a hidden gem.

reviews.name: bug.juice.184
reviews.comment: I love love love this, it makes me happy, it is so good, well balanced, just perfect summer scent. I was worried that perhaps I will not like it because of jasmine, but no, this is just magnificent, so as the other two I have, Vanille Fruitee and Amber, I adore all 3! I think Molinard is the best currently in quality - beauty - unpretentious wizardry - price ratio. This is connected with Light Blue and Eau des Merveilles, I love them both, but it is it's own scent, though have eroticism of both.

reviews.name: robertvhebert
reviews.comment: I own about 300 fragrances at this point. This gets the most compliments. It’s refreshing, green citrusy, and addictive. If this was made by any other niche house, it would cost $400 for 100 ML.

reviews.name: PepperedOnion
