reviews.comment: Love the smell of fresh Calmansi. Would love to try this one and see if it’s good 😊

reviews.name: Descartes
reviews.comment: A wonderful intense dose of aromatic citruses, very similar to Escentric Molecules 01 on the dry down, a complex, sexy, and long-lasting fragrance for the summer. 100% recommended. Unisex.

reviews.name: scentine
reviews.comment: I've been trying out a number of unisex citruses as of late. It's my impression that the vast majority of citrus fragrances fall into only a few sub-types. There's the light and fresh variety that's usually distinctly green and even watery over being deeper, more complex and aromatic. There's the sweet, feminine and summery kind that mostly makes use of orange, mandarin and bergamot and then the kind that's a more masculine fragrance where citrus actually takes a passenger seat behind vetiver and pepper for example. This is not something that fits into those catecories to me. What I love about Citrus Noir is how it defies all the usual tropes while remaining a distinctly citrus fragrance and without being overcomplicated at the same time. To me it's something that won't be out of place in any season and can adapt to a lot of different occasions. It's inoffensive enough to be worn in a work environment, but complex and long lasting enough to be worn out for an occasion. It has a freshness, but in a darker, rather than light and airy way, courtesy of the cypress. I love the pepper in this which becomes more prominent as it warms on the skin. The incence isn't particularly strong, nor the elemi or amber and it's hard to distinguish them, but they transform and elevate the citruses and pepper to something that theoretically isn't unique but that somehow still manages to surprise, because even though citrus fragrances are very common I can't think of a single fragrance that doesn't lean significantly towards any of the sub-types I mentioned. There's only a little bit of sweetness in this and the vetiver doesn't dominate over the cypress, citrus notes or pepper. This is probably one of my most successful blind buys ever, but its supposed neutrality is deceptive because this is not everyone's citrus.

reviews.name: ErikaPrasauske
reviews.comment: Very nice scent for realistic lemon scent lovers. I adore lemons, so I am very happy that this perfume house has issued something for fresh and citrus scent lovers.

reviews.name: Jay67
reviews.comment: My personal opinion on Molinard is that it is possibly the most underrated fragrance house we have. They get very little love in the community, which I find a real shame but can’t help feeling that they’ve lost their way in the morass of the current market. From this new La Fraîcheur line, I picked up Citrus Noir last week and I’m very happy to report that it is absolutely wonderful! An unusual spicy, dry-peppery citrus that has surprisingly good longevity for the style of fragrance & perfect for summer...... to the point where I immediately ordered two more bottles. In fact, it does remind me of Lalique White in regards of the dry-peppery accord but with a natural amped-up citrus at the opening: it’s more a grated citrus peel than juicy citrus to me but certainly, this suits the overall dryness very well & the Elemi resin supports it wonderfully. The florals are very light in the middle, as are the Vetiver & Amber in the base but they support the lightness beautifully. I can’t say that I find the incense/smoky accord too noticeable but that’s not a bad thing, as it would be too domineering if it were. I’m really looking forward to trying the rest of the line, although with a heavy hit of Calone in a couple of them, I’m a bit wary but certainly worth a test.

reviews.name: thiagovidotto.1
reviews.comment: Very interested in this one!

title: Citrus 08 Kayali Fragrances perfume - a fragrance for women and men 2018
description: Citrus 08 by Kayali Fragrances is a Citrus Aromatic fragrance for women and men. Citrus 08 was launched in 2018. Top notes are Pink Grapefruit, Bergamot, Rhubarb, Black Currant and Pink Pepper; middle notes are Bulgarian Rose and Damask Rose; base notes are Musk, Oakmoss and Tonka Bean. "A contemporary twist of innovation, CITRUS | 08 subverts olfactory expectations. Sparkling citrus notes of Italian Bergamot and Pink Grapefruit mingle with Rhubarb, Blackcurrant and Pink Pepper. Immaculate floral chords of Rose Centifolia and Rose Damascena from Bulgaria lie at its heart. A lingering veil of Musk, Tonka and mystical Oakmoss grounds the hypnotic scent in nature. " - a note from the brand. Wear CITRUS | 08 alone or layered with others. The fragrance is available as a 100ml Eau de Parfum.

pros.label: Tart rhubarb with a musky aura
Excellent for layering with other fragrances

cons.label: Poor longevity for some users
Can smell like citrus dish soap
Musky base notes overpower the citrus
Synthetic chemical scent during dry down
Leans heavily feminine despite unisex label
Can develop a musty or dated quality
Misleading name; dries down to rose

peopleWhoLikeThisAlsoLike.title: Eden Juicy Apple | 01 Eau De Parfum
Lovefest Burning Cherry | 48 Eau de Parfum

reviews.name: enchantey
reviews.comment: oh my goodness... this smells exactly like the type of perfume my mother (who passed away too soon, sadly ❤️🩹) would have loved and worn, so this reminds me of her ❤️

reviews.name: Fragwoman273
reviews.comment: Good for summer. Wish it lasted longer though.

reviews.name: Áttrah
reviews.comment: Oh how i miss you, my beloved Please Mona bring her back we need her more than ever

reviews.name: Poundcake2089
reviews.comment: Okay this is like gold dust. You cannot get it anywhere. I treasure my 50ml bottle. I spray every now and then. And I love it more than when I first got it! It is bright and fresh with a sweet dry down! The rose is NOT strong (and I am not a floral girlie). I paired with Musk 12 AND let me tell you what a combo. Maybe this will be on its way back too

reviews.name: Dramaloladiva
reviews.comment: One of my favorites from Kayali. Such a beautiful fragrance which I treasure because it is discontinued and who knows if Mona will ever bring it back?🙄🙄🙄

reviews.name: Jieky
reviews.comment: I love this one, the only one from Kayali for me btw. I also own old and new YSL in love again and Hermes rhubarbe and Jour. These scents tend to be kind of freshy but not alike at all imo. Friendly and delicious and not an overpowered sugar oud bomb which many people seem to like to scare others away with these days.

reviews.name: evie71
reviews.comment: I agree with the smell alike votes that this is very similar to YSL In Love Again. The only real difference is that this lacks the tomato leaf note which for me is a good thing. It also brings to mind Jour d'Hermes which brings to mind Gabrielle. The grapefruit is very prominent at the opening but mellows out quickly and leaves you with a very clean fruity rose.

reviews.name: JasmineSky
reviews.comment: This smells wonderful! And almost exactly like Calvin Klein IN2U. The CK fragrance lasts like 5 minutes though, this was has better lasting power. I'm surprised by all the comments saying it smells like cleaning spray or piss. I get almost all the notes listed, except for the dry down...there is oak moss but I also smell a hint of vanilla...maybe it's the tonka bean I'm smelling. It's too bad it's been discontinued. If you don't enjoy grapefruit though you won't like this, it's very strong in the opening- zingy but a bit bitter. I love grapefruit so I enjoy it!

reviews.name: AnneAnne888
reviews.comment: Seeing all the mixed reviews, I would say this a case of different body chemistry reacting different ways, as well as olfactory senses. That being said, on me, the citrus stays there throughout the entire wear time, but I tend to bring out the citrus notes in any perfume. The bergamot and grapefruit are still there from this morning and have beautifully blended with the rhubarb and rose, creating a soft, clean, sparkly fragrance, reminiscent of a Lolia scent In used to have. It's wearing well, but I do use body cream, which helps scent adhere better. I really like this a lot. I don't see it as unisex, it leans quite feminine.

reviews.name: Nina Eli
reviews.comment: This was one of the fragrances that really taught me how important it is to actually wait for the drydown before I decide to buy a full bottle. I love the opening on Citrus 08. On my skin it's a fresh, uplifting, summery grapefruit scent with a little bit of musk and a spicy kick from the pink pepper. So far so good. Ten minutes later the summery citrus is gone and replaced with an old school rose scented bar soap... Two hours later it's completely gone. I can't stand soapy fragrances (if I want to smell like soap I can just wash my hands), especially not the rose scented ones. If I ever find a linear fragrance that smells like the top notes in Citrus 08 I will buy it immediately. However, Citrus 08 is not it. It should've been named Rose Soap 08. I was going to give it a 3, but since the longevity is unacceptably short I will knock off another point. 2/10.

reviews.name: JoyousOne
reviews.comment: Smells like citrus cat piss

reviews.name: knics33
reviews.comment: Just a heads up that the 50 ml is currently available on the Huda Beauty website.

reviews.name: cinamins
reviews.comment: I have many Kayali fragrances, which I love, but this one was a HUGE disappointment. The worst one of all for longevity. Scent is not great either.

reviews.name: seaglassbay
reviews.comment: On me this is zero citrus but a very intense rose fragrance. It dries down powdery and there's moss and Tonka. But no citric notes whatsoever.

reviews.name: loves2read
reviews.comment: A lovely citrussy smell. Not too sweet. But I would love it to last longer.

reviews.name: Shmooksey
reviews.comment: This smells like black rooibos tea with honey and lemon slices. But then the rose shows up and it turns granny. Quite lovely, but not the scent for me. Also the opening is like rubbing alcohol, but if you let it dissipate you get an immediate cup of rooibos.

reviews.name: Antoshka2
reviews.comment: Another extremely feminine ladies fragrance labeled as unisex. In fact I’d go as far to say every Kayali perfume is for women but labelled unisex. They are very good quality as is reflected in their prices but I’m getting very very tired of purchasing ladies perfumes for which I have no use under the guise of them being unisex, at those prices. This one somewhat resembles ck one but is much more feminine and floral, mostly rose and cassis which gives cat urine. I wouldn’t recommend to anyone looking for a unisex perfume as I was but it is definitely nice for a woman or someone who likes extremely feminine floral fragrances, especially female ck one lovers.

reviews.name: Flexwithviv
reviews.comment: Received this today in a travel size. I have never been so happy with purchasing a travel size and not blind buying it a bigger bottle. I just can’t stand it, it’s not the type of citrus I am into. It’s like a cleaning product /dirty citrus fragrance, it doesn’t last long thankfully because it’s just not for me.

reviews.name: Kapuzik
reviews.comment: It's a sparkling r fresh rose fragrance. why is it called Citrus is a question. It's very gentle and very pretty. Clean girl scent. Beautiful spring summer

reviews.name: kissmeoverthegardengate
reviews.comment: This is a warm and spicy citrus. I was expecting something more fresh and juicy but I really like it! Edit: it’s the next morning and I still smell it. It’s softened to a sweet, peppery scent. Love it. But I wouldn’t put it in the citrus category.

reviews.name: Hedabo
reviews.comment: I find this fragrance quite delightful and refreshing. I layered Dolce and Gabanna Q over it and it has produced the most gorgeous long lasting fragrance I’m totally hooked on. Kayali citrus is special because I love it on its own but the layering combinations are endless. Im staggered at how rude some of you people are. It almost seems like you pounce on things and spray negativity which such delight its pathetic. You clearly have too much time on your hands and add nothing constructive.

reviews.name: perfumequeen234668
reviews.comment: I have a 1.7oz of this anyone wanna swap?

reviews.name: vivaLVmarie
reviews.comment: I’m testing my bottle on a cold snowy weather. In my skin, this perfume doesn’t change from first spray, no journey there so I’m a little sad. But honestly I don’t hate this. It smells like something Dolce Light Blue would make as a flanker. It’s a nice and classy citrus. It reminds me 90% of Pinrose Merry Maker’s dry down, the one with nectarine if I’m not mistaken. I quite like it. My experience with Kayali is always like: first time getting the perfume, it smells yuck. After revisiting the bottle 6 months later, airing it out, letting it develop, forget about it… then I love it. So maybe give it another chance later? You may like it. ♥️

reviews.name: OTA Mom
reviews.comment: Really less citrus, the name misleads. Mainly Bulgarian rose and unpleasantly sour rhubarb.

reviews.name: Morgan45
reviews.comment: I gave this a few good wears to make sure, because I find I need a few tries to really "get" Kayali fragrances sometimes, but this is definitely my least favorite Kayali. The citrus in the opening is nice enough, but then it dries down into a musky, powdery rose scent that's mildly nauseating to me. Luckily, the lasting power isn't that great so it fades away in not too long. Glad I only bought 10ml, but wish I hadn't. This one's not for me. Edit: Did I forget to mention the faint note of burnt hair? I kept trying to like it but I think I quit.

reviews.name: JenAvery
reviews.comment: This is a beautiful light airy sweet scent. I can smell the citrus on the initial spray, then the sweetness comes in. It’s very first warm spring day in a sundress at brunch fragrance. It’s cute and flirty, it’s the only citrus scent I own; I’m usually a jammy rose fan, but it’s something different.

reviews.name: fereyal
reviews.comment: I bought this blind since I was looking for a citrus fragrance for the summer and what a mistake! I do not like this scent so much so that it has made me vow to never get anything else from this brand especially if I haven't tried it before.

reviews.name: Dexter G
reviews.comment: So pleasantly surprised by this fragrance. A perfect addition for a summer day! Light and refreshing with such a lovely scent. The initial burst of citrus is replaced by sweet and oh so pleasant floral notes. This is not a huge scent but for those who love perfume as a personal affair...it most certainly does the job. Great for those summer days when the only person you want to impress is you!

reviews.name: Skinscents4Fusspots
reviews.comment: This doesn’t smell anything like I expected. It’s not particularly citrusy and it smells quite perfumey in a classical sense (probably the oakmoss!). Having worn the modern version of Yves Saint Laurent’s In Love Again as a signature scent for a couple of years, I can hand on heart say that this smells just like that perfume. Imagine a floral muscat wine…. A sweet desert wine. There is some acidity but it’s lightly syrupy and sweet with some delicate florals and on my skin it’s fairy linear after the drydown. The price of In Love Again and Citrus are comparable so I wouldn’t know what to recommend - that’s how similar they smell!

reviews.name: fretrace
reviews.comment: I must be an outlier because I haven't enjoyed many of the Kayali scents I've tried (the most popular ones, Vanilla and Eden are both a bit repellent to me) - but this is a favorite, not just from the line but overall. And I'm not a huge citrus (or rose) fan! I agree that the name could be misleading for those searching for a more traditional citrus; this is more of a rose that is brightened and amplified by the citrus/rhubarb/fruit. Since rose can turn musty on me, I find that the citrus helps tremendously. And it's not an overly sharp citrus, which can give me a headache and overwhelm my nose. Overall this is a perfect balance, an easy reach in warmer weather, and nice to layer with sweeter or darker scents to add some sparkliness (today I'm wearing with Burberry Her, for example). I picked up a 10ml, which may not seem like much investment, but considering 99% of the time I'm wearing and trying new samples, is a significant indicator! TLDR, this is a lovely grapefruit-rose - for anyone who wants something that isn't too floral, too citrusy, or too heavy.

reviews.name: IloveperfumeJR
reviews.comment: Not sure why all the hate, but this perfume is beautiful. I think the name throws people’s expectations off, but the scent is classy and sophisticated. It’s more floral with a hint of citrus. It smells high quality and well formulated. I get the same vibes that I get when I wear Gris Dior. I think the oak moss grounds it and adds that extra level of sophistication. TRY it yourself before judging it based on reviews alone!

reviews.name: Kaleigha
reviews.comment: In my dreams, I love every scent in a brand and buy all the matching, pretty bottles. After picking up and enjoying Kayali's Eden Juicy Apple, I figured that Citrus was a sure thing for me. I was wrong. So wrong. Couldn't scrub this one off fast enough. It was harsh and jarring and somehow smelled dirty on me. Shudder. No, thanks. I am not a fan of returning things, but this is heading back to Sephora. Pronto.

reviews.name: Seranity
reviews.comment: Oh God, this was awful. I do love citrus scents, as long as their balanced out with floral notes and deeper notes that are well blended and complimentary. This perfume was revolting. It's literally like someone cut a grapefruit in half and squeezed it over their head...or my head. I was gagging and seizing, like i had a brain freeze on citrus. And the bitter citrus doesn't disappear for at least an hour. It just keeps going and going. It's screechy, pungent and headache inducing. It EVENTUALLY dries down to something more tolerable and smooth but by then the headache is rolling in and your wanting a full shower to get that crap off your skin. Never again.

reviews.name: HecateZeusIsis
reviews.comment: Many years ago I was travelling to Jordan, there was a quaint store with body and bath oils, fragrance mists and soaps. I picked up this beautiful bottle and sniffed it, that was my first experience with Middle Eastern perfumes. That bottle smells very very similar to kayali citrus 🍊 it was a blast of nostalgia because I had saved that perfume for years before it got spoiled. It is not a sour citrus, it’s more like a sweet and musky citrus perfume. Very different from most other ones atleast try a decant or the 10ml 😀

reviews.name: mariboo
reviews.comment: It’s a pleasant scent but I’m not crazy about it. Reminds me of a car freshener. I enjoy it but it doesn’t carry that powerful and flirty mood I usually want for my perfumes. I’d describe it as clean, soft, and fresh. Overall OK.

reviews.name: JayAltayeb
reviews.comment: Exactly like ( diptyque baies ) ! ♥️😍

reviews.name: avb37
reviews.comment: This surprised me. It’s not a sour citrus. It’s bright but musky at the same time. When i first bought it, i sprayed too much and felt like i was covered in dish detergent. A couple sprays is enough for a clean, very pretty scent that projects more on the intimate side.

reviews.name: girldeadbug
reviews.comment: A nice basic citrus scent for warm/hot weather. Sour and juicy. I particularly like it for its lack of lemon and orange. An easy grab for when you don’t feel like thinking about what to put on.

reviews.name: arina.parshina
reviews.comment: I'm still trying to figure this one out.. it was very sharp in the beginning and i put the tester away, when i tried to smell it later i suddenly really liked it, it changes a lot after some time. I kept feeling like it reminds me of something really familiar. after a whole day i realized that it reminds me in some way of my favorite Montabaco by Ormonde Jayne, while it has no tobacco at all, and is way less complex. I know it's odd, but that's the epiphany i had earlier :D must be the pink pepper, bergamot, rose, tonka and moss combination..I dont know yet if i will be buying full size, but I did like it. i imagine it pairs well with their Vanilla, or even Musk.

reviews.name: firstmate423
reviews.comment: This is a scrubber for me! Wayyy too much rose to be called citrus! Hate it.

reviews.name: nirvana95
reviews.comment: I like this fragrance but it’s not a love. It is a grown up citrus that is attention grabbing but for me it’s a little bit too earthy. It has a great longevity for a citrus fragrance, around 5 hours. I definitely pick up the grapefruit and rhubarb and everything else takes a back seat. Overall I’ll enjoy my time with the fragrance but probably won’t repurchase.

reviews.name: akoyumi
reviews.comment: When I purchased this, I was worried it would smell exactly like Grapefruit Incense by ZARA. I received it today, and, to my surprise… it smells EXACTLY like Grapefruit Incense by ZARA. Go for the ZARA, it’s so much cheaper lol!

reviews.name: sarayup
reviews.comment: I thought this perfume was going to be just a one note citrus, but I was pleasantly surprised! You get the grapefruit in the opening and the tartness of the rhubarb, but I also got the rose right off the bat. As it dries down, I still get the rose and a hint of the grapefruit, but with a musky aura. Overall, very beautiful fragrance!

reviews.name: iinyy
reviews.comment: The initial spray is more complex than the drydown. It starts as very fresh citrusy (almost like a dish soap), little bit musky. Dries to more floral. Not my favorite but works as a casual perfume in the summer.

reviews.name: Shan101beauty
reviews.comment: This is a very pretty scent, i get the citrus plus a pretty kind of clean powdery scent which i am guessing is coming from the rose. Very nice. Makes a nice summer clean approachable scent in the heat.

reviews.name: CamilleMarie
reviews.comment: I initially wrote this off pretty quickly as citrus scents aren’t usually what I like. However, the more I wear it the more I like it. It starts off very tart, but after the top dries off a bit it becomes a very nice fresh sparkling rose. I think if it were called Rose instead of Citrus it would get more attention. This is great for work and I think will be really nice during the summer heat. I see this usually at the bottom of everyone’s kayali list, but I would urge people to give it some time they may end up really liking it.

reviews.name: ravenna
reviews.comment: One of the few frags where I can pick up on most of the notes listed, minus the tonka. This also genuinely reminds me of a dishwashing liquid that I cant remember the name of. But why do I like it so much??

reviews.name: Sreza7
reviews.comment: This is my least favorite of the Kayali line. But I’m not a huge fan of citrus dominated fragrances. Just smells like grapefruit to me.

reviews.name: BiancaDrumea
reviews.comment: I don’t know why Citrus 08 gives me strong Jour d’Hermes vibes but less orange blossomy and I am kinda bummed I left both my JdH bottles in Miami so now I can’t compare. In any case, it is a nice citrus and it would work very nicely in warm climate. Like all Kayali scents I tried Citrus 08 has that delicate sprinkled sugar crystals accent. It is the least sweet of them (on myself) which is probably why it is my least favorite out of the 7 I tried. Still lovely.

reviews.name: LovelyLifestyle
reviews.comment: The opening is alcohol-heavy. I get a really strong hairspray vibe for the first 5 minutes. It made my nose sting and my eyes water. Yuck. After about 10 minutes the alcohol calms down and some of the citrus starts coming out. The grapefruit is very sour and tart. Then the black currant makes it a spicy sour. I hate this too. The dry down smells chemical. This reminds me of hair dyes when they try to mask the chemical smell with synthetic fruits, but they're not fooling anyone. I've bought $10 body sprays at Victoria's Secret that smell better than this. Better quality too. This is a mess.

reviews.name: 2whit2quit
reviews.comment: I received a sample of Citrus 08 to try and it immediately reminds me of the Ellis Brooklyn line, in particular, Raven. Both have a rhubarb citrus combo at the top and musky bases. I quite like it, but probably won’t purchase a FB.

reviews.name: RainbowGirl
reviews.comment: This is the last fragrance to try out of my mini discovery set, and it's another really good one. I am super impressed with these Kayali fragrances, and to be honest, I didn't really expect to be. Rumors they were good, combined with an opportunity to purchase a slightly used discovery set, is the only reason I am able to write these reviews. I would never have blind purchased these fragrances, but I think three, out of the four in my set, are full bottle worthy. The opening of citrus 08 is a super beautiful bergamot, then I get the grapefruit and black currant. I did not get the pink pepper at first, it was drowned out by the cirtus, but I can smell a hint of it right before it settled. I did not get the rhubarb, but that doesn't mean it's not in there. This had a super fresh and sour opening, so it was probably amalgamated with the citrus. It settles into a light fresh rose, very light, with a nice pretty grapefruit. Very citrus, but not sharp like a cleaner. One of the best citrus fragrances I have tried. I don't get a lot of projection, but I get a little. It is a pretty strong skin scent, with pretty great longevity for a citrus fragrance. This gets a little sweet in the drydown, probably from the tonka bean, a hint of musk, and if I get oakmoss, it really just smells like the rind of a fresh green citrus. This is a pretty citrus fragrance, and I like it better than all of the citruses I already own. When I am in a citrus mood, I will reach for this one first.

reviews.name: MissAnnette
reviews.comment: Initial spray: Oh my gosh! This is too sharp! It feels like it burned the hair in my nose! This is the perfume that is guaranteed to wake you up in the morning. So after surviving the harsh top notes, I can smell a little of the black currant and rose. It did grow on me, it did get sweeter and milder as it goes on, but the over all impression is still bathroom cleaning product. Will update once I experiment more with this. Update: layered this with Vanilla 28. The result is lemon vanilla custard slice!!! You gotta wait for approx 30 minutes to get this result as the citrus overpowered the vanilla. I haven’t tried spraying Vanilla on top of Citrus though. I’ll try that next time. Tested from carded sample

reviews.name: SuperSueChick
reviews.comment: Dawn Orange AntiBacterial Liquid Dish Soap. This is exactly what my hands smell like after hand washing a load full of dirty dishes, pots, and pans. Either dishwashing liquid, or what the musty fellowship halls of those churches that have Bazaars in October selling arts and crafts ran by a group of powdery perfumed seniors. Gosh, Huda, I expected waaay better! I mean the bottles are okay cute, but ALL of the Kayali Scents smell, to ME (IMO), like sweet old church ladies. ALL of them, ugh. My husband leand in to kiss me, then he smelled me, and exclaimed "ugh, what is that"?! as he backed off holding his breath, and didn't even kiss me. Excuse me as I go shower and wash this off.

reviews.name: snoopyQ
reviews.comment: This one opens citrusy and then quickly turns into a rosy musk. Hints of citrus remain through out the drydown ledning it a soft spicyness. The drydown smells to me exactly like Florabotanica by Balenciaga which has been discontinued.

reviews.name: JerZgal94
reviews.comment: Bought this by accident as I thought I was getting their Vanilla 28 (smh). This scent is nice and classy but unnecessary in my collection. I already have citrus classy, boss-mode fragrances that blows Kayali Citrus out of the water. IMO, if you have Miss Dior, Coco Mademoiselle or Valentina Donna you do not need this. I’m starting to believe that Kayali fragrances have longevity issues. I had to reapply this scent twice during a work day. 3.5/5 rating from me.

reviews.name: scentobsessed01
reviews.comment: Literally smells like an air freshner/ wash up liquid. It comes down to preference though. My friend loves

reviews.name: d_silent_one
reviews.comment: I sprayed this perfume for the first time, and upon drying down after a few minutes, it started smelling very familiar, but I couldn't put my finger on it.... until then, I got it! This could very well be a dupe for Dior's Blooming Bouquet. No questions asked. The first impression is nothing like it because this smells very very lemony, but then it converts into Blooming Bouquet.

reviews.name: Stephlo2010
reviews.comment: This is a beautiful well rounded fragrance, the opening is absolutely spectacular, opulent, zesty! It has been an hour and it has become a more powdery musky smell with the grapefruit still around! It is lovely! This fragrance on its own is stunning, but I bet would be beautiful layered as well.

reviews.name: mychinarose
reviews.comment: I think by now it's widely known, at least among us Fragrantians, that citrus notes are among the most fleeting, just as a matter of course. Not all notes have the same ability to stick around, no matter what. Because this is the case, most fragrances have longer-lasting notes like oakmoss, patchouli, musk or vetiver (or combo of any of those) in their base to at least keep the blended dry-down intact in some incarnation. But for a fragrance like this that is literally called "Citrus", I'm not entirely sure that it was a huge desire of the brand to have the base notes overpower the intended fresh citrus scent. So yes, the fragrance is quite beautiful, and yes, it does soften down to its muskier, sweeter notes quite quickly, and yes, it is not a powerhouse scent by design. All of that said, especially since I got my Kayali scents in the mini form, it feels pretty clear to me that they were designed to be layered. I have found that Citrus and Vanilla are a really beautiful pairing...but I'm also experimenting with layering my Solinotes Tonka fragrance with Citrus (tonka, not vanilla, is a note listed in the perfume pyramid on Fragrantica), and my current "trick" to make my fragrances last longer is to take a note from a perfume's pyramid and amp it up with some kind of solinote. More often than not it works! I'm also keen to re-purchase a bottle of Fresh's Hesperides Grapefruitl and layer it with Kayali Citrus to see what it does to it.

reviews.name: Supermonipower
reviews.comment: A beautifully blended citrus fragrance! The opening is a boost of citrus Notes. You could smell the pink grapefruit and the blend with the roses and bergamot. The rhubarb is beautifully blended in this fragrance as well, it gives it a lovely touch. The tonkabean makes this sweet and creamy. I think this is an amazing citrus scent! The longer it sits on my skin, the warmer And creamier the scent gets. But the citrus disappears a bit in the background. Here it comes: the BIGGEST complaint is that this fragrance has absolutely one of the worst performances I have ever had with perfumes. It disappears after a few minutes and immediately becomes a skin scent. To me it even vanishes after 30 minutes from my skin. Terrible performance and longevity on me! But the scent is just gorgeous, too bad... Final result: 6/10

reviews.name: golden.signorina
reviews.comment: It's my favorite citrusy fragrance ever.. Usually I don't like citrusy fragrance but this one I felt in love with first sniff.. Isn't a traditional citrusy smell instead of that it's a very nice combo of peppery floral citrusy smell which has its uniqueness.. Love it definitely ❤

reviews.name: denisa_iv
reviews.comment: I had a sample of this. A pleasant citrus but not very complex or long lasting. The Kayali line is supposedly meant for layering but if you do that, apart from the initial citrus burst this will fade into nothing and completely lose any distinctive feature. Same for the musk fragrance in the line. A nice and inoffensive scent, but not long lasting or strong. I found that layering this with Kayali Vanilla smelled a bit similar to Atelier Cologne Vanille Insensee, which I love. But not quite as nice, complex and long lasting as AC and also, I wouldn't buy two or more bottles of Kayali just to be able to layer, considering their price, when I can just buy one bottle of the finished result.

reviews.name: linds12
reviews.comment: Okay if you truly enjoy citrus fragrances, I would recommend so many others more before this one. D&G Light Blue intense is a way better option for example. Anyhow, the opening of this fragrance is sharp and heavy on the bergamot, which isn't a bad thing for a citrus fragrance. However, the dry down in my opinion is musky and powdery, and the citrus notes become virtually undetectable. The scent that remains was honestly unpleasant and I washed it off after a couple of hours bc I couldn't stand it. I would recommend literally any of the other Kayali scents before this one.

reviews.name: KayleeAp
reviews.comment: I liked this fragrance at first whiff, particularly the citrus notes, but then the more I wore it, the more I disliked it. I had return it. It turned into a rose-focused scent on the dry down which was not what I was looking for. The citrus completely disappeared. Someone mentioned that it smelled like "old lady", and while I hate using that phrase, I tend to agree with that assessment unfortunately. Anyone can wear any perfume that they want, no matter the age, but the dry down smells musty, and dated on my skin. Perhaps someone else's chemistry can pull this one off. Longevity of this was poor - maybe 3-4 hours on me, which is unlike Kayali's vanille 28 which lasts a long time. Surprisingly, the dry down smells a lot like Guerlain's Aqua Allegoria Rosa Rossa, but Rosa Rossa is one that I enjoy, and it doesn't bring up any musty or dated connotations to me.

reviews.name: Angeldna
reviews.comment: I really liked the citrus beginning on this fragrance. I was hoping for more, as citrus is in the name, however after about 10 minutes citrus hid behind musk and rose. Normally that would be ok but musk felt like it was covering up something old while grandma Rose was aging at an accelerated rate. Loved the first 10 minutes citrus...but could so do without dusty grandma holding velvet roses.

reviews.name: ShinningLightOfThunder
reviews.comment: It's rather generic citrus scent. Nothing too special, nothing outstanding, just nice bitter lemon.

reviews.name: Malibu
reviews.comment: Musk and pink pepper are both 'I either love it or hate it, no inbetween' scent notes for me, but here they are perfectly balanced with the rose, so fall on the love it side for me. The citrus and rhubarb notes are the stars of the fragrance and liven it up, whilst adding a drop of fresh sweetness, whilst the woody notes gives everything some depth. A good all-seasons-all-occasions fragrance. The only downside scent-wise is that this is quite a linear fragrance - it is part of a layering collection though, so I knew it would be. Great to wear alone, and layering to add lightness to heavy floral fragrances. The fragrance lasts between 8 and 9 hours on me, and it goes close to the skin quickly. Not the greatest, but I still think it is very good for what is intended as a layering fragrance, and for the purity of the scent notes.

reviews.name: Shinder13
reviews.comment: I just received a sample of this from Sephora and was pleasantly surprised at the fresh and citrusy elements. Can't get any of the tonka or musk though. It's definitely invigorating and girly but with some whiffs I smell like citrus dishwashing liquid. Will do more of a wear test before deciding if I want the full size. Edit: I DO want the full size. Have fallen utterly in love with this fragrance, it's just so energizing!

reviews.name: Silkpuppet
reviews.comment: This fragrance is another soft, fleeting number from the Kayali family. If you recall the long-retired B&BW scent Cool Citrus Basil and miss it dearly, you need a bottle of Citrus 08. Despite basil being absent from the listed notes, I get a distinct green herb element in the initial spray and the drydown. While I don't want to carry on the stale tradition of gendering fragrances, I will say that this is the least sweet and most neutral of the Kayali scents I have tried. Apropos, I could see many men enjoying this scent. I don't get much of the rose core, and I get zero tonka or musk. This fragrance is linear, but cheerful. Slightly more herbal than your traditional citrus a la Guerlain (though it does somewhat mimic Aqua Allegoria Herba Fresca). It reminds me of a lot of recent kitchen hand soap scents with the citrus+herb combos, but think Element or Mrs. Meyers, not Pine-sol or Cascade. I feel that this scent would be pleasant spritzed on linens or light curtains.

reviews.name: waskotransportation
reviews.comment: This is one of the most beautiful and long-lasting citrus perfumes that I have ever tried. I received a small sample of this and cannot wait to get the full size bottle! I love citrus smells but literally every citrus perfume that I own has very poor longevity and no projection. This has both! It is a winner.

reviews.name: Dawnfiller
reviews.comment: I saw this citrus perfume on Sephora’s website during VIB Sale and blind bought it. This is a pretty floral citrus scent definitely feminine. Scent trail is good on me so I can smell it when I cross my own path. It radiates out to 6 feet on me. Arizona desert heat even in November burns up perfume fast on my skin so it lasted about 4 hours before it became a skin scent. After the initial blast of citrus (lemon and lime) within 10 minutes it changed to floral, rose mixed with hint of berries, and it stayed that way on me. I never smelled musk, oak moss, or Tonka. Maybe a hint of amber but maybe that is my imagination. Overall I like this perfume despite not being a fan of florals. I don’t know why they call it a citrus perfume because that is only in the opening. I will wear this as a date night perfume or when I’m feeling girly. Men, this is not unisex perfume so sample do not blind buy.

title: Citrus Molecule RudRoss perfume - a fragrance for women and men 2023
description: Citrus Molecule by RudRoss is a Floral Woody Musk fragrance for women and men. Citrus Molecule was launched in 2023. Top notes are Ambroxan, Pomelo, Vetiver, Iris and Violet Root; middle notes are Lemon, Jasmine and Violet Root; base notes are Ambroxan, Vetiver and Iris.

pros.label: Versatile unisex scent for all seasons
Bright refreshing lemon and citrus start
Great value compared to niche brands

cons.label: Linear development after initial spray
Harsh synthetic or chemical opening
Perceived as cheap Arabic-style clone
Too loud for sensitive environments
Sharp 'screechy' musk profile
Overpowering vetiver note for some
Can feel heavy and suffocating
Lacks the original's airy crystal refinement
Aggressive 'dirty' ambroxan in base
Smells like dish soap or detergent

reviews.name: JaneFlo
reviews.comment: It seems here the brand was making a copy of Escentric 02, but again improved it, in my opinion. The fragrance starts with juicy citrus notes, then goes into Molecule. But the fragrance is not sharp and straightforward, like Molecule, but thinner and more airy, gradually floral nuances are heard. If I see Escentric 02 for men, this fragrance is quite feminine.

title: Citrus Ginger Dossier cologne - a fragrance for men 2023
description: Citrus Ginger by Dossier is a Citrus Aromatic fragrance for men. Citrus Ginger was launched in 2023. Top notes are Peppermint, Ginger and Lemon; middle notes are Pink Pepper, Vetiver and Nutmeg; base notes are Sandalwood, Cedarwood and Incense. Inspired by Chanel's Bleu de Chanel, Dossier's Citrus Ginger opens with a fresh blend of peppermint, zesty lemon, and spicy ginger. The heart introduces aromatic vetiver, pink pepper, and nutmeg, while the base combines warm sandalwood, incense, and cedarwood for a sophisticated woody finish. This fragrance presents a clean, masculine profile emphasizing timeless elegance through its balanced citrus and aromatic composition.

thisPerfumeRemindsMeOf.title: Bleu de Chanel Eau de Parfum

peopleWhoLikeThisAlsoLike.title: Bade'e Al Oud Oud for Glory

reviews.name: jroman0725
reviews.comment: Inspired by: Bleu de Chanel Eau de Parfum (lighter, more citrus-forward take) Longevity - 4–6 hours average Projection - First hour: moderate Mid: light scent bubble Late: close to skin Not a strong projector Weather Suitability - Best: Spring / Summer / daytime ☀️ Ideal: work, gym, errands Not for cold weather or nights [ The Opening ] Lemon, Bergamot, Ginger, sharp citrus burst Experience: bright lemon and bergamot noticeable ginger spice slightly fizzy freshness First impression: clean, sharp citrus with a spicy kick [ The Heart ] Neroli, Aromatic Notes, clean floral freshness Experience: smoother and less sharp neroli adds a clean, slightly soapy floral tone citrus still present but toned down First impression: fresh shower-clean with light floral brightness [ The Dry Down ] Woods, Musk, clean masculine base Experience: soft woods, clean musk, slightly warm but still fresh Final stage: clean woody skin scent with a fresh edge Imagine the Smell - You step out of the shower and catch a burst of fresh citrus, lemon and bergamot hitting your senses instantly, bright and energizing. Then a spicy ginger note kicks in, adding a little heat to the freshness, keeping it from feeling boring. As it settles, it becomes a clean, soft woody scent, like fresh clothes, clean skin, and a subtle masculine edge that stays close.

reviews.name: ih8n8
reviews.comment: Dont tell people im wearing a dupe! but the deal on this was too good I had to pick it up. Yeah it smells like BDC maybe a little brighter but the longetivity is like 30% less. Smells real good, really affordable, would buy it if you dont want to spring for BDC, super blind buy safe. Also its got a magnetic cap and pressurized atomizer, nice touch.

reviews.name: Nomisfragrance
reviews.comment: 90% BdC, I was super surprised by the great quality of this one for $30. Very fresh and citrusy, and while you'd expect the performance to not be very good for a fresh fragrance, it's actually fairly decent. Only downside is the basic ass bottle, but even so, the magnetic cap and pressurized atomizer make it seem like more of an intended minimalist design.

reviews.name: ShadowsofLight
reviews.comment: I'm not smelling BdC concurrently alongside this, so I cannot attest to how accurate of a dupe it truly is it isn't. But I absolutely can detect the resemblance. It has a fresh, aromatic green tone to it. Quite masculine in the opening. Bracing and herbaceous. This is quite an energizing fragrance, but chic as well. And...well, it's refreshing to smell something that isn't overly sweet. I enjoy sweet things of course, but it's nice to experience a fragrance that remains crisp. This is laced with spice, but none of them shout. I have the same feeling I always do with BDC; which is that this is a notably balanced composition. As this dries it does reveal a softer (but not overly plushy) woody backbone. Still, I smell the ginger though. Instead of a candied, sugary ginger; it's earthy, root-y and warm. Almost sunned. It's also quite dry in tone, and reminds me greatly of Coco Mademoiselle. Now...please don't misunderstand me, this does NOT lean feminine (in fact, I don't feel at home in it as a woman personally, I prefer things a bit more femme or unisex), but it is absolutely Coco Mademoiselle's twin fraternal brother. BdC and CM are of great family resemblance (which is a compliment in my book). I like them both. They both feel exceptionally reliable, chic, classy, but also energetic. Overall this is slightly less smooth than I recall BdC being, though it's still a decent option.

reviews.name: m_poisson
reviews.comment: Smells kind of like Bleu de Chanel when sprayed on paper, but on skin, even less so. Super strong, even one spray can be overwhelming. This perfume smells like a department store -- all the smells, all at once -- from the cosmetic counters, the carpets, the racks of clothing and shoes. Not terrible, just gives an uncanny feeling of being a walking embodiment of a Macy's or Nordstroms (if you're in the US).

reviews.name: DanielDean07
reviews.comment: In the opening you get a nice bitter, lemon zest note. In the drydown it transitions into a more woody and familiar scent. Whether or not this is a 1-for-1 of BDC EDT or not, its a really nice, clean, masculine smell.

reviews.name: Icookreal
reviews.comment: This smells nothing like bleu de Chanel. It smells like minty watery breath spray. Do not buy it

reviews.name: austeeezy
reviews.comment: Very pleasant freshie. Opens up with a combination of citrus and ginger. Woods and incense in the drydown. Also has this soapy vibe throughout (in a good way). Projection is pretty moderate. Longevity is around 8 hours. I'll spray this on in the morning and can still smell it on my skin after work. As far as value goes, I found this on clearance at Walmart for $11 (50 ML) so I can't complain. Even at $30 retail, I'd say you're still getting a nice bang for your buck. It's a nice fragrance by itself. As a clone to BDC I it's actually fairly close to the EDT, at least to my nose. It has all the notes but just isn't blended as well.

reviews.name: Jammies
reviews.comment: I don't own BDC so I cant confirm whether its a 1:1 copy, but this fragrance is fantastic on its own disregarding that its trying to be a dupe You get this citrusy, minty scent at the start and when it dries down you can smell a lot of the more woody notes and the ginger, the smell you get is not offensive but not boring either Longevity is pretty decent, same with the strength of the scent, as for occasions for wearing, I feel like you could wear this at literally any time, any season, its a great all-rounder scent For the price its a great fragrance, worth a buy if you're looking for a good, all occasions frag

reviews.name: rigatonipasta
reviews.comment: Very decent fragrance and can easily be purchased on sale for ~$20. I haven't smelled BDC, but the initial spray is reminiscent of Dylan Blue, but fades into a nice gingery and citrus scent. Quality is really good for the price, projection is good for the first 1-2 hours in heat/warmth and slightly better in cooler or air-conditioned areas for about 2-4, and afterwards sits moderately close to the skin but is still noticeable up close for upwards of 8-12 hours. Most reviews I've seen have said that it's a close(ish) recreation and for at least 1/5th the price of the original it gets the job done.

reviews.name: esteban747
reviews.comment: Besides the comparison with Bleu de Chanel, Citrus Ginger is a very nice perfume in its own. I would describe it as a smooth version of Bleu, the mint as quite noticeable at first, more so than the citrus. Then it turns into a clean scent.

title: Citrus Coast House of Noya perfume - a new fragrance for women and men 2025
description: Citrus Coast by House of Noya is a Woody Aromatic fragrance for women and men. This is a new fragrance. Citrus Coast was launched in 2025. The nose behind this fragrance is Jean-Louis Sieuzac. Top notes are Lemon, Pink Pepper and Grapefruit; middle note is juniper berry; base notes are Tonka Bean, Amberwood and Patchouli. Inspired by the sunlit beauty of the Italian Riviera, the dreamers’ perfect summer escape. Reminds you of the charm of Cinque Terre, a colourful coastal gem of natural heritage. The sun-kissed haven, filled with the zesty aroma of the blooming citrus trees. A tribute to ‘dolce fare niente’ lifestyle. Top notes: Lemon Heart, Grapefruit, Pink Pepper Middle notes: Balkan Juniper berry Oil Base notes: Tonka Bean, Amberwood, Patchouli Heart Accord Arrives in a size of 50ml.

reviews.name: Jers
reviews.comment: Fresh bitter lemon and grapefruit opening slightly peppery I get a cucumber lemon water on the dry down great for summer or spring

reviews.name: Sheherazad
reviews.comment: Refreshing, elegant citrusy floral-fruity masculine-leaning Summer scent. Light, bright, zingy, uplifting.

reviews.name: viaticum
reviews.comment: This smells exactly like Sceptre Malachite but heavier on the citrus. I find it hard to believe people would buy this for 200$ when it smells like 20

reviews.name: MuscleMan
reviews.comment: This reminds me of pacific chill so much, but I like this one even more. I got this in a sample set of 30 scents and this ended up being my favorite one, this house needs more recognition.

title: Citrus Blonde Lake & Skye perfume - a fragrance for women and men
description: Citrus Blonde by Lake & Skye is a fragrance for women and men. Top notes are Pink Pepper and Bergamot; middle notes are Wild Orchid, Peach Blossom and Jasmine; base notes are Cedarwood, Patchouli and Amber. Citrus Blonde by Lake and Skye is a fragrance that highlights vibrant citrus and floral notes. The composition opens with Australian pink pepper extract and Italian bergamot, transitions to a heart of wild orchid, jasmine petals, and peach blossom, and settles on a base of crisp amber, cedarwood, and Indonesian patchouli.

reviews.name: Smell-mi
reviews.comment: IT was kismat that I happened upon an open box at a TJ MAX. I smelled it, sprayed it & left the store without it. It brought me calmness. It was refreshing. I had a small bubble. And it was so lovely in the air. This is not so much about the citrus, but about the delicate florals, which remind me of the florals on the trees I pass during my morning walks. Of course I went back for it. CITRUS BLONDE does not last long- about 2-3 hrs (unless that’s when I am nose blind to it). This is what a hug looks like in a scent for warmer/ hot weather. It is worth reapplying. Also, I think men who have a natural deep musky scent will appreciate the refreshing florals to counter balance & lift their natural smell in high humidity.

reviews.name: kerion
reviews.comment: Not a fan. Barely there boring citrus that doesn't compare to the other offerings.

reviews.name: Rehderja
reviews.comment: I wanted to love this but it’s barely there on the skin. I sprayed a lot and still nothing. So sad, so i listed it for sale. Could be a good layering scent i guess.

reviews.name: tmckenney
reviews.comment: I love this perfume so much. I smelled it once and couldn’t stop thinking about it for months. I would not describe it as floral. It is a super solid woody citrus that doesn’t have a gourmand backdrop. Absolutely love and a staple.

title: Citrus Verbena Intense L'Occitane en Provence perfume - a fragrance for women and men 2008
description: Citrus Verbena Intense by L'Occitane en Provence is a Citrus Aromatic fragrance for women and men. Citrus Verbena Intense was launched in 2008. The nose behind this fragrance is Jérôme Epinette. The fragrance features Lemon Verbena and Lemon.

thisPerfumeRemindsMeOf.title: Light Blue Capri In Love Travel Retail Exclusive

reviews.name: AuthenticAF
reviews.comment: I had this a while back, i dont recall which release but it looked like this one. It smelled very strong of lemon, like a lemon cleaner. No one in my family liked it, and i wonder why i did as well🤷♀️

reviews.name: larissamorn
reviews.comment: Perfect example of how skin chemistry impacts fragrance. On me this is nothing but a heaping dose of three day old sweaty socks. Gak. I don’t get lemon or verbena, just stale grapefruit and salt. Not a safe blind buy, unlike their classic verbena which, while it has no longevity, smells amazing on basically everyone.

reviews.name: ANNAFRYS
reviews.comment: I dont think I ever got lemons so pretty as in this bottle. The are so subtle and lye down so gorgeous. In my humble opinion lemons are blended in Musky; Pink Pepper there is also a bit sweetness from Cheeries with Sugar and in delicate second plan pretty Verbena. Its not fresh a fresh; green one. This Verbena is aftermath... The sun burn the plant to Hay almost and that dry essence is added to second plan. In that dry down there also Sweet Oranges. These all ingrediens compose a really lovely scent from Provence. So if you ask me how France smells in Paris and Versailes like Mounsier Guerlain and Insolence.... When you ask me how south smells its like...this: Citrus Verbena Intense L'Occitane en Provence....

reviews.name: KittyDeBeauvoir
reviews.comment: As I said in the Perfume Timeline section - I smell like a huge glass of G&T, and I have zero problems with this! *** Kitty rating: 8.5/10 Hubby rating: 8.5/10 (!)

reviews.name: thinkingover
reviews.comment: A very fresh and beautiful scent. I always feel energised wearing it!

reviews.name: leschoux
reviews.comment: Citrus Verbena Intense is just another L'Occitane perfume that works on me. I received this for my birthday and I really love it! I used it quite heavily before I retired the little amount I have left. If there's a thing that's rather disappointing from this perfume, it's the sillage and longevity. Sillage is practically non-existent if you don't use this liberally. Longevity is also rather poor. It's freshly sour in the first 1 or 2 hours, then it turns into this very faint sweet smell that disappeared very quickly. I even had to jam my nose into the spot a few hours after application to get a very faint whiff of this sweet citrus smell. If you can get over this fact, though, it's a great smell. Perfect for summer when it's hot outside, because it gets better with heat somehow. Overall, I love this, but you'll need to reapply it quite often to maintain the sillage and longevity. Thankfully, the small bottle is not a hassle to bring along in the bag. Unfortunately, it's only 20ml inside so you'll likely to drain it very fast if you keep reapplying it. Solution? Get more bottles when it's available if you're really into this. I definitely am thinking of getting myself another. Rating: 8/10

reviews.name: DresdenDoll
reviews.comment: I really like verbena, but this one did smell like washing up liquid. Washing up liquid that faded to lemon sherbert. Maybe it depends on chemistry, but this wasn't quite what I wanted of it.

reviews.name: pandaeyes
reviews.comment: The first couple of times I wore this it reminded me of washing up liquid. I came back to it recently and now love the smell and the long lasting wear. It's fresh and clean, slightly overpowering on the first spray but clams down quickly

reviews.name: carolynnj
reviews.comment: Smells like cleaner, but fades nicely. It is not a sweet smell. Very lemony. I spray it over a rose scent to make it lighter and more airy. This is a smell that I could take or leave. It'd be cheaper to just go buy some essential oil and dilute it a bit than it would be to buy a bottle of Citrus Verbena.

reviews.name: Jenny-Rebecca
reviews.comment: I had the lotion & shower gel for this once. The top heart opens with a burst of fresh clean lemon softened by the verbana and warm sugary elements. So summery and citrusy in a very fresh and pleasant way.

reviews.name: Svezzy
reviews.comment: I gave it to someone else in the family, caused me headaches all the times i used it. Apart that, i think it is one of the best if you like citrus.

reviews.name: Miss Reem 72
reviews.comment: Very fresh and lovely , but doesn't last at all :(

reviews.name: Efflorescence
reviews.comment: I have to be in a very special mood to enjoy this perfume. It smells very strongly of verbena and lemon, along with freshly mown grass. In other words - it smells like a bathroom re-freshener. But not the cheap stuff - the expensive type a wealthy wife would buy to impress her husbands co-workers. I can wear it on summer days when I just want to smell clean and citrusy, accompanied by the ice tea in my bag, but the stuff have been starting to run out... since my mom has started to use it to make the bathroom smell great. Heh. Guess she felt the same thing about this scent as I did. My cat hate it though, which is kind of to expect, since this is a natural repellent to them. It lastet a long time on my skin in my opinion, but I don't demand very potent stuff either, so I guess it's all just a matter of taste.

reviews.name: porcelain ninja
reviews.comment: This smells great on greasy skinned people like me who tend to smell like the sun on a hot day. It totally counteracts it. Great on kids too the dry down is like lemon candy.

reviews.name: LuckyStar1
reviews.comment: Delicious and very fresh but downside is it doesn't last at all. Shame really. It great if you don't mind carrying a bottle around with you and re applying every hour or so.

reviews.name: NebraskaLovesScent
reviews.comment: This was my signature scent for Summer 2008. Nothing says summer quite like a good citrus scent. This was a pleasant lemon citrus scent with just a touch of green herbs, and was 100% linear. Citrus Verbena Intense was like a true, tart burst of juice from a fresh-squeezed lemon. Hold the sugar, hold the honey. There is not even a hint of sweetness or sugar in this fragrance, and it is definitely unisex. The only drawback was that the fragrance was very short-lived on my skin. On the other hand, the scent was so mild and pleasant, I could reapply every few hours and not worry that my scent was too overpowering or offensive to anyone.

title: Citrus Affair Novellista perfume - a fragrance for women and men 2023
description: Citrus Affair by Novellista is a Floral Fruity fragrance for women and men. Citrus Affair was launched in 2023. The nose behind this fragrance is Marie Huguenot. Top notes are Lemon, Bergamot, Grapefruit and Tangerine; middle notes are Ginger, Jasmine and Rose; base notes are Musk and Sandalwood.

peopleWhoLikeThisAlsoLike.title: Acqua di Parma Blu Mediterraneo - Fico di Amalfi

reviews.name: bringprivateaccordback
reviews.comment: just got out of a shower after a long, sunny spring day. clean apartment, chores done. decided to test this for the first time - so charming! fresh, juicy, equally sweet and spicy. definitely a spring-summer scent for daytime, but I'm still very much enjoying myself lying in bed with this doing nothing. just might get a small bottle of this for the summer time, my collection is missing something like this. honestly, Novellista is such a good brand, I'm always excited to test their scents!

reviews.name: MultitudeContainer
reviews.comment: Very nice straightforward juicy lemon, freshly squeezed. Perfectly unisex. The only downside is the price tag, the fragrance is too simple and too weak for that price level. Lasts for about two hours on the skin.

reviews.name: LintujenAika
reviews.comment: It's ok.

reviews.name: Ruxandra cara
reviews.comment: I really like the smell but for this money it lasts 2h , sad sad sad

reviews.name: Jax22
reviews.comment: Hey guys please do watch my video on this one on my YouTube chanel! As always I have given my honest thoughts, link below : https://youtu.be/Z2XmfHiif5U?si=Lq0xLx5xAStDuFAs @below? Sweet?candy? You sure you didn't get a fake buddy, this one is kind of Hi-brid between Afternoon swim and Bvlgari Tygar

reviews.name: Bunnyance
reviews.comment: It opens very summer ripe juicy citrus with a tinge a sweet tangerine and a bit of spicy ginger bakcground. It is a lot sweeter than I would expect from a unisex fragrance but still unisex if you ar guy that does not mind a bit of sweetness. After 5-10 min the strong citruss fades and transforms into this sweet fluffy citrus cotton candy. It's a lot lighter than I expected as chopards lemon dulci which is in a similar profile lasts a whole lot longer but is sharper. Yes - if you ar looking for a soft, sweet and light citrus No - if you are looking for a pimarily strong and fresh longlasting citrus Vibe wise it is like falling asleep on a fluffy pillow on a sunny and warm summer day in the italian countryside after eating lemon gelato

reviews.name: Hunblina
reviews.comment: I'm so glad I have found this one. It starts very fresh and when it dries down it actually calms me down. I feel safe and happy when I wear it.

reviews.name: velvetulipe
reviews.comment: A beautiful citrus cocktail on the skin. It's refreshing and zesty ! Perfect after the shower... makes me want to snuggle and read a good book. Totally unisex. It really reminds me of "Green tea" by EA... the same dna, but here you smell the skin of the citrus as well. The longevity is much higher in the Novellista perfume. (I mean as much as citruses can last in perfumery ^^) Very nice... love this very much !

reviews.name: Totalcrazys
reviews.comment: Beginning is super lemony , middle reminded me of some cheap perfume I had as A teenager from a regular drugstore, the ending sweet soft and comforting ( at the end of the day ) I kind of liked and kept on smelling my wrist . So iam a bit confused now lol Will definitely try it again .

reviews.name: Bucket_list
reviews.comment: Very fresh and zingy with lots of bright citrus and sparkly jasmine for like an hour, then it fades out to nothing pretty rapidly. Completely unisex.

reviews.name: Pepeb007xx
reviews.comment: One of the best parfumes I ever smelled, very nice scent, very well blended, citrus, zesty, for summer, summer heat its rly close to LV Afternoon swim, with little bit of better projection and longevity it cost me 43€ for 75ml, already have 3 bottles (2 new, backup bottles) for this price, its must have

title: Citrus Tea Dossier perfume - a fragrance for women and men 2022
description: Citrus Tea by Dossier is a fragrance for women and men. Citrus Tea was launched in 2022. Top notes are Black Tea, Bergamot and Fig; middle notes are Bay Leaf, Vetiver, Tobacco and Jasmine; base notes are Hay, Cedarwood, Incense, Musk and Peach. Citrus Tea writing is very linear, at the opposite of traditional top/middle/base fragrances. It offers a unique and lasting freshness, made of bergamot, tea leaf, bay leaf, fig and clear jasmine. Denser notes of wood, incense and hay infuse through this clarity, without ever weighting down the fragrance. Peerless, utterly original yet still incredibly easy to wear, Citrus Tea carries a permanent oscillation between lightness and shadow, freshness and depth. This fragrance is inspired by Le Labo's Thé Noir 29.

reviews.name: kindacantsmell
reviews.comment: Havent smelled The Noir so I cant really compare the two, but to me, this perfume smells like a way too sharp version of Above the waves by ELdO. Kinda made my head hurt :(

reviews.name: Thomas Mudge
reviews.comment: Amazing tea scent plenty of compliments.

reviews.name: THE_Scentaholics
reviews.comment: Enough people do not give credit to the natural smelling smoothness of Dossier scenta. I own both this and the OG. Its about 95% accurate and indistinguishable often times in the air. Decent longevity. Like with houses like Dossier no one should smell regular. Bathe. Lotion. Eat well and douse with Dossier..lol. Nope. Not one cent paid to me. Just calling it like it is. There are some clone houses out here making frag companies sweat. I highly recommend. 4.2 overall

reviews.name: klaudynafragrantica
reviews.comment: Wonderful unique tea scent with great longevity. It’s the elevated spring summer scent it projects beautifully i love it

reviews.name: DrSLBerry
reviews.comment: If your wardrobe is mostly pink, your favorite fragrance so far is anything with marshmallow in it, and you'd give your right arm for Taylor Swift concert tickets, this perfume is not for you. If you love hole-in-the-wall coffee shops and bookstores and are a little on the freaky side, please come right in. 😂 (I'm only mildly kidding here.) But seriously, this is not a "crowd pleaser" fragrance. I love unique and somewhat unusual scents, and this fits the bill. I'll start by saying that I have not experienced Le Labo's Thé Noir 29, so this review is strictly on the merits of this perfume and not how it stacks up to its inspiration. In a few words, this scent is woody, dark, earthy, and spicy. But that hardly does it justice. The first thing I notice is the scent of a strongly brewed cup of Early Grey tea. The bergamot (and maybe a slight hint of cardamom???) is just lovely, and makes me feel like I should be wrapped up in a cozy sweater and blanket sipping on that tea. But don't let that description make you feel like this is a winter or fall fragrance only. In my opinion, Citrus Tea can be worn year 'round, because it isn't heavy, if that makes sense. Even when the tobacco note (which I love) settles in with the cedar wood and incense, it still remains light. By the scent profile alone I would have found it impossible to believe, but honestly it is ok for spring and summer too. (And while I usually role my eyes when someone paints a scene just from smelling a perfume, Citrus Tea makes me think of an overcast day in early September, when the weather is still hot and humid, and I'm running into a used bookstore to escape a downpour. Weird, I know, but some perfumes just tell a story, and this one is mine.) The sillage is moderate and not overpowering, but this perfume is LONG Lasting. Well over 12 hours on my skin (which is super unusual for my body chemistry), and lasts on clothing until you wash it. (I pulled out a sweater I wore weeks ago and the scent is strong enough on it I didn't feel like I needed to apply any to my skin.) Two or three sprays will do the job. I can easily see this being a unisex fragrance, and some have called is out right masculine, but I think (like all fragrances) you just have to be the right woman and it has to speak to you.😊 It's also, in my opinion, a great value for such a complex scent!

reviews.name: ScentedbyCaesar
reviews.comment: Citrus tea On its own ; it’s a beautiful black tea steeped with lemon. The bay leaf I believe is the connecting note between the citrus and the tea. There is a spiciness that comes out as it dries down. Cant say much for longevity yet, but it only lasted about an hour or so on skin ( but this is my first testing). I’ve also smelled the inspiration The Noir 29 from Le Labo and while CT is good on its own, it doesn’t have the complexity of The Noir 29. On my skin, The Noir 29 has this beautiful powdery/ musky tea scent. The tobacco is more prominent but doesn’t push it into masculine land. As it dries down there’s a sweetness that shows itself . Such a wonderful combination of notes. Last longer on skin as well. A must have. Both great scents, but TN29 takes the medal.

reviews.name: evsheps
reviews.comment: oh this is lovely! been wanting a tea fragrance for a long time but have been trying to mind my finances...this is beautiful. fresh, clean smelling, a little more masculine than most of the scents in my collection, but definitely truly unisex imo. I actually am a big fig hater normally so it's kind of crazy that I love this. this is going to be my top wear-to-work frag this season i can already tell

reviews.name: Nickt24
reviews.comment: Tried and it’s a no for me…at initial spray ya maybe 80% similarities but much more sour. Drys after 30 min you smell a leather note and then just a mix of everything. To me it is not the same at all. I will continue to purchase the real thing. 4th time trying a dossier fragrance and will be my last. Another dupe company that got me.

reviews.name: HollyKu
reviews.comment: I think this works well as a companion to Thé Noir 29. It’s lighter, fresher, and less complex, for days when being the second most mysterious person in the room is sufficient

reviews.name: konarzewskaasia
reviews.comment: Beast mode, stays in the room for hours. A bit complicated. Definitely not a people pleaser. I mostly smell tobacco and bay leaf

reviews.name: RichardStroker
reviews.comment: I've been fortunate enough to own Le Labo's Noir 29 for years and have refilled it 3 times. I just finished my bottle and truthfully didn't want to pay for my 100ml refill just yet so I decided to give this a try. I would say it's a 90% dupe of Nior 29. I think because of the way it's composed it last longer/isn't as thick as the original so it feels like you're getting an airier version which I don't mind. If you want a cost efficient The Noir 29 experience, my recommendation is to purchase the scented massage oil ($80~) or fragrance balm ($100~) from Le Labo and use it as a base layer along with a good unscented moisturizer and then spray this on. That combo lasts on me all work day and creates a SOLID scent bubble that I enjoy and is very reminiscent of the original fragrance at a solid discount. Overall not a bad interpretation as there aren't very many on the market. Just my 2 cents.

reviews.name: Kisunase
reviews.comment: Nah, no clue how similar it is to Le Labo, but this stinks to me - veeery sharp smell, I imagined it would be a citrusy tea, but this is a grandma-like scent, the one they wear to church.

reviews.name: rei012
reviews.comment: Initially, it came off as simply quite sharp and spicy, but after a while it seems to settle into a gentler, deeper scent. However, I don't really smell any fruitiness or sweetness. It definitely smells quite green, almost a bit like herbal medicine but in a pleasant way. It's a nice, fresh scent. The performance is quite good, I find that one spray is enough for people to noticeably smell it in the same room. It also lingers for quite a while.

reviews.name: MayJailer
reviews.comment: Since I dropped a hefty sum on a real oud Strangelove fragrance over my holiday break, I decided to be financially responsible and opt for an "impression" of Thé Noire 29, before re-buying the OG, due to the great reviews. I'm so glad I did! This IS thee Thé Noir. Fresh, bergamot heavy Earl Grey tea opening, unfolding into the crisp, grassy/woody notes of vetiver + cedar, finally drying into the clean fig, bergamot, and cedar fragrance we all know and love in Thé Noir 29. There is little, if anything, to distinguish the two (right down to the unlisted touch of rose like the original); and now I have my day to day "work safe" fragrance back in rotation without dropping the entire $300, rather 10% of that. I feel so responsible ☺️. Now I wonder if their Rose 31 impression is this good? That's next on the list. If it is, Le Labo ought to really reconsider their pricing...

reviews.name: maria0802
reviews.comment: To me the slightly intense opening lasts only couple of minutes. And I would not call it very unpleasant, just rather masculine. But then it goes straight to the The Noir territory and stays there. I love the OG, but the prices of Le Labo are just crazy. This one is a fantastic alternative and exactly hitting the spot. I would not say any note jumps at me, just feels like a great smooth blend of pleasant things. If The Matcha is more fun spring/summer perfume, this one is a great fall/winter alternative. Would suit day and night. Performance is good! You can easily smell it in your scent bubble for couple of hours. Prices of dossier are not dirt cheap, but with discounts it feels like an okay deal. Highly recommend if you like the OG!

reviews.name: Sjess
reviews.comment: I didn't like The Noir a few years ago but I somehow got my hands on a new bottle of this Dossier version and it has been love ever since. So either my taste has evolved or this is just such slightly different making me love it..? My good guess would be the first ;). I just really love the combination of sweet, woody, fresh & green going on here, and where TN smelled too boozy on me, I don't have that issue with this one. Quality is really good for this price point, longevity & projection are both excellent and I have gotten many compliments on it, even though I'm a conservative sprayer (2-3 max). Well worth getting imho. However, I'm not a fan of how they do business in the EU, they advertise with 20% discount when you become a subscriber to their mailinglist, however, I've never received a code. They really want everyone to buy multiple bottles and you get a stacked discount for it, but since you can't actually sample any of them you end up buying too much of something you might not like. I have bought this bottle of Vinted for a decent price and will keep an eye on the brand there because I do think (basing it on just this one) that the quality is really good. I especially would love the Citrus Matcha one, since I'm a big The Matcha fan, but it isn't sold here for unfathomable reasons.

reviews.name: Sleepy_cactus
reviews.comment: I think this is one of the best dupes out there! Smells nearly identical to Thé Noir. On me it stayed as consistent as the original throughout all three stages. This is ideal for the office because then you’re not wasting your most expensive perfume for everyday wear. Lasts forever on clothes.

reviews.name: samuelrb
reviews.comment: I just got this one. I've owned thé noir 29 and it's my favourite scent. At first I hated this one because I thought it smelled nothing like it, and it really doesn't while it dries but after an hour or so it smells exactly the same. Love it!! It's a really good economic alternative just don't panic while it sets lol. At first it smells REAL citrus-like and strong, even a bit sour but it doesn't set like that. I'd say if anything this one smells a tiny bit more masculine than thé noir, it's a bit stronger like stingier, but it's still a pretty low-key unique scent.

title: Citrus Bigarade Creed perfume - a fragrance for women and men 2020
description: Citrus Bigarade by Creed is a Citrus Aromatic fragrance for women and men. Citrus Bigarade was launched in 2020. The nose behind this fragrance is Julien Rasquinet. Top notes are Petitgrain Paraguay and Italian Lemon; middle notes are Italian Mandarin and White Pepper; base notes are Haitian Vetiver, Ginger and Mint.

thisPerfumeRemindsMeOf.title: Les Exclusifs de Chanel Eau de Cologne

reviews.name: Cologneinhaler
reviews.comment: Man that opening is to die for, bright lemon, mandarin and ginger, with a petitgrain and pepper spice to compliment it. Vey refreshing, and it dries down into a very pleasant herby musk. Definitely overpriced for what you get, but you cannot deny if you like freshies with scent profile that's not really typical, it might be worth the price tag. I get a solid 6-7 hours, but the projection is more intimate; it's more of a scent bubble. Edit: I've tried a few from this line (zest, neroli etc..) and IMO CB is the best one so far.

reviews.name: LoveinWhite
reviews.comment: In my opinion, Citrus Bigarade is a wonderfully refined, clean, and understated fragrance that’s seriously underrated. Every component blends together seamlessly, creating a perfectly balanced composition without any unnecessary embellishments.

reviews.name: scentaddicted
reviews.comment: Very realistic lemon smell but it doesn't project or last long. The vetiver is high quality so I can say that I really enjoyed the smell and if I had unlimited money might buy this. You can smell the quality of the ingredients. However, at this price point, I think you can expect it to last longer than it does. I received a sample of this from the Paris shop. Creed has other fresh fragrances that offer better performance.

reviews.name: David_S
reviews.comment: It's essentially a lobotomized version of Selection Verte with all of that fragrance's nuance and personality sanded away. Longevity is atrocious; I might get an hour or two out of it on a good day before it fades away to a mildly pleasant skin scent...but even that is very short-lived. I've read reviews that describe it, variously, as Hall's cough candy, lemon drops, or cheap lemon cake flavouring; all are correct.

reviews.name: Mouse011
reviews.comment: The nose behind Citrus Bigarade isn't a Julien Rasquinet, it's a verified information by Julien itself.

reviews.name: Itsalladream
reviews.comment: This citrus forward presentation by Creed is good but nothing to write home about. The fragrance starts off with a bitter citrus kick that lasts for about 1.5/2 hrs on my skin before the spicier elements start to predominate the fragrance. The middle has a spice element that mingles rather well with the fading citrus to give it a nice little tangy kick that usually lasts for a good 2 hrs on my skin. The dry down has a nice minty ginger with subtle vetiver and faint citrus accords that make it my favourite part of the fragrance. This goes on for 3 - 4 hrs giving the fragrance a total of 8-ish hrs. Price: As usual Creed is asking for a steep sum. At $440/50ml it is rather costly. Edit: It is actually $440/100ml Longevity (7.5/10): It has pretty good longevity on my skin, especially seeing the fragrance is citrus forward. I think this can owe it's longevity to the spicer elements, whereas if this was strictly a citrus scent or an even more citrus forward scent like its close relative (Zeste Mandarine), it would be lacking. Projection (7/10): Again, as mentioned above, if this did not contain its spicy elements, I don't think this would project as well. With that in mind it does have pretty decent projection for a good 3 - 4 hrs before settling into a more intimate scent. Sillage (7/10): It is not a trail monster by any means, but it does leave a beautiful scent trail of roughly 3 - 4 ft for a solid 3 - 4 hrs. Scent: 6.5/10): Even though it has a pleasant smell from start to finish, I feel like this scent needs to be above and beyond for what they're asking. I don't think I would buy a full bottle of it now per se but I wouldn't rule out a future purchase either. It's good but not $440 good. I enjoyed this scent, and I think you would too with application in warmer weather. With that being said, I feel that the added spicy elements are a nice addition to the citrus, but it is not mind blowing, especially as stated earlier with a price tag of $440 you sort of expect the fragrance to go beyond the ordinary. Creed did a better job here than with Zeste Mandarine in my opinion, but I still feel it is lacking that special "Creed" feel.

reviews.name: Skywarp
reviews.comment: No one talks about this and i wonder why. It smells like natural lemon supported by a subtle fresh and clean vetiver and mint. For people who are into simple citrus and clean scents that just wants to smell clean, and not like a typical fragrance. And by natural, i mean truly natural smelling because this doesn't smell like it has the generic supporting accords like musk and cheap vetiver that is heavily used in clean scents. This is clean and subtle citrus from beginning to end. Boring? Yes, it has no depth to it, acts as a molecule scent you could say. Just gives a clean aroma around you that doesn't smell like a perfume, and no one around you will think you wear a pefume. Just giving the impression that you have good hygiene. Take it for what it is based on what i explained, and this could very well be your signature scent during the hot summer. If you've tried Acqua Viva by Profumum Roma that has that clear citrus clean and nothing more, this has that type of DNA.

reviews.name: matty64
reviews.comment: I haven’t seen this in the USA.

title: citrus kawaii Pour Toi perfume - a new fragrance for women and men 2024
description: citrus kawaii by Pour Toi is a Citrus Gourmand fragrance for women and men. This is a new fragrance. citrus kawaii was launched in 2024. The nose behind this fragrance is Erik Constantin. The fragrance features Bergamot, Sweet Notes, Powdery Notes, Iso E Super and Salt.
