reviews.name: CageCam
reviews.comment: This is a great scent profile. Airy, not too thick, unique. Excellent blend of citruses and woods. It's a shame it's been discontinued. Just scooped up the last 2 bottles I could find online

reviews.name: CageCam
reviews.comment: Smells great. Performance is terrible.

reviews.name: Afc_Croatian
reviews.comment: For those who don't know, Abercrombie has a sale right now 20% off almost everything. So I picked up this fragrance for $59 CAD (Retail!!!!), what a deal this was. First off, what a lovely clean opening, really paying to its name, it's a lovely woody tea scent. There isn't much depth on it but it's what I like about it, stays clean all throughout. I received compliments everyday on it since I've bought it, it's always the less expensive scents that get me the most compliments! LOL 8.5/10 I overspray it for some longevity.

reviews.name: reesedude
reviews.comment: I work at A&F and I really like this, it's green and woodsy. I find that everything turns very vanilla on me (idk why is just happens) and this on my skin is super good.

reviews.name: dj71
reviews.comment: The notes are wrong on this one. Read the correct notes on the picture of the bottle.

title: Citrus Verbena Summer L'Occitane en Provence perfume - a fragrance for women and men 2006
description: Citrus Verbena Summer by L'Occitane en Provence is a Citrus fragrance for women and men. Citrus Verbena Summer was launched in 2006. Top notes are Lemon and Grapefruit; middle note is Lemon Verbena; base note is Cedar.

thisPerfumeRemindsMeOf.title: Águas e Brisas Festa de Alegria

reviews.name: Eray Gulerler
reviews.comment: Cheap smelling citrus. 3/10

reviews.name: Cedar girl
reviews.comment: This Citrus Verbena Summer was so perfect, that I don't understand why it isn't continued. The current Verbena Citrus of l'Occitane is not addictive like the summer one. It's interesting to see that a little change of notes the scent loses its seduction.

reviews.name: humairum
reviews.comment: Simple fresh lemons, nice for summer.

reviews.name: noy815
reviews.comment: Literally lemons. This is hands down a lemon scent. It’s pretty, simple, and clean. Longevity isn’t the best, but not so bad either. My boyfriend isn’t a fan of lemons so I didn’t sniff twice, but it is a very nice one to try.

reviews.name: Fraggie2100
reviews.comment: pure lemon extract

reviews.name: ronin.jose
reviews.comment: I remember a year ago; we went on a vietnam-style restaurant in our country and I ordered a lemongrass flavored iced tea and the after-taste of it taste exactly like the smell of this fragrance. And also I remember when I used to squeeze a little lemon in my earl gray tea and I sniffed a freshly sliced lemon in half and I smiled cuz it smells exactly like this!. It's lemony but not sharp and masculine, but rather tart. My classmate said that it smells like a lemon flavored hard candy and I totally argee. But I prefer the original verbena. 8/10

reviews.name: pauroj
reviews.comment: OMG i love this, is sweet and fresh and smells like the most amazing lemon candy you'll ever have and of course because faith would have it some dummy ruined it for me by comparing it with cheap hotel soap, so while i think is super yummy some people find it too sharp and citric , oh wel spray with caution since is so fresh overspraying can be a problem..

reviews.name: Red Rose
reviews.comment: Have no idea this version is from 2006 as L'occitane store just opened where I live & I don't buy things online so visiting the store last night was a pleasure. The fragrance itself is so light & refreshing & yes you could bath in it without choking or anything like that. It smells like freshly squeezed lemon with lemon peels & leaves. I have a lemon tree at my house yard but it has no lemonds fruit yet, the leaves smells exactly like this fragrance, it has a bitterness to it & its zestness it unmistakable, very natural & luxurious in a generous 150ml bottle with yellow juice that is so precious & perfect for hot summer days. Guess L'occitane keep producing this edition every summer or so it seems cause it's my first time dealing with them & getting anything at all from them.

reviews.name: moloxa123
reviews.comment: So fresh and invigorating! I love this!

reviews.name: mitsuki44
reviews.comment: First of all, I'm just starting to dip my toes in exploring perfume so I hope this I can correctly express my thoughts on this cologne! I think this is a great, simple summer scent! I love how authentically citrus and bright it smells - like fresh lemon and grapefruit zest - when you first spray it on the skin. After an hour or two, it turns subtly sweeter, like someone has sugared the flesh of the fruit. On my skin, it lingers softly and turns a bit more musky before disappearing after about 4 hours. While I love the top notes and drydown very much, I wish it could just last longer on the skin - it's just much too expensive for the 4 hours it gives me.

reviews.name: susi_susina
reviews.comment: People need to remember that this is a Cologne so it doesn't last very long. But boy oh boy is it amazing!. I spray it all over my body when I come out of the shower and its very invigorating!. You can smell the main 3 notes...Verbena, Lemon & Grapefruit! I especially love the grapefruit in it. So gorgeous and refreshing! Just like summer. A tip is to spray it on your clothes if you want it to last longer :-)

reviews.name: Hodgessarah
reviews.comment: This is a highly addictive fragrance for me. I got samples of the shampoo and conditioner in one L'Occitane order, and I didn't think I would like it, since I'm really not a fan of green or citrus scents. However, this is my one exception. It is heavenly. The citrus is perfect; not too tart or too sweet; but the note that makes the entire fragrance for me is the cedar. It warms the citrus perfectly. I absolutely love this scent and hope it's never discontinued.

reviews.name: feelhot
reviews.comment: First I bought it by mistake :))LOL-I thought it would be EDT, so for the price of L'Occitane 150ml-5,1fl oz. it was quite affordable/vs other prices on L'Occitane website/ so as I love grapefruits, verbena and citruses it was truly me. A box with all my L'Occitane shopping arrived and I discover it is Eau Fraiche-and it is very fragile, delicate fragrance which doesn't last long-that's why it was quite cheap, but I do love it, as it is just perfect for summer time, not overwhelming bursting of freshness cocktail of citruses, verbena and my favourite grapefruit. It is sparkling mist of citruses with hints of verbena, great refreshing and quite long stay on skin, but it is not as strong like EDT, even less then EDC-truly Eau Fraiche :) I strongly recommend it to all fresh-citruses-verbena-grapefruit lovers, is it just ocean of delicate lemonade, not too strong, not sharp, just sparkling and fizzy-full of sunshine, leaves and flowers of grapefruit, verbena leafs. And ... the bottle -is just state of art-love it really:)-will keep for years as reminding of great London Summer, Summer full of Dreams, London tears of Happiness:)

reviews.name: wesleyhclark
reviews.comment: Yep. it's citrus, all right. It's somewhat boring, but you could certainly pay a lot more and do worse. I agree with the reviewer who said that it's not really a perfume, but more of a body mist - putting us firmly in Bath and Body Works territory.

reviews.name: OTA Mom
reviews.comment: Very lemon-y, incredibly fresh and sweet, direct and not complicated. My husband and small children approve, which is quite rare. I love this so much--and could love it a lot more if it didn't have to be applied hourly. :( Good thing I got the 5-oz bottle!!!

reviews.name: perfumebox
reviews.comment: If a smell could sparkle, this would. The hit of verbena-spiked lemon is so beautifully fresh it evokes that moment of pain-pleasure when you take a first sip of ice-cold, freshly-squeezed, oh-so-sour lemonade, rounded out with just a dash of sugar to make it friendlier and more addictive. I tend to get bored of simplistic, linear scents very fast and this is the only one that has stayed with me through the past couple of years, and STILL brings a smile to my face when I smell it. It's my hot-weather comfort scent, perfect for searing hot days and loose cotton dresses when even Chanel No. 19's sharp-green zing is not sufficient to cool me down. While it's a citrus, it's also extremely gourmand the way Serge Luten's Jeux de Peau is a gourmand. Not of the typical sugar-sweet variety, but UNDENIABLY foody nonetheless. (Just thinking of the sour-sweet tang makes my mouth salivate slightly.) Definitely worth checking out if you like interesting food-like fragrances. Two minuses: As you can imagine, this doesn't last too well. It acts like a cologne and seems to be all effervescent lemon topnotes. After an hour, it dissipates and has to be reapplied, which brings us to the next problem. This only comes in that tiny bottle. I need a huge one!!!

reviews.name: KasiaS
reviews.comment: This is absolutely lovely, of course as a typical summer scent. It is basically lemon all the way, plus nice herbal-green and sweet, sorbety undertones. It smells like freshly peeled lemon along with some green, lemon-tree leaves, and lemonade with ice. Some people say they smell grapefruit, I'd say it's rather lemon peel, but it's a very similar, bitter and citrusy note. I figure using it abundantly as a body mist on a hot hot day. It has nice staying power and reasonable projection.

reviews.name: knife
reviews.comment: Well "migotka",according our so called genius we all going to have a tropical heat even in Sibiria sooner or later probably sooner.The global warming should arrive for about five or sex years ago... So fill up your basement,with this perfume bottles especially if it`s so low staying power.In our country the winter turn to be colder then somebody should except,especially this winter.It`s a fine line from genius to idiot.However I don`t care a shit about the weather when I use perfume.The main thing is to read description`s and trust people when I searching a lemony fragrance and several people writing: HEAVY SPICY!! That is not a lemony perfume. Thank you all for warning me of this particular matter!!! I`m tired of loosing money with blind buy.

reviews.name: erilika
reviews.comment: My friend loves this and she bought a bottle. On first sniff Nestlé Lemon Tea instantly came to my mind. Not a bad smell, really, just, very lemon tea.

reviews.name: HappyShopper123
reviews.comment: A very sweet fresh citrus fragrance that unfortunately only lasted about 10 minutes on my skin.

reviews.name: spidermonkey
reviews.comment: This is one of my favorites! Unfortunately, the scent lasts for about 1-2 hours & you will go though a bottle in three months tops. The verbena and lemon is a perfect blend & I always get complements...perfect for summer!

reviews.name: Migotka
reviews.comment: This fragrance is a burst of grapefruit. It's like those sweets that contain fruit sparkling mousss inside, as they dissolve in your mouth, the mousse bursts in your mouth leaving a funny feeling on your tongue. It's like a lemonade from freshly squeezed citrus mixed with sparkling water. And it's like a lemon hand cream I used to use a few years back during wintertime. There is not much to this fragrance apart from the sparkling, sharp citrus notes. I think they re a bit too sharp, and there is nothing here to make them softer and rounder. I am not too fond of drinking an undiluted lemon juice, and I feel the same about Verbena. It's nice, but it feels like a citrus overdose, although perhaps would work better in a hotter climate.

reviews.name: Anneka
reviews.comment: I just bought this scent and really love it. It gives me an uplifting, happy feelingthat makes me want to keep smelling it more and more. It does fade away..but I found if you use the body sorbet lotion first and then spray the fragrance, it lasts a lot that longer. Normally, I dont wear citrusy type fragrances, but this is a perfect upgrade for summer from the regular L'Occitane Verbena scent.

reviews.name: melanie
reviews.comment: This is not really a perfume, it's more a fragrant body mist that is perfect in summer, I can not think of a better refreshment, or perhaps EdC 4711, also very pleasant. Citrus fragrance is not my ultimate choice, even though they are very comfortable in the hot summer months. Citrus Verbena Summer smells like sweet lemonade with a few drops of honey, stayes on the skin with clean, lovely scented trail. Body lotion is enough in combination with body mist, I like even more than some of the later editions. Sometimes smells like cheese cake with lemon, or apple pie, but less gourmet, maybe ice cream with lemon and vanilla. Fine fragrance and certainly I was not reminded of the smell of toilet cleaners, it's a very nice summer scent. Actually there are many different scents with citruses, this is one of those I liked.

reviews.name: Mellyhelly
reviews.comment: I bought a big bottle of this fragrance and don't know when I will finish it! It's a totally different type of scent for me as I usually go for Floral Oriental Vanilla, but I was looking for a fresh and clean type of scent to be easily worn in offices where heavy perfume is often annoying and air is not always pure and light... Too many humans packed. I like most L'Occitane fragrances, because they're quite light but distinctive, not mainstream, maybe simpler than most popular design concoctions and not overly expensive. They also stay true to their formulas. It was natural for me to look into their lines for a fresh scent. I already knew their traditional Verbena, absolutely lovely. Citrus Verbena is less sharp and sweeter. First it smells like those frizzy lemon bon bon sealed in single papers that I used to buy for very little money in the parish's bar when I was a child. After only 3-4 minutes it fades away a bit and verbena starts its lingering herbal typical aroma. They go on happily hand in hand. It's true that it fades quite quickly compared to more intense perfumes but I think that it was meant to be so. It's a beautiful pick-me-up with a sparkling lemony burst to wake up and a gradual fading, not so quickly fading for the kind of fragrance, after all. It last more than 2 hours on my skin and it also mingles nicely with heavier perfumes you put before. Both lemon and verbena are said to be brain stimulating and efficiency enhancing in aromatherapy. They remove sluggishness and clear the mind. They're also "clean" smells in a herbal way, not laundry musky clean which I don't like. They should have anti-bacterial properties, but I'm not sure they work in this form. I spray 1 single squirt while at office and people commented that it smells so fresh and clean! As for me I like the sensation of delicate push-me-through-work effect. Sometimes I only spray a small hanging puppet to boost a little aura around my personal squared meter. It goes very well with natural environment desktops :-D As a perfume I think it's a nicer and less expensive other choice for lovers of common mainstream like D&G Light Blue or Moschino.

reviews.name: ruxxy
reviews.comment: I don't know how the plant smells like, but to my nose "Citrus Verbena Summer" is a mix of lemon-vanilla ice-cream and lime. Nice fragrance for those who like the smell of floor cleaners with an extra hint of sweetness. I have nothing against it;))

reviews.name: averillann
reviews.comment: At first this is a great fresh,clean perfume and is especially good when it is warm and you are looking for something refreshing to wear. However, I have found that it dies too quickly - but maybe it is just my skin type.

reviews.name: Boggi
reviews.comment: I only got to squeese a little amount on my skin from a tiny little wet cloth packed from loccitane. BUT I will try my best; its a very natural citrus scent. I reminds me of a certain type of lemon bonbons that has been around here in Norway long before I was born. The scent is not too sharp, its rouned with sweetness. I guess it could remind of insect repelants made of real essential oils or wet cloths handed of on airplanes. Its a common smell, but I like it anyways. A honest, non-offencive and I would say delicious smell.

title: Citrus Garden Première Note perfume - a new fragrance for women and men 2025
description: Citrus Garden by Première Note is a Citrus Aromatic fragrance for women and men. This is a new fragrance. Citrus Garden was launched in 2025. The nose behind this fragrance is Mathieu Nardin. Top notes are Spearmint, Ginger, Green Mandarin and Basil; middle notes are Peony, Mugane, Lavender and Tomato Leaf; base notes are Fig Milk, Amber and Sandalwood. A summery stroll along the Amalfi coast, where the orange hue of citrus blends with the blue tones of the sky and sea. The sparkling opening of green mandarin and fresh ginger meets a floral-green heart of lavender, tomato leaf and peony. The whole is softened by fig milk and enriched by the creamy woodiness of sandalwood. A reminiscence of Mediterranean tradition, freshness and sunshine.

title: Citrus & Green Tea The Dua Brand perfume - a new fragrance for women and men 2025
description: Citrus & Green Tea by The Dua Brand is a Citrus Aromatic fragrance for women and men. This is a new fragrance. Citrus & Green Tea was launched in 2025. The nose behind this fragrance is Mahsam Raza. Top notes are Black Tea, Bergamot and Fig; middle notes are Bay Leaf, Jasmine and Vetiver; base notes are Cedarwood, Incense and Musk. A fragrance combining citrus freshness with green tea nuances, crafted to deliver a bold and sophisticated olfactory experience. While specific details about this particular scent aren't explicitly mentioned in available sources, Dua Fragrances specializes in creating long-lasting perfume extracts using high-quality natural oils sourced from industry leaders like Givaudan and Firmenich. Their fragrances undergo rigorous quality control processes supervised by founder Mahsam Raza.

reviews.name: Danielsan24
reviews.comment: Very clean fragrance. Not offensive or challenging in any way. Starts with clean citrus that quickly is wrapped in fig and/or tea leaves that makes its subtle but beautiful. For the price I cannot complain. Btw I have yet to try the OG

reviews.name: Jmonchet
reviews.comment: I'd love to try this one.

reviews.name: mis.anderson
reviews.comment: I just got this one yesterday. It kind of just disappears after the initial citrus burst. I’m going to see if letting it sit for a while helps. Edit: two and a half weeks later it’s developing into something stronger, so far so good.

title: Citrus and Wood Yardley cologne - a fragrance for men 2011
description: Citrus and Wood by Yardley is a Woody Spicy fragrance for men. Citrus and Wood was launched in 2011. The nose behind this fragrance is Christian Provenzano. Top notes are Lime, Bergamot, elemi, Mandarin Orange, Lemon, Orange and Grapefruit; middle notes are Birch, Pepper, Ginger, Tea, Carrot Seeds and Cumin; base notes are Vetiver, Cedar, Moss, Sandalwood, Amber, Patchouli, Tonka Bean and Vanilla.

reviews.name: Scott 2
reviews.comment: I adore this fragrance, and you can't beat the price. My only complaint is that it lasts no more than about 30 minutes on my skin.

reviews.name: Carlitos
reviews.comment: MY YARDLEY McLAREN F1 SOUVENIR Yardley is one of those brand names that I recall very well from my childhood. I still remember the beautiful McLarens M19 and M23 that raced at Brands Hatch and Silverstone in Yardley livery, driven by Denny Hulme, Peter Revson and Mike Hailwood. Peter Revson (of the Revlon family of cosmetics and fragrances) won the 1973 British Grand Prix in a Yardley McLaren M23 at the Silverstone circuit and I was there to witness it. Drivers such as Jackie Stewart, Emerson Fittipaldi, François Cevert, James Hunt, Ronnie Peterson, Jody Scheckter, Niky Lauda, Clay Regazzony, Jacky Ickx and John Watson were all on that Formula One grid. The week after that F1 race, a bottle of Yardley (I can't remember which one) was the very first perfume I bought with my own money. I was 10 at the time. Citrus & Wood was released in the last decade and it's a very classy scent. It comes in a handy 50ml bottle that you can easily slip into your pocket. The citrus notes combined with the resins, birch, spices, amber and woods make a bold impression. The staying power is average, which is a shame because the ambery-woody dry-down is very pleasant. I have already bought three 50ml bottles of Citrus & Woods. I have more than 850 perfume bottles in my wardrobe and I wear this Yardley fragrance quite often. I like its earthy vibe and the fact that it slips neatly into my raincoat pocket on rainy days. I also wear Terre d'Hermes quite often. As a motor racing aficionado, my real personal problem with Hermés is that they have never sponsored McLaren or any other Formula One team, either in the 70s or in recent years. So I continue to reward Yardley by being a loyal customer of their fragrances. The fragrances - Citrus & Wood and Terre d'Hermes EDT - have an obvious similarity, but the Yardley bottle costs twelve times less per ml or ounce to be precise. The performance of the Yardley fragrance is inferior, but all I have to do is take the handy flask out of my pocket and spray it again. So, following my head and my heart, the Yardley flask took a chunk out of my Terre d'Hermes EDT usage. And my nose never complained about my head and heart's decision. I hope you don't mind a few little white lies in the text above. I still have the Terre d'Hermes EDP and EDT and use them a lot. It's a fantastic scent. My 50ml bottles cost me €10.00 each. At that price, I would only suggest that you buy at least one bottle blind. MUSIC: "Faster" by George Harrison Note: "Faster" was written and performed by George Harrison. The song was inspired by Harrison's year away from music in 1977, during which he travelled with the Formula 1 World Championship, and by his friendship with drivers such as Jackie Stewart, Emerson Fittipaldi and Niki Lauda.

reviews.name: tyleam
reviews.comment: When I had Citrus and Wood by Yardley, I did not like it. It was a weaker clone of Terre d'Hermès but smelled way sharper than TdH did in the opening. I almost trashed it but kept it and sprayed it on my off-season clothes. One year later, my clothes smelled like woods and vetiver; it was heavenly! Now I wish I had bought more bottles of Citrus and Wood by Yardley just as fabric refreshner.

reviews.name: Tiger84
reviews.comment: This is brilliant, I bought it a long time ago, and now I can regret I only got one bottle. Although I have two similar fragrances (a Terre dupe and Roger & Gallet's L'Homme Sport), so I'm set for decades. A forest full of clean air, green mosses, fresh air... It's so green... like in the movie Death at the Funeral where they just keep yelling how green is everything. Yes it's pretty much the same as Terre.

reviews.name: rosequeen
reviews.comment: I would never have thought of Yardley as being a great visionary perfume house, especially when I bought a bottle of Citrus & Wood, probably soon after it came out in 2011. I came across it looking for their cocoa butter soap and then also buying their citrus & wood soap too. I bought the perfume/edt as sort of an afterthought, mainly because it was so cheap. I probably paid less that $40 online for it. And promptly wore it a few times, but it apparently made little impression on me, and I don't know why. I'd had other woody scents beforehand that I liked. I must have bought something I liked better because Yardley Citrus & Wood got relegated first into odd parts of my perfume closet and eventually out of it entirely into the depths of a drawer I seldom open. Then just yesterday I thought, "gee, rosequeen, you have a lot of new wood fragrances on your wish list..." And "don't you have a bottle of that Yardley stuff...?" So now I'm considering Yardley a visionary. At least with Citrus & Wood. They certainly came to the woodsy party before almost any other perfumer house, niche or hugely commercial. I don't usually look at the perfumer profiles but I discovered Christian Provenzano, who made this, has since then made any number of exotic fragrances, some of them with oud and other woods that people are paying lots more for. To be clear, I'm talking about the original Yardley Citrus & Wood bottle, the one pictured here at Fragrantica. Square bottle with deep brown label. Some eBay seller is calling this bottle "the original formula," implying they've monkeyed around with it since 2011. I don't know what that bottle looks like, but a cursory search of other Yardley men's scents on eBay indicates that Yardley has at least changed the design of their bottles since 2011. This Citrus & Wood is a great fragrance, rich and woodsy and long lasting; 6 hours on my skin. The citrus is bracing without being overwhelming. Not at all sweet; men will like this one. I just noticed it isn't unisex like I thought; oh well, I'm a woman and I really like it. Good year round; well, on a cool 65 degree summer day anyway. Don't remember wearing it on a sweltering 90 degree day. And still very inexpensive online, but not a lot of them around. At least not today; maybe people bought up the supply for Father's Day. And you'd get away with buying it for your father too; it smells enough like the higher priced wood fragrances to be a good gift, especially to a father who thinks you spend too much on perfume, even when you've hidden most of your purchases from him. A frugal father would wear this stuff often instead of waiting for "special occasions." Frugal fathers consider no occasion is so special to warrant wearing a glitzy obviously expensive scent. (Which is why we see so many of them from varying eras on eBay.) I don't see any of the Yardley Citrus & Wood soap online though. Good thing I didn't blithely use my last bar thinking I could get more of them at any time. It's not 2011 anymore, rosequeen... Edit, April 8, 2022, nearly 2 years after my review: Yep, it's a huge clone of Terre D'Hermes, just like everyone says. So close I wonder if Hermes didn't sue Yardley, or press them very hard, to take the Citrus & Wood off the market. It certainly seems to have disappeared quickly. No sign of it on eBay either, now or about a year ago when I last looked.

reviews.name: im.atwork1
reviews.comment: I didn't liked it much, and the performance is very weak. It smells like a bit of rotten oranges, with some medicinal vibe to it. I actually got it before I can lay my hand on Tere D'Hermes. But after smelling it, I decided to give the TDH a miss.

reviews.name: myrmecophag
reviews.comment: If you want an ersatz of Terre d'Hermès at 10% of its price, go for Yardley Citrus and Wood. Sure, it's less complex, has a weaker performance BUT when I happenened to wear it, everybody around thought it was Hermès...

reviews.name: finality1111
reviews.comment: $7.95 in my country and nobody talks about it. (All the better for me haha) Longevity is good with 4 sprays (6/7hours) in climate control (aircon room). Moderate projection for the first 2 hours. (1 arm's length/across the table) Longevity is halfed in the heat but it PROJECTS when mixed with your sweat. I used it for dragonboat training and once got a remark from a mate that he felt thirsty for Calamansi after practice!! It's not a perfect clone, Yardley is more Lime than Orange but the woody notes come very very close.

reviews.name: Nagaraj K
reviews.comment: Opens with loads of pepper, woodiness & orange. In the dry down that woodiness becomes smoother with a very tiny hint of pepper. Moderate longevity & projection. More mileage can be obtained by using the body spray (which smells smoother!) with EDT.

reviews.name: Kalendula
reviews.comment: A simple and inexpensive scent when I am in the mood for something uncomplicated and uplifting. It's very woodsy. I am a woman and I love it; find it to be a unisex scent. Longevity is non-existent.

reviews.name: theo.sudarja
reviews.comment: I have the talc powder version. I guess it is the same smell more or less. The smell is definitely vintage and classic. I would describe it as a cologne-ish scent. It's comparable or similar to my: Fragonard Confidentiel

reviews.name: Fraggie2100
reviews.comment: the price for this is dirt cheap. But the performance after 5 mins spray is a skin skin and fades away soon! exactky what you paid for a cheap performance, zero longevity.

reviews.name: 2words
reviews.comment: citrus n wood

reviews.name: Sirwan
reviews.comment: Citrus and Wood smells exactly like Terre de Hermes with less performance but also the price is 5 times lower then Terre De Hermes. Very masculine smell. This is worth giving a shot. This has the same notes as Terre de Hermes. I blind buy the bottle and I am satisfied that I bought this bottle.

reviews.name: Carlitos
reviews.comment: Yardley is one of those brand names from my boyhood. I still remember (very well) the McLaren M19 and M23 racing in Brands Hatch and Silverstone with the Yardley colours. Peter Revson (from the Revlon family) won the British Grand Prix in 1973 driving an Yardley McLaren M23 in the circuit of Silverstone. Guys like Jackie Stewart, Emerson Fittipaldi, François Cevert, James Hunt, Denny Hulme, Ronnie Peterson, Jody Scheckter, Niky Lauda, Clay Regazzony, Jacky Ickx and John Watson were all in the grid. An Yardley bottle (which one?) was the very first cologne I bought at the age of 11 years. .....welll.... Citrus and Wood by Yardley is a magnificent Woody Spicy fragrance. I bought my third bottle last week. I have about 200 perfume flasks in my closet and what do I wear? .... Citrus and Wood!!!! .... and by the way, I used to wear Terre d'Hermes with some frequency. The real issue is that they never sponsored McLaren or any other Formula One Team neither back in those days, nor in any other year. The scents are really alike and the Yardley flask costs kind of 10 times less per ml or oz (13 times to be precise). So I just follow my head and heart and now the Yardley scent took the place of Terre d' Hermes. And my nose never complained with my head and heart decision.

reviews.name: VJG
reviews.comment: Maybe there is a difference in the bottle manufactured in UK and the one done in UAE. Got the UAE one. The opening is horrible .Dry-down (Orange) sandal note . Cant get any other notes mentioned here. Sucks

reviews.name: Riftwind
reviews.comment: I will agree that this is very similar to Terre D'Hermes. I think the performance of Hermes is better, but for the price, Yardley is a winner. The scent is not too complex and stays pretty much the same from start to end, besides the citrus "blast" in the beginning. Lasts about 4 hours on clothes, and with my chemistry, about 3 hours on the skin. To be honest, I have only used this scent in the Winter thus far, so performance could change in the warmer months when heat and humidity will play a role!

reviews.name: mrluctoretemergo
reviews.comment: read my other reviews and find out if you (more or less) agree with my humble opinion(s)... this one is indeed soooo similar to terre d'hermes...that i rather buy this cheapie...fact. Spray on your clothes for better longevity. Nah just kidding. The OG is still better but when you´re on a budget, you can get this one for sure. 8,5/10

reviews.name: Robwiz
reviews.comment: Yardley is one of those brand names from my childhood. You bought their talcum powder as a gift for your granny! I hadn't given the brand serious consideration since! However, being very impressed with Terre d'Hermes after extensive testing, I did a lookup here to find close alternatives and Citrus & Wood was top of the list. I bought blind online. I'm not disappointed. It really does open and dry down like Terre d'Hermes. That bitter citrus opening note is captured perfectly and the heavier earthier notes follow up the same. I wish the longevity was better, however, the upside is that you can spritz this on generously without fear of being called 'Mr Cologne'! And at a fraction of the cost of the Hermes original, being generous with the spray certainly won't break the bank. A fun fragrance and strongly recommended.

reviews.name: sarabx5
reviews.comment: It's a Terre of Hermès with less performance but 5 times cheaper. Up to you to decide.

reviews.name: Satyajeet73
reviews.comment: I'm enjoying my third bottle of this fragrance....99.9% similar to Terre 'd'hermes and slightly airy with amazing performance at extremely low cost of $12 for 100 mo. This is just marvelous and my signature scent.

reviews.name: KIZILBAY
reviews.comment: Şu günlerde Türkiye de Gratis mağazalarında 35 tl ye bulabileceğiniz parfüm. Burada yazarların büyük bir bölümü bunun TERRE Hermes e çok benzediğini yazmış. Ancak ben bir benzerlik bulamadım. Terre de portakal turunçgiller çok bariz bir üstünlüğü sahip bunda ise durum farklı. Ancak çok benzediği bir Hermes var o da Hermes Voyage. Pek parfümleri birbirine benzetmeyi sevmem ancak buradaki benzerlik çok açık. Hem Terre yı hem Voyage ı bununla 3-4 defa karşılaştırdim ve açık ara Voyage a benziyor. İşin ilginç tarafı Voyage edt den daha güçlü parfüme ise yakın bir yoğunluğa sahip. Kalıcılık çok güçlü değil. Ancak beni asıl şaşırtan bu daha keyifli bir kullanıma sahip. Voyage ise özellikle ilk başlarda benim "yagli gibi" dememe neden olan bir havaya sahipken bu daha fresh tarafa yakın. Aradaki fiyat farkını söylemeye gerek yok. Bence bu fiyata çok başarılı. Voyage dan 1 yıl sonra piyasaya verilmiş ve açıkçası başarılı bir taklit olmuş. Ancak halen bunu onlarca kişi nasıl Terre ye benzetiyor anlamış değilim. Çünkü alakası yok.

reviews.name: Forget Freeman
reviews.comment: Yardley's Citrus and Wood is a fantastic affordable scent and is (IMHO) a hidden gem. Well blended, lasts 3-4 hours on me, and reacts well to heat, especially if you are out and about. I'd recommend it for transition periods of the year (Autumn and Spring). Citrus and Wood gets compared (understandably) to Terre d Hermes. Although quite similar there are some distinctions. Longevity on TdH is better but at 3 times the price that is to be expected. Citrus and Wood is a little more restrained, it also is more linear. The Orange note from TdH is substituted with Lime, but, to be honest, if I smelled either on someone else I would be hard pushed to tell the difference. I got a boxset with 50ml Edt and 200ml Shower Gel for £16.95 (around $22 for friends across the Atlantic) and is an absolute steal for that price point. As dubairob states in their review buying the toiletries line is a great way to compliment either Citrus and Wood or Terre d Hermes fragrances (the later's toiletries line is criminally overpriced in my opinion) so can save you some money. Excellent!

reviews.name: Satyajeet73
reviews.comment: This is turning out to be a fantastic scent.... Amazing smell, good projection and longevity and at on fifth the price of Terre d'hermes .... it's a signature worthy scent. I just love this one. 10/10 from me.

reviews.name: chagatai
reviews.comment: It smells like Citrus and Wood:) And yes a clone of hermes but extremely cheap version however this scent is not bad but actually I like it a lot. It so suitable for daily use masculine woody smell. Longevity is not su much but keep the half of the day. Especially for that price definitely it should try and wear.

reviews.name: farook
reviews.comment: This is a wonderful scent that exudes calm, maturity and class. Subtly refreshing and a gem for its price alone. Call me selfish but I hope less people find it

reviews.name: Rie60
reviews.comment: I just got this and as a woman, I love it- I'm a big cedar fan as well as citrus and this is just the right combo with out all the fluff usually associated with womens cedar scents.Not in the notes but to me it's got a very marine quality-salty air and a little seaweed smell....my new favorite

reviews.name: Houdini4
reviews.comment: I was very, very impressed with this fragrance initially, it goes on strong and really is full of Citrus and woods as the name suggests. Sharp citrus opening which is neither lemon, grapefruit, bergamot but then slightly orange too. The balance is added by Cedar, vetiver and birch smoke, hint of spices in particular ginger and oakmoss, which had me thinking I'd found a real masculine gem in Yardley Citrus and Wood. The main problem comes when it doesn't even last an hour on my skin, before it just goes 'poof' into the ether, leaving nothing behind. I'm pretty forgiving when it comes to performance but I can't rate a fragrance that is this poor on longevity, no matter how good the opening is. Sorry this gets a thumbs down from me.

reviews.name: JGideon
reviews.comment: Princ Charles's signature scent. And he can wear whatever he wants!

reviews.name: eliweisz
reviews.comment: What a glorious summer fragrance! Harsh lime and vetiver opening, tart, dry, almost medicinal, quickly moderated and enriched by luxurious cedarwood. Nice birch and oakmoss notes, too. Longevity is moderate, 5-6 hours on my skin (and more on cotton shirt), sillage is quite strong in the first two hours. Pleasant woody drydown, with a drop of bergamot oil. Very affordable, it doesn't feel cheap at all! Recommended.

reviews.name: wonderwoman77
reviews.comment: i am a woman and i wear this all the time i find it very hard to find a good mix of citrus and wood....and womens fragrances are always laced with vanilla or something sweet

reviews.name: dubairob
reviews.comment: For those who really enjoy Terre d'Hermes, I would recommend the Yardley Citrus and Wood deodorant spray and talcum powder to complement the fragrance nicely. They are essentially the same fragrance as the original Hermes and both are good products. I have tried this Yardley EDT but unfortunately the performance is poor for me. I'll still stick with TdH as an EDT. (As an aside, I am going to finish my bottle of TdH and not replace it as I am noticing it all over the place here in Dubai. Just way too popular.)

reviews.name: iwhiffwaft
reviews.comment: Man, I really like this stuff. It smells pretty close to cedarwood essential oil. Not as strong and abrasive, but its there. Terre D'Hermes has that too, except more blended with something like violet or something. This is a slightly purer cedarwood. I had this before Terre D'Hermes, so I actually prefer it to it. I have to say that both of these are in my top 10 though. I've gotten lots of complements from women with both. I don't think this stuff would be for everyone though. Its not an aquatic, so its not really sporty or anything. Its woody and light though, just with some sophistication. This is a scent of a man. Not a younger man who isn't mature yet. This is a mature scent without being old. Mature, sophisticated, non-offensive. Absolutely, not synthetic. Very woodsy. If this scent works for you it will work well, if it isn't your style I'd pass. It's definitely for me.

reviews.name: TouchyMonkey
reviews.comment: I found this online for a very cheap price as part of a gift set. I checked the reviews on here and noticed that one person likened it to David Beckham Homme. Homme to me is an extremely underrated scent that actually got me started in collecting fragrances and to this day it remains one of my favourites and I can totally see when that one person said it was similar. The opening citrus blast is almost overwhelming, painful even but fortunately that harshness only lasts for a few seconds, then it immedately settles down into a delicious scent. I dig this one, a lot! Decent longevity and silage for the price. Get on it.

reviews.name: Arbre Amer
reviews.comment: Undoubtably this is a Terre d'Hermes smell alike from the first sniff. It opens with a very beautiful, dark and tart vetiver note that sings a perfect duet with the citrus and made me close my eyes and inhale deeply. As it progresses it mellows down but never stops reminding me of a simpler TdH, the eau fraiche edition. Gone are the flint aspect, the strong sillage and the monster longevity of TdH as this wears much closer to skin. Not a bad thing though as this has a natural feel about it that makes it a "feel good" scent. One of these rare and often inexpensive gems that put a smile on my face (RnG vetiver was another one). The drydown is mossy and slightly musky but still retaining a sweet citrus character. Best news about it is that since it is much cheaper that TdH it offers itself up for abuse and overdose ;) Love it, wish it came in a bigger bottle!

reviews.name: Coffee King
reviews.comment: Had to add a quick Christmas review, what a great Christmas gift this one turned out to be, hate to say I turned my nose up a little when I saw it, but wow what a cracker. I have to agree that the drydown does share a lot of similarities with Hermes. Having revisited this I will be using this a lot more then the last bottle, which for some reason is way off the new one. Gorgeous lime opening, love it..

reviews.name: smellavision
reviews.comment: As i haven't tried Terre des Hermes, I am not able to say whether they smell alike. What I can say is that C&W opens with a sharp, alcoholic blast of lemon that within the next 30 minutes evolves into an extremely pleasant, refreshing drydown with birch, vetiver and sandalwood. It's a very pleasing allround scent for adult (30+) fellas that can be used for work as well as leisure time - only be aware you shouldn't use this as a refresher in betweens meetings - or your colleagues will think you've just downed half a bottle of gin until the drydown settles ;-) I really like this little cheapie, though the "standard" bottle with the paper label does look really - well, cheap! Sillage is medium/OK, but the longevity rather poor - I get a max of 3 hours from this baby. A recommendation for Yardley should be to give it some steroids, make a long lasting intense or EDP version and stuff it into a somewhat more classy bottle. They could easily double the price, and I would still be buying it.

reviews.name: LordCharfield
reviews.comment: Nattonline. The logo used by Creed that you mention is the heraldic French symbol, the "Fleur de Lys" meaning "lily flower". This has been used across Europe for centuries and, thus familiar to most people living on our continent. The symbol that you refer to on the bottle for this (Yardley) fragrance is the heraldic badge of the Prince of Wales, made up of feathers and a crown of alternating crosses and Fleur de Lys. The two badges (the other belonging to the British monarch) on the bottle indicate that Yardley supply products to both of these royal households. Companies using these coveted seals will have had to go through a rigorous process in order to be entitled to use either of them. I'm no royalist by the way!!!! I'm waiting for delivery of a bottle as I write. At under £10 delivered from Amazon, it's worth a go to satisfy my curiosity. Incidentally, Yardley have updated the packaging to look more contemporary, sophisticated, and befitting of the contents (and put the price up!), but original bottles are still available. Well, it's arrived and after an hour, still going strong! It does smell extremely similar to TDH EDP and nicely tamed by the addition of birch. Well worth the blind buy. A true bargain, so another visit to Amazon, I think!

reviews.name: psebi101
reviews.comment: Exactly like it's name is suggesting, this is a woody citric fragrance. After owning this and Terre d'Hermes for a while, I can say that they share some similarities, Yardley being more synthetic and dry and it lacks the creaminess and richness. Also, the orange is substituted by lime. If you want a cheaper version of Terre, this is one. But beware, it feels synthetic and it performs worse. Scent: 7/10 Longevity: 5/10 Projection: 3/10

reviews.name: RobbieX
reviews.comment: To start we have a lovely opening of lime and bergamot nicely blended. Then it gets more potent, woody and soapy with great sillage - it's a very pleasant long lasting drydown. Yardley have produced a gorgeous EDT here and it wasn't expensive - a very good purchase - nice bottle and packaging too. I tried Terre de Hermes at the airport duty free and immediately thought "Yardley Citrus & Wood". A gorgeous frag and a bargain. Never mind the price, this is up there with the very best - a gem.

reviews.name: Tiger84
reviews.comment: Very similar to both Terre d'Hermes and Montale Red Vetyver. Also very similar to Roger & Gallet L'Homme Sport. The name sums it up very nicely: you smell citrus and woods. Though the simple and effective name doesn't quite describe how masterfully they are blended. Simple and amazing, a deep green scent. Smells excellent quality, especially compared to its price. Or should I say, regardless its price. I'm a woman who loves orientals but has to find something fresh for the summer, and men's scents such as this are just what I need for summertime. However I must add that the woody notes give it enough depth to be used in colder weather, too. Longevity is also much better than you would expect of a citrus scent.

reviews.name: Gokhanburak53
reviews.comment: So similar with terre but terre is more vivid, you can smell orange and grapefruit in terre so easily, terre has vivid orange and grapefruit. Yardley is not same with terre but you can make them so similar with adding some orange and grapefruit oil into Yardley. Then there is yours cheap terre d'hermes :))

reviews.name: Coffee King
reviews.comment: I agree very much so with the previous commenter, I blind bought a bottle of this, think it cost me £7, what a revelation. Zesty and woody, worked nicely with the chemistry of my skin. Smells fairly expensive, didn't last amazingly on, but for how much it costs it doesn't really matter. I wonder had Yardley branded this differently, maybe under a different name how would it have done. Yardley's history of Lavender and Soap does not help. Great fragrance all round.

reviews.name: ericrico
reviews.comment: Well - what is this...Yardley? I find your bar soaps to be fair (and classic) when I can grab one at the Dollar Store. But, this bottle of juice is something else - entirely. I give you a round of applause. I read some other online reviews of this and went blind on a bottle as it is relatively inexpensive. Very, very natural smelling! I love how the citrus has a soft and warm element with the wood immediately - there is no sharpness here. Wonderfully smooth and harmonious. The notes don't lie - there is Birch wood in here (I know - I used to work in a wood shop). The warmth of the amber enriches the base here so nicely with the cedar and sandalwood. Nice pinch of pepper - not overly done. This is truly as 'advertised', Citrus & Wood. A slight touch of moss, but just enough (a dash is perfect here and, honestly, an unnecessary but very welcome addition). A solid scent, but it is one you need to be close to, or on the skin to admire. Longevity...fair, about 30 minutes in and this is fully integrated and it lasts for several hours on skin, but not loud. I am going to the back of my hand repeatedly - nice, very nice. However, I would have to say that it wears so naturally that if it was full of synthetics to make it last a little longer, it would lose all of its 'au naturelle' goodness. I like it, as is. This is a great little creation (a hidden gem that is talked about more now - finally, as I got my bottle in very early 2012, soon after it was released). One that will wear in warm weather nicely as it develops...I think this shines at 75 degrees or warmer and radiates more. But, it smells awesome and warming on a cool, crisp winter day - so wear it whenever. Surprised me more than a little - even though the other reviews (by people whose noses, know their scents) brought me to buying this a while back...cheers!

reviews.name: Syzygy73
reviews.comment: What a deceptive scent. Don't let the mundane name, packaging and bottle fool you, this scent has substance, even depth. It is cheap but doesn't smell cheap. It smells zesty and fresh with citrus that gives way to a lovely leathery/wood dry down. Admittedly its sillage and longevity are mild, but it's a deliciously calming and rejuvenating fragrance and well worth a place in any collection.

reviews.name: disgruntledbastard
reviews.comment: smells like nail varnish. if it smells like terra d'hermes, im going to save my money by not buying it. I just don't like the smell at all, its just so boring.

reviews.name: onihan
reviews.comment: just like hermes terre but more than 10 times cheap

reviews.name: fitzmar1994
reviews.comment: The ironic part of our little hobby, is the variety with which we perceive the same thing. I have always read reviews ,but am not really influenced. If I can afford and most times I can, I will buy a fragrance that is said to be like another I may know and judge for myself. This now is another classic example. My nose is not as cultured or to the extent some claim their noses to be. For those of us whose noses are not, I say go for the gusto with this scent, you are missing a lot in not getting it and you are gaining a classic. A ton remains in your pocket and you will be noticed wearing this scent.

reviews.name: Marangoni
reviews.comment: As I die-hard niche perfumery fan, wary of wandering anywhere below Serge Lutens, I got the surprise of my life today. A friend asked me to accompany her in a pharmacy, of all places, and there it was - the perfume I'm going to drown in all winter, with a 15 euro price tag! A-ma-zing perfume, I sprayed it heavily on me, my clothes, and every piece of paper I carried with me, I'm in love! When I see quality, I know it, and this marvel has it. I'd love to wear Terre d' Hermes too if it were available in my country. Oh, and I'm a girl :)

reviews.name: jahmoon
reviews.comment: One of my favorite parfume is H'ermes Terre and this is really like it. Not bad but not good enough, only positive side of it, more cheap than Terre. But can't be the same.

reviews.name: bearinganhourglass
reviews.comment: Did not like it. it is cheap and smells cheap. Does not smell fresh to me. start and a middle gave me headache, a single nauseous vibe. The base is ok though. I would rather have a single splash of Kenzo Homme and this one goes for my shoes. be careful with this perfume, it is not for everyone.

reviews.name: olfactorgasmic
reviews.comment: Residing in an olfactory bubble of citrus and wood, or is it wood and citrus. Drying down nicely, keep cupping my nose in the residue of my palm, always a good indication. Not going to be evening romancing on this, but would do something active and productive...might even go into the workshop having been invigorated by its after shower freshness and mingle it with the scent of worked iron and sweat. Could equally enjoy it at the end of a days travelling in the heat after that first shower in a gem of a hotel, replacing the staleness of the day, on a small arid Greek island as the crickets begin their nightly chirp. Then I am joined by someone beautiful at the bar overlooking the moon lit sea and I was wrong, romance is in the air and the soft rythmic swoosh of sea on sand is the perfect foil for the scent haze and the contrasting smell of my new companion.

reviews.name: steve0580
reviews.comment: The dry down is identical to M7 Fresh. I put M7 Fresh on my wrist with Citrus and Wood on my arm. After 20-30 minutes, I was unable to tell any difference at all.

reviews.name: Lock1454
reviews.comment: *I do not have a nose that is well trained - this is my rudimentary opinion of this scent* Exactly as the name says, this smells like citrus and wood. A very nice scent that projects well for a good 4-5 hours and hangs around on the skin for several hours after that. This is a very versatile cologne that I think could really be worn on any occasion - formal or casual, work or play, day or night. In the cold weather it may not get noticed too easily but going with a couple extra sprays should help a bit. I've only had the opportunity to wear this a couple of times but one of those times was back home in the kitchen with my mother preparing dinner. I was sitting at the table and she passed by to snatch up a plate, she stopped dead in her tracks, leaned in and exclaimed how absolutely fantastic I smelled. She's a fan of masculine scents - not one who likes a man wearing sweeter scents like A*Men. To quote my dear mother, "Wow, you smell amazing! Wear that and you'll get yourself a nice little chick-a-do." Gotta love her =) The kicker, I got it for a quite nice price, ~15 for a 1.7oz bottle. I've looked to pick up an extra bottle to keep on hand but I can't seem to find it anymore for nearly the same price(only a couple of weeks after I got my first bottle), wonder what happened to it?

reviews.name: Bigsly
reviews.comment: If you like the idea of Terre d'Hermes but it is too sharp or has too much iso e super for you, then this is the one to make sure and sample. I'm not a big fan of this kind of scent, preferring more complex and smoother ones, but I don't find anything "wrong" with it and I look forward to sampling it again during the warmer months.

reviews.name: TheLumineri
reviews.comment: Terre D'Hermes' younger less-accomplished brother. Very nice for the price, but doesn't replace the beast known as TDH in anyway! This is the temporary fix to the cloud-nine high provided by TDH! Overall a good scent though! Do not misunderstand - this is NOT a bad review...simply noting that, although it does smell similar to TDH, it will not replace it simply because of the price tag. Shame it only comes in 1.7oz. If you find it at your local bargain store, do not hesitate to buy it! VERY SAFE BLIND BUY! Enjoy. UPDATE: Ended up ordering 3 more as backups...I REALLY like this scent!

reviews.name: Deefit
reviews.comment: I am amazed by how closely this resembles Terre d'Hermes EDT. What a beautiful scent and at such a low price, wow. Sillage is moderate but longevity is actually pretty good, more than I had expected. Most definitely worth the money!

reviews.name: oliver1h
reviews.comment: I love this perfume! On a side note: it really resembles Terre D'Hermes a lot. Both Yardley and Terre are great scents. I love both! The biggest difference between them is that, Yardley's most important note is BIRCH. Before Yardley I did not know I love this note. Birch with vetiver... Everyone should try this great scent! Also has a rather good price tag, and for that price, you will not find many well blended scents like this!

reviews.name: bradford67
reviews.comment: Wow I'm so impressed with this blind buy all most to good to be true Ive spent up too $350 of all times and as low as $25 for the most unique perfumes I spend allot of my time reading and testing perfumes for men i have a large collection of fine perfumes and now excited to to share with you that Yardley has earned a a spot in my top five list.This juice is hrs and some from the start to the finish three good sprays.Once this juice hits the main stream look out!!And yes if you like Terre d'Herme's you will love Yardley Thanks [email protected]

reviews.name: Wise Owl
reviews.comment: This was a blind buy and I was worried that it might smell like lemon funiture polish. However considering the low price and the very favourable reviews I decided to give it a go and was very pleased with my purchase. What you get is a very refined and very classy smelling fragrance. It may be a 'cheapie' but in no way does it smell like it. Essentially it does exactly what it says on the bottle in the fact that it smells of both citrus and wood. Not a particularly helpful statement you may think, but it delivers exactly what it promises and that is never a bad thing.

reviews.name: funnyboy
reviews.comment: Gets up my nose.Doesnt soften after wearing into the sexy beast that Terre D'hermes is. I suppose this is o.k for office wear but I find it too linear and on me it has gone in an hour. Like one of the previous reviewers I purchased from that famous U.K discount outlet. I also bought the shower gel which helps with longevity but even with this it still needs more lasting power. Glad I didnt pay full price a bit too peppery for my liking.Might be donating it to a "favorite" uncle sometime in the near future. Out of 10,all round, a 4.5 from me...

reviews.name: JohnMc
reviews.comment: Uncanny. Uncanny the degree to which this fragrance offering from Yardley resembles so nearly the heavyweight that is Terre d'Hermes. Unbeatable too, in the price department. For anyyone who has expensive tastes, but not the money to indulge, try this alternative as it is so close to the aforementioned fragrance as to be positively plagiaristic and yet so affordable. A low cost winner in all senses (excuse the pun).

reviews.name: ronnie1975
reviews.comment: Cheap, lime , birch, inexpensive TDH, nice smell, i like it I like it better than TDH. i have them both. this is more woody, and not so heavy

reviews.name: Odiee99
reviews.comment: I bought this one blind from Marshalls around a month ago. When I 1st smelled this one I knew I smelled this before so I went through all of my samples and ahaa..... Terre D' Hermes. I'm not a fan of Terre D' Hermes, it's just a little to harsh for me. For anyone that wants to save around $40-$50 go with this instead of Hermes. I don't dislike this scent, it's just not my style.

reviews.name: Smellsgood59
reviews.comment: The best men's fragrance from Yardley. Refreshing. Makes you feel good. But the fragrance does not last long.

reviews.name: dancinleroy
reviews.comment: I bought this for $8 @ tj maxx the other day as a blind buy because it was so cheap and I love woody fragrances with light citrus. This is a great smelling little fragrance. It's really sharp and to the point on opening. I smell the citrus immediately...more like grapefruit with lime and spicyness. It settles down to a crisp vetiver/bergamot combination that smells very very good. It doesn't last very long on me... maybe a couple hours at most but at 8 bucks, who cares? I bet it will last a lot longer in the hot weather. Oh, I've never smelled Terre de hermes but I get a much lighter Encre noir on the drydown, maybe its the vetiver and wood.

reviews.name: Charles
reviews.comment: Terre d´ Hermes? Yes, but minus patchouly.

reviews.name: MarFreire
reviews.comment: Really, this is the twin-brother of Terre d'Hermes. For lovers of Terre that have a small pocket, this is a great option.

reviews.name: babybash44
reviews.comment: By the way, this is a great spicy and earthy smell. I put it on when I'm wearing brown or if I'm feeling environment friendly. Very natural, out-doorsy, and deep. It's not your everyday freshie aquatic predictable fragrance, this is more along the lines of Terre D'Hermes.

reviews.name: saint james
reviews.comment: Found this in TJ Maxx a few weeks ago. They were having a huge fragrance sale. I wondered why something so recently released was at such a discounted price? I bought two w/out even smelling it. I love the Yardley Sandalwood powder so I took the risk. It's a good woody scent with a citrus note that I wish were more tangerine than orange. I have worn it twice. Women seem to like it. I also use it to scent my bedding. I hope this scent helps to push Yardley further into the present frag market.

reviews.name: nattonline
reviews.comment: Hmmm... Is that the same logo on the box that can be found on Creed? Anyway, I have been a fan of Yardley soap products for a long time, and it's no brainer not to get this fragrance when I saw it at a bargain (Gotta love TJ Maxx!) Yes, I get a similarity to Terre d'Hermes too. The obvious differences that I can think of would be the Yardley is more simple and more about clean green lime while Terre d'Hermes is more complex and rich in orangey notes. Plus, I get a lot more of bitter vetiver than heavier cedar. It is fresh, invigorating, strong, and can be a little sharp. Nonetheless, it is nice and very inexpensive for what it is. I welcome it 100% to my collection.

reviews.name: babybash44
reviews.comment: The title alone made me buy it. I found it at TJ Maxx and decided it was time for something new, and this sounded so interesting and fancy... Yardley in London! How fancy does that sound! Anyway, it smells quite like Terre de Hermes as others have pointed out, but a little more noticeable (not that subtle is a bad thing). I like this frag, it's a great, unique addition to anyone's collection.

reviews.name: incenselover100
reviews.comment: this smells as close to terre de hermes as you will find. I do have terre de hermes and quite love it. However, today I just came across a bottle of Yardley Citrus and Wood. I purchased it without testing as the price was to good to pass up. Once home, upon intial spray I said to myself this smells really familiar. Then it hit me. Terre de hermes ! It basically has the same notes. Citrus on the top and vetiver in the base. Yardley C W is a nice fragrance though it smells like something I already own. There are slight differences but nothing big or unpleasant. If you already own terre de hermes, Yardley C W will still be a nice addition. This is something you can put in your gym bag or take during traveling, while saving your terre de hermes for more special moments. If you don't have terre de hermes and find it to expensive, Yardley C W is a nice solution as it doesn't come off smelling like a cheap imitation. I do like this one.

reviews.name: colago
reviews.comment: We've been waiting long for the legendary manufacturers of toiletries to provide men with a new fragrance but the wait was worth it. This little gem which can be purchased at a very reasonable price will never let you down. The citrus notes don't seem to dominate the whole, the scent develops rather towards the woodsy overtones - and that's very, very masculine. To cut a long story short - go for it! It's bound to become a modern classic soon.

title: Citrus Ester Aether perfume - a fragrance for women and men 2016
description: Citrus Ester by Aether is a Citrus Aromatic fragrance for women and men. Citrus Ester was launched in 2016. Citrus Ester was created by Amélie Bourgeois and Anne-Sophie Behaghel. The fragrance features Grapefruit, Methyl Pamplemousse and Rhubarb. "If I remember correctly, it was on the first day of the world. One of those days that seems to stretch out forever, and you are not quite sure there is going to be a second. The moment quivers with energy. An energy ghost, bright (methyl grapefruit) and caring, tinged with a slight taste of primordial fruit (firascone, rhubafurane). Life, the beginnings of life". Citrus Ester by Aether is a Citrus Aromatic fragrance for women and men. This is a new fragrance. Citrus Ester was launched in 2016. Citrus Ester was created by Amélie Bourgeois and Anne-Sophie Behaghel. The fragrance features grapefruit, rhubarb and methyl pamplemousse.

thisPerfumeRemindsMeOf.title: This is Not A Blue Bottle 1.5

peopleWhoLikeThisAlsoLike.title: Acqua di Parma Blu Mediterraneo - Fico di Amalfi

reviews.name: XxRosexX
reviews.comment: For something that's supposed to only smell like grapefruit & rhubarb, I'm getting a sharp perfumey vibe like there are fixative base molecules hiding in this keeping the volatile top notes from flying away too quickly. It's kinda spicy smelling. Yes it's obviously a bitter tart grapefruit rhubard scent, but I swear it also smells like there is black pepper. Maybe it's just me...

reviews.name: muzzbait
reviews.comment: I put this on today after almost a year -- it was in that 'too difficult' basket; I wasn't sure if I liked it, or if I LOVED it. Anyway, I went to the storage unit last night and picked this one up, just to help me make my decision, and it seems that yes, I still like it, and yes, I wish to buy it. It will be entirely contingent on a WHOLE heap of things going right, and so, hopefully, I'll cross this off my 'to buy' list and bring the whole 12+ years of sampling to an end! It's a sharp, bitter (but pleasant) citrus, offset by a dirty note -- part metallic, part caustic, part cumin. I don't have the right words for this, unfortunately, but I REALLY like this one, and I'm looking to buy it!

reviews.name: muzzbait
reviews.comment: I'm really REALLY getting into this one. It's a dirty citrus -- possibly cumin, but with a blunted metallic element, as well as muted moist/overripe notes. It works -- a synth citrus with an unclean element. As with other Aether scents, I'm picking up familiar notes with other known scents. And this one is reminding me of something. First year of WSC, basically the start of my mass-produced niche journey, so, I'm inclined to say L'Artisan, possibly Batucada or maybe Timbuktu. Anyway, it's quite pleasant and enjoyable, especially without that bitter-sweaty seawater note...I really need to think on this, and even try it out again. I don't know if I need this in my life, but I am enjoying it...

reviews.name: Fatter
reviews.comment: This is not really like Pamplemousse Rose or Un Jardin Sur Le Nil. It has no floral note. It is grapefruit yes, but it isn’t the same kind of grapefruit in either of those perfumes. It’s a bit soft, sweet, almost has a candied aspect. Citrus Ester doesn’t quench my grapefruit craving as it’s not musky, bitter, or juicy enough. Pleasant though. Inoffensive. An idea would be to layer this with a scent that does contain grapefruit to enhance that note. I’d be curious to know if this was similar to that discontinued L’Occitane grapefruit and rhubarb scent.

reviews.name: CarolineF
reviews.comment: Smells soo good but last 1-2 hours.

reviews.name: thethe
reviews.comment: Wonderful stuff!

reviews.name: mrtnlkn
reviews.comment: plain and simple: love it 💕

reviews.name: Pantheon_West
reviews.comment: Completely agree with the reviewer that said this doesn't get the attention it deserves. Actually this perfumer seems to be maligned by most. I digress; opens with a unique grapefruit citrus that is quite sharp. Fades into a very pleasant rose that maintains a diminished, but still noticable, more lemony citrus on the back end. Then you notice an ever increasing Woody/ earthy scent. This base note takes center stage but the rose/citrus remains close for the life of the fragrance. All stages of this scent are sharp, which some may attribute to a "chemical" smell but I find invigorating.

reviews.name: _christine_
reviews.comment: Æther nails simple perfection with this one. Sometimes simple fragrances are incredibly beautiful. This is a blast of grapefruit rind followed by a fizzy grapefruit soda (Jarritos!). The dry down is tart rhubarb and lightly sweet citrus. For something so citrus-focused the longevity is great.

reviews.name: tinyhedgehog
reviews.comment: This is a delightful, fresh-sweet citrus, with almost vanillic aspects and a floral tinge that brings to mind some of my favourite rhubarb desserts. It's not meant to be a photorealistic grapefruit, but more of an artistic impression of one - the scent of it blending with the warm air of a bright, joyful summer day. I usually like my citrus a bit on the bitter side, but I still like this very much. I'll definitely come back to it when the weather is a bit warmer; I have a feeling this would take a whole other dimension on slightly sweaty skin.

reviews.name: IronBitters
reviews.comment: Smells like Terre D'Hermes but with a lemon.

reviews.name: yann33
reviews.comment: very good release by aether, rhubarb mixed with grapefruit and pamplemousse is awesome . definetly unisex and very good for hot weather.

reviews.name: Ascention
reviews.comment: Great fragrance that unfortunately doesn't get the attention it deserves. It's sweet but at the same time got some really interesting undertones to it (if I had to describe it, I'd say tard ones. Perhaps it's the grapefruit. Anyway, it's definitely something you have to experience yourself). Despite it not being a natural perfume, it doesn't smell synthetic at all. I really love it!

reviews.name: infernofromoccultdiary
reviews.comment: Più che agrumi ha l'odore di cenere. Di sigaretta.

title: Citrus Grove Skylar perfume - a fragrance for women and men 2021
description: Citrus Grove by Skylar is a Citrus fragrance for women and men. Citrus Grove was launched in 2021. The nose behind this fragrance is Sarah Horowitz. Top notes are Grapefruit, Mandarin Orange and Pistachio; middle notes are Magnolia, Honeydew Blossom and Mint; base notes are Almond Milk, Musk and Amber.

peopleWhoLikeThisAlsoLike.title: Eden Juicy Apple | 01 Eau De Parfum

reviews.name: cocoarose
reviews.comment: This smells exactly like fresh squeezed orange juice on me. Nice but not really something I want to smell like.

reviews.name: Lykopa
reviews.comment: The opening is a sharp citrus. Like straight out of the grapefruit peel realistic. I don’t love the rest of it as much. My rollerball leaked everywhere. Unfortunately, there is this minty green note, which I normally love when done well, that just smells synthetic and kind of puts a damper on the fun for me. The nuttiness is nice. I am a lover of strong, pronounced citrus scents and this fit the bill, but something about the top notes are off to me. It smells like an hor-d’oeuvre or a palette cleanser shot if that makes sense. EDIT: This smells like a bouquet doused in Cuties orange juice when layered with Gucci Gorgeous Gardenia. Fun!

title: Citrus Connection Earth Love Life perfume - a new fragrance for women and men 2025
description: Citrus Connection by Earth Love Life is a Citrus Aromatic fragrance for women and men. This is a new fragrance. Citrus Connection was launched in 2025. The nose behind this fragrance is Florent di Marino. Top notes are Cassis, Bergamot and Lemon; middle notes are Gardenia, Jasmine and Tea; base notes are Musk, Clove, Patchouli and Orris. A refreshing, cheerful fragrance celebrating human connection. Citrus Connection captures the cozy ambiance of citrus tea moments shared with loved ones. “Step outside, Reconnect… Feel alive…”

title: Citrus Splash Alexandria Fragrances perfume - a fragrance for women and men 2017
description: Citrus Splash by Alexandria Fragrances is a Citrus Aromatic fragrance for women and men. Citrus Splash was launched in 2017. The nose behind this fragrance is Hany Hafez. "Summer's best friend. Citrus splash indulges those who wear it with a bright neroli wrapped around a refreshing elixir of citrus fruits like lemon and mandarin. Add some fresh herbs such as mint, tarragon, coriander and basil and we have a party! This beauty holds its powers at the basenotes with a gorgeous musk, civet and labdanum mix. Perfect for a sunny day. Citrus Splash is sure to be your best companion throughout the day. Beware, just because this is a fresh scent does not mean you should underestimate the power of this magic elixir. It will stick by your side all day." - a note from the brand.

peopleWhoLikeThisAlsoLike.title: Bleu de Chanel Eau de Parfum

reviews.name: Cologneinhaler
reviews.comment: This smells fantastic, it reminds me of a ADP type profile. Natural, bright and perfect for summer. I had another clone of MDA (Just Jack), and CS blows it out the water as far as quality of scent profile.

reviews.name: Mr Inquisitor
reviews.comment: I enjoy this scent. Not too overpowering

reviews.name: My5Scents
reviews.comment: It’s definitely very close to Mandarino di Amalfi but not an exact copy, most apparently in the opening. In Citrus Splash the mandarin note smells a little less clean, perhaps not as fine or pure, it’s more embedded into the other surrounding notes whereas in the OG the mandarin initially is more distinct. This is a minor difference though that’s only noticeable in the opening. In the dry down, once things settle I can’t tell them apart. The most significant difference between the two is longevity, this is where Citrus Splash shines as it outlasts Mandarino di Amalfi by a good few hours. I can still smell it on my hand after 12 hours. I’m very happy that despite the Tom Ford version now being discontinued, through Alexandria Fragrances one of my favourite fragrances gets to live on and I look forward to wearing this in summer.

reviews.name: DrNDJas
reviews.comment: Summer Simplicity: realistic citrus blend creamified and amplified with neroli. A slight aromatic herbal accord helps avoid the dreaded ‘bathroom cleaner’ effect. Smooth composition, no jagged edges or clunkiness. Easy to wear, unisex (as citrus and neroli are quite genderless) but the herbal notes might skew it just a bit masculine. Recommended!!

reviews.name: MNadz
reviews.comment: Sweeter interpretation of the OG...which, is a positive for me. I really like orange...and the longer it lasts, the better. This goes on citrusy and fresh. As it dries down, the orange blossom is super pronounced. I think of other fragrances that I love the orange opening, but then I’m bummed it goes away. Not here, the orange here gets louder. It is in the realm of the OG, but the fact that the orange is dominant makes me like this one a bit more. Projection is pretty strong for awhile. Longevity is a good 5/6 hours. Both are better than OG. Overall, as it stands on its own..,it is really good. If you are holding for an exact copy, you might be disappointed. As always, get a sample and check it out before bottle purchase.

reviews.name: iceezzyyy
reviews.comment: Yes, this is a Tom Ford clone, of one of my favorite fragrances from his house. It does a very good job of impersonating Mandarino di Amalfi, but to my nose, it is a little bit more creamy. Somebody said it resembles orange candy, which is pretty accurate. It is like an orange creamsicle ice cream pop. It is definitely not a bad fragrance, and smells quite good. I actually layer this with my bottle of Mandarino di Amalfi to make it last a bit longer. Also, as we all know, Mandarino di Amalfi smells amazing, and is a perfect summer time fragrance for a day out on the boat or the jet ski, but it doesn't quite have any lasting power, but Citrus Splash lasts about 5 hours on my skin after sweating in the sun and having a dip in the ocean. Good fragrance,and definitely allows me to stretch my bottle of Mandarino di Amalfi.

reviews.name: Vaiphonic
reviews.comment: Very fresh citruses mandarines, orange and Bergamot made with obvious fine ingredients project perfectly throughout the summer heat and last for a long time. It is one fragrance that is although niche quality it’s easy to wear and appreciated by a variety of audiences. Longevity is at least 8 hours

reviews.name: ReturnToScenter
reviews.comment: I love this scent, but it took some time, and I wanted to warn everyone about this with Alexandria Fragrances: lots of times their fragrances haven't had time to settle and fully blend, so they smell and perform completely different after 2-3 weeks than they do when they first arrive. This juice did have a bit of a "synthetic" (more like "fake," because let's be honest, most juice is technically synthetic these days) orange tint when I first got it, like more of a bathroom hand soap smell, and it only lasted on my skin for maybe 90 minutes. I left it on my shelf in disappointment, but came back to it about a month later just to remind myself what it smelled like, and I was stunned. It was so much deeper, richer, and more powerful than what I had remembered. Now I wear it all the time in the warm weather, and I absolutely love it. I get closer to 7-8 hours of longevity from it, too. So a word to the wise! Let your AF bottles rest for a while before you decide if you like them or not!

reviews.name: Ahmed1919
reviews.comment: its not as expected no similarity to TF, very synthetic and annoyed me.

reviews.name: Gumbinator
reviews.comment: I just received my package for Citrus Splash this morning! WOW! Hany got it right once again! Initial spray you get a very sweet mandarin orange note.As some people have said on both the website and the review below me; it is a sweeter orange. Like a candied orange. Surprisingly it does work a lot nicer than Tom Ford's opening. But after a few minutes, the orange fades into the background. It is still present, but then this is where Citrus Splash shines! Where the clone aspect kicks in. After the opening, it is all Mandarino Di Almalfi baby! I am talking pretty much 99% spot on with the 1% being the sweeter orange opening. Citrus Splash projects very very nice from start to finish (I have yet to experience the finish as this one is still going strong 3.5 hours after application.) The one thing I cannot get over is how precise this smells to the real deal! I saved about $200 ish because of Citrus Splash! This is probably my favorite citrus freshy! Pair this with a crisp white shirt and a open buttoned dress shirt, a pair of nice blue jeans, and your favorite shoes. Alexandria Fragrances is on a roll! I also received a sample of Monarch (Clive Christian X for Men clone) which is excellent! There is no page for that one, but that one is another great fresh fragrance! 12/10

reviews.name: leogusso
reviews.comment: Inspired by Mandarino di Amalfi by Tom Ford. The thing that I love about the original Mandarino di Amalfi, is that its citruses are not strong and powerful, but they can create a sort of magic that I haven’t found in Citrus Splash. Too sweet, too powerful. In my opinion it smells like an orange candy. But don’t misunderstand me: the quality of the ingredients is really high. If you love sweet citrus scents, than this is the one for you.

title: Citrus Pop Hollister perfume - a new fragrance for women 2025
description: Citrus Pop by Hollister is a Citrus fragrance for women. This is a new fragrance. Citrus Pop was launched in 2025. Top notes are Grapefruit, Mandarin, Orange, Lemon and Lime; middle notes are Jasmine, Freesia and Lemon; base notes are Vanilla and Musk. Hollister Citrus Pop is a refreshing yet warm body mist inspired by the feeling of sun-kissed skin. The fragrance opens with a vibrant burst of citrus fruits including sparkling mandarins, Persian limes, lemon, orange, and grapefruit that energize the senses. The heart reveals delicate florals with sheer jasmine, yellow freesia, and lemon chiffon that create an uplifting and confidence-boosting scent. The fragrance settles into a warm and comforting base of vanilla and musk that provides a subtle, lingering trail.

reviews.name: nakota
reviews.comment: Straight up Dolce&Gabbana’s Devotion, with typical body mist parameters.

reviews.name: scentsational taste
reviews.comment: I purchased two from this range which smell nice but zero projection and no staying power. Admittedly they were only 4$ each so what can I expect. The smell is actually very nice doesn’t smell cheap or juvenile but just constantly re-spraying.

title: Citrus & Bergamot Kruidvat perfume - a new fragrance for women and men 2024

The fragrance features Bergamot, Jasmine, Musk, Violet, Ambergris, Cedarwood, Lemon and Rose. "Discover the timeless charm of Kruidvat Citrus & Bergamot Eau de Parfum - a refreshing fragrance that enchants with sparkling citrus notes and soft floral accents. The fragrance gives a harmonious fusion of different notes, enhanced by a base of musk and amber, giving a lively and versatile fragrance experience." Brand website.

reviews.comment: Finding out Kruidvat makes fragrance was not on my bingo card

reviews.name: catflap
