It opens with a fresh combination of lemon, mint, bergamot, and pink pepper, followed by a spiced heart of ginger, jasmine, and nutmeg. The base brings together amber, cedar, sandalwood, and patchouli, giving the composition a warm woody finish.

reviews.name: BurningGiraffe
reviews.comment: The DNA: A surprisingly high-fidelity take on the Bleu de Chanel profile. It hits that "expensive shower gel" vibe perfectly, opening with a bright, effervescent grapefruit and mint that transitions into a clean ginger-incense heart. The Comparison: It bridges the gap between the BdC EDT and EDP. It has the citrus sparkle of the EDT but the smoother, woodier backbone of the EDP. In the air, the scent trail is virtually indistinguishable from the original. Performance: This is the "budget" trade-off. It projects well for the first hour but settles into a skin scent by hour 4. The Verdict: Not a "beast mode" fragrance, but an elite "dumb reach." At this price point, you can afford to overspray or keep a bottle in your gym bag. If you want the Chanel DNA on a budget, this is a total no-brainer.

title: Citrus Woody B.ATES perfume - a fragrance for women and men 2022
description: Citrus Woody by B.ATES is a fragrance for women and men. Citrus Woody was launched in 2022. Citrus Woody was created by Arturetto Landi and Bünyamin ATEŞ. Top note is Grapefruit; middle notes are Ambroxan and Sandalwood; base note is Woody Notes. Thıs specıal fragrance, whıch you wıll experıence the fresh smells of the wınter months on you for four seasons, wıll make you feel the unıque beauty of nature wıth the harmony of grapefruıt and cıtrus. With the ıncense effect created by ambroxan and basıcally sandalwood and woody notes, your soul will be healed and revıved. Top Notes; Grapefruit Heart Notes; Amboxan, Sandalwood Bottom Notes; Woody Notes

title: Citrus Bloom Inspires perfume - a new fragrance for women and men 2025
description: Citrus Bloom by Inspires is a Citrus fragrance for women and men. This is a new fragrance. Citrus Bloom was launched in 2025. The nose behind this fragrance is Jean-Pierre Bethouart. Top notes are Mandarin Orange, Violet Leaves, Lemongrass, Orange and Lemon; middle notes are Cherry Blossom, Cranberry, Camellia, Verbena, Lilac, Rose and Cotton Flower; base notes are Powdery Notes, Sandalwood and Musk. Fresh and airy, this fragrance unveils a harmonious blend of floral and citrus accords. The opening sparkles with the vibrant freshness of lemon, lemongrass, and violet leaves, bringing softness and brightness to the composition. At its heart, a delicate floral bouquet is enhanced by aromatic touches of verbena, while the base of sandalwood, powdery notes, and musk adds elegance, comfort, and sophistication to the scent.

title: Citrus Splash Pocket Scents perfume - a fragrance for women and men 2024
description: Citrus Splash by Pocket Scents is a Citrus Aromatic fragrance for women and men. Citrus Splash was launched in 2024. Top notes are Bergamot and Orange; middle note is Ginger; base note is Ambergris. Inspired by Afternoon Swim, Citrus Splash is an energetic unisex fragrance featuring warm Amber notes complemented by Citrus, Sweet, and Spicy elements. It opens with a burst of fresh Orange and spicy Ginger. The scent contrasts the bright Citrus accords of Orange and Bergamot with the rich, deep aroma of Ambergris. Citrus Splash is a versatile fragrance perfect for any occasion. Whether it’s a formal event, a casual outing, day or night, Citrus Splash adapts effortlessly, making it an ideal year-round scent.

title: Citrus Blossom Sarah Horowitz Parfums perfume - a fragrance for women and men 2018
description: Citrus Blossom by Sarah Horowitz Parfums is a fragrance for women and men. Citrus Blossom was launched in 2018. The nose behind this fragrance is Sarah Horowitz. The fragrance features Orange Blossom and Citruses. "Light, Bright, Heavenly. Beautiful citrusy floral fragrance capturing the scent of orange blossoms on the breeze"- a note from the brand.

title: Citrus Chill Victoria's Secret perfume - a fragrance for women 2020
description: Citrus Chill by Victoria's Secret is a Citrus Aromatic fragrance for women. Citrus Chill was launched in 2020. The fragrance features Tangelo, Almond Blossom and Ice.

title: Citrus Fresco The Perfume Shop perfume - a fragrance for women and men 2019
description: Citrus Fresco by The Perfume Shop is a Citrus fragrance for women and men. Citrus Fresco was launched in 2019. Top notes are Lime and Fruity Notes; middle notes are Geranium, Lavender and Rosemary; base notes are Indian Patchouli, Brazilian Redwood and Labdanum.

title: Citrusdal Wild Olive Artisans perfume - a fragrance for women and men 2017
description: Citrusdal by Wild Olive Artisans is a Citrus Aromatic fragrance for women and men. Citrusdal was launched in 2017. The fragrance features Frankincense, Petitgrain, Orange, Neroli, Lemon and Hyrax. A compilation of citrus notes inspired by a drive through the orchards of an African landscape where wilderness and farmland coexist in harmony, a blend of modern and of ancient If you ever pass through Citrusdal’s orchards in season, take a moment to acknowledge the scent in the air... you will remember this perfume.... A potent distillation of perfume extract revealing all the parts of the citrus tree – the bark, the leaves, the twigs, the flowers and the fruit of the lemon, grapefruit, mandarin, orange, bergamot and lime, with a dash of surrounding fynbos Crisp, modern and above all, elegant 100% natural and botanical

title: Citrus Shores Arran Aromatics perfume - a fragrance for women
description: Citrus Shores by Arran Aromatics is a Floral Fruity fragrance for women. The fragrance features Bergamot, Lemon, Green Leaves and Cassis.

reviews.name: sparklecloud
reviews.comment: The citrus is lovely on this fragrance, although I think I like it even better on the Arran Aromatics soap and moisturiser.

reviews.name: Globalm
reviews.comment: I must agree wholeheartedly with the previous poster. This scent is great for men too. I visited the Arran Aromatics factory recently and talked to the sales and manufacturing staff. They told me that, except for older lines which had already acquired a particular reputation, they did not categorise scents by gender and kept 'For men' and 'For women' off their advertising as far as possible. This one is definitely a unisex and is delightfully fresh with a warm and soft dry-down

reviews.name: wonderwoman77
reviews.comment: constantly spraying this on me...... its beautiful

reviews.name: fuzzybutt59
reviews.comment: Although this is defined as a female frag. I just love it.If you are a lover of Monsiuer Balmain or Jaque Fath Green Water or similar zingy citrus scents,you will adore this. The dominant citrus notes,such as grapefruit,bergamot & lime soften to a fresh minty long lasting drydown.A soft herbal base lingers for ages without being overpowering.

title: Citrus & Leather Perfume 13 Incense Parfums perfume - a fragrance for women and men
description: Citrus & Leather Perfume 13 by Incense Parfums is a fragrance for women and men. Top notes are Saffron, Clove and Oud; middle notes are Coriander and Agarwood; base notes are Leather, Amber and Musk. Citrus & Leather Perfume 13 by Incense Parfums is a fragrance from the aromatic family, characterized by a fresh and fruity scent profile. The composition is dominated by notes of orange and bergamot, with spicy and intriguing top notes, a warm heart, and a base that imparts a mysterious atmosphere.

title: Citrus Natural Scent Apivita perfume - a fragrance for women and men
description: Citrus Natural Scent by Apivita is a Citrus fragrance for women and men. The fragrance features Lemon, Orange, Bergamot, Grapefruit, Rosemary, Clary Sage and Petitgrain.

title: Citrus Ananas Lotus Cosmetics London perfume - a fragrance for women and men 2018
description: Citrus Ananas by Lotus Cosmetics London is a Floral fragrance for women and men. Citrus Ananas was launched in 2018. The nose behind this fragrance is Sandra L C. Top notes are Bergamot, Cassis, Apple and Pineapple; middle notes are Patchouli, Birch and Jasmine; base notes are Amber, Moss and Musk.

title: Citrus Royale Scent Studio perfume - a fragrance for women and men 2022
description: Citrus Royale by Scent Studio is a Citrus fragrance for women and men. Citrus Royale was launched in 2022. The nose behind this fragrance is Bernadette Lim. The fragrance features Bergamot, Grapefruit, Orange Blossom, Musk and Amber.

title: Citrus Man Dame Perfumery cologne - a fragrance for men 2016
description: Citrus Man by Dame Perfumery is a Citrus fragrance for men. Citrus Man was launched in 2016. The nose behind this fragrance is Jeffrey Dame. Top notes are Lime, Lemon and Grapefruit; middle note is Jasmine; base notes are Musk, Sandalwood and Cedar. Citrus Man by Dame Perfumery is a Citrus fragrance for men. This is a new fragrance. Citrus Man was launched in 2016. Top notes are lemon, lime and grapefruit; middle note is jasmine; base notes are sandalwood, cedar and musk. Calm, cool and collected, Citrus Man is available in a cologne concentration.

reviews.name: Br'eauDeCologne
reviews.comment: Dame Perfumery is one of those houses where it's hard to go wrong with a blind buy. I did that with Citrus Man about a year ago. It was on sale for half price on the site. 100ml for $35? Okay. I started using it a couple weeks ago. I concur with most of the positive comments here. It is a light yet persistent EDC that definitely leans masculine. It is not a barber shop scent yet it is a bit soapy; an occasional flash of "foamy hand sanitizer" which is blunted (to borrow the prior reviewer's perfect word) by the expertly blended jasmine that is just present enough. This is not an animalic musk at all. It's a clean musk with a linear citrus background that persists with that jasmine, arm in arm; an unusual effect that I'd imagine was a bit tricky for Jeffrey to pull off. Bravo. It's also worth mentioning that sometimes - I get the impression that I'm wearing an "Eau Fraiche" version of Fierce; like a cousin of that powerhouse which has been toned way down (not hollowed out), with the equalizer on different settings. Citrus Man is an overlooked gem. I suspect the reason for the sale price at the time is because it was discontinued; it's no longer on the site and seems to be scarce online. Like anything I've encountered from this house it's more than worth its price (even at regular retail), and worthy of your consideration.

reviews.name: RisingChaos
reviews.comment: A musky woody base, flavored with a melange of fresh citrus and a surprisingly prominent jasmine that blunts any citric sharpness and adds its signature uplifting character. Lime-heavy on my skin. Moderately soft projection for 2-3hr, longevity 6-8hr. Smells okay but there's a distinct lack of focus on citrus here, which I'm disappointed with.

reviews.name: WildDove
reviews.comment: Sandalwood dominated on me in Citrus Man. I would have liked more citrus, especially lemon, or a lot less sandalwood. Reminds me somewhat of Bergamot 22 by Le Labo and D&G Lt Blue.

reviews.name: Manny44
reviews.comment: Whoa...this is really good. Great initial blast of citrus and then a genuinely great base of Sandalwood , musk, Jasmine. Exceptionally good. Above average sillage and good longevity. 9/10. I remember liking it alot when I first tested it but letting it sit around for 6 months has really opened it up. as someone below me noted, it has an entertaining progression.

reviews.name: TexasNoelle
reviews.comment: Citrus Man smells fantastic on my man, an intensely pleasing citrus and jasmine, complemented very nicely and in a manly way by the woods and musk. The first time he wore this we were hiking on Kauai, and I could smell that gorgeous citrus floral wafting behind him several feet down the trail! He wasn't wearing a lot of it, either. This was a unique experience, I think because out there my senses aren't hampered by allergies. I said, "You need a bottle of that", and got him one soon after.

reviews.name: landshark321
reviews.comment: I very much enjoy the evolution of Citrus Man from a relatively bitter, lemon/bergamot-intensive opening to a very sweet drydown, anchored by the cedar and (even more so) the three different kinds of musk in the base. The opening is very sharp but gives way within an hour or so to a sweeter, semi-woody dry down. It's a fun combination of citrus, musk, sandalwood, and cedar. The musk overlap is significant with New Musk Man but not prohibitively so. Performance is decent, as well, especially for a citrus-dominant fragrance, and accordingly, it's priced very reasonably (as are most of his fragrances) at $65 for 100ml. A very nice clean, citrus, musky fragrance that might have to be my next buy from the line, along with New Musk Man. 7 out of 10

reviews.name: Jish80
reviews.comment: Very weak citrus scent. This scent is more of an Irish twin of Gendarme. A very clean white musk focused scent with soapy sandalwood drydown. Why it has citrus in the name is beyond me

reviews.name: kinetisphere
reviews.comment: A very light hearted citrus. It definitely has the musk man dna like Sebastian from lfsg mentioned in his video. The initial spritz reminds me of a lemon based tart or like a pie. And quickly transitioned into it's mid notes. Within 15 mins this was settled down into a soft musky creamy pleasant scent. To be honest i only got like 5hrs of longevity and a 2inch from skin projection. It left me yearning for more. It is a good scent. But lacks in performance. This could be a good scent to wear out on a brunch date with friends. ** revised. After further sampling from my dabber. I have concluded this does last 6-8 hours. If it was being sprayed from a bottle this definitely would last a decent amount of time.

title: Citruss Dzintars perfume - a fragrance for women and men 1964
description: Citruss by Dzintars is a Citrus fragrance for women and men. Citruss was launched in 1964. The fragrance features Lemon and Citruses.

reviews.name: Forda
reviews.comment: The aroma of cheerfulness is similar to lemon sweets with a cup of black tea. Fresh lemon juice in fudge or hard candy. It also brings back childhood memories.

title: Citrus Floral Otoori perfume - a fragrance for women and men 2016
description: Citrus Floral by Otoori is a Floral Woody Musk fragrance for women and men. Citrus Floral was launched in 2016. Top notes are Sweet Notes, Fruity Notes and White Flowers; middle notes are Woody Notes and Powdery Notes; base notes are Amber and Musk. Otoori Mix and Match Collection includes 21 fragrances with a range of Essential Notes, Mixed Notes and Fine Notes. They’re not only letting you to create your own signature scent, but also providing a whole new experience of three levels of fragrances induced by extravagance, fashion, passion and magnificence. Citrus Floral belongs to Mixed Notes. Citrus Floral by Otoori is a Floral Woody Musk fragrance for women and men. This is a new fragrance. Citrus Floral was launched in 2016. Top notes are sweet notes, fruity notes and white flowers; middle notes are woody notes and powdery notes; base notes are amber and musk.

title: Citrus Infusion Acorelle perfume - a fragrance for women and men
description: Citrus Infusion by Acorelle is a Citrus Aromatic fragrance for women and men. Citrus Infusion was launched during the 2000's.

title: Citrus Escape Tisserand perfume - a fragrance for women and men 2014
description: Citrus Escape by Tisserand is a Citrus fragrance for women and men. Citrus Escape was launched in 2014. The fragrance features Lemon, Orange and Mint. "A unique collaboration combining Tisserand's botanical expertise with the rich heritage of National Trust. This Eau de Toilette spray has been expertly blended with natural essential oils and extracts of lemon, orange and mint, all known for their uplifting benefits. Green and pithy, this blend is all about citrus. Inspiring this blend were Hanbury Hall and Saltram with their grand orangeries and aromatic citrus plants." - a note from the brand. Citrus Escape by Tisserand is a Citrus fragrance for women and men. The fragrance features lemon, orange and mint.

title: Citrus Sugar Cricket Avenue perfume - a fragrance for women and men 2024
description: Citrus Sugar by Cricket Avenue is a Citrus Gourmand fragrance for women and men. Citrus Sugar was launched in 2024. The fragrance features Citruses and Sugar.

title: Citrus Freedom The Dua Brand cologne - a fragrance for men 2022
description: Citrus Freedom by The Dua Brand is a Citrus fragrance for men. Citrus Freedom was launched in 2022. The fragrance features juniper berry, Amalfi Lemon, Pink Pepper, Tonka Bean, Bergamot, Cardamom, Oakmoss, Pepper, Nutmeg, Peony, Thyme and Gin. Citrus Freedom by The Dua Brand is an aromatic spicy fragrance for men. It is classified as an Extrait de Parfum, ensuring a long-lasting and concentrated scent experience. The fragrance is inspired by the popular niche brand Frapin's L’Humaniste, known for its refreshing "gin and tonic" vibe. Citrus Freedom offers a more affordable alternative to Frapin fragrances while still providing a complex and versatile scent that can be enjoyed in various settings. With its blend of citrus, green, and spicy notes, this fragrance is perfect for warmer days as well as professional occasions when a touch of refinement is desired. The notes in Citrus Freedom include Amalfi Lemon, Bergamot, Cardamom, Pink Pepper, Pepper, Juniper Berry, Nutmeg, Thyme, Peony, Gin, Oakmoss, and Tonka Bean. This unique combination creates a rejuvenating blend of aromas that captures the bright and energetic essence of citrus. The spicy cardamom and aromatic thyme complement the citrus notes, adding depth and complexity to the fragrance. Overall, Citrus Freedom is a mass-appealing scent that can be used more frequently than other fragrances in your collection due to its versatility and affordability.

title: Citrus & Tonka Usual Parfums cologne - a fragrance for men 2020
description: Citrus & Tonka by Usual Parfums is a Woody Aromatic fragrance for men. Citrus & Tonka was launched in 2020. Top notes are Mandarin Orange, Mint and Cypress; middle notes are Pepper and Sage; base notes are Tonka Bean and Musk.

title: Citrus Dream Victoria's Secret perfume - a fragrance for women 2013
description: Citrus Dream by Victoria's Secret is a Floral Fruity fragrance for women. Citrus Dream was launched in 2013. The fragrance features Grapefruit and Red Lily. Citrus Dream by Victoria's Secret is a body mist introduced in 2013 as part of the Sparkling Citrus collection. The fragrance focuses on a simple accord of grapefruit and red lily, combining a citrus impression with floral nuances for an easygoing, everyday body spray.

title: Citrus & Musk Atelier Faye perfume - a new fragrance for women and men 2025
description: Citrus & Musk by Atelier Faye is a Citrus Aromatic fragrance for women and men. This is a new fragrance. Citrus & Musk was launched in 2025. Top notes are Bergamot and Lime; middle notes are Sea Notes and Basil; base notes are Cedar and Musk.

title: Citrus Viridi Apotheca perfume - a fragrance for women and men 2019
description: Citrus Viridi by Apotheca is a fragrance for women and men. Citrus Viridi was launched in 2019. The fragrance features Citruses and Green Notes.

title: Citrus Notes Palette No. 1 Melange Perfume perfume - a fragrance for women and men 2010
description: Citrus Notes Palette No. 1 by Melange Perfume is a Citrus fragrance for women and men. Citrus Notes Palette No. 1 was launched in 2010. The nose behind this fragrance is Denise Estrada. The fragrance features Jasmine, Bergamot, Grapefruit, Lemon, Mandarin Orange, Tea, Black Currant and Pomegranate. Citrus Notes Palette No. 1 by Melange Perfume is a Citrus fragrance for women and men. This is a new fragrance. Citrus Notes Palette No. 1 was launched in 2012. The nose behind this fragrance is Denise Estrada. The Citrus Notes Palette No. 1 contains four individual solid fragrances—Grapefruit & Pomegrante; Citron & Cassis; Bergamot & Jasmine; and Mandarin & Tea—which are designed to be worn individually or layered, to create unique scent compositions. Each perfume in the palette is assembled and hand-poured in a base of natural Beeswax & Jojoba Oil. Citrus Notes Palette No. 1 was launched in 2012.

title: Citrus Inspiration Iruela perfume - a fragrance for women and men 2019
description: Citrus Inspiration by Iruela is a Citrus Aromatic fragrance for women and men. Citrus Inspiration was launched in 2019. The nose behind this fragrance is Sandra Iruela. Top notes are Bergamot, Tea and Mint; middle notes are Watery Notes, Jasmine and Damask Rose; base notes are Cedar and Musk.

title: Citrus Charm Eau de Nous perfume - a fragrance for women 2010
description: Citrus Charm by Eau de Nous is a Citrus Aromatic fragrance for women. Citrus Charm was launched in 2010. The fragrance features Water Lily, Grapefruit and Mandarin Orange.

title: Citrus Forest Eau de Parfum Aqua Savon perfume - a fragrance for women and men
description: Citrus Forest Eau de Parfum by Aqua Savon is a fragrance for women and men. Top notes are Bergamot and Lemon; middle notes are Magnolia, Jasmine and Rose; base notes are Sandalwood, Patchouli and Musk. Citrus Forest EdP by Aqua Savon offers a gentle blend of fresh citrus, floral, and woody notes. The fragrance features natural soap-like nuances, creating a soft and comfortable scent suitable for daily wear in various settings.

title: Citrus Neroli Jean Rish cologne - a fragrance for men 2016
description: Citrus Neroli by Jean Rish is a Citrus Aromatic fragrance for men. Citrus Neroli was launched in 2016. Top notes are Mandarin Orange and Grapefruit; middle notes are Guaiac Wood, Bay Leaf and Jasmine; base notes are Patchouli, Ambergris and Oakmoss. Citrus Neroli by Jean Rish is a men's fragrance released in 2016. It opens with grapefruit and mandarin orange, develops through a heart of guaiac wood, bay leaf, and jasmine, and settles into a base of patchouli, ambergris, and oakmoss.

title: Citrus X Paradisi Maison Jacynthe perfume - a fragrance for women and men 2022
description: Citrus X Paradisi by Maison Jacynthe is a Floral Fruity fragrance for women and men. Citrus X Paradisi was launched in 2022. Top notes are Grapefruit, Bergamot and Lemon; middle notes are Jasmine, Davana and Pear; base notes are Blood Orange, Tuberose and Amber.

title: Citrus Leather Francois Deli cologne - a fragrance for men 2012
description: Citrus Leather by Francois Deli is a Woody Spicy fragrance for men. Citrus Leather was launched in 2012. Top notes are Bergamot and Mandarin Orange; middle note is Floral Notes; base notes are Musk, Ambergris and Cedar.

reviews.name: austrian
reviews.comment: A very beautiful and fresh floral citrus perfume with a very nice musky base. Refreshing and quite masculine.

reviews.name: gabrych
reviews.comment: Bought this perfume online, I LOVE LOVE it.I also bought a strange one from the same company, Fume Toxique. It's impossible to put this in a specified category. No one can distinguish al the notes. It lasts a lot and stay beautifully on my skin

reviews.name: 4drewjr
reviews.comment: Worst Company EVER!

title: Citrus Limon Atelier Faye perfume - a new fragrance for women and men 2025
description: Citrus Limon by Atelier Faye is a Citrus fragrance for women and men. This is a new fragrance. Citrus Limon was launched in 2025. Top notes are Bergamot, Cassis and Lemon; middle notes are Clary Sage, Nettle and Lemon; base note is White Musk.

title: Citrus Shine Arman Manoukian Parfums perfume - a fragrance for women and men 2020
description: Citrus Shine by Arman Manoukian Parfums is a fragrance for women and men. Citrus Shine was launched in 2020. The nose behind this fragrance is Arman Manoukian. Top note is Grapefruit; middle note is Caramel; base note is White Musk. "The night breaks into spicy laziness. Explosions of citrus peels are in the fireworks of interjections. The strands of emotions and feelings stretch in distortion and duality. Dreams are inspired by light and spicy rays of allegory. In sensations there is no understatement, abstinence and limits. Other bodies are under the soft pile of the subconscious. Other forms, fleshy, subcutaneous dolphins. Tendons are stretched under thin skin. The sharp edges are dull. Compliance and bliss. A subtle perception. Some more sweet impressions. Some sugar and honey in a sleepy languor. Caramel haze in grapefruit dust." - a note from the brand.

reviews.name: irina_t
reviews.comment: A beautiful long- lasting fragrance! It starts with juicy notes of grapefruit, very bright and energising to start a day with. Smoothly the juice gets caramelised. Melting bits of slightly salty caramel- temptation for more. The fragrance for joy and freedom. Very optimistic and playful. It is not too sweet as it might seem. White musk adds lightness and airiness to the whole composition. It is beautiful for cold seasons to warm you up and great for summer to add more sunny vibes.

title: Citrus Storm Twins & Icons cologne - a fragrance for men
description: Citrus Storm by Twins & Icons is a fragrance for men. Top note is Bergamot; middle notes are Lavender, Geranium and elemi; base notes are Patchouli, Ambroxan, Vetiver and Cedar.

title: Citrus Fusion Twins & Icons perfume - a fragrance for women and men
description: Citrus Fusion by Twins & Icons is a fragrance for women and men. Top notes are Mandarin, Orange and Lime; middle notes are Thyme and Basil; base notes are Patchouli and Vetiver.

title: Citrus Eden Panthenol EXTRA perfume - a new fragrance for women 2026
description: Citrus Eden by Panthenol EXTRA is a Citrus Aromatic fragrance for women. This is a new fragrance. Citrus Eden was launched in 2026. The fragrance features Green Leaves, Spicy Notes, Bergamot and Musk.

title: Citrus Serenade whif. perfume - a new fragrance for women and men 2025
description: Citrus Serenade by whif. is a Citrus Aromatic fragrance for women and men. This is a new fragrance. Citrus Serenade was launched in 2025. Top notes are Grapefruit, Bergamot, Mandarin and Juniper; middle notes are Rosemary and Pepper; base notes are Oakmoss, Incense and Musk. Citrus Serenade by Whif is an extrait de parfum released in 2025. The fragrance opens with grapefruit, bergamot, mandarin, and juniper, followed by a heart of pepper, rosemary, and rosewood. Incense, musk, and oakmoss form the base, giving the composition a fresh citrus character with aromatic, slightly woody undertones. The brand presents it as a Mediterranean-inspired scent.

title: Black Citrus Vilhelm Parfumerie perfume - a fragrance for women and men 2015
description: Black Citrus by Vilhelm Parfumerie is a Woody Aromatic fragrance for women and men. Black Citrus was launched in 2015. The nose behind this fragrance is Jérôme Epinette. Top notes are Calabrian bergamot and Cardamom; middle notes are Mate and Violet; base notes are Birch and Patchouli. "CLEAN BREATH OF LONDON. Serious freshness without frivolity. The fragrance is inspired by the clean breath of the city after the showers pass. Gentlemen step out from under protective storefront canopies, their impeccable British style uncompromised. Bergamot, cardamom and violet establish a strong core, tempered by a birch tree and patchouli base. Zesty and triumphant with a soft echo of the storm." - a note from the brand. Black Citrus by Vilhelm Parfumerie is a Woody Aromatic fragrance for women and men. This is a new fragrance. Black Citrus was launched in 2015. The nose behind this fragrance is Jerome Epinette. Top notes are cardamom and calabrian bergamot; middle notes are mate and violet; base notes are patchouli and birch.

cons.label: Too smoky for citrus lovers
Expensive for the performance provided
Redundant if owning Aventus-style scents
Feels dated and overly mature
Reminiscent of cheap body spray
Divisive herbal and vegetable facets
Weak projection after first hour
Dries down to generic base
Citrus disappears almost immediately
Poor longevity on many skins

reviews.name: meroo
reviews.comment: This is fun! a mix if the modern Aventus vibe (about 15-20%) and your masculine sweaty uncle who smells nice when he sweats. Classy but easy to wear, remains fun, performs excellently. Different enough from Aventus and its clone for people like me who loathes smelling generic.

reviews.name: joeyjoejoe
reviews.comment: This is not good. Generic duty free airport vibes

reviews.name: PeopleTent
reviews.comment: Who unboxed my new bottle of the great great Black Citrus by Vilhelm Parfumerie. Who in my over crowded house had pleasure of being the first person to spray the bottle for the first time and experience what a unisex masculine leaning Aventus type a fragrance should be. The good news is that it will not be hard to find them because they are going to be smelling noticeably incredible good for at least the next 12+ hours.

reviews.name: aramisbyjoe
reviews.comment: Smells like a smoky (it's the birch) citrus fragrance supported by the accords of earthy, wet forest. Some would describe this as an earthy Aventus. I like this one. Also: Reformulations have started. I went to a Scent Bar in Downtown LA, and the fragrance has been completed neutered. No scratchy smoky citrus. No earthy dry-down.

reviews.name: Snuffeltje
reviews.comment: Too smokey for me, because I'm not a smoke lover. Otherwise an interesting mix with a very realistic, edible citrus.

reviews.name: smellslikejustspirit
reviews.comment: Of the Vilhelm scents I tried this is one of the few I was pleasantly surprised by. A clean, sharp scent, not much more to add. Feels great to wear when you want to feel put together, ready, and pleasant, but not sweet. It’s not worth the price tag IMO, BUT then I’m also not a citrus or “fresh” scent enjoyer, and considering that I do like it. Not the most original thing ever, but good! I see why some people who enjoy very fem scents might not like this, but I also wholeheartedly disagree.

reviews.name: Diogenes0
reviews.comment: Quick bergamot opening that is quite reminiscent of aventus (comparing side by side with my bottle) which lasts 5 minutes before becoming a beautiful lightly smoked clean tea scent. very clean and inoffensive. I might have to pick up a full bottle, im rarely not in the mood for this. Source: official sample decant

reviews.name: davvy
reviews.comment: smells gresat but is too masculine for me - giving to my brother for sure

reviews.name: Dumbplings
reviews.comment: Someone below me called this poor mans aventus... lol mecca selling this for $450 so definitely not a poor mans aventus. No poor man can afford this, but it does smell like aventus anyway so right about that

reviews.name: Hankchinaskitells
reviews.comment: This must be called "Poets of Berlin" instead of actual one. You are getting a pretty beautiful citrus, but not a fresh-happy "sunny day" type, it's dark, even cold, like in forgotten laboratory, I guess Camus and Sartre would love to wear this fregrance..if you know what I mean. Not for a blid buy, but great artistic fregrance, if you love dark and depressive scents

reviews.name: Perfumedrops
reviews.comment: I have mixed emotions about this one. Sometimes I like it. Sometimes I don't. Like a fresh cucumber or like a moss. I wouldn't recommend a blind buy for this scent.

reviews.name: caitlin1776
reviews.comment: I am a moderately feminine woman who generally prefers feminine-leaning unisex scents. IMO, this is true unisex, so suitable for any gender presentation. Black citrus, to my nose, captures the smell of opening an Earl Grey tea bag. My non-frag-head husband agrees and finds it "pleasant" on me. This is one of my current favorites, and if you like a compliment-getter, one friend stopped what she was saying midsentence to tell me "you smell SO good!" Longevity is fairly good, 4-6 hours though a very light tea scent clings to the skin for a long time. The sillage smells amazing and is moderate to my view. I personally enjoy how sweat influences this fragrance on me, and look forward to using this in summer. As far as the packaging, that is very well done. I plan to keep this in the rotation. I just wanted to add a counterpoint to all the folks indicating this may not be suitable for women.

reviews.name: esteban747
reviews.comment: Opens beautifully. Bright and clean, as if sun rays start to cut through heavy rain clouds. Easy to wear, truly beautiful. But the uplifting phase only last for about 20 minutes. At least on my skin, what I get after the promising start is a linear patchouli-birch that won’t let go until they disappear and take with them the whole scent. I will see how it behaves outside.

reviews.name: martin_jsgn
reviews.comment: A poor man’s Creed Spice and Wood: cheaper, simpler, more youthful. Nothing bad about being a poor man’s Creed S&W since it has been sadomasochistically priced for 3rd-generation’s wealth and tech billionaires. Well blended, airy and velvety freshness; It has no sharp edges to it. The drydown is earthier but remains velvety. I’m not sure if thats good or bad. It’s kind of non-descript to me as far as drydown goes. Performance on my skin is not great. Too airy too velvety, somewhat fleeting. I love this citrus aromatic spicy type. But this one lacks something. It either needs a better drydown, or a better projection.

reviews.name: pepperwhy
reviews.comment: I love the scent for this fragrance, such a good spring and summer vibe. Love!!!!!

reviews.name: KerriZ
reviews.comment: I was really hoping for one of those fragrances that, like its name, was a contradiction of terms. Like Fat electrician or Akro Ink. This is not that. It is a very astringent citrus and herbaceous dark twisty green. Definitely more of a strong and appealing daytime male-leaning scent.

reviews.name: Buckstermonkey
reviews.comment: Very nice indeed. Recommended to me by the Liberty London Beauty team. Nothing about it reminds of London after a rain storm but its fresh and welcoming scent. More masculine than feminine. The scent lingers well into the day. I love the Vilhelm Parfumerie brand. Black Citrus is up there as one of its best

reviews.name: Chicago Tony T
reviews.comment: More floral than citrusy as it dries down. A green/citrus/floral scent with a dark twist? Longevity is excellent at 10 hours and this is a 4 Season scent so could easily be the right persons signature scent but that person ain't me. No way is it unisex as it screams macho manly.

reviews.name: j4yt33
reviews.comment: Bright, juicy tart bergamot, the astringent, hesperidic notes rounded off by a careful dose of violet. Birch and mate give it some substance, the musky notes give it a soft fluffiness. I can see the marketing blurb about rain in London fitting the impression of flowers after a brief summer rain. I don't get cardamom at all. This is not close to Aramis at all imho. I wouldn't have thought of Aventus or Morning Chess if it hadn't been mentioned in the reviews. Clean, musky, slightly floral bergamot with some woody backbone. Perfect masculine daytime fragrance

reviews.name: NDStars
reviews.comment: First blast of spicy dark green, a ticklish punch of cardamom, then velvety herbaceous mate. Very nice.. it's woody and earthy yet cool and a touch fruity as well. This should be the new normal for a man who wants to smell clean and spicy but not like everyone else. The drydown really features that leafy mate note and shares it with an almost soapy patchouli. Masculine and would suit all weather.

reviews.name: calitrav
reviews.comment: I agree with MikesPlaza below: this shares some noticeable similarities to Creed's Aventus, but is different enough that you could own both. The 2 most obvious differences are the opening fruit note (Citrus here) and the spicyness. I'm really enjoying the scent and will give it at a 4.5/5 -- could be worth the price if it performs well enough. Note: this is one of those frags that smells a bit different on skin than on paper (where the Citrus and pepperyness seem to last longer) so the same might be true for skin vs clothes (some "weak" scents last for days on clothes).

reviews.name: belladonnaofsadness
reviews.comment: Was bamboozled by the name and the promise of the opening, which did have a citrus feel, hoping for something like a citrus perfume for nostalgic goths. Instead it fades immediately and all I get is powdery violet and something astringent that I pick up in more niche perfumes occasionally. It's a pass for me.

reviews.name: Rodgers52017
reviews.comment: To me this is straight up Mens Aramis. Absolutely no difference what so ever. Definitely gonna be for guys 35 and older and in no way would I say this is unisex. Wouldn’t want any woman I was around smelling like this. Way to manly for a woman but any older guy could rock this. Save you money and just pick up a bottle of Aramis at the discounted stores

reviews.name: The Wolf
reviews.comment: First impression: On my dry skin, all I got was a super strong citrus and citrus rine for a while. Afterwards I start getting a dark tea and some birtch, patch, and a black tea leather vibe. I wouldn't call this a leather fragrance, it's more like Le Labo Noir 29 than leather. I'm impressed with its staying power without smelling like aroma chemicals. Interesting dark citrus just as advertised. In the ballpark of Noir 29 and Morning Chess combined, but I rate N29 higher because it has much more depth, and is a bit more interesting. ------------------------------------------------- Scent 7.5/10 Performance 7.5/10

reviews.name: Eupatoria
reviews.comment: It opens up peppery and green, with a strong astringent note of blackcurrant leaves. It’s opening reminds me of Frederic Malle’s Rose et Cuir in its green intensity (although Rose Et Cuir is greener and devoid of patchouli). It is crisp and fresh. Somewhere deep in there is an animalistic note, but you really have to look for it. It’s not a perfume you will get complimented on, and it’s not a first date perfume as it is not very sensual. It would be great for an office setting as it is interesting, fresh, and inobtrusive. I don’t believe in gendering perfumes, but this one if distinctly unisex.

reviews.name: toosimilar
reviews.comment: If you know how orange peel smells like - then u've already tasted this perfume ;) Middle to base are much more pleasant and nice, the beginning is a disaster :)

reviews.name: OTA Mom
reviews.comment: The bergamot is completely lost in this concoction. Very strongly green aromatic, which my brain reads as sage. I do sense a tiny bit of violet.

reviews.name: jacquavit
reviews.comment: It's what it says on the bottle; a dark citrus. However the actual scent itself really reminded me of cheap men's body spray. Maybe I'm missing something, but the week I tried this scent I felt like I was being followed by a sweaty, over-bodysprayed gym bros armpit. Yuck.

reviews.name: Imcheap17
reviews.comment: Very crisp, peppery citrus

reviews.name: smmegst
reviews.comment: Nice but a bit heavy on me, would prefer to smell it on a boyfriend. The birch note is interesting, reminds me of walking into a woodworking shop!

reviews.name: Shayziep
reviews.comment: I liked this, but don’t love it. I get very little citrus, lots of woodyness, a little violet, and at fist smell an earthy scent like wet potting soil. Not unpleasant, just unexpected. There is a hint of lime after dry down, but mostly an almost sandalwood smell, although I didn’t see that listed in the notes.

reviews.name: Thatbwoy
reviews.comment: Nope, not a bad scent. Just not my cup of tea; thing is I can respect this but I get a ‘pine-y’ aroma that gets right into my head switches on my headache button. It’s jarring and green, no that’s not right It’s FU**ING GREEN, I can’t do green scents. But it’sa weird fresh wood green it just irks me. I get the artistry being this I can definitely respect the talent of the perfumer, but it’s just not my slice of cake. 5/10 Edit: The dry down is very reminiscent of Antaeus I adore Antaeus Soo for that I’m going to improve the score 6/10 Edit: 02/07/2022: Thought I liked it. Edit: 14/11/2022: Wore it today, hate it. 4/10

reviews.name: R¡ch
reviews.comment: Wow! This is fabulous! I get a blood orange vibe on the opening, then spritzy lime and violet. Then it's warm and woody. Love the cool green note that contrasts the warmth. Birch perhaps? The cardamon goes nicely with the patchouli here. NR bleu noir is also a favourite of mine that uses cardamon well. Getting a note of bitter white grapefruit on the drydown too. May treat myself to a bottle!

reviews.name: amoraelen
reviews.comment: The description on this fragrance really sold me: It's inspired by "a snapshot of London after a rainshower, when the city scent is filtered through a cool veil of water." How cool and mysterious does that sound? It has topnotes of bergamot and cardamom, middle notes of violet and mate leaf heart, and bottom notes of birch and patchouli.

reviews.name: quarterback
reviews.comment: I wanted a lighter citrus that lasted longer and could be an evening wear and I picked this one up. I'm still living with it, but I got the smaller travel size as I think it'll be one that can be worn flexibly. Even though it fades fast, that just opens me up to putting on some of the other faint ones, as I'm not super jazzed about wearing the same scent all day.

reviews.name: DoogieIT
reviews.comment: I did a side-by-side comparison of Black Citrus and Morning Chess and found both have the same flaw. While the opening is nice, they quickly dry down to something pretty unremarkable and indistinct. Within a few hours Black Citrus on paper had the same rather lifeless character I'm normally used to finding on a test strip after a full day or two. The best thing I can say is that it's quite pleasant for the short period of time it lasts.

reviews.name: njwooding
reviews.comment: Kind of glad this one was a weak performer because it was almost offensive. Not a fan of the citrusy/licorice smell at first, and then it just dries down to something really generic. Not for me, don't see how anyone would really love this one.

reviews.name: uchini
reviews.comment: OMG!!!!. this is freaking awesome!!!: I have tried all the collection and Morning Chess and this one are the best in my humble opinion. This is so fresh, but intriguing at the same time. It is so smooth and well blended....

reviews.name: _smellingreat_
reviews.comment: What is this nonsense? It opens with an interesting burst of citrus mixed with a darker patchouli/ oak-moss, then as if in a flash, the citrus vanishes and you're left with a cheap smelling, old school barbershop scent. A scrubber.

reviews.name: avocat123
reviews.comment: I hated this so much that I washed it off as soon as I got home. I really wanted to like it given the dark gothy vibe it promised. I dont know if it was the cardamom or the bitter tea that I find so offensive. My mother said it smelled like floor cleaner at the opening and cheap Aqua Velva on the dry down. Double yuck!

reviews.name: Stormodig
reviews.comment: Opens up with a distict, extremely fresh citrus which is still oddly warm and bright. Quite unique! As it settles I get more and more oakmossy notes, might be the violet. Then the birch chimes in and all this adds up to a beautifully soothing scent that actually reminds me of Timbuktu. Lovers of Terre d'Hermes and its Eau Tres Fraiche might also find some similarities after drydown. If you can cope with heavier tar there is another related scent in 2015 Le Phénix by Les Bains Guerbois. I've been an avid fan of Timbuktu since its release but would still go for Black Citrus if I had to choose between them, as it holds the same exotic warmth with less tar, cleaner smoke and less sweetness. Amazing longevity, far more than 12 hours.

reviews.name: Jella7ine
reviews.comment: Ugh, I love this smell but the top notes disappear almost instantly (to my novice nose, anyway). The remaining patchouli reminds me of my father's hair brush - which I guess might be the suggestion of leather being referred to. I couldn't possibly afford a bottle of this given how much I just want to smell the first 5 minutes of it forever. It's quite complex, and i get that kaleidoscope effect of notes hitting you in quick succession.

reviews.name: L.bss
reviews.comment: This is actually very nice! Super happy fragrance, floral and a bit herbal on a base of white wood( not birch tar) very refreshing and uplifting, still not 210€ for this juice..come on

reviews.name: Ambroxer
reviews.comment: Now this is an unexpected and joyful surprise. Black Citrus is exactly what it says: a fresh, warm weather citrus fragrance reimagined for wear after dark. Sharp, even sour bergamot and lime intermix with smokey birch nearly as soon as the fragrance hits the skin. It smells familiar, but with a unique twist. The birch seems much more prominent than the citrus, and as you get into the mid and dry down, totally overtakes the fragrance. You’re left with an ashen woody/leather scent that has a stern, serious character accented by citrus sparkling in the background. In some ways, I’m reminded of Acqua di Gio Profumo. Not in terms of actual scent, but in having something very fresh and familiar turned dark and given an incredible amount of depth. Black Citrus is darker than Profumo, certainly, and feels more naturally at home in the night. It’s almost like it could be an Acqua di Gio for Vampires flanker. All in all, a very cool fall evening citrus scent. As Halloween approaches, Black Citrus would fit in perfectly with any and all festivities in a warmer climate. The more I smell it, the more I want to smell it more. That’s a sure sign of success.

reviews.name: alanwake
reviews.comment: Bitter bergamot and violet leaf, wrapped around smoky birch with dark patchouli nuances. The opening is fresh, bitter and green. A little spicy, with an herbal violet leaf thats the heart of the scent, ultimately drying down to a patchouli and birch concatenation. The patchouli is the dirty elegant smelling kind, and the birch is smoky/dark but lifts the entire scent to a bright and gloomy interesting frequency. Overall this feels like a darker and smokier Narciso Rodriguez For Him. Well composed, but not recommended nor suited for those that dislike vegetal fragrances.

reviews.name: Andy the Frenchy
reviews.comment: This has been properly named, since it's a heavy and dark citrus frag centered on birch tar (aka impression of bitter leather) supported by a strong bergamot (mostly in the opening). The drydown is pretty linear, with the bergamot who fades out (but is still present to keep things fresh), and the birch tar who takes the lead. That, coupled with violet, mate and cardamom (and probably also a hint of smoky vetiver) nearly gives a bitter green tobacco feeling, strongly reminding me of a fragrance I love: Monsillage Aviation Club. I can tell some patchouli is there as well, but as a supporting note only. On my skin, performance is great with heavy projection in the first hour, moderate projection for the following 3-4 hours, and above average longevity. If you already own the latter, it might be redundant. If not, I'd highly recommend to get one or the other. (underlining that Black Citrus has better performance than Aviation Club, but the latter can be found for great prices on ebay). Spring/Fall days, 25+ (note that love for green bitter notes is required to love this one!)

reviews.name: gerry.madoni
reviews.comment: Why oh why do small niche perfume houses like Vilhelm even try ?. Over and over again, similar creations just miss the mark and prove to be an absolute waste of time and money. Out of approx. 20 samples of late, this one from Vilhelm is easily one of the worst I have ever come across. Abrasive and unsettling in my wearings, a cheap and no where near completed effort coming at a hefty price tag. This scent from Vilhelm is purely for sheepish followers of small nice perfume houses feeling special about blindly adoring the obscure.

reviews.name: AndySmellsGreat
reviews.comment: Lovely scent - deep dark bergamot unlike no other - I disagree with the voted note table in that it is bergamot and patchouli that assault you (not that it is a patchouli bomb) and the birch and mate (very dry) help bind the gap between top and bottom making this quite seemless. The middle notes begin to dominate after an hour so I’m not sure if what’s left is what I like as it smells like just spices but wow! What an opening!

reviews.name: MikesPlaza
reviews.comment: While Morning Chess is considered to be Vilhelms Parfumeries take on Aventus, it is obvious that Black Citrus also is heavily inspired by the former. Black Citrus opens with a really fresh and juicy bergamot and dries down dark, woody, smokey and slightly herbal. The heavy birch/patchouly base is very reminiscent of smokier Aventus-batches, but its still very much its own scent. All in all its a lovely and very high quality fragrance.

reviews.name: nyse4
reviews.comment: I really like this perfume. It has a citrusy yet sweet smell that gets better as it settles on the skin. Downside: not very longlasting. People's skin pH might change the scent of perfumes so whatever works for someone may not work for someone else. Best is to try. Scent: 10/10 Longevity: 6/10 Sillage: 8/10

reviews.name: TheTomFordEnthusiast
reviews.comment: It's not bad, but for whatever reason, I get a little bit of an asparagus smell during the dry down...

reviews.name: ninakix.
reviews.comment: This citrus is a bit weird. I like citruses to be light and airy, but this is the opposite: kind of a heavy, gothic citrus. It must be the cardamom giving it that crazy dark vibe.

title: Italian Citrus DS&Durga cologne - a fragrance for men 2011
description: Italian Citrus by DS&Durga is a Citrus fragrance for men. Italian Citrus was launched in 2011. The nose behind this fragrance is David Seth Moltz. The fragrance features Citruses, Bitter Orange, Musk, Blood Orange, Ambrette (Musk Mallow), Green Notes, Amalfi Lemon and Mandarin Orange. Italian Citrus is a refreshing washmen's cologne that contains the aromas of the Italian coast. Notes: bitter citrus Chinotto, red orange, lemon, green mandarin, ambrette seed and clean musk. Available as 50 ml Eau de Cologne.

thisPerfumeRemindsMeOf.title: Acqua di Parma Blu Mediterraneo Bergamotto di Calabria

reviews.name: pantslover22
reviews.comment: Go girl give us nothing. It's super similar to Coriander and Bistro Waters, but where Coriander is one note and lovely and Bistro is complicated and grows on you, Italian Citrus is kind of a nothing burger. It's nice on card and it's fine on skin. Just doesn't stand out in any specific way.

reviews.name: zestysexysweet
reviews.comment: The chinotto claim really comes through; it's an acquired taste, but one I personally love. Combining with the green, it gives parma violet a little bit. A nice, not overly sweet gourmand. Slightly aquatic to me. Better longevity than a lot of DS&Durga scents, the citrus lingers but it dries down mostly aquatic and fresh.

reviews.name: The birdhouse
reviews.comment: The opening was a big beautiful blast of citrus which very quickly turned to a medicinal bitter scent. This is Not for me.

reviews.name: coffeejellyz
reviews.comment: a iced cola cola with a slice of lemon in it. heavy on the lemon note tho, maybe it's more accurate to say that it smells like a fizzy lemon drink wiith a note of coca cola in it. so do i love it? yes. definitely considering buying this for the summer, it smells amazing and so refreshing.. however that price is ticking me off a bit

reviews.name: lolajayne
reviews.comment: I'm not sure how or why, but this smells like straight up carrots. Literally the only thing I could smell for most of the time I was wearing it was carrots. I think it might be the combo of musk and citrus? Either way - it's not for me

reviews.name: Hannyhanny
reviews.comment: Not my favourite, I do however tend to like my fragrances on the sweet side and this is not at all. It's definitely leaning masculine. There’s a watery/metallic note, I would call it a sort of cold scent. Also I have a hard time with the musk, I don't find this fragrance well blended enough, too aldehydic.

reviews.name: ws1482
reviews.comment: The opening is great but it lasts all of about 5 minutes. What i'm left with is a faint smell of orange rind and musk that does not smell that great.

reviews.name: dhsa
reviews.comment: agree with the comment about “rancid sangria” but i kinda like it about it xD

reviews.name: babepdx
reviews.comment: I'm a citrus fanatic and this was not for me. In my opinion, it's way too aquatic and super short lived.

reviews.name: Fragreek
reviews.comment: Ugh this is a horrid scent. Smells like rancid sangria.

reviews.name: peacedeli
reviews.comment: Italian Citrus is both generic and unique. Generic because it is simple and pleasant in the way many fresh citruses are, and is thus somewhat forgettable. Unique in that if the scent is focused on, one realizes that not many other citrus fragrances are quite like it. On my arm, Italian Citrus opens with a classic bergamotty citrusy floral, but the freshness is grounded in something that reminds me of fresh BMW leather (maybe evocative of driving down the Italian coastline in a convertible leather car) but that leather is an incredibly fleeting note. Next this turns into something very aperol-y, minus the bitterness. Finally, it settles into a clean and somehow unique citrus—it is very limoncello-like (minus the cloying sweetness) grounded in some kind of expensive note like silk(?). Eminently pleasant, but not something I'd be super excited to get for myself (maybe again owing to its "generic" quality). Would be lovely to smell in the environment, on clothing, in a store, or in a car though.

reviews.name: Fragranooga
reviews.comment: More interesting than the notes would suggest - it definitely has DS & Durga DNA in there, the drydown reminds me a bit of the drydown of Radio Bombay, and somehow, on an unrelated note, even a bit of the drydown of Ganymede. Does not seem like the lasting power is gonna be great, but not bad if you can find it for cheap.

reviews.name: martha_again
reviews.comment: pleasingly dark and bitter and a little boozy on me in a nice contrast to the one-note brightness of most citrus scents; mediterranean holiday but once the sun has gone down and you've had to shrug a shirt on over your white linen to go out for cocktails

reviews.name: Kyra_lou
reviews.comment: I love DS and Durga but unfortunately their scents do not last at all. They are very light and honestly great for layering. This is definitely a luxury product for how much you need to use and how often you need to reapply. This one is the same story. Absolutely beautiful, smells like a lemon summer. But it is so light. Great brand for people who get bothered by scents easily

reviews.name: KerriZ
reviews.comment: A very fresh astringent citrus. So many orange and lemon notes....It is sitting at an outdoor table on a narrow cobblestone street in the middle of a hot summer day in Capri sipping a limoncello spritzer. I am keen to layer this one with my Tom Ford Neroli Portofino or Jo Malone Red Rose - maybe both! :)

reviews.name: bumbog6
reviews.comment: Was happy to be able to sample this very interesting brand in NYC while visiting last week. Italian Cirtus is one of my favourites. I do not find that it leans masculine, and is perfectly unisex suited for whatever skin chemistry vibes well with it. I prefer feminine and unisex-feminine leaning scents, and on me IC is soft, warm, with the bright opening of light citrus. I think the mallow rounds out and softens sharp edges and makes it creamier. Maybe it's the time of year - deep summer in Canada - as I don't get anything harsh or astringent as others have noted (other than..like, it IS citrus, so if you immediately think of cleaning products, you might with this one). It is light, and lovely. Uncomplicated. I am not sure if I'd get a FB yet but, I do keep going back to my sample...

reviews.name: perfumedboyfriend
reviews.comment: This perfume really got my hopes up only to disappoint. It’s supposed to lead with lemon and blood orange, with herbal notes of Chinotto, evoking Campari, with a balsamic base chord and touches of violet leaf and incense. As a negroni devotee and lover of bitter Italian sodas, that all sounds fantastic to me, but on my skin, I find it mostly just smells like cheap orange dish detergent and then dries down to something unpleasantly powdery (that’s the violet and the musk ambrette, probably). I simply do not like it, which is a bummer, because I really wanted to.

reviews.name: alicali3
reviews.comment: You are the granita man - a hot Italian summer night - cobblestone Amalfi streets - white linen pants sticking to the sweat behind your knees - lightly sweet lemon juice drying around your mouth and stinging the cracks in your sunburnt lips - a hint of amaro at the back of your throat - a cigar not too far in your future

reviews.name: snackwithtea
reviews.comment: Fresh, simple, sweet, and subtle. Definitely a scent that matches the vibes of the Italian coast. It evokes feelings of lemon print, coral, and limewash. Very understated, which I prefer. This didn't strike me as a masculine scent. I was trying to decide between this and Coriander, and actually thought this one had a slightly more feminine edge in comparison. I ended up going with Coriander because it was slightly more complex and interesting on the opening.

reviews.name: mrsmellsgood
reviews.comment: Italian Citrus is having friends in the backyard for an afternoon bbq, marinating chicken and making cocktails. Tons of cut citrus, the pulp squirting out and the pithy tart citrus skin all over my hands and shirt. It’s bright and light but like a good cocktail it’s not fruity, sweet or boring. It’s just great. I think of this as a dark citrus and similar to making cocktails there is a seriousness to it. It’s fresh but it’s not just a summer day kind of vibe, on the hottest days it might even be too thick, but on a cooler day, an evening bbq with friends, a date with my wife or to work in the morning it’s awesome. The best part is I get no floral at all and for that I’m grateful. I have been trying a lot of citrus scents and colognes and too many go white floral/lavender and that just didn’t work for me. This stays as a dry citrus and just sort of sinks into a musky smooth base over time. I us a lot of sprays on my skin and on my clothes to get some staying power and it’s not a beast in any way though I have had compliments long after spraying so it does stick around. I like DS&D, I pop into their store from time to time and have several samples and decants. This is the only one I bought straight, full retail - no regrets. Some of their other stuff is weird, some of dosnt work for me though I like it, and there are others I want to get. This one is a winner.

reviews.name: Enrium
reviews.comment: DS & Durga is a hip niche brand that I admire overall, but I haven't met a DS&D scent yet that is full bottle-worthy for me. Italian Citrus certainly won't do anything to change this. I received a sample of this for free with a purchase, and expected a straightforward citrus cologne. Their scents are usually quite creative, but this is pretty dull (despite the radiance of the citrus opening), and has limited performance. IC is, predictably, a blend of citrus notes on a light, musky base. Orange is most dominant to my nose initially, and some tart lemon follows. It is quite sharp, leaning dangerously close to cleaning products. However, the clean musk creeps in early on, softening the sharp edges nicely. I get a little grassy greenness too. It is quite pleasant for a little while, but then fades to nothing in under an hour - quite a disappointment. It would work well as a refreshing scent in hot weather or for sports, but it is way overpriced for what it is. Sillage is intimate and longevity is weak. It is a unisex scent for hot days. More of an eau fraîche than an EDP as labelled, IC is ultimately disappointing. I have also smelled better colognes. Buy 4711 instead for about 5% of the price. 1.5/5.

reviews.name: TBonetheFella
reviews.comment: A bougie sea side country club in the midsummers afternoon. After your workout and subsequent shower, you sit on the porch of the clubhouse sipping on the lemon infused water they keep in the lobby to rehydrate and refresh before facing the day. The fresh zest of the gym cologne you brought, the clean and fuzzy bathrobe, and the smell of the fresh cut grass mixing together as your skin is heated up by the sun towering above. Life is good. Probably one of the simplest scents from DS and Durga (not that it’s a bad thing), but it fully encapsulates the post gym/shower smell in the summer. A pleasant medley of citrus, smooth and clean musk, and a ozonic/fresh cut grass backbone. It’s like you mixed bits of “Cologne” by ELDO, “Green Irish Tweed” by Creed, and “You” by Glossier. This would make a really good gym fragrance if it wasn’t $210 at retail. Overall a VERY easy wear, but the opening on paper can come off a bit rubbery (this goes away on my skin). 6/10 Moderate projection and sillage.

reviews.name: Dmiddy
reviews.comment: Straightforward, linear, exactly what it claims to be.

reviews.name: notfrannie
reviews.comment: very simple for D.S. and Durga, just straight citrus, but I don’t find it very soapy, just bright and plays well with others

reviews.name: Lykopa
reviews.comment: It’s an astringent, green citrus. Straightforward and not as well rounded as I had hoped. Smells cheaper than it is. It’s steamy and kind of old man-ish. It starts off with this unpleasant ambrette like note and settles into a lemon that’s not zingy or fresh.

reviews.name: fuggerone
reviews.comment: Plasticky smelling and very faint. Absolutely overpriced!

reviews.name: emgailba
reviews.comment: Smells like something a sleezy con artist would wear. Muted, almost mentholated citrus with hints of bad shaving cream and plastic. Leans masculine. A scrubber for me.

reviews.name: creeper
reviews.comment: Pasta al limone-the zest of the lemon, a bit of the pepper and a muted steamy starchiness in the form of musk that ultimately dries down to what your hands smell like when the dish is complete-the smell of soap that you used to try to scrub the garlic from your fingertips.

reviews.name: chrisk252
reviews.comment: Nice Citrus with a Chanelade like drydown quality...aldehydic, musky, very sleek. Can't say I love it...bit too perfumey and not as fresh, bright, and non-perfumey natural as what I'd want something called "Italian Citrus" to be. More sleek French than Italian.

reviews.name: death
reviews.comment: Picture this: You're suntanning on the Amalfi Coast of Italy, an older gentleman approaches you. He's tan, a bit overweight, and has been sweating from being out in the sun too. This is the scent of his slightly bad breath, aftershave, and body odor. In other words, this is definitely what Bert from the 2nd season of White Lotus smells like. Personally, I don't like it at all, I don't like to wear it, and I definitely don't want to be emanating the same vibes as him. But if that's the kind of feeling you're after, go for it.

reviews.name: McG1993
reviews.comment: It's clean but it's very sweet - almost bubblegum-like. It's nice but it's so faint, it has absolutely no presence at all. It's like sweet-smelling water. Lasts less than a full hour on my skin with absolutely no projection. Definitely a skin scent. Not worth the price tag by any stretch of the imagination, I'm afraid.

reviews.name: QuincesI
reviews.comment: Limonceeelloooo. Very fizzy, almost thick, syrupy, yellow-white top notes, Amalfi lemon sorbet-like revisited trou normand, candied citron. All of this little world is quickly followed by something sweeter, white tea type, ambrette, musky hint, which turns the lemon into a clementine, with the green leaf-branch combo left hanging. Not the most amazing of their collection, but nice and well-balanced.

reviews.name: cait_lynne
reviews.comment: This is a beautiful, high quality and well blended fragrance from Ds & Durga. It opens with a burst of citrus - not sweet but zesty and green, with a tiny bit of bitterness. I love the lemon in this - I’m not usually into lemon but it is super refreshing. I don’t personally get the incense, but the balsam resin and oak moss are there and there’s no hint of citrus cleaning products in this fragrance at ALL. My partner bought this for me not realising it’s supposed to be a scent for men (I’m a woman), but although it’s a little on the masculine side it’s quite unisex. I see what they were going for with the fresh out of the shower mens cologne feel and this is 100% the vibe it’s giving. I can’t wait to wear this in summer, it’s so cooling and refreshing.

reviews.name: carlosrafael
reviews.comment: A bit of a disappointment … It was a blind buy. How can I go wrong with any perfume that suggests a heavy mix of citrus notes? Additionally, I like DS Durga perfumes. Sadly, Italian Citrus is not what I expected. I was expecting a heavy sweet cocktail of citrus. However, IC is actually citrus-shy. I don’t perceive a distinctive trace of the citrus trifecta: lemon, lime, or bergamot. I certainly don’t get mandarine, which is usually a sweet citrus note. The citrus scent is rather tame and leans heavily on the green-citrus side of the spectrum. All I get is a bitter green citrus smell, not the customary citrus sweetness I get from other citrus perfumes. The citrus scent I get reminds me of the green orange trees my mother planted at our house when I was growing up. I smell the green orange leafs, twigs, and branches. Italian Citrus is very clean-musky. It's got a lot of that clean white musk note that I don't particularly care for. So there is nothing here that I particularly look forward to wearing under the hot sun in Florida. Perfumers, be careful when stamping a suggestive olfactory name or word on a perfume. If you include words such as citrus, oud, amber, tobacco, rose, Santal, etc, etc, etc to a perfume name, something that sets an olfactory expectation, then make sure the perfume celebrates and has in its limelight the suggested smell. Not the best and most refreshing citrus perfume for me. If you like a bitter green citrus perfume with a lot of white musks, try this one. If you are looking for a refreshing sweet citrus perfume, probably try Eau d'Hadrien by Annick Goutal or Profumo Roma’s Aqua Viva. A different one I really enjoyed from a relatively new house of late is Maison Crivelli’s Citrus Batikanga.

reviews.name: trabuquera
reviews.comment: Agh, another to add to the really short list of absolute failures from D&D. Usually there's always some twist of wit or source of interest in their scents, whether they're your (or my) thing or not; mostly I adore them. This? A muted whisper of citrus which sometimes points at sweet-synthetic, sometimes gestures towards something quirkier and muskier, but whose voice dies so fast it's left gaping and mouthing some message at you which you just can't pick up. What on earth is this trying to say? A tiny ripple of freshness spreading a few inches off the skin for under an hour and ... that's all folks. I'm sure that my #1 hate object in the scent world, the tiny .5ml dabber format tester, didn't do this one any favours but pffft. Really! File under BAFFLING MISFIRE.

reviews.name: SonnyCol
reviews.comment: WEAK. After a few seconds I can’t smell it at all. And I have been giving it a good chance. Maybe I got a dud? My husband can’t detect the scent on me at all, no matter how much I spray on or how recently.

reviews.name: librabeing
reviews.comment: Pretty forgettable. Still haven't found a lemon-forward fragrance that doesn't smell either one-dimensional or like hand soap...

reviews.name: Jkr111
reviews.comment: This is a fresh, pleasant but very light citrus/ambrette/green combo. It's a LOT like Nasomatto's Silver Musk. I like both but they're just so light (or maybe I'm anosmic to the musks) that I wouldn't buy. Zero projection on me.

reviews.name: pennypencil
reviews.comment: A warm and zesty citrus scent with an interesting, twisty opening of something a bit industrial (in a good way). After having sampled a lot of citrus fragrances, I rate this one highly. It's juicy with a thick middle that nicely lingers. Completely gender inclusive, it manages to be uplifting and aromatic whilst also sticking around thanks to a musky base. Layered with Crystal Pistil, it's elevated further, adding top notes of sweet, bright neroli.

reviews.name: ingeneuxo
reviews.comment: -If you could bottle summer at a beach resort, this would be it. -Italian Citrus opens as a sweet, slightly tart citrus scent. -Very photo-realistic, well-balanced scent with depth to it. -The dry-down reveals incense notes and some ambrette (i initially thought it was vetiver), both of which help ground an otherwise nondescript opening. (+)

reviews.name: kyohaine
reviews.comment: The musk and citrus combo isn't working for me here. Citrus itself isn't even that interesting, it smells rather synthetic.

reviews.name: alyllauren
reviews.comment: This really impressed me! I get lemon and orange from this more than anything--perfectly bright and definitely unisex. It's a lovely citrus: you get some of the sharpness but it's also softened by the musk without becoming sugary-sweet, somehow. It doesn't have crazy sillage but I was still catching whiffs here and there at the end of the work day, and when I actually smelled my wrists 12 hours after application it was still there!

reviews.name: DesertRose23
reviews.comment: very herbal on my skin. The citrus smells synthetic and there is an undeniable green/bitter note. Didn't work at all. I would recommend Le Labo Bergamote for a very fresh Mediterranean citrus

reviews.name: SuzScents
reviews.comment: Italian Citrus is a delightfully clean, zesty, ever-so-slightly-sweet and slightly musky understated scent. Nothing earth shattering or ground breaking, to be sure. However, it wafts softly around you and wraps you in a nice scented bubble. It's marketed toward men, but I'd totally wear this and enjoy it.

reviews.name: GlacialImpala
reviews.comment: Dries down to exactly the same fragrance as Rose Atlantic. Too bad because on paper it smells like a great natural lemon.

reviews.name: E1
reviews.comment: Notes from their website: Top: pressed lemon, chinotto, blood orange Heart: green mandarin, violet leaf, incense Base: copaiba balsam, musk ambrette, oakmoss

reviews.name: Kloppo
reviews.comment: Why the hell does this smell like the leather in a new car, it was itrus for about 30s and now all i get is car leather and tyre rubber (not burnt). I guess someone COULD like this if they were so into cars they sniffed them.

reviews.name: pktsqr
reviews.comment: I love - or at least like - nearly everything I've tried from this house, but this is definitely one of the exceptions. There are all sorts of similar options out there that do the same thing better, for less.

reviews.name: bintTapputi
reviews.comment: A safe, clean, musky fragrance. Pretty relaxed and unassuming. For my own tastes, quite boring but well executed. I think it has a beautiful dry down/skin scent stage which easily brings this up to a 7.5, but I have to say the majority of when you notice the fragrance it isn't that interesting. I absolutely love the dry down, though, it is deliriously relaxing. 6/10

reviews.name: fishwife
reviews.comment: Not much else to add here that hasn't already been said, but this is definitely DS's safest option...no one would not like this, and it's very light, more musky than fruity to my nose...worth trying if you like light, inoffensive background perfumes, something we all need once in awhile...

reviews.name: Q80
reviews.comment: It's more of a bitter blood orange than citrus. It has a fair bitter blood orange, grapefruit, and ambrette. It does have hints of green in it. It's a fair blend and not the best citrus (Italian citrus) in the market. I prefer "Kobe" by Xerjoff, and the amazing "Tuscany" by Aramis.

reviews.name: Hamcollins
reviews.comment: skittles.

reviews.name: miracleborgtech
reviews.comment: This is a perfume James Bond 007 would wear to a yacht party in the Mediterranean. The name says it all! Italian Citrus! Yum!

reviews.name: rbalkris
reviews.comment: A modern refreshing take on classic eau-de-cologne, this "safe" beauty from DSD combines notes of citrus, orange, lemon, mandarin, ambrette and musk to create a light airy and masculine beauty that pleasantly freshens without overwhelming. Moderate in sillage and long lasting this is a really inoffensive and clean smelling scent that can be worn actually by either genders in almost any setting. A real classic offering from this eclectic line, a must for citrus musk lovers, and beautifully blended.

reviews.name: Ignas39
reviews.comment: Italian Citrus is a very nice peppery citrus mix with the addition of musk (I smell some pine as well, though it's not in the note breakdown). This is a very masculine, mature fragrance and I find it very enjoyable. It does remind me of Terre D'Hermes a bit due to the citrus and pepper, however this one is a lot more clean due to the lack of vetiver that is in Terre D'Hermes. Sadly, as it is nothing really new and special, the price asked for it is a bit too steep. There are many citrus fragrances that have the same depth for a lower price, so I couldn't see myself getting a whole bottle for myself or as a gift to anyone else.

reviews.name: OTA Mom
reviews.comment: So clean and masculine, wish my SO would wear it. Strong, strong citrus, nothing sweet or playful or childish about it.

reviews.name: bcoops
reviews.comment: I have been wearing this a full month and enjoy it immensely. A very rich citrus with excellent performance.Someone mentioned chewing gum but this kinda reminds me of a citrus starburst for some reason. I've gotten unsolicited compliments from both men and woman....very happy ...if u dig around u can get it for 105$. If your a citrus lover i think u must check this...I do also get a soapy johnson and johnson vibe but in a very good way.... i have been switching between this and xerjoff 1861....its beeen a very good summer :) and the one from beautyhabit.com for 106.00 is an EDP not EDC like it says

reviews.name: landshark321
reviews.comment: Italian Citrus is a citrus blend coupled with musk and an undefined sweetness, perhaps brought on by the mandarin orange, that reminds me of fig, though fig itself is nowhere found in the note breakdown. A unisex citrus warm-weather fragrance that might have a lot of appeal to different people but nonetheless does not sit well on my skin, where it comes off a little old and feminine. Longevity and projection are decent, though, for a fragrance that seems to lean toward warm-weather usage. Still, this is another example of a D.S. & Durga EDP that strikes me as a bit pricey for what it is (at the standard cost of $145 for 50ml). Only their heavier options have so far (Bowmakers, Burning Barbershop) have struck me as potentially worthwhile at this price point. Nonetheless, intriguing to try. 6 out of 10

reviews.name: RUDOLFO512
reviews.comment: Italian Citrus EDP is a delightful citrus fragrance. It smells fresh and clean. At first spray I smell a sharp blood orange, lemon/citrus. Soon it dries down to a perfectly rounded citrus fragrance. I am a huge fan of citrus based fragrances and I love this one. Perfect for the summer months here in South Florida.

reviews.name: Jassar
reviews.comment: I thought I would love it. It turns soapy as can be after a few minutes. Disappointed.

reviews.name: hullhockey91
reviews.comment: Holy wow...can you say BUBBLE GUM. Very pleasant fragrance. It really makes me want to chew my wrist seeing as it smells like total bubblegum...amazingly weird but good!!

reviews.name: draugr
reviews.comment: Really classy citrus EDP. Beautiful sharp citrus opening - feel like I've just stepped out of a bath of blood oranges. Decent longevity and though just on the male side of unisex, the other half loves it as do I on her. Sorry Allure Edition Blanche, this stunner has taken your summer number one spot.

reviews.name: Tigerlillian
reviews.comment: Clean, delicious and freshly showered with Johnson & Johnson Baby Shampoo--just divine on the skin. The citrus radiates a feeling of ambrosial comfort. Ambrette gives a lovely botanical muskiness. I'd gravitate toward any man wearing this. Simple, pure and beautifully modest. Unisex too--perhaps a little generic, but I love wearing it.

reviews.name: GrecoRoman
reviews.comment: Tried this at the JCrew mens shop in SoHo and fell for it immediately. The Citrus and musk hit you immediately and the sweetness starts to come in within an hour. Perfect for spring and summer.

title: Narcotix Citrus Lorenzo Pazzaglia perfume - a fragrance for women and men 2022
description: Narcotix Citrus by Lorenzo Pazzaglia is a Oriental Floral fragrance for women and men. Narcotix Citrus was launched in 2022. The nose behind this fragrance is Lorenzo Pazzaglia. Top notes are Pineapple, Yuzu, Bergamot, Green Notes, Black Currant, Pepper, Neroli, Green Apple and Orange Blossom; middle notes are Birch, Patchouli, Jasmine Sambac, Jasmine, White Flowers, Juniper, elemi, Bulgarian Rose and Turkish Rose; base notes are Cedar, Ambergris, Ambroxan, Oak and Bourbon Vanilla.

pros.label: Modern, intensified version of Aventus DNA
High-quality, realistic citrus and pineapple notes
Reliable compliment getter with high mass appeal
Higher essential oil concentration than typical niche
Extremely versatile for any season or occasion
Fresh, sparkling Yuzu and bergamot opening
Exceptional longevity, lasting 24+ hours on skin
Excellent performance in high summer heat
Rich dry down with birch and ambergris

cons.label: High price point for 50ml bottle size
Potentially cloying and invasive in crowded spaces
Limited availability, sold mostly online or in Modena
Harsh, metallic scent profile in dry down
Overwhelming ambroxan dose causes physical discomfort
Less refined than the original Creed Aventus
Performance reports vary drastically between different users
Lacks originality compared to other inspired fragrances
Strong birch note smells like graphite pencils
Heavy leather notes dominate the late dry down

thisPerfumeRemindsMeOf.title: Club de Nuit Intense Man Parfum
Club de Nuit Intense Man Limited Edition Parfum

reviews.name: RajahSaheb
reviews.comment: Sounds like Cedrat Boise. Anyone tried ?

reviews.name: iambmw622
reviews.comment: It’s okay… it’s got the Aventus DNA we all know and love, but make it more woody and remove the smoke. A beast mode take on Aventus, but loses the depth of the original in the process. 5/10.

reviews.name: Leeon
reviews.comment: can Anyone please if its worth a try? it feel like i need to try it, it could be exactly what I’m looking for. I’m not willing to buy the original Aventus because of the poor longevity. Lately I’ve been wearing Club de Nuit parfum... it does the job longevity wise, but it feels synthetic and cheap. From what I’m seeing, Narcotix Citrus could be perfect for me because I really like that DNA.

reviews.name: xarbeda
