reviews.comment: good, but lil harsh after spray, dry down is good and for me its kinda similar to cedrat boise, not something special for that price

reviews.name: SATAM
reviews.comment: Light citrus crisp on top after initial spray but develops into a harsh metallic scent reminicent of aerosol body spray. Personally not my taste, but I can see how it could be a daily wear for some people.

reviews.name: aspirina
reviews.comment: Aventus and Aventus for her, very nice in the first minutes until the nice citrus notes go away and you start realizing that the scent is actually harsh and drowning in ambroxan. I guess I am not having any luck this week. Everything or almost everything I have tried reeks of ambroxan. Aventus has ambroxan but the dose is much more tolerable and the whole fragrance is very beautiful. I am talking of ambroxan doses that make you feel discomfort.

reviews.name: AlAmmari
reviews.comment: Love the initial blast, but the dry down is very leathery and I am not a leather fan. And the performance is very average.

reviews.name: Victor Mazz
reviews.comment: You want a good Creed inspired perfume but with actually good longevity and a bit more pineapple ? This is the one, i've tested a lot of Aventus like colognes and this is by a mile, by a long shot the best one i've smelled. I got my best friend to smell all of my collection and he ended up picking this as his favourite. In the opening you get the note of Yuzu, if you do not know what does it smells like it is a combination beetween bergamot and pineapple, really sweet juicy and probably the best citrus note out there. and to be frank i get this note on Aventus too TBF... Once it dries down yo get those with birch wood accompanied by a sweet gentle pineapple, florals and undertones of ambergris. even the dried down is very similar o aventus although the dried down of aventus still has nuances of pink pepper that this do not have. so for the dried down i would still prefer aventus over this. Now let's talk about the sweet stuff, the notes are incredibly realistic very well done, not synthetic or scratchy, luminous yet juicy and at last LONG LASTING this last on me realistically 8hrs with 2 hrs of projection whereas creed only 2/3 (with less than avg projection). Outstanding, perfumery i've smelled 4 of Lorenzo pazzaglia and this is the best. 9.5/10

reviews.name: SlappingItOnYourTongue
reviews.comment: Boring. You've smelled it before but you can get it from Lorenzo too

reviews.name: Nidza1982
reviews.comment: Nishane Hachivat thats how smells. With more less spice..

reviews.name: TenkeraHUN
reviews.comment: It's almost like Aventus,but too much woody notes and too strong.When i first smell it was a fresh sharpened pencil...smell like a graphite and a little metallic vibe...it a little bit disturbed,that's why I sold...

reviews.name: crys2979
reviews.comment: Another Creed Aventus copy, but of good quality. It doesn't excel in any way compared to the original.

reviews.name: Demani
reviews.comment: Aventus inspired Pineaple opening then woody aromatic Is aight but im personally so sick of this DNA

reviews.name: Ayad barwari
reviews.comment: Better than Aventus

reviews.name: croatus
reviews.comment: Aventus should refer to Narcotix Citrus as "sir" and Narcotix Citrus should call Aventus "young boy". Aventus is a shell of what Narcotix Citrus is - literally, remove the body and base of NC and you get Aventus. Aventus also has around 40% of intensity NC has. This is not my style of perfume and I'll stick to my 5ml decant, but for what it is - 10/10

reviews.name: Advision
reviews.comment: It's definite variation to Aventus. But Aventus always better than clones. Always

reviews.name: OnyxLotus
reviews.comment: I have the official LP 22 2 ml sample set and plan on reviewing the whole line. It’s been a while since I’ve been this excited to experience new scents. This is essentially Aventus extreme, everything intensified to the maximum. There’s many out there but this is the best I’ve personally tried. Quality is unmatched, better than the OG and perfected. If you don’t think this performs well, you either got a fake or became anosmic. This is absolutely fallout nuclear and was still steadily projecting beyond an arms length after 6 1/2 hours and lasted over 24 hours on my skin.

reviews.name: Scottishscentguy
reviews.comment: Aventus vibes but different. Does not perform like the votes suggest.

reviews.name: kevinfinettoph
reviews.comment: fragrance along the lines of Aventus Creed but with substantial differences. This perfume years ago represented a first old challenge for dear Lorenzo as he delighted in creating a fragrance of his own that was on the vibes of the Creed best seller. Then in the future the Perfumix perfumery, seeing that it was a fragrance DESERVING attention as it was appealing to more and more people, wanted it as a sales exclusive in its perfumery. Opening reminiscent of a much fresher and sparkling version of aventus, as we have yuzu, notes that give freshness and sparkle, bergamot and orange blossom, and then discover a fresh, floral and woody heart. There is a lot of similarity with Aventus with the difference that here we are dealing with an extract in high concentrations of quality essential oils. Absolutely versatile, for any season, for any occasion you want. On my skin it has excellent performances, a must have if you are a lover of the genre. Recommended .

title: Ishatar Citrus Oud Arabiyat Prestige perfume - a fragrance for women and men 2023
description: Ishatar Citrus Oud by Arabiyat Prestige is a fragrance for women and men. Ishatar Citrus Oud was launched in 2023. The nose behind this fragrance is Angeline Leporini. Top notes are Cardamom and Mandarin; middle notes are Woody Notes and Jasmine; base notes are Patchouli, Cedarwood, Musk and Oud. Ishatar Citrus Oud by Arabiyat Prestige is a fresh and vibrant fragrance that offers a sparkling twist on oud. This unisex scent opens with fresh notes of mandarin, complemented by spicy and aromatic cardamom. The heart reveals a sensual blend of jasmine and woody notes, leading to a rich base of oud, patchouli, musk, and cedarwood.

reviews.name: hvnscnt
reviews.comment: The mandarin is prominent in the opening and feels very fresh but slightly bitter and not that sweet. It's more reminiscent of mandarin peel rather than the fleshy part of the fruit. It smoothes out after a few minutes and the woody notes become more prominent. The oud can't be smelled in the air but is noticable when smelled up close, which is a positive thing to me. Once it has settled, I find this to almost have a woody-citrusy graphite type of smell, probably coming from the cedar. I haven't smelled this accord in the world of middle eastern fragrances yet. The blend comes together very nicely and makes for an elevated citrus-woody fragrance with a twist. Don't be scared off by the name; I'm not *that* fond of oud -- but I took a chance on this and I'm glad I did.

reviews.name: Teotanja3010
reviews.comment: 90% same as original cedrat Boise

reviews.name: Hamdanii
reviews.comment: I think this is more like cedrat boise intense, much smoother than CB that got too strong dense citrus. Edited: Need to overspray on clothes,i do it on chest area.15spray and scent stay with you all day at 10hours with moderate projection. I wear it at home its stay until next day. So good buy, i will buy again and skip the OG that double the price

reviews.name: juanolvera7735
reviews.comment: This is great. I picked this up after catching wind of it being a Cedrat Boise clone, and I've heard good things about the brand. Upon opening the boys and spraying, there wasn't much resemblance. It wasn't bad, but it wasn't great. After letting it sit a few days, it has become much better. While it has some similarities to Cedrat, it's not the same, and I like that. It has a similar citrus fruity woody scent profile, but in a slightly different way. I kept catching whiffs as i did the dishes, and they were nice. Solid pickup on its own. CB is not much more, and that is definitely worth getting. So is this. As someone who likes the scent profile I'm happy to have both.

title: Summer Citrus Maison Viegas cologne - a new fragrance for men 2025
description: Summer Citrus by Maison Viegas is a Woody Aromatic fragrance for men. This is a new fragrance. Summer Citrus was launched in 2025. Top notes are Bergamot, Grapefruit and Currant buds; middle notes are Vetiver, Green Tangerine and Geranium; base notes are Dry Wood, Cashmere Wood and Ambergris.

reviews.name: dOOEA
reviews.comment: Péssimo, cheiro de produto de limpeza, quase não da pra sentir o citrico, só um verde intoxicante e pessimo, não comprem esse, comprem o heaven ou o legend

title: Classic Citrus Banana Republic perfume - a fragrance for women and men
description: Classic Citrus by Banana Republic is a Citrus fragrance for women and men. Classic Citrus was launched during the 2000's. Top notes are Grapefruit, Clementine and Black Currant; middle notes are Pollen, Honeysuckle and Honey; base notes are Musk, Ginger, Animal notes and Pine.

peopleWhoLikeThisAlsoLike.title: Luna Rossa Carbon Eau de Toilette

reviews.name: Imcheap17
reviews.comment: Smells like a sweet frosted lemon drop. Close to pure white but with grapefruit

reviews.name: bvarney_zip
reviews.comment: Fresh & citric but extremely weak & fleeting. Literally a mere skin scent within minutes.

reviews.name: veggiebeautycosmetics
reviews.comment: This was an accidental blind buy for me. I thought I was getting the original classic scent, not the citrus version. The ambroyxn in this mixed with the black current deficient gives me Armani Si vibes without the patchouli, so if you like that perfume but aren’t a patchouli lover give this one a go. It has a sharp grapefruit/lemon burst upon spraying, but fades down to mostly the black current, amybroxyn, maybe a small touch of the pine it mentions, but this does not smell like pine sol if that concerns you. This perfume also reminds me of a lighter Light Blue Forever, which is about double the price and limited edition. The longevity isn’t the greatest, I’d say 3-4 hours, sillage is a couple of feet tops. I can see this working as a summer scent, particularly in the evenings.

reviews.name: ekrapf
reviews.comment: I bought a full size of this from my local marshalls for about $20 USD. I assumed I got the Classic (non-citrus) as my bottle does not say citrus. However my bottle isn’t green, it’s slight tinted… super light brown-ish? Super light pink-ish? Not sure. But it has the black cap like the citrus version. Alas, my bottle doesn’t say citrus, but everything else about it points to it being the citrus version so I guess maybe it was misprinted. This fragrance is a green citrus scent, I get no lemon, but I smell the green rind from standard citrus. Scent doesn’t last long on the skin so I reapply. Definitely unisex freshie. Overall this is a good affordable unisex cheapie, light but when I don’t want to smell like a typical girly body spray. I ended up selling it.

reviews.name: SarahTheBoss
reviews.comment: This has become one of my favorite easy reach fragrances. Satisfying citrus to cool you on a hot day, just strong enough to be noticed in passing but never offensive. It's addicting in a way and also very versatile; I've worn it both to work and to work out. I don't detect any animal notes or ginger but the piney musky undertone is what helps make this stand out in my opinion. This is an affordable underrated gem!

title: Kyphenzé - Citrus Rex Pierre Guillaume Paris perfume - a new fragrance for women and men 2025
description: Kyphenzé - Citrus Rex by Pierre Guillaume Paris is a Citrus Aromatic fragrance for women and men. This is a new fragrance. Kyphenzé - Citrus Rex was launched in 2025. The nose behind this fragrance is Pierre Guillaume. Top note is Citruses; middle notes are Liatris, Sage and Spices; base note is Resins. An exceptional citrus, balancing aromatic tension and resinous strength. A radiant citrus crowned with spices and resins. Diffusive, tenacious, distinguished. Citrus, in its imperial form. THE INSPIRATION A dry heat, almost sacred. Scorching stones, the sun at its zenith, and in the air, a vibration of herbs, blazing citrus, and ancient resins. Kyphenzé is born from this tension between raw light and buried mystery: the evocation of a forgotten ritual, between ancient Mediterranean and Oriental reverie. It is not about reconstructing the past, but exhuming its aura to feed a contemporary gesture — imposing, fascinating, luminous. THE FRAGRANCE Citrus Rex. A citrus with authority. The opening grips: an accord of sharp, juicy, bright citrus — almost incandescent. What follows imposes an unexpected verticality: spices, sages, and liatrix sculpt the material. At the base, a rare resin — sandarac — exhales a golden, balsamic, mineral breath. The fragrance anchors, extends, asserts itself. It doesn’t just pass by, it reigns. THE NAME Kyphenzé: a portmanteau, a world-word. It evokes both a timeless elsewhere (kyphi, the sacred incense of ancient Egypt), and a modernity freed from its roots. It is a creation of meaning, a name without ties, but full of echoes. In the wake of Kyphenzé, one senses the shadow of a temple, fire, lemon trees, the surge of solar pleasure. A name that asserts its singularity without explanation. Like a seal. Like a spell. MANIFESTO — Kyphenzé We create presences. Powers. Mental landscapes. The light is raw. It cuts. It reveals. Sometimes it burns. We love tensions, contrasts, materials that clash and rise. The gesture is contemporary, but the echo is ancient. Kyphenzé was born from this clash. An imperial citrus, of solar insolence. A breath of citrus, sages, and resins, taut like a spell. Citrus elevated to a rite. To a reign. Fragrance is an invisible signature. A silent power. A way to stand in the world, without concession. With Kyphenzé, we don’t explain everything. We let the trail speak for us.

peopleWhoLikeThisAlsoLike.title: Le Musc & La Peau Extrait de Parfum

reviews.name: Tshahb
reviews.comment: Enormous sage, with very sweet, nutty resins underneath. Liatris gives it a more fuzzy herbal feel rather than just the clean sage. The first spritz is immediately reminiscent of 80s masculins. Reminds me of being bored looking at cars with my step-dad when I was very young. It has a sort of dirty car ashtray, aramis nostalgia. While that evocation doesn't go away, there is more to it than that. It has a modern resin feel and of course, any such scent in the 80s would have had oakmoss and/or patchouli, both of which are absent here. I think this is a good perfume but I can't imagine wearing it in public, I don't want to smell like a cockney smoker from the 80s. I don't get the name, as citrus is the last thing I get of all the notes listed. As I'm writing this it's drying down, and it's getting more and more stale smelling. Unfortunately I think it's going to be a scrubber for me, both for the scent and for what it reminds me of. Update: hideous in the far drydown.

title: Sporty Citrus Michael Kors perfume - a fragrance for women 2013
description: Sporty Citrus by Michael Kors is a Citrus Aromatic fragrance for women. Sporty Citrus was launched in 2013. The nose behind this fragrance is Honorine Blanc. The fragrance features Orange Blossom, Orange, Musk and Mandarin Orange. The new collection by Michael Kors, which includes not only fragrances, is launched in August 2013. A step towards beauty and care products will enable the modern woman to select new cosmetic products and Michal Kors make-up, besides various fragrant collections. Each of the three directions of the collection—Sporty, Sexy or Glam, will be complemented with special colors of make-up, various shades of nail polish; eye shadows and powder available in three shades accompany the sporty, sexy or glamorous look. The names of the new fragrances are Sporty Citrus, Oriental Sexy and Jasmine Glam, and they are available in tall, glass flacons as 30, 50 and 100ml with a golden color stopper. Sporty Citrus is the work of perfumer Honorine Blanc, who provided the fragrance with a sweet union of orange and mandarin along with scents of fresh orange blossom accompanied with musky trail.

reviews.name: Smellycheetrapit77$
reviews.comment: Two notes jump out zesty strong citrus and musk. Longevity is moderate. Most citrus notes do not last as long as in this perfume. I think the name suits it well it's a sporty-strong citrus rather then dainty citrus. I got lucky many years ago i bought whole box of samples on ebay and still use it to this days. Finally decided to get a bottle found out is discontinued. At least I have plenty of samples to use.

reviews.name: sidramasood1996s
reviews.comment: amazing

reviews.name: amy33
reviews.comment: I tend to find citrusy fragrances to smell silly and cheap, but this one is a love love for me! It just screams summer and fun and mind-free to me. It can be hard to find, took me a while and many airports shops visits to find it again.

reviews.name: yellowhat
reviews.comment: The divine, perfect, absolute citrus fragrance! I adored it! So disappointed that it was limited edition!

reviews.name: Markellam92
reviews.comment: I love that one!!! And let me tell you that I'm not a citrus person AT ALL! I like sweet oriental sometimes heavy perfumes BUT a girl needs a perfume for every occasion and that on my skin is the perfect fresh yet sweet citrus I have ever smell! It stays for hours and in the summer is a must for me! It was a gift so I didn't expect to like it when I read it's name but I fell I love when I smelled it on me

reviews.name: Ashalia
reviews.comment: So disappointed! I bought this on a blind buy, which has been somewhat successful on the Michael Kors line. I don't smell any citrus here :( It is more of a floral scent to me with lots of musk, which is disappointing. I had counted on this perfume being my 'wear to the gym' fragrance. No way. In my opinion, this has fragrance was poorly named. :(

reviews.name: bashe
reviews.comment: Is this one getting discontinued slowly? Does anyone know? I’m having hard time to purchase this for an average price and availability is also poor lately...just wondering...

reviews.name: waskotransportation
reviews.comment: So I like citrus, but when I got my rollerball of this in the mail, I decided right away that it is not full-bottle worthy. Here we are a year later and I am searching for the best deal on a full-size bottle. At first (last year) I thought that it was too sharp of a citrus. It was so strong that it shocked me and smelled even a bit masculine to my nose. It is funny how my taste has changed so much over the last year of collecting perfumes. I never ever thought I would ever ever like an oud scent, and that now, mixed with rose or citrus, is the BEST. Now in my collection, all of the bottles that I adored the first year of collecting, basically sit on a shelf. I discovered Kilian and Byredo recently and am in love with many of those. Just takes a bit longer to save for my new taste. This is supposed to be a review about Sporty Citrus and I kind of got off track, but the main point that I am trying to make is even if you get a perfume that you don't love right away, do keep it because your taste may evolve over time, and it could become a favorite later, just like what happened to me with this frag.

reviews.name: mirisfrik
reviews.comment: Review based on original 1,5ml sample. It starts quite potent, juicy an lemony. Almost at the edge of sour and sharp, but not too much. Unfortunately it develops into something sticky, artificial and musky. So strong that you want to wash it off, as if you were wearing cleaning detergent or sth with simmilar scent.

reviews.name: Gingerwoman
reviews.comment: I liked this the first time I tried it. I liked the musky dry down. But the second time I tried it it had something so chemical about it that I'm really glad I didn't buy it the first time. Has a harsh cloying chemical element. As someone said synthetic oranges, soap and I'd add musk on the dry down. It's the synthetic quality that makes it not for me in the long run.

reviews.name: ReneeW1
reviews.comment: On my skin the mixture of citrus and musk turn this into a scent that somehow gives off an almost cedar smell. Its clean and fresh and definitely lives up to the sporty name. A unisex fragrance.

reviews.name: Fragrantnatic
reviews.comment: I'm not sure if the sample i have is a fake or the real thing but this scent is non existent on me! There was a very faint citrus at the beginning and after 15 minutes, nothing! Thank goodness I only had a tiny sample.

reviews.name: Camerynn
reviews.comment: I can't believe how much this smells like Ange Ou Demon Le Secret on my skin. I wondered when I tried this on today why it smelled so familiar, and why I loved it so much! At first it is quite green & citrusy, then calms down to a slightly (very slightly) masculine, fresh, herbaceous scent. Then an hour or so later it settles into a sexy, earthy citrus floral. Don't let the "masculine" description put you off if, like me, you're not a fan of unisex fragrances. This is more a strong, confident woman rather than truly masculine. Mmm-mm-mmmmmm...

reviews.name: cedar_lea
reviews.comment: Very sharp citrus and very little orange blossom. At times it does remind me of sporty men's colognes. I definately get a strong turpentine note. The combition deoesnt suit me very well.

reviews.name: DebiL1211
reviews.comment: While I like this fragrance I rarely reach for it because it is being phased out and I keep it for my hubby. He loves this fragrance so I only wear it for him. I smell mostly orange blossoms with a hint of oranges and he said he smells a lot of the orange with a hint of the musk. With 2 sprays on my skin I get about 4 hours. It doesn't last but it has good projection. Longevity: 4 hours with two sprays Sillage: arm's length Recommend: yes and no. Yes because it is nice but no because the company is phasing it out and it won't be around. Blind buy safe: read the notes and decide/ could be hit or miss for some people.

reviews.name: Gingerwoman
reviews.comment: Tried this again at the mall. lol Glad I didn't buy it last time. I've gone off it now. It's just too soapy. I don't remember it being so soapy last time but the soapiness is kind of irritating after awhile. I think I will be buying more of Lancome's Miracle instead of this now.

reviews.name: bashe
reviews.comment: this perfume isn't masculine at all. it is fierce and fearless. so are the women for whom it was created.

reviews.name: Gingerwoman
reviews.comment: This isn't what I expected. I expected more CKOne with a name like "Sporty Cirtus" or something like O de Lancome. It's not like these. On me it it more feminine and actually sexy. The citrus at first is kind of weaky. Oddly then it blends into a lovely clean fresh musky dry down, that is stronger than the original citrus burst, and lasts quite a while. I want to buy it now after having it sprayed on me in the store. I didn't buy it because I wanted to see how quickly the scent would disappear but the scent lasts and is warm, spicy and pretty. I want it, but can't justify the purchase when I have a number of other scents at home.

reviews.name: Daisy143
reviews.comment: Masculine.

reviews.name: minaggizete
reviews.comment: not at all sporty! but great fragrance with pretty good sillage and longevity. It is really chic, a bit chypre but with more citrusy notes. I used to wear it till my mom (who is 74) "found" it and took my bottle! well it smells on her great too (usually on women of that age perfumes tend to smell a bit weird) very fresh! it reminds me a lot of "Still" by J.Lo which is a favorite, but discontinued.

reviews.name: Beadvised
reviews.comment: Nice clean sweet citrus blend. Nothing overpowering or artificial.

reviews.name: jenniferkuo423
reviews.comment: My favorite out of the three Michael Kors collection perfume samples I got from Macy's. I think it's a misnomer: Sporty Citrus is not sporty but rather quite elegant and rich. I adore citrus scents and I use this as a special occasion fragrance; the orange/musk blend is very lovely and refined.

reviews.name: genny17
reviews.comment: I am surprised by the latest reviews!! This is lovely, soft citrus, feminine, summery and easy to wear,.I don't like woodsy, and this is only so slightly woodsy to my nose..it has a clean fresh musky dry down, lovely!! I bought it for myself and my son' S girlfriend, she needed something light for work, it fits the bill, there is nothing sporty about that scent but neither is it too perfumey!?

reviews.name: dan_megan
reviews.comment: I blind bought this today because usually I enjoy Marc Jacobs but oh my gosh this smells awful. So manly, it has major cedar notes and is downright awful.

reviews.name: dglightblue
reviews.comment: Masculine citrus. Sporty like Ralph Lauren does sporty. I prefer fresh or watery, no manly deodorant undertones thank you very much

reviews.name: AnnaT
reviews.comment: Love it. it is almost all orange on me, but firstly a spicy orange (think of when you peel the skin of an orange off) than a bitter orange. It is not a complicated scent but very sophisticated in light of the sea of sweet perfume popular in these few years. Longevity and sillage is good. It does not strike me as 'sporty' though... I wear it to work.

reviews.name: Ancutza77
reviews.comment: I've sprayed on me and I honestly thought that I've put a man fragrance by mistake. Definitely not my style but reading the positive reviews I'll try one more time

reviews.name: ScarlettX
reviews.comment: This is my favorite perfume from this line of perfumes. It smells sporty indeed, it's exactly citruses and orange blossom. Fresh, uplifting and very nice.

reviews.name: gillbert
reviews.comment: mostly, citrus vibe doesn't last on my skin. i have many sporty fragrances in my collection unfortunately its doesn't' last. this one is better than other fragrances in my collection. mostly i wear it for casual and window shopping.

reviews.name: AmoreProfumi
reviews.comment: I really took a loving to this one, I thought of the 3 the Sexy Amber was my fav. but after wearing this a bit it's surpassed the Amber..This is really nice, clean refreshing, pretty much just citrus & musk. See a few people commenting it smells a lil masc. maybe that's why I like it..It just smells so good! When I had it on though it kept reminding me of something then it hit me, I bought my mom Zen white heat edition, && it kind of favors that. Really happy I just randomly sprayed it on, now it's one of my contenders for a new spring/summer scent. :D

reviews.name: Tovah
reviews.comment: UPDATE: I can smell it now - and I love it! This is fabulous for warm Arizona weather! You know what's scary? Anosmia! It's like being completely numb. No matter how many coffee beans I sniff, or whether this is on a scent strip or my skin, I can't distinguish what it smells like. I know there's a scent there...it's just hiding in that kind of musk that my nose simply cannot detect. So disappointing, because this sounds like something I'd really enjoy. I'll keep trying this in stores, in hopes that one of these days I'll have a "breakthrough". I enjoy so many Michael Kors fragrances I hate being left out by this one.

reviews.name: celebrity89
reviews.comment: By far the best of the three. Almost leans towards cyphre. Sort of like a new and improved Britney Spears Believe. Super ladylike.

reviews.name: Jenyjane
reviews.comment: I have a love/hate relationship with this perfume! The first time I smelled it, it reminded me of the very first "perfume" I had and it came with a Hawaiian Barbie! It was a scented balm and I loved it! I will never forget that first time a scent took me away and I couldn't get enough! However the next time I put it on, a musky spice took over the citrus and it was not my beloved 1st Scent! Now this is like my bi-polar friend, where I never know which one is coming out to play. I haven't figured it out, but the good thing about that musky scent that jumps out at times, is it's a memoir of my late other mother (in law). So technically it's a love/LOVE relationship!!! Should be called Spicy Citrus though for sure!

reviews.name: pretty mini collector
reviews.comment: Top notes: Walking into Goodyear or Freedom tires. Who doesn't love the scent of fresh tires? Middle notes: Pencil shavings. Not freshly sharpened pencils, but more like a pencil bag full of sharpened pencils. Base note: Almost citrus, but more like tennis ball. This is a very *interesting* perfume. It is definitely different than any citrus perfume out there, in the way that it is barely qualified as a citrus perfume. But there's really not a perfume category called "tire shop" or "tennis ball".

reviews.name: elenmart
reviews.comment: I love it! Although I dislike any citrus perfumes in general. This one is different, it has this thick, dark, deep, sexy and classy undertone which is amazing. This undertone is more prominent on me than the citrus itself! Hence, I find this fragrance more night/classy/sexy than day/sporty.

reviews.name: TeacupBreaker
reviews.comment: It's not bad. Sweet, citrusy, and a little on the masculine side. Excellent staying power. Nothing groundbreaking but it gets the job done.

reviews.name: wind83
reviews.comment: i love michael kors, and this citrus is strong, is great to wear for a day not night i guess. and its match for the name sporty citrus.. a little masculine too. i gave 7.8/10

reviews.name: cieciorka
reviews.comment: I liked the smell, but it coused a terrible headache :(

reviews.name: SeleneHdez27
reviews.comment: I agree it´s too masculine, and I smell a lot of pachuli

reviews.name: Togiii
reviews.comment: To me it is a very masculine scent.

reviews.name: harsha.patel1
reviews.comment: I'm not very happy with this perfume. It has no lasting power. It smells ok to start off with, nothing amazing but then adds insult to injury by just disappearing into nothing. What a waste. Not cheap either. Bleh.

reviews.name: evacecilias
reviews.comment: I tried this today for a second time, and was amazed by the little ambery darknes I got from it. This is not only sporty citrus , something way edgy is lurking underneath. Don't get put of by the flanker name , if you're not sporty or dislike citruses. Go ahead try it ! It might appeal to you anyway. Up on my wantlist it goes... Update : Finally own this little piece hidden gem in a bottle ! I would almost say that if YSL Belle d'opium is nighttime for you...thia is a daytime Belle d'Opium. Rich and warm citrus gold , rather than cold citrus silver.. And I get a very SMALL vibe from Palazzo..ha - that'some perfume !

reviews.name: Silences
reviews.comment: I like Michael Kors fragrances and I actually do not mean this in a negative way at all but Sporty Citrus and Avon Imari Blossom smell the same. I was floored!

reviews.name: AWHIFFANDAPRAYER
reviews.comment: I actually have a "hanker for this flanker!" :) I really like this which knocked me over since I usually dislike soapy & citrus, so I couldn't imagine them put together. All I could imagine was throwing a bunch of grapefruit and lemons into your washing machine in the middle of doing a load and that sounded utterly unappealing. There is something really groovy about this and I get a bit of a 1970's Estee vibe coming through. It almost smells like a really fresh deodorant. Inoffensive, safe & just a nice smell. Perfect for the office. I'm also pleasantly surprised at how little of the original notes come through which always makes a perfume that is piggy backing off of an original a bit more interesting.

reviews.name: reborn
reviews.comment: it is neither citrys nor sporty.very ordinary smell and low lasting power

reviews.name: the_new_esta
reviews.comment: The name is a bit misleading - lucky I smelt it anyway. I don't get much citrus, but it is still fresh - just more like gardenia/frangipani fresh. It lingers and is soooo yummy! I got this in a set with the body cream and can't tell whether the cream is just the normal Michael Kors or this one but it's super yummy too. Get your hands on this!

reviews.name: Rene007
reviews.comment: smell like fresh laundry, i also think that is the best from this series,

reviews.name: Mortazuma
reviews.comment: Wearing this...I wouldn't have to tell people that I love citrus...my body chemistry would say it for me because the sillage from this was MONSTROUS. It would have been something I would have sprayed around when I was working in the E.R. What else can smell so clean and bright as lemon? I know others detect orange but all I projected was lemon. Body chemistry is unpredictable at best, hm? I love lemonade, I love citrus, I love Micheal Kor's cosmetics line. However, to spend $60+ to smell like I stuffed my face with a tray of fresh baked lemon bars before hitting the gym is not my idea of a good sports fragrance. Nor is it worth it for me to smell like a living, breathing can of Pledge. Sorry but, not for me. Kudos to all who smell nice in it though!

reviews.name: almarie
reviews.comment: Believe it or not, I actually do like this one. It kinda reminds me of the Givenchy Dahlia L'eau, for some reason. Either way, I do like it, but I will not pay $98.oo USD or whatever it is.

reviews.name: Mirabele
reviews.comment: This one is not what I typically would go for, but I got it as a gift from someone dear. I believe I normally do not like neither sporty nor citrus, so I was afraid to test it for a while. I spent some time sniffing the bottle and trying to prepare myself for the worst – that I would not like it. Luckily, it didn’t happen. I like it and enjoy it. It is very wearable and easy likable. The name is misleading. I was expecting cold fresh generic smell. Luckily, to my nose, it is neither typicall sporty nor dominant citrus. It smells mostly orange blossom to me. There is definitelly citrus there, but not in a refreshing sporty way, but more in a warm and comforting feeling. I would call the perfume something about orange blossom and simple elegance. It is very soft and wearable for work. Normally, I dislike musk, but here I am completely comfortable with it, it is very soft and feels natural. There is some sweetness here, but not too much, and I like that. It is simple lovely skin scent. Sits close to skin. I like it more and more. Longevity may be better; it lasts on my skin for not more than 2-3 hours. I have also a body cream, and it smells very nice. By layering these two the longevity is acceptable.

reviews.name: nicesmell66
reviews.comment: I am not one of those who get compliments about my perfumes on a daily basis. To be honest, it doesn't happen that often. However, Sporty Citrus, is actually the one(apart from Amazing Grace) that people compliments me on quite often. I love to smell nice and I love hearing it. So I tell them it is Sporty Citrus by Michel Kors. Like many others already have said, this name does not fit the perfume at all! It makes me think of a fresh showergel. But I saw the beautiful glassbottle and decided to (still)give it a try. I am glad I did. I spritzed my wrist and had to go back to the store the next day to buy the perfume. I really fell in love with Orange Blossom. Beautiful scent. Not a citrus splitz-splash, but a soft, warm scent that actually stays on my skin quite a while. It is most often only noticed by someone who is sitting close to me. If you are looking for a perfume that that can be smelled after you have left the room, this is not it. Don,t get me wrong. I love TM Angel. But most of the time I want to wear something more soft and feminine. And this is it: Sporty Citrus, IMO, the worst name ever, but at the moment, one of my absolute favourites.

reviews.name: sapphira07
reviews.comment: I like this one the least of all 3. Sexy Amber is my favorite, then Glam Jasmine. I couldn't smell this one very much at all - to me it has weak sillage, and the top notes evaporated so quickly leaving a weak trail of citrusy musk behind. Not a horrible scent, just not really my thing.

reviews.name: Arlene-Beatrix
reviews.comment: The best of 3 Michael Kors new fragrances. Very, very energetic, good to spray in the morning, for a happy day start. Oranges are very noticeable so if you like them, this scent is for you.

reviews.name: naaym.agecra
reviews.comment: I like citrusy scent and I was excited to try this. But this one disappointed me. I didn't like it. I like the Sexy Amber ONLY among the 3 MK samples that I got from Macy's.

reviews.name: 2746cstreet
reviews.comment: This is a rather pretty, musky orange blossom fragrance on me. I don't think the title "Sporty Citrus" is all that apt, and it actually made me hesitate to try it. It's quite warm and feminine, not at all sporty to my perception. Orange is definitely present, but it's not obnoxious, sharp or cleanser smelling.

reviews.name: Newcents
reviews.comment: I also had my interest caught from the scented store flyer and ordered it. It warms on my skin quickly and has a warm musky (in a good way) orange scent. It is not a dusty musk. Everything blends and smells wonderful. Sticks to the listed notes. I have had it on all day now and still smell it on my skin. I didn't notice huge sillage but that's fine with me. I've gotten wisps of it all day. A blind buy and a keeper. It reminds me in a way of a simpler orange version of Hermes Jour De Hermes with a tad more musk. Both are great scents!

reviews.name: ballagirl336
reviews.comment: I am in love with this scent I smelled it in macys and want it sooooo bad

reviews.name: Kaylan
reviews.comment: I didn't think I would like it because "sporty citrus" isn't really my thing. But I gave it a shot and I actually really like it. It's fresh and not too cleaner smelling because of the musk. If you're attracted to sporty scents and are looking for more of a day and nighttime scent I'd go smell this.

reviews.name: Zarya
reviews.comment: The name of the perfume is totally misleading! What a WRONG name for this warm-cold-spicy BEAUTY! It is very similar to Lalique Perles :O which I love and have in my collection. (great all year round scent).

reviews.name: ArtzGirl
reviews.comment: There is something unique about the first spray of this one. I'm liking it so far, and prefer this to the other two that I am reviewing now. The samples came in a threesome - so I'm trying all three at the same time to capture the flavor of what each one is like. This goes on a bit softer than the other two. I like the fruity-tropical smell, it is unique. I haven't smelled anything like this before. I can't wait to see how this one wears. This is my favorite - so far (the early stages of spraying). This has a full bodied chord, very similar to some of Estee Lauder's traditional type fragrances. It reminds me a little bit of the original "White Linen". This is much more fruity than White Linen, but it has a full bodied quality to it that smells like a full rich chord of ingredients.

reviews.name: cherie97
reviews.comment: My favourite out of the three. It smells lovely, natural. Reminds me of flowers and citruses in a bit watered-down version. Opens up fresh and clean, warms on the skin within minutes. It has a good balance between fresh and sweet. Could definitely be my every day scent. The only thing I dislike about Sporty Citrus is it's name. Why call everything citrusy sporty? I find myself rather elegant, I do not pick up sporty clothes, while most of all I do love citrus scents.

reviews.name: Medi
reviews.comment: Now I got it and it smells divine. It so reminds me of Still by J.LO. Longlivity is quite good.Its perfect for every occasion. Dont think its that "sporty". New Love!

reviews.name: sherryberry
reviews.comment: Something about this one reminds me of Stella by Stella McCartney.

reviews.name: katjoeus
reviews.comment: Smelled this in a magazine and fell in love!! Very disappointed to find out this is only sold at Macy's as very hard to get a better deal...Will have to get it at some point, though, as it's the most wonderful scent I've discovered in a LONG time - and I do love my perfumes!! Would have to agree, though, that the name doesn't do it justice - it's much, much nicer than a "sporty" scent (of which I have a number that I usually wear to the gym). This one is def. a very, very feminine, all day long type of fragrance! If anyone bought it and doesn't like it, please contact me :-)

reviews.name: chillalot
reviews.comment: I loved the smell of this in the macys circular..I was tempted to blind buy it..the bottle is very cute too..sprayed it on today and its very loud/too strong and gives me a headache...so now I'm sitting on the couch instead of being all sporty

reviews.name: genny17
reviews.comment: I tried the 3, and they are all nice! Sporty Citrus surprised me, it isnt a lemony outdoorsy scent. it is like a soft veil of a fresh scent, nicely done, feminine, fresh, easy to wear, uplifting. It is smooth, nice, I really think I might buy it! I dont find the lemon to be harsh, more a blend of citrus and musk, which produces a light interesting scent.

reviews.name: sallyjwong
reviews.comment: I love this one! The sales lady at Macy's convinced me to try it, and I'm glad I did. The name "Sporty" does not fit this fragrance. On my body chemistry, I get a lovely combination of orange blossoms and musk, which smells like soft spices mixed with warmth on my skin.

reviews.name: grace2424
reviews.comment: just got this today...impulse buy. I hope I love it like the original Michael Kors!

reviews.name: jonr951
reviews.comment: This is definitely the best of the bunch. I definitely need to test it more. Of course Macy's didn't have samples to take home. : (

reviews.name: KelseyRaven
reviews.comment: Smelled it at Macy's, and it's exactly what I thought it would be. Strong citrus with a bit of musk.

title: Amalfi Citrus Alfred Dunhill cologne - a fragrance for men 2020
description: Amalfi Citrus by Alfred Dunhill is a Woody Floral Musk fragrance for men. Amalfi Citrus was launched in 2020. The nose behind this fragrance is Olivier Gillotin. Top notes are Lemon, Bergamot, Green Mandarin, Mandarin Orange and Bitter Orange; middle notes are Neroli, Green Tea, Rosemary, Jasmine and Cardamom; base notes are Vetiver, Musk, Woody Notes and Amber.

pros.label: Superior performance compared to rival colognes
Shines in high heat and humidity

cons.label: Projection becomes skin scent quickly
Redundant if you own similar neroli scents
Requires frequent reapplication during the day
Incredible opening settles very fast
Leans too mature for younger audiences
Performance is inconsistent across skin types
Citrus notes can feel overly bitter
Weak performance at full retail price
Linear scent profile lacks evolution
Bulky bottle design impractical for travel

thisPerfumeRemindsMeOf.title: Acqua di Parma Blu Mediterraneo Arancia di Capri

peopleWhoLikeThisAlsoLike.title: The One for Men Eau de Parfum
Gentleman Eau de Parfum Reserve Privée

reviews.name: chatters1981
reviews.comment: Incredibly fresh, uplifting, zingy and refreshing scent for summer. Tons of citrus and neroli up top. It's basically a longer lasting, much cheaper version of Neroli Portofino by Tom Ford (notes are very similar across the board). For the price, it's a great fragrance.

reviews.name: DrNDJas
reviews.comment: Unremarkable in a good way. Citrus fresh? Check Well blended? Check Smooth character? Check Understated gentlemanly projection? Check Like a good, decent British chap of upstanding character clad in fine Jermyn Street tailoring. When standing out in the crowd simply won’t do, this is a very fine choice. You’ll smell properly clean and presentable in any occasion.

reviews.name: Fa0012
reviews.comment: بالفعل قريب جدا لارتزيان بيور حمضي، عشبي، بعض الورود صعب جدا تفرق بينهم، يكاد يكون عطر تقليد له. الفروقات بسيطة وقد لايشمها ويقدرها الا انف متمرس هذا اكثر حمضية قليلا بشكل بالكاد ملحوظ حتى لو قلت انك تريد نسخه اعلى جودة من ارتزيان بيور فالجودة متطابقة. هذا وارتزيان بيور فيهم رائحة كريهة اندولية andolic قليلا من الياسمين بالاضافة لنوتة مثل فاكهة متعفنة جميل ٧.٥/١٠ - Very close to Artisan Pure Citrusy, herbal, with some florals It’s very hard to tell them apart, it’s almost like a clone. The differences are minimal and may only be noticed and appreciated by a trained nose This one is slightly more citrusy, but barely noticeable Even if you’re looking for a higher quality version of Artisan Pure, the quality is basically identical Both this and Artisan Pure have a slightly unpleasant indolic facet from jasmine Along with a note that smells like, almost rotten fruit Nice 7.5/10

reviews.name: mostafa385
reviews.comment: Performance was very underwhelming specially in terms of projection maybe the longevity was ok but I had to dig my nose to smell it. I did not really know how this smells in the air. I over sprayed & still got the same experience. The scent itself is nice & uplifting but the very limited projection ruins the overall experience.

reviews.name: TheDogfatherHTX
reviews.comment: Continuing my time with Dunhill's Signature Collection, I now move from the cool, icy atmosphere of Nordic Fougère to the sunlit shores of Amalfi Citrus, trading Nordic fjords for the bright, coastal warmth of the Amalfi Coast. Where the previous fragrance leaned into crisp, cool, airy freshness, this one suggests a shift toward something more radiant and uplifting, built around clarity, vibrancy, and that effortless Mediterranean brightness. The name alone evokes images of citrus groves, ocean air, and sun-soaked landscapes, setting the expectation for a fragrance that prioritizes freshness and refinement over depth. Let’s see if this one truly captures that sun-drenched citrus glow once it hits the skin. Opening: The opening is citrusy, floral, and aromatic. The primary notes I get are lemon, neroli, mandarin, and green tea, with lemon and neroli leading the composition. Lemon immediately takes the forefront with a sharp, bright, and zesty burst of citrus that feels crisp and energetic. There is a subtle aromatic bitterness present as well, likely coming from the bergamot, but the overall impression remains distinctly lemon-driven. Neroli follows closely, introducing a clean white floral note with a distinctly soapy and solar quality that adds a polished and refined freshness to the opening. Supporting these, the mandarin notes, both green mandarin and mandarin orange, layer in additional citrus nuance. They come across as juicier and slightly sweeter than the lemon, while the green mandarin adds a faintly fresh, green undertone. There are also subtle hints of rindy bitterness from the bitter orange woven into the citrus blend, adding complexity and preventing the opening from feeling overly sweet. Lastly, green tea contributes a soft, airy aromatic quality with gentle leafy and slightly earthy undertones, bringing a sense of calm and balance to what is otherwise a bright and invigorating introduction. The result is a vibrant and refined opening where sparkling citrus, clean florals, and airy aromatics come together in a fresh and uplifting composition very reminiscent of Tom Ford's Neroli Portofino or John Varvatos's Artisan Pure. Dry Down: The dry down is floral, aromatic, and citrusy. The primary notes I get are neroli, green tea, lemon, and bergamot, with neroli and green tea leading the composition. This stage leans more heavily into the floral and aromatic facets of the fragrance, with neroli providing a clean, soapy, and radiant white floral presence that feels smooth and refined. Alongside it, the green tea becomes more pronounced, offering a soft, green, and slightly powdery aromatic quality that adds a calming, almost serene character to the dry down. Lemon shifts into a supporting role, contributing a gentle tartness and brightness that helps lift the composition without dominating it as it did in the opening. Finally, bergamot blends seamlessly into the citrus accord, reinforcing that subtle aromatic bitterness and adding a touch of refinement. The result is a smooth and airy continuation of the opening where clean florals and soft aromatics take the lead, supported by a restrained citrus brightness. Performance: Projection opens at a strong level, reaching out to about five to six feet within the first hour and establishing a noticeable presence. Sillage follows closely behind, leaving an easily perceptible scent trail that carries the fragrance’s bright and clean character. After approximately three hours, performance begins to soften slightly, with projection settling around four to five feet and the scent trail becoming quieter, though still noticeable. As the fragrance continues to develop, it gradually tapers off. Around the seven to eight hour mark, it transitions into a skin scent, suggesting that the most effective window for projection and sillage sits within the first five to six hours. When to Wear: Amalfi Citrusy is best suited for spring and summer, where its bright, clean profile can shine in warmer conditions. The fragrance feels most natural during the daytime, when its fresh and uplifting character aligns with lighter, more casual settings. It works well for everyday wear and is particularly well suited for the office, where its airy and unobtrusive presence feels polished and appropriate. However, its light and refreshing nature makes it less ideal for special occasions or formal environments, where a fragrance with more depth and presence would typically be preferred. Blind Buy: If you are a fan of fragrances in the style of Tom Ford Neroli Portofino or John Varvatos Artisan Pure, Amalfi Citrus will likely feel familiar and comfortable, making it a fairly safe blind buy within that category. The scent profile stays true to that citrusy, floral, and slightly soapy DNA without introducing anything particularly challenging or unexpected. For those who are unsure about that style, however, sampling is recommended, as this type of bright neroli-forward composition can be somewhat divisive depending on personal taste. Final Verdict: Amalfi Citrus closely echoes my experiences with Tom Ford Neroli Portofino and John Varvatos Artisan Pure. Neroli Portofino is a fragrance that never quite worked for me, largely due to the “bug spray” quality that I and many other people associate with it. Artisan Pure, on the other hand, was far more enjoyable thanks to its stronger emphasis on citrus over florals. Amalfi Citrus falls somewhere in between the two, resulting in a fragrance that I can appreciate and enjoy, but not one that fully captures my attention. I will say that this is the most unisex-leaning fragrance I have experienced so far from Dunhill's Signature line, and the performance is respectable for this type of bright, citrus-driven profile. It successfully captures the feeling of its namesake, delivering a clean, sunlit freshness that feels true to the Amalfi Coast inspiration. That said, while it is well executed, it ultimately does not offer enough distinction or personal appeal for me to justify seeking out a bottle. It is a solid and well-crafted fragrance, just not one that I feel the need to add to my collection. Scent: 7/10 Longevity: 6/10 Sillage: 6/10 Versatility: Average Compliment Factor: High Gender: Unisex, leaning masculine Value: Fair

reviews.name: Garykenneth
reviews.comment: Nice classy fragrance. All the Dunhill's I have purchased from discounters have had a Made in the U S A label on them. Interesting to me as we make few fragrances here. Great price on this closed out brand at discounters.

reviews.name: Sloth
reviews.comment: Very much reminds me of Creeds ‘Royal Water’. Citrus and musk basically. It lacks the mint of Royal Water, but gains a little neroli, so they’re not identical just similar. Starts out very much citrus dominant, quite sharp and a little sour but in a good way. As it dries down the citrus remains, albeit more subdued and you notice a hint of neroli. However this is not a neroli fragrance as such so don’t think your getting that as you will be disappointed. Dry down is a nice clean musk, over the now soft citrus with neroli shyly hesitating in the background. Not a beast by any means but great for casual and office for those who prefer to whisper not shout. Safe blind buy (if you must) for those who like this scent profile but only if discounted to below or around £50. It lasts several hours. I won’t state specifically as it will vary from person to person, but it’s certainly not only 30 mins or as much as 8 hours. It does however become soft and gentle after an hour, maybe two. Ignore posts claiming extreme ends of longevity, they are simply untrue. Sloth.

reviews.name: docelder
reviews.comment: Going to date myself here. This reminds me of Hai Karate. The 60's and 70's after shave that had the tagline "Be careful how you use it" It had , best that I recall bergamot, lemon, heliotrope and some musk. Probably some more things, but I was a kid when I got a bottle at a discount store that sold dented can items and the black wellington boots I got every year (a size too big so I could grow into them) before school year started. I had a couple dollars saved up, smelled it and talked my mom into letting me get it. But to the review, when I sprayed this one, it all came back. This Amalfi Citrus could have come from the 70's. It has a barbershop vibe to it. I like it and mostly wear it around home when I feel nostalgic for the old days. It lasts a few hours. I like this, but people who want something longer lasting may not. It's maybe comparable to a Varvatos blend in longevity and projection. It would project 1-2 hours and you can sort of smell yourself for 4-6 hours when you move around. something good for the office when you have a meeting in the conference room coming up and don't want your cologne to be what people remember after the meeting. Side note: If I ever found something that smelled like Burma Shave that would be great.

reviews.name: That_Guy
reviews.comment: Just got this in the mail, and I'm disappointed but only slightly. From the description I got from others, I thought this was going to open with a bright and juicy citrus, but the citrus is actually very dry and somewhat sour. Then you get an overdose of neroli which gives the scent a strong white floral and green soapyness. I'm surprised no one has made this connection yet, but it smells almost exactly like Irish Spring soap. Before I encountered this fragrance, I thought Mugler Cologne was the closest thing to Irish Spring soap, but Amalfi Citrus took it to another level. In my opinion, if you took Mugler Cologne and combined it with Prada L'Homme L'Eau, that's pretty much what Amalfi Citrus smells like to me. It's natural-smelling citrusy white florals with a green undertone and some light musk. That's not to say this fragrance doesn't have its own character and depth though. Thanks to the combination of green tea and amber, there are many layers to the dry down which is a trait you normally only experience with niche fragrances. You pick up something different every time you go in for a whiff, and I give high praise to fragrances that are able to achieve that. There's also something sweet in the dry down that gives off a Lemonhead candy type of vibe. That might be my favorite part, but, at times, that same note can come off as Lemon Pledge if you get too close. I tend to judge fragrances based on their smell and not performance, but I can definitely see this one being a weak performer if that matters to you. Citrus fragrances are notorious for having poor performance, so that's just something you're going to have to accept. If the citrus was more juicy off the top, this would be a Must Buy recommendation for spring and summer, but due to its sour and soapy nature, I can only give this a strong Must Try recommendation for people looking for a citrus fragrance with other nuances. Overall Score: 8.75/10 The scent is more complex than it initially appears, but the opening is not something that will excite most people. I'm very glad I have this in my collection, but if you miss out on picking this one up while it's available, just go get "Under the lemon trees" by Maison Margiela. I think it does the same thing but maybe just a little bit better.

reviews.name: JRR4
reviews.comment: Just purchased this and the Agar wood Signature scent after some research here and on youtube. Gotta say this and French Avenue Aether are my two favorite citrus-type scents. Good for warmer weather. Tempted to get a second bottle for backup from Jomashop ($52 approx currently) as I hear they are scaling back their cologne production.

reviews.name: zoler
reviews.comment: I don’t know what kind of fakes people below me bought on eBay, but this perfume is actually really good. For a summer scent, 4–5 hours of longevity with moderate projection is more than enough.

reviews.name: Felixthreeone
reviews.comment: Decided to add this one to the collection after reading reviews here and elsewhere. Waste of time/money for me. As alluded to below, very faint. Opens strong, practically gone in less than 30 minutes. Smelled great when it was detectable. The neroli is well done. But there is no point in this at all if it cannot be smelled. Even the Artisan pure performs better than this. I'm disappointed, but not terribly surprised. Many of the higher-rated fragrances on this site aren't worthy of the rating. And there are MANY that have low scores that should be tops. Just the way it goes, I guess. Oh well. This one will be easy to give away to one of my friends that doesn't like strong fragrances.

reviews.name: timmybils
reviews.comment: This was a citrus bomb for 5 minutes, then it was a big green fragrance. Faint. Warm to hot weather only. The dry down reminds me of the Dunhill Icon in the silver bottle. I would not necessarily rank it second best in this Dunhill line, but it's not bad. Spring/Summer wearable.

reviews.name: jojohanhan
reviews.comment: Lemon citrusy opening, great for hot weather. Drydown into green herbacious. If artisan pure for younger male, this leaning toward older male in late 30 to 40s. Sophisticated!!

reviews.name: gio
reviews.comment: Masterpiece so elegant fresh clean.. reminds me of Moschino Friends Allure Sport {Cologne} edition

reviews.name: ScentManJojo
reviews.comment: Tart zingy citrus from the start. The opening, for a citrus scent, does last close to 2 hours for me. After it's a closer to the skin vetiver citrus mix with some amber. Quality of the fragrance is fantastic. Most online retailers have this at an amazing price. Def Full Bottle Worthy... Enjoy!

reviews.name: Smirky
reviews.comment: Opens with a very strong citrus. Struck me more as lemony than orange or grapefruit. Very fresh and does not smell synthetic at all. My first thought was oh, another Xerjoff Fiero. But no, as Amalfi dried down I got more of the light florals combined with the woody notes. Very pleasant and refreshing. I will definitely be adding it to my summer rotation. I tested Amalfi Citrus on a very cold day (subzero) and was expecting poor performance. It actually wasn't that bad. Longevity was a good 6 hours with only average projection. It will certainly perform even better when I wear it in summer.

reviews.name: SweetPeaPea
reviews.comment: Citrusy 4711 opening with a clean almost pool chlorine (in a good way) dry down. I wonder how long it will last... Icon is still the winner here.

reviews.name: awgg
reviews.comment: Very lemony feel to it and its quite refreshing scent I had a few mls left in a bottle from my family to try, I'm quite unsure if i will buy it as i find it okay at best, personally i would not wear it but that's just me. Silliage is mid, Not that long lasting and sticks to the skin just an okay green fresh scent

reviews.name: DallasM
reviews.comment: This opens with a bold bitter citrus reminiscent of a bitter yellow grapefruit. The opening is not synthetic at all. Later a classy floral citrus dry down replaces the bitterness, still with a bitter citrus grapefruit smell. At first I was turned off by the bitterness but I like it more and more as I have worn it. I put this into my rotation in the bold citrus category b/c of its differences with others on this list: Silver Mountain Water (Bold Citrus Lemon), and Versace Eros Energy (Bold Citrus Orange). Like I said, this scent grows on you. The longevity could be better, but in terms of citrus scents it does well (Not many of these ever reach beast mode)

reviews.name: Sloth
reviews.comment: Mont Blanc Explorer?....are you kidding? At no level or stage does this share anything with MBE other than they are both a fragrance.

reviews.name: rezafarokhi
reviews.comment: Very similiar to Montblanc Explorer at base notes

reviews.name: Mahdi ellahi
reviews.comment: mostly linear lime and iso e super scent with slight hints of rosemary, white florals and green notes. late dry down is still citrusy and slightly woody musky. very good longevity and performance. i would choose artisan pure personally, that one in not as linear or iso e super heavy as this one.

reviews.name: Apocalypse6vs8
reviews.comment: A very easy wearing fragrance. A nice burst in the opening of a citrus & green like aroma that has a juicy tartness to it. That probably lasts 10-15 minutes and for me Amalfi Citrus goes into a slight woods & aromatic vibe with a tinge of a floral component. Very subtle but, it adds an element of airy class with deeper wood accords. This is not a screamer of a fragrance nor will it pop off of one's skin announcing you arrived at the party. Rather for me, it delivers a nice mellow clean & classy vibe. The dry down is slightly sweet with a tea like note and soft musks that actually blend in well with my skin. It's a simple citrus, woods & musk creation that exudes a chill and laid back vibe through & through. Longevity is actually quite good for me with this juice lasting close to 8 hours though in the final 1/3 it sits very close to the skin as if it were a clean musky morning dew. Projection is not its strong point, 45 minutes to maybe an hour tops but, again that's just on me. It really is a very well done chill fragrance for the laid back individual that doesn't want to be front & center in any summer or warm weather activity. If you want something that may garner more attention and will give you a livelier wear, there are others that will take you there like JV Artisan Pure, TF Private Blend Neroli Portofino line up & Mandarino plus niche quality scents like Xerjoff Torino21 & 23, ADP Colonia line and a couple from Mancera. An honorable mention to Al Haramain's Portfolio Neroli Canvas (excellent summer vibe fragrance) with more punch and arguably better or worse performance. They are all louder than Amalfi Citrus by Dunhill but, this one is the casual & laid back king of backyard summer outings and cool summer night's out. And for the money and real good quality of fragrance composition, it's definitely worthy of consideration and a real good bang for buck creation.

reviews.name: Frasknas
reviews.comment: This definitely comes across as a lighter more citrusy Icon. And I'm all for it. Can't speak for perfomance yet. But it seems decent. All in an solid scent. And love the bottle design.

reviews.name: ahmedzkhan
reviews.comment: Dissapointing. Not really citrus and neroli so much as it is rosemary and cyprioil. Initially very similar to Dunhill Century, which is a cyprioil bomb. Dry down is a bit more refined, the vetiver and neroli coming thru ever so slightly, but still over powered by the strong lemon. Really wish it had more of the fresh citrus, bitter citrus, neroli and jasmine notes, with a vetiver backdrop and dry down, but not to be. Overall, just ok, nothing really special at all, 100% for hot, hot weather, and a bit on the younger side.

reviews.name: Thisisali
reviews.comment: This is surprisingly a great citrus scent that actually performs, bitter orange, neroli and vetiver are quite prominent here, it's abit bitter "orange leaves kind of bitterness". I recommend it for hot, humid wheather, not cool springs. It cuts thru the heat and humidity and shines, the more you sweat the better it gets. Suitable for day wear, at the office or getting stuff done. Can be dressed up too. Not a generic citrus profile. Allow it to macerate, it will shine brighter and stronger. I dont see strong resemblance to artisan pure. Shares only the neroli aspect with Tom Ford NP, but this certainly performs better and is more "Beafy". and not as zingy or sparkly as NP. Longivety is above average or moderate especially for a citrus fragrance. I'd say it's long lasting. To my taste it's the best in the line. If you want a semi bitter citrus scent that smells natural and actually performs, go for it.

reviews.name: jsu888
reviews.comment: Soapy. Generic. Agar, Egyptian Smoke, Sandalwood are more interesting.

reviews.name: jlog23
reviews.comment: Smoother, less astringent than Artisan Pure. More citrus, less white florals. Seems to perform a little better as well. Happy to have both! Pick this one up neroli lovers.

reviews.name: chrisk252
reviews.comment: This definitely reminds me of John Varvatos Artisan Pure. Refreshing transparent crisp citrus with a light woody background. A little less of the icy vibes of Artisan Pure, but very similar. Maybe a touch denser and more natural. Might be redundant if I already have Artisan Pure, but nice to know this is out there for when I run out. Scent: 8.5 / Sillage: 7 / Longevity: 7

reviews.name: perlindvall
reviews.comment: Na, Dunhill Edition is much better and actually a fantastic perfume. This one is just flat and boring, I can hardly even smell it, whatever it is.

reviews.name: Unmodernize
reviews.comment: I had hopes but this is not living up to them. Got it for 70 bucks so i would be pissed if i payed 150 (advice never pay heavily for light citrus scents). I like Dunhill and their style. I like that this is classic gentleman, I picture a guy who was 20 years old in 1978 showing up in a white shirt at a function wearing this. This is dry citrus neroli, but note it is like 70% neroli and not much else getting to shine. I can’t smell the vetiver or rosemary for example. I see what people say about Artisan Pure, but this is actually also like Morillas offbeat release Gucci Guilty Cologne. Anyone in Stockholm and want to buy for a very cheap price? Hit me up.

reviews.name: JusttheFactsMan
reviews.comment: #531 in my "Tour de Fragrance" So far I am as impressed with the Dunhill "Signature Collection" as I was unimpressed with the "Icon" series which was very humdrum and not at all iconic. This is slightly more potent than EDTs Bvlgari Aqva Amara or Dior Eau Sauvage, also very neroli forward fragrances like this, as are Versace Pour Homme, Acqua di Gio, but not as sour and astringent as the ADG. I you like neroli and don't already have a citrus-neroli freshie for summer that is not sweet, this is the juice. If Varvatos Artisan Pure had a ton of neroli added it might smell like this, but it doesn't. As a long time fan of Dior Eau Savage I concur this is similar enough to be a replacement, and at $55 it's way cheaper than the Dior. So if you're looking to avoid wasting money (as all smart consumers should be) this is Dior Eau Sauvage for half price with better than the EDT performance of Eau Sauvage. So when my bottle of Eau Sauvage runs dry, and it's getting close, I'll replace it with this. Maybe business wear for just a day at the office, but I wouldn't wear this to high level meetings or for formal wear. Spring and Summer, daytime casual wear for the beach, even gym wear. Very versatile.

reviews.name: Owen Bawn
reviews.comment: Hot summer night. The air thick and close. A swim in the moonlight. Dry off and to bed. Three or five healthy sprays of Amalfi Citrus and lights out, a silent overhead fan moving the air. Citrus sweet and bitter. Green jasmine tea, and a long clean soapy dry down. I wouldn't wear this to work, but I like this fragrance for summer weekends.

reviews.name: Trung
reviews.comment: Very closed to Artisan Pure

reviews.name: mravenec
reviews.comment: One can sense that this is done by a French perfumer - it's a French interpretation of an Eau de Cologne. Light, airy, classy, sophisticated. Very soft and has a soapy quality, but a very high quality premium soap. A beautifully blended creation for a gentleman. Got this for less than half of the original price at a discounters and could not be happier.

reviews.name: Takuuuya
reviews.comment: トップからシトラス、ネロリで僅かに苦味を感じるとてもフレッシュな香りが弾けます。トムフォードのNeroli Portofinoに少し似ていると感じました。シトラス系は香りの持続時間が短いので数時間で消えてしまいますが、しつこさのない涼しげな香りは暑い季節にぴったりです。 6.7/10

reviews.name: LMBelvi
reviews.comment: This starts with a very tart bitter citrus which I quite enjoy. That stays maybe a half hour. Next phase is much softer and kind of nondescript blending of musk floral and wood. Longevity about three hours. I wouldn’t buy more than the sample but I enjoyed trying.

reviews.name: Arguss
reviews.comment: It’s nice. My first Dunhill, I was expecting something greener/sweet but it’s very masculine and different. Something to wear during summer, spring or maybe fall. 👍

reviews.name: Mina Abdo
reviews.comment: Good citruses combo. Suitable when its 40+ degree outside.

reviews.name: LMBelvi
reviews.comment: Very sharp and clean citrus floral. Mature, not playful. Even though it’s citrusy I wouldn’t call it sporty. You’d wear this to be taken seriously.

reviews.name: Endlessfumes
reviews.comment: squeezed and mushed grass and lemon peel. LOVE IT

reviews.name: buzzmander
reviews.comment: Wonderful scent! Nice and fresh. I did not detect musk, but that's not a concern for me. It's likely there, just in the midst of the other notes, blended very well. Four hours after application, I cannot detect even a skin scent. It's weak juice, but beautiful while it lasts. Knowing this, I'll carry a decant with me to re-apply throughout the day. This is one of my top warm season fragrances now.

reviews.name: tecfield
reviews.comment: Very soft, smooth, and settle citrussy and aromatic fragrance. Silage and longevity are both below average. This needs to be stronger. It has a super heavy bottle. You cannot carry it around to reapply. But it smells good while it lasts. To me this is very unisex.

reviews.name: Dandy1980
reviews.comment: The best fresh fragrance in my collection. Very underrated.

reviews.name: AndySmellsGreat
reviews.comment: Totally underrated. I have an extensive perfume collection (as per profile) and for the money the Dunhill Signature series packs a whallop. This is an oily tart bitter lemon rind on sweet Neroli and a touch of greeness thanks to some rosemary. It has a very slightly soapy drydown almost having a laundered feel. Very fresh and uplifting. Somewhat reminiscent of Eau Sauvage but focused on the citrus fruit experience on a linear plain - Negative reviews can be dismissed as bargain basement hunters expecting Xerjoff performance. Has a stronger sillage than you may register - my only complaint is after 30 minutes the amazing opening settles powering away in a linear fashion becoming anosmic - though other people keep commenting how strong and good it is when you think it’s gone. Great buy, as was Nordic Fougere (also the subject of mundane reviews) for which its performance and offer is completely misrepresented on Fragrantica. I own quite a few from the series and again for the money they are pretty schmick and definitely for enthusiast fragheads looking for something very well made for not much money. I’ve reached out to some fellow collectors involved in the industry and we agree Signature line is sadly a well kept secret.

reviews.name: Moode
reviews.comment: I love citrus fragrance. I own Tom Ford Mandarino di amalfi, Neroli Portofino and Acqua di parma. I bought this after some hype. And i have too say that this is my new Favourite fragrance. Wow, it is so good!

reviews.name: kapaluamak
reviews.comment: Just a quick comment. I will prefer a cheaper version of a more expensive brand when possible. (For instance, I prefer Detour Noir vs Layton) Dunhill Amalfi Citrus is often compared to John Varvatos Artisan Pure. but Dunhill Amalfi Citrus is so superior to John Varvatos Artisan Pure that the extra money you spend on Dunhill is well worth it. Amalfi Citrus is a little better in virtually every category one would rank a fragrance, except for price; and the difference in price really isn't that big.

reviews.name: Dixieland Delight
reviews.comment: This is very high quality, superior JV Artisan pure. It's much better, but that vibe is unmistakeable. This is actually one of the better citrus fragrances than I have smelled in recent years. I have my favorites, Frency Lavande etc., but this smells of very high quality bordering on niche level quality. If you love bright, bitter, crisp green citrus aromatics - this is a must buy, simple as that. As a comparison, the citrus notes and quality level destroys Dior Eua Sauvage. This is more on par with a Xerjoff Fiero or something in that class. Rating: 8.8

reviews.name: FirstSense
reviews.comment: If you name a fragrance "Amalfi Citrus", than you know what to expect. As the great citrus lover and collector that I am offcourse I was interested in the heavy discounted 100 ml bulky box and bottle from Dunhill. This one opens indeed with a pleasant lemon green mandarin and bitter orange. It's not a blast but very gentle and not sharp. It projects decent in the first two hours and after that Citrus Amalfi becomes a real nice slightly bitter spicy lemon neroli smell. Inoffensive and perfect for office or official meetings and that kind of activities. I also detect a very nice lemon soap accord that is very energetic and sparkling until the end. So there is some high concentrated lemon oil in this one that combines greatly with the tea note and yasmin and the other mentioned rather masculine notes. AC reminds me of Dior Eau Sauvage Cologne and John Varvatos Artisan Pure. This rather modest citrus edp feels pleasant in the fall and winter because of the vetiver, wood and amber in the rather masculine base. I wonder how Amalfi Citrus behaves in the spring and summer because the performance is not that great but I really like the smell so I will be over spraying this one for shure.

reviews.name: OversprayEverything
reviews.comment: I really like heavy glass bottles, so I like the bottle pretty well. However, this is a very weak performer, and at the supposed retail price of $100 or $150 or whatever, I would tell you that this is a very bad value. Right now, you can get these for around $60, and it still has pretty weak performance at that price. But it still smells fine and is pleasant.

reviews.name: brand117
reviews.comment: This is also a personal favorite of mine- such a pretty, light citrus scent. What it lacks in longevity it makes up for in depth and complexity.

reviews.name: Oscarjavier
reviews.comment: Very very nice fragance. It's a love. An easy 9/10. Now, I'm waiting for Agar Wood from the same line for this fall/winter.

reviews.name: danshobiz
reviews.comment: I saw a couple of very positive reviews on this and, being a citrus fragrance lover, felt I needed to try it. Have to admit the name caught my eye as well, as I’m planning a trip to il bel paese. My verdict: very impressive! The juice looks beautiful and goes on quite fresh, tangy, juicy and GREEN. Some florals and tea notes underneath and then settles in nice and soapy. It’s a beautifully understated masculine fragrance. By that I mean it leans masculine, certainly more than most neroli releases, but it’s not in your face like Bvlgari Wood Neroli, which I like but find they overamped the ambery-woody drydown. It wears pretty close to the skin like most true citrus frags, so you can go a little heavier on the trigger to lengthen your enjoyment of it without destroying nearby nostrils. You won’t smell it after 3-4 hours (others will if close by) so you’ll want to re-apply. It can’t match the Zegna Essenze releases which are the ultimate in masculine citrus (and last forever), but I’m wearing and enjoying the heck out of the Dunhill in my humid climate. As noted, the cap and bottle are impressive to see on your counter but overly difficult to work with, but worth it to get at what’s within.

reviews.name: omega_garlic
reviews.comment: Yeah, juicy citrus. It's in the name. But where this really shines for me is the drydown after the citrus compounds have evaporated - superbly well blended green floral smoothness. Worth the current price at discounters, probably my 2nd favourite from this line, right after Agar Wood. Great summer scent, not too cloying at all but noticeable after hours.

reviews.name: TomW
reviews.comment: A very well done citrus perfect for warm weather, described by Persolaise as “sunshine in a bottle” and I couldn’t agree more. Refreshing and rindy with a nice drydown. I can’t vouch for the similarities with Tom Ford’s Neroli Portofino as I’ve not smelled that! This has to be one of the rare perfumes I’ve tried where I can actually detect most of the notes. The citrus and neroli radiate off my skin beautifully while the cardamom and jasmine stay closer, mixed with the light touches of the Vetiver. I own a few citrus scents and thought this might be a bit samey but it stands out on its own. Very nice! Not a huge fan of the bulky bottle and cumbersome cap however, but that’s a minor criticism.

reviews.name: PushaHndrxx
reviews.comment: Really well blended citrus scent. To me this comes across strikingly similar to John Varvatos artisan pure, but with more lemon. For a fresh citrus scent, performance is pretty good with 3-4 hours of solid projection before the soft spices and woods come out and it turns into a skin scent, for around $60 this is a really good pickup. Definitely my favorite from the line that I’ve tried. I’m trying to get my hands on Egyptian smoke and Nordic Fougere

reviews.name: a7_mad1991
reviews.comment: bright sparkling citrussy scent However, its quite linear and does not offer anything exciting projection is pretty weak

reviews.name: pauladeyemo
reviews.comment: I think this is a very nice fragrance if you like your citrus'. To the mainstream nose this may see just seem like a bitter mess. Opening is a very strong blast of the top citrus notes until it dries down to a slightly floral and cardamon middle and then woody base. Reminds me of Tom Ford Neroli Portofino of course.mixed with 4711 but with slightly better performance in terms of sillage. But like most citrus fragrances, the performance is not still not great but that is what you will expect from a citrus fragrance. If you like your citrus' you can't go wrong with this one and can't wait to put it on in the summer where it wlll shine for Real.

reviews.name: JanioScents
reviews.comment: Amalfi Citrus is Awesome its very citrusy to the fullest. i like how its all about citrus it gets, the Accord notes Blended really well very refreshing citrus. if you guys lookin for all year around fragrance in the tropical this would be it.

title: Smoky Citrus Amouroud perfume - a fragrance for women and men 2022
